title: ADR-0001: Single-VPS-per-install (v1) + future Connection entity migration plan
description:
WHAT
====
ADR-0001 in docs/adr/0001-single-vps-per-install-v1.md per §4.1. Documents the decision: in v1, a Hoopoe install pairs with EXACTLY ONE VPS, and that VPS holds N projects. VPS state and project state are two independent lifecycles, not one combined machine. The VPS must reach 'ready' before any project on it can advance past 'imported'.

WHY
====
Multi-VPS adds significant complexity (Connection entity in domain model, profile management, switching, per-VPS auth) that doesn't justify itself for v1's solo-builder + small-team use cases. The implicit single Connection has no UI + no schema presence in v1.

MIGRATION PLAN (FUTURE)
=========================
When multi-VPS becomes a real need:
- Connection entity owns VPS state.
- Projects key to a Connection.
- UI gains Connection picker (possibly merged with Project picker).
- Auth model gains per-Connection bearers.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §4.1 — VPS lifecycle vs project lifecycle.
- ADR template per industry standard (Status / Context / Decision / Consequences).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- ADR-0001 written + committed under docs/adr/.
- Cross-references to §4.1 + relevant code paths.
- Future migration plan documented but not implemented.

title: Phase 7: Bead detail drawer (overview, context, deps, traceability, mail, files, tests, commits, findings, audit)
description:
WHAT
====
Bead detail drawer (slide-over from the right) showing every facet of a bead per §7.2 'Bead detail drawer'. Tabs / sections:
- Overview: title, ID, status, priority, owner, age, ETA, lifecycle history.
- Full context: description, acceptance criteria, links to plan section.
- Dependencies: incoming + outgoing; click to navigate.
- Plan traceability: from traceability.json — which plan sections this implements.
- Mail thread: Agent Mail thread for this bead (thread_id = bead-ID).
- Files / reservations: files-touched (from implementation-evidence ledger), current reservations.
- Tests / health: last build/test result, current coverage/complexity for files-touched.
- Commits: from implementation-evidence ledger.
- Review findings: posts attached to this bead (Phase 12).
- Audit history: every action that touched this bead.

DESIGN
======
- Sticky header with bead ID + title + status pill + priority chip + owner agent.
- Tab navigation for the sections.
- Each tab loads on demand to keep TTI snappy.
- Files-touched section shows code from the local clone (§7.7) with current coverage gutter (§7.4.1).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 — Views, drawer.
- §7.4.1 — per-bead 'files touched' health rollup (Phase 11 wires fully).
- §7.7 — local clone for file content.
- §15 #11-12 — evidence graph.
- audit log epic.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Drawer state persisted in URL so deep links work.
- Performance: opening a drawer should not re-fetch the entire bead list; cache via TanStack Query.
- Files-touched preview uses ripgrep on local clone for fast highlight; falls back to daemon RPC for VPS-WIP files (§7.7).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Drawer renders all 10 sections.
- Tab loading on demand.
- Deep links work.
- Code preview from local clone with coverage gutter (Phase 11 fully wires gutter; Phase 7 just shows code).
- Audit history visible.
- E2E test: open drawer → view files → click commit → view diff in local clone.

title: Phase 10: Pre-script runner + wakeAgent + [SILENT] + typed deterministic action intents
description:
WHAT
====
Layer 2 of §8.1: pre-script runner. Pre-scripts are deterministic Go subprocesses; output structured JSON; final line is {"wakeAgent": bool, "context": {...}}. May also emit typed deterministic action intents (§8.3) which the action executor runs through the same pipeline as agent ActionPlans.

WHY (§8.3 wakeAgent and [SILENT] patterns)
============================================
A swarm-tending job ticking every 4 minutes with nothing wrong costs ZERO TOKENS — pre-script returns wakeAgent:false. Hard thresholds (budget, rate-limit, disk full, push policy) are pre-script-only and never wait for an LLM. Judgment is for 'should we send marching orders or reassign this bead'; safety is for 'budget cap crossed, halt swarm now.'

PRE-SCRIPT CONTRACT
=====================
- Input: stdin = job definition + canonical-state snapshots (NTM, br, bv, mail, git, caut, srp, etc.) + capability registry view.
- Output: structured JSON to stdout. Final line MUST be {"wakeAgent": bool, "context": {...}, "actionIntents"?: [...]}.
- Side effects: action intents are NOT executed by the pre-script directly — they go through the same action executor as agent ActionPlans (§8.3, §8.3.1), with idempotency keys, audit, postcondition verification.

ACTION INTENT FLOW (§8.3)
===========================
- Pre-script emits {"actionIntents": [{type, target, idempotencyKey, preconditions, postconditions}]}.
- Action executor: capability check → resource locks → policy/approvals → preconditions → dry-run where possible → execute → verify postconditions against canonical state.
- Actor field on audit entries: pre_script:<jobId> (vs agent:<runId> for agent-driven).
- Deterministic-safety-specific approval policy (immediate execution for halt-on-budget-breach).

[SILENT] PATTERN
==================
When agent does wake but decides nothing was warranted, replies starting [SILENT]. Delivery to Activity panel SUPPRESSED, but run STILL audited (model used, tokens, decision). Healthy stretches stay quiet.

CONTEXT MANIFEST (§8.3 'Every agent wake also has a context manifest')
========================================================================
{runId, jobId, contextHash, sourceSnapshots, skillsLoaded, included, excluded, redactions, tokenEstimate, tokenBudget}. Stored alongside the tending run record + linked from audit log; agents are expected to cite evidence refs from the manifest when proposing actions.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Pre-script runner spawns Go subprocesses with standardized stdin/stdout schema.
- wakeAgent:false ticks produce no LLM call.
- [SILENT] reply suppresses Activity-panel delivery; audit preserved.
- Action intent flow: deterministic intent → action executor → postcondition verify.
- Context manifest written for every wake.
- Test scenarios from §8.8 evaluation harness pass.

title: Phase 1.5: Make Mock Flywheel drive real stage UI end-to-end
description: Move Mock Flywheel Mode from main-process/unit-test proof to renderer-visible product proof. Planning, Beads, Swarm, Hardening, and Activity must consume fixture-backed daemon-shaped data instead of static placeholders where Phase 1 promises replayable UI behavior. Include scenario switching or a fixed local-demo scenario, structured logs, and no VPS/network dependency. Acceptance: a clean desktop boot in mock/local-demo mode renders fixture bead/agent/mail/health state through the four-stage shell and Activity panel; e2e proves it without a VPS.
close_reason: Mock Flywheel healthy-hour corpus now drives Beads and Swarm renderer stages via TanStack Query; e2e spec added for fixture-backed Beads/Swarm navigation.

title: Phase 2: SessionCredentialService (HMAC bearer 30d + WS-token 5min stateless + signing-secret rotation)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the bearer + WS-token half of the auth model. HMAC-signed bearer (base64url(claims).base64url(sig), 30-day TTL); HMAC WS token (5min, stateless with sid claim); single signing secret in ServerSecretStore (32 bytes random) — rotating it revokes everything.

WHY (§5.2 step 2-3)
====================
The bearer is the steady-state HTTP credential; the WS token is the just-in-time channel credential issued from a valid bearer. Two separate tokens mean a leaked WS-token can do limited harm (5min window, single channel) and the daemon doesn't have to track every WS connection in a server-side session table.

BEARER CONTRACT
================
- Format: base64url(claims_json).base64url(sig_hmac256(secret, base64url(claims_json)))
- Claims: {sid: ULID, role: 'owner'|'client', issuedAt, expiresAt}
- Validated on every HTTP request via Authorization: Bearer header.
- 30-day TTL by default; refreshable via /v1/auth/bearer/refresh (returns new bearer, invalidates old).
- Persisted on desktop via safeStorage.encryptString.

WS TOKEN CONTRACT
==================
- POST /v1/auth/ws-token (with valid bearer) → {wsToken, expiresAt}
- Format: base64url(claims_json).base64url(sig_hmac256(secret, base64url(claims_json)))
- Claims: {sid: ULID matching bearer's sid, ws: true, issuedAt, expiresAt} — 5min TTL.
- Validated on WS connect via ?wsToken=... query param. After validation, the connection is bound to the sid; if the bearer's sid is revoked, all WS connections under that sid drop.
- Stateless — no per-token DB lookup; verifies signature only. The sid is checked against the bearer table once at WS connect time.

SIGNING SECRET ROTATION
========================
- 32 bytes random in ServerSecretStore (~/.hoopoe/auth/server-secret.json or daemon SQLite).
- Rotation: POST /v1/auth/rotate-secret (owner-only, requires explicit approval per §2.6 'rotate-secret' note).
- Rotating invalidates ALL bearers + WS tokens. Owner must re-pair desktop. Audit entry written.

SESSION REVOCATION
===================
- POST /v1/auth/session/revoke {sid} — owner can kill a specific session.
- 'hoopoe auth session list' / 'revoke' CLI surface.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.2 — full auth model.
- §2.6 — auth endpoints.
- Appendix B 'Patterns lifted' — t3code/apps/server/src/auth/Layers/SessionCredentialService.ts.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- HMAC verification must be CONSTANT-TIME (use crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare) to avoid timing attacks.
- Bearer + WS-token claims are validated against the daemon's clock; allow ±60s skew.
- Bearer storage on desktop: safeStorage.encryptString; never logged.
- WS-token: passed as query param (cannot be passed as Authorization header due to browser WS API) — query params are the ONLY workable approach; redact in logs.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- POST /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer issues a bearer.
- POST /v1/auth/ws-token issues a WS token from a valid bearer.
- WS connection rejects missing/invalid wsToken with 4401.
- Rotation invalidates everything; audit entry written.
- Session revocation drops associated WS connections within 5s.
- Constant-time HMAC verification benchmarked + documented.
- Unit tests cover token round-trip, expiry, signature mismatch, skew tolerance, rotation invalidation.

title: Phase 3: Tool inventory screen + CAAM subscription verification
description:
WHAT
====
Tool inventory screen surfacing every tool the daemon knows about (per the capability registry §2.8) with version, source, status, and CAAM-driven subscription verification for each agent CLI (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI).

WHY (§6.1 step 12, §13 'Subscription requirement')
====================================================
Hoopoe is subscription-only; users discover this requirement up-front during onboarding rather than after install. CAAM verification reports which CLIs are signed in (Claude Code → Claude Max, Codex CLI → GPT Pro, Gemini CLI → Gemini Ultra). Warn (don't block) if zero subscriptions configured.

INVENTORY VIEW
===============
- Each tool: name, repo, version detected, source (ntm serve / --robot-* JSON / CLI / fixture / missing), capabilities reported, last-checked, fixturesVersion.
- Per-tool status pill: ok / degraded / missing / blocked-by-policy.
- Click-through to Diagnostics for repair actions.
- 'Refresh inventory' action re-runs probes.

CAAM SUBSCRIPTION CHECK
========================
- For each agent CLI, ask CAAM: 'is this CLI signed in?' / 'which provider account?' / 'how many accounts available?'
- Surface counts so the §7.3 swarm composition picker can warn when requested agent count exceeds available accounts.
- Top-bar subscription pill (§7.6) reads from the same source.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.8 — capability registry (the data source).
- §6.1 step 12 — Status Check.
- §7.3 — composition picker reads CAAM account counts.
- §7.6 — subscription-usage pill (uses caut + CAAM).
- §13 — subscription requirement.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Refresh-inventory must respect bounded semaphores (§2.7) — concurrent probes throttled.
- Missing tools surface a Diagnostics 'install/repair' action where possible.
- 'Zero subscriptions configured' state shows a clear pointer to subscription pages (Claude Max, GPT Pro, Gemini Ultra, ChatGPT Pro).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Tool inventory page renders every tool from the capability registry.
- CAAM-driven subscription check runs at the end of Phase 3 wizard.
- Zero-subscription warning shown clearly without blocking onboarding.
- 'Refresh inventory' tested.
- Inventory data feeds §7.6 top bar.

title: packages/schemas/provider-plugin.yaml — abstract provider plugin contract (Region/Size/Instance) for Contabo + future Hetzner/DO/OVH
description:
WHAT
====
Define the abstract provider plugin contract in packages/schemas/provider-plugin.yaml so the Contabo plugin (hp-9fo) is the FIRST implementation, not the only-possible shape. Future Hetzner / DigitalOcean / OVH / Linode plugins slot in without schema work.

CONTRACT (§6.2)
=================
The contract has 5 typed methods:

```yaml
# packages/schemas/provider-plugin.yaml

ProviderPlugin:
  id:            string  # 'contabo' | 'hetzner' | 'do' | 'ovh' | ...
  displayName:   string
  homepage:      url
  authMode:      'api-token' | 'oauth' | 'basic-auth'

  listRegions: () -> Region[]
  listSizes:   (region: RegionId) -> Size[]
  createInstance:    (opts: CreateOpts) -> Instance
  destroyInstance:   (instanceId: string) -> {ok: bool}
  estimateMonthlyCost: (opts: {region, size}) -> {usd: number, breakdown: LineItem[]}

Region:
  id:           string
  name:         string
  country:      ISO3166-1
  city:         string
  available:    bool

Size:
  id:           string
  cpuVCores:    int
  ramGB:        int
  storageGB:    int
  storageType:  'NVMe' | 'SSD' | 'HDD'
  bandwidthTB:  int
  monthlyUSD:   number
  recommended:  bool  # 'recommended' / 'workable' / 'minimum' tier per §6.2 sizing
  tier:         'recommended' | 'workable' | 'minimum'

CreateOpts:
  region:       RegionId
  size:         SizeId
  sshPubKey:    string  # OpenSSH-format public key Hoopoe generated/imported
  name:         string  # human-friendly, e.g. 'hoopoe-acfs-2026-05-02'
  imageId:      string  # Ubuntu 24.04+ image ID per provider; default to provider's latest LTS

Instance:
  instanceId:   string
  ip:           IPv4 | IPv6
  status:       'provisioning' | 'running' | 'failed'
  createdAt:    RFC3339
  region:       RegionId
  size:         SizeId
  monthlyUSD:   number   # actual billing cost the user will see

LineItem:
  label:        string  # 'compute', 'bandwidth', 'storage', 'datacenter-surcharge'
  usd:          number
```

WHY ABSTRACT vs INLINING IN HP-9FO
=====================================
Contabo (hp-9fo) is the first plugin per §6.2 'Contabo first.' But §6.2 also says the rollout is 'Contabo → OVH → Hetzner / DigitalOcean.' If Contabo's bead defines the schema, the next plugin re-shapes it. The abstract contract avoids that churn:
- Daemon code consumes `ProviderPlugin` interface; never knows which provider is loaded.
- Plugin tests are uniform: any plugin can be swapped in for the same test harness.
- The provider catalog UI (wizard 'Provision new VPS' screen) renders from the contract, not from per-provider components.

INTEGRATION POINTS
====================
- Wizard's 'Provision new VPS' path (currently hidden in v1; Phase 13 exposes for Contabo).
- Cost estimate card shown in wizard before provisioning; matches §13 cost-transparency numbers.
- Teardown action in project settings (audited as destructive).
- Tested via mock plugin + real Contabo plugin (hp-9fo) in Phase 13.

PROVIDER REGISTRY
===================
Daemon loads provider plugins from a registry:
```go
type ProviderRegistry interface {
  Register(plugin ProviderPlugin) error
  Get(id string) (ProviderPlugin, error)
  List() []ProviderPlugin
}
```
v1 ships with Contabo only; the registry is open for future plugins without daemon-binary changes (registered via init() in plugin packages).

UNIT TESTS
============
- Schema validates against TS-generated types + Go-generated types (round-trip).
- Mock plugin implements all 5 methods + schema validation.
- Cost estimate matches §13 numbers (~$40-56/mo for 64GB Contabo Cloud VPS 50).
- Region/Size enumeration deterministic across calls.

E2E TESTS
===========
- Mock-plugin scenario: full wizard → 'Provision new VPS' → cost preview → provision → SSH connect → destroy.
- Real Contabo scenario: gated by `HOOPOE_E2E_CONTABO_TOKEN` env var; skipped in normal CI; runs nightly on a separate billing-allowed CI runner.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every contract method call logs: { plugin, method, opts (redacted), durationMs, result, errorClass }.
- Provisioning failures surface a structured error with provider response.
- Cost estimates stamped with the catalog version that produced them.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.2 — provider rollout, sizing recommendations.
- §13 — cost-transparency numbers.
- hp-9fo — Contabo plugin (first concrete implementation).
- hp-jqos — provider-plugin test suite.
- hp-r3i — packages/schemas (parent epic).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- packages/schemas/provider-plugin.yaml ships with all 5 methods + types.
- TS client + Go types generated.
- Mock plugin + schema validation tests pass.
- Documentation in packages/schemas/README.md covers how to add a new provider plugin.
- hp-9fo (Contabo) consumes this schema rather than inlining its own shape.

title: Phase 1: Vendor t3code desktop lifecycle files (clientPersistence, backendPort, etc.)
description:
WHAT
====
Lift the t3code desktop lifecycle files listed in Appendix B into apps/desktop/src/vendored/t3code/, with the MIT-attribution header preserved on each file, and minimal in-place edits (mechanical rebrands only). Adaptations (rebranding env keys, swapping schema types, updating regex parsers) happen in adjacent Hoopoe-owned files that import from vendored/.

WHY
===
These files solve concrete Electron-lifecycle problems Hoopoe needs from day one: encrypted persistence of bearer tokens via safeStorage, dynamic backend port discovery, parser for daemon's 'listening on' line, three-store settings, update state machine, runtime-arch detection, shell-environment sync, window-reveal helpers, confirm dialogs, app branding. Lifting saves weeks and inherits hardening already shipped.

FILES TO LIFT (per Appendix B)
================================
- apps/desktop/src/clientPersistence.ts → vendored/t3code/. Adaptation: replace T3CODE_* env keys with HOOPOE_*; keep safeStorage.encryptString logic intact.
- apps/desktop/src/backendPort.ts → same. Adaptation: default port → Hoopoe's (TBD; document in source-provenance.md).
- apps/desktop/src/backendReadiness.ts → same. Adaptation: replace t3code stdout signature with Hoopoe daemon's 'listening on' line.
- apps/desktop/src/serverListeningDetector.ts → same. Adaptation: update parser regex.
- apps/desktop/src/desktopSettings.ts → same. Adaptation: schema swap (HoopoeDesktopSettings).
- apps/desktop/src/updateMachine.ts → same. No adaptation.
- apps/desktop/src/updateChannels.ts → same. No adaptation.
- apps/desktop/src/updateState.ts → same. No adaptation.
- apps/desktop/src/runtimeArch.ts → same. No adaptation.
- apps/desktop/src/syncShellEnvironment.ts → same. No adaptation.
- apps/desktop/src/windowReveal.ts → same. No adaptation.
- apps/desktop/src/confirmDialog.ts → same. No adaptation.
- apps/desktop/src/appBranding.ts → same. Adaptation: replace branding strings.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Find-open-port (port-conflict resolution) — Appendix B anti-pattern #5 says t3code lacks this; we must add findOpenPort(preferred) in BackendLifecycle.ts (separate bead).
- safeStorage.encryptString must work even before user-data dir exists; clientPersistence already handles this via fall-through to in-memory cache that flushes on writeable.
- Adaptation files in apps/desktop/src/main/ should NOT import directly from t3code module names; they import from ./vendored/t3code/<file>.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All listed files exist under apps/desktop/src/vendored/t3code/ with MIT attribution headers.
- Each file's adapted-version (if any) lives in apps/desktop/src/main/ and imports from vendored/.
- Type checks pass for all vendored files (apps/desktop/tsconfig.json includes vendored/**/*.ts).
- Smoke unit tests exist for clientPersistence (encrypt → decrypt round trip), serverListeningDetector (regex against fixture stdout), runtimeArch (detection on arm64 + x64).
- ENV-key rebrand documented in docs/source-provenance.md.
close_reason: 13 t3code lifecycle files vendored under apps/desktop/src/vendored/t3code/ with MIT attribution headers; _shims.ts hosts type/Effect/Net/shell stubs so vendored compiles without dragging in @t3tools/contracts or @t3tools/shared; mass renames applied (T3 Code→Hoopoe, T3CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE→HOOPOE_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE, default port 3773→3779); 3 required smoke tests pass (clientPersistence encrypt→decrypt round trip, serverListeningDetector regex, runtimeArch arm64/x64/Rosetta); typecheck/lint/test/build green; UBS clean

title: Phase 1: Vendor t3code build pipeline (build-desktop-artifact, mock-update-server, release.yml)
description:
WHAT
====
Vendor the t3code build pipeline files into apps/desktop/scripts/ and .github/workflows/, adapt for Hoopoe. The orchestrator stages all dist artifacts to a temp dir, synthesizes a self-contained package.json resolving the workspace catalog into concrete versions, runs 'bun install --production' there, then invokes 'bunx electron-builder' against the staged dir.

WHY (§3 desktop layer source provenance, §11, Appendix B)
==========================================================
The staged package.json is the source of build truth, NOT the repo's. This is what avoids electron-builder's flaky monorepo workspace support — a known pain point t3code has already solved. Lifting saves weeks.

FILES TO LIFT (Appendix B)
===========================
- t3code's scripts/build-desktop-artifact.ts → scripts/build-desktop-artifact.ts (strip Linux/Windows from default; keep code paths under flags; replace @t3tools → @hoopoe)
- t3code's scripts/mock-update-server.ts → same (no adaptation needed)
- t3code's scripts/release-smoke.ts → same
- t3code's .github/workflows/release.yml → same (strip Linux + Windows matrix entries; keep mac arm64 + x64; update secrets CSC_LINK / APPLE_API_KEY / GH_TOKEN; rename workflows)
- t3code's .github/workflows/{typecheck, lint, test}.yml → same (adapt to Hoopoe's command names)
- t3code's apps/desktop/scripts/{dev-electron, start-electron, smoke-test}.mjs → same
- t3code's electron-builder config (in apps/desktop/package.json / build.yml) → same (update productName, appId, signing identity)

ADAPTATIONS REQUIRED
====================
- electron-builder productName: 'Hoopoe', appId: 'com.hoopoe.app' (or similar, document in source-provenance.md).
- Strip Linux AppImage and Windows NSIS targets from the DEFAULT matrix in release.yml; keep the code paths under feature flags so Phase 13+ can re-enable cross-platform without re-deriving the build (§11 'Cross-platform stance').
- Update GitHub secrets references: CSC_LINK (signing cert URL), CSC_KEY_PASSWORD, APPLE_API_KEY, APPLE_API_KEY_ID, APPLE_API_ISSUER, GH_TOKEN.
- Rebrand env keys: T3CODE_* → HOOPOE_* (this is consistent with the clientPersistence rebrand).
- Mac signing identity must be a Developer ID Application certificate; notarization via APPLE_API_KEY (App Store Connect API).
- mock-update-server: NO adaptation, used as-is for local update testing.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- electron-updater publishes to GitHub Releases, channels `latest` / `nightly`; channel selection per-user via desktop-settings.json (§11). 15s startup delay before first update check, 4-hour poll.
- 'bun run --publish never' on builder; manual upload in a separate CI step (more control).
- Daemon binary distribution is SEPARATE from desktop DMG — the daemon is a single static Go binary downloaded via signed release URL with checksum + signature + provenance attestation (§11). This bead doesn't include daemon distribution; that's a Phase 2 deliverable.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- 'bun run dist:desktop:dmg:arm64' on a clean checkout produces a signed/notarized DMG that installs and launches on Mac (smoke test).
- 'bun run dist:desktop:dmg:x64' produces an x64 build.
- mock-update-server boots locally and advertises a fake update; UpdateMachine (Phase 1 separate task) polls and surfaces it.
- release.yml runs end-to-end on a tag in CI (test branch).
- All vendored files have 'Originally from github.com/pingdotgg/t3code (MIT)' header (Appendix B license attribution).
close_reason: Vendored Hoopoe desktop release workflow, build artifact script, and mock update server from t3code pipeline

title: Phase 9: Activity drawer UI (cross-stage, persistent unread badge, filterable timeline, click-to-pivot)
description:
WHAT
====
Cross-stage Activity drawer per §7.5. Slide-over from right edge, available from any stage. NOT a route — overlays the current stage. Persistent unread badge on the toggle. Filterable timeline using TimelineRow components.

LAYOUT (per design/mockups/v1)
================================
- Toggle button in top-right (badge with unread count).
- Drawer width ~480px; full height; backdrop overlay.
- Filter bar at top: event categories, time range, related bead, related agent.
- Timeline list of TimelineRow items.
- User-to-orchestrator chat input box at bottom (fixed).

TIMELINE ROW VARIANTS (one per event type)
=============================================
- agent.registered
- mail.sent / received / urgent
- bead.claimed / status_changed
- reservation.requested / renewed / released / conflicted
- build.started / completed / failed
- rate_limit.detected
- pane.wedged
- orchestrator.intervention
- review.request / finding
- commit.created
- health.snapshot_updated
- user.message_to_orchestrator
- orchestrator.message_to_user

INTERACTIONS
=============
- Click bead pill → bead drawer opens (Phase 7).
- Click agent chip → agent grid in Swarm stage.
- Click file path → reservation view.
- Right-click event → 'Reply as human overseer' / 'Broadcast to swarm' / 'Create bead from message' / 'Mark acknowledged'.

PERSISTENCE
=============
- Drawer open/closed state persisted in client-settings.json so it survives stage navigation.
- Unread badge resets when user reads (clicks into) the event.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.5 — full Activity panel spec.
- §7.3 — Activity panel is one of the three swarm-dashboard surfaces.
- TimelineRow component — design system.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Drawer renders with all event-type variants.
- Unread badge updates correctly.
- Filter bar works.
- Click-to-pivot navigates correctly.
- Storybook stories for each variant.
- Keyboard nav: ⌘/ toggles drawer.
- Phase 9 acceptance: drawer shows real events from a running swarm against research-spike VPS.

title: Phase 2.5: Seed REST contract — implement §2.6 endpoints with generated schema tests
description:
WHAT
====
Implement every endpoint in §2.6 with handlers that round-trip through the generated Go types from packages/schemas/. Add CI-enforced tests that prove every route's request/response matches the OpenAPI spec.

ENDPOINTS (§2.6 full list)
============================
System: /v1/health, /v1/version, /v1/compatibility, /v1/capabilities, /v1/system/specs, /v1/system/processes
Auth:   /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer, /v1/auth/ws-token, /v1/auth/session/revoke, /v1/auth/rotate-secret (owner only, explicit approval)
Events: /v1/events/replay, /v1/events/sse, /v1/events/ws-token, /v1/events/ws (WS)
Jobs:   /v1/jobs, /v1/jobs/{id}/cancel, /v1/jobs/{id}/log?offset=, /v1/jobs/{id}/artifacts
Bootstrap: /v1/bootstrap/preflight, /v1/bootstrap/acfs/start, /v1/bootstrap/acfs/resume, /v1/bootstrap/daemon/upgrade
Projects:  /v1/projects (list+create), /v1/projects/{projectId} (get), /v1/projects/{projectId}/activate, /v1/projects/{projectId}/readiness
Git:       /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/status, /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/staged-diff, /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/unstaged-diff, /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/unpushed-commits, /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/push
Plans:     /v1/projects/{projectId}/plans (list+create), /v1/projects/{projectId}/plans/{planId}/rounds (POST), /v1/projects/{projectId}/plans/{planId}/lock, /v1/projects/{projectId}/plans/{planId}/artifacts
Beads:     /v1/projects/{projectId}/beads, /v1/projects/{projectId}/beads/graph, /v1/projects/{projectId}/beads/ready, /v1/projects/{projectId}/beads/{beadId} (GET+PATCH), /v1/projects/{projectId}/beads/conversion-runs (POST), /v1/projects/{projectId}/beads/polish-runs (POST)
Swarm:     /v1/projects/{projectId}/swarms (POST), /v1/projects/{projectId}/swarms/{swarmId} (GET), .../swarms/{swarmId}/broadcast (POST), .../agents/{agentId}/send|interrupt|stop (POST)
Mail:      /v1/projects/{projectId}/mail/messages (GET+POST), .../threads/{threadId} (GET), .../reservations (GET), .../reservations/force-release (POST)
Health:    /v1/projects/{projectId}/health/summary, .../health/files, .../health/snapshots (POST)
Reviews:   /v1/projects/{projectId}/reviews (POST), .../reviews/{reviewId} (GET), .../findings (GET), .../findings/{findingId} (PATCH)
Tending:   /v1/projects/{projectId}/tending/jobs (GET), .../jobs/{jobId}/run (POST), .../jobs/{jobId} (PATCH)
Approvals: /v1/projects/{projectId}/approvals (GET), .../approvals/{approvalId} (GET+approve+deny+extend)

CONVENTIONS (§2.6)
====================
- All write endpoints accept Idempotency-Key header.
- All errors use RFC 7807-style problem+json.
- All state-changing calls write audit entries (audit log epic).
- Generated TS client + Go types from packages/schemas/ — drift between hand-coded and spec is forbidden.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- packages/schemas/ epic — owns OpenAPI spec.
- §2.6 — full endpoint list.
- audit log epic — every state-changing call writes audit.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Every endpoint above has a handler.
- CI runs: spec lint (spectral), generated-types compile, contract tests (every endpoint exercised against fixtures from packages/fixtures/).
- Every write endpoint accepts Idempotency-Key and tests retrying same key returns same result.
- All errors use problem+json shape.
- docs/api-seed.md generated from the spec.

title: Cross-cutting / Diagnostics: Audit log explorer UI — filterable browser over ~/.hoopoe/audit.jsonl with search, time range, actor/action/correlation filters, export-redacted-slice action
description:
WHAT
====
The user-facing inspection surface over Hoopoe's append-only audit log (`~/.hoopoe/audit.jsonl`). §1.4 mandates: 'Hoopoe should never feel like hidden magic. Every meaningful automated action produces an event capturing who/what triggered it, which command or API call ran, why, what input scope, what output, and which artifact stores the evidence.' That is *only valuable* if the user can actually find and read those entries when something looks wrong, when an approval is questioned later, or when a tending agent took an unexpected action.

WHY THIS IS A FOCUSED BEAD
==========================
Existing coverage:
- **hp-g73** is the audit-log infrastructure epic: writer + JSONL format + monotonic seq + correlation/causation IDs + redaction sink. That writes the log.
- **hp-je1p** is the redaction layer (bearer / pairing / model keys / SSH / cookies / paths) — produces redaction-clean entries.
- **hp-6d7** is the §10.2 repair-action catalog including 'Replay events from sequence' — replays *events*, not the audit log itself.
- **hp-if5s** captures pane snapshots into the audit log on kill — produces specific entries.

What's missing: the **viewer** surface. §5.3 ('Approvals can be viewed from the Activity panel and Diagnostics') and §1.4 imply this exists, and §10.2 names the repair-tray and tool inventory but not an audit browser. Without it, the user must `tail -f` the JSONL file by hand — a usability cliff that breaks the §1.4 trust contract.

INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
========================
A new Diagnostics tab: **Audit log**. Companion to the existing 'Recent problems' panel (hp-g6sp) and the 'Show raw pane' toggle (hp-yf9). Three-pane layout:

1. **Filter rail (left, 280px)** —
   - Time range: 'Last hour / 24h / 7d / 30d / Custom range'.
   - Actor kind: user, agent, tending_job, repair_action, mock (when MFM, hp-lddj), pre_script.
   - Action category: project, plan, beads, swarm, mail, health, review, tending, approval, repair, auth, config.
   - Correlation ID search (with autocomplete from recent IDs).
   - Causation ID drill-up ('show what caused this').
   - Project filter (multi-select).
   - Severity: info / notice / warning / urgent.
   - Outcome: success / failure / blocked / silent.
   - Free-text search (over redacted descriptions only — never raw payloads).

2. **Timeline list (center)** — Virtualized list (1000-row window via TanStack Virtual). Each row: timestamp + actor pill + action name + outcome chip + truncated reason. Click → detail pane. Color-coded background by severity. Group-by toggles: by correlation chain, by hour, by actor.

3. **Detail pane (right, 520px)** — Full audit entry with:
   - Header: `actor`, `actionType`, `outcome`, `time`, `seq`, `correlationId`, `causationId`.
   - Linked artifacts: clickable to plan / bead / swarm / approval / pane snapshot.
   - Command preview (already redacted by hp-je1p).
   - Postcondition results (where applicable).
   - 'Show in correlation chain' button → filter to all entries sharing the same correlationId.
   - 'Show what this caused' / 'Show what caused this' navigation via causationId graph.
   - Linked approval (if action was gated): clickable to Approval lifecycle (hp-v0g).
   - Copy-as-JSON, Copy-as-curl-command-replay, Open-evidence-bundle.

DAEMON SUPPORT (new endpoints — extends §2.6)
=============================================
`GET /v1/audit/query` — typed filter params + cursor pagination. Streams NDJSON for large ranges. Bounded by §2.6 idempotency-key handling.
`GET /v1/audit/correlation/{correlationId}` — full correlation chain with causation tree.
`POST /v1/audit/export` — redacted slice export (Date range + filter → `audit-slice-<runId>.tar.zst` with manifest, sha256, redaction profile noted, schema version). Approval-gated when range >7 days OR actor includes 'agent' (avoiding accidental over-export).

Read-only on the user's perspective; no audit entries can be deleted or modified through the UI. The export action *is itself audited* ('Audit slice exported by user X covering range Y, redaction profile Z').

PERFORMANCE
===========
- p95 < 500ms for queries returning ≤500 rows on a 100k-entry log (SLO).
- Server-side filter + pagination; client never holds >1000 rows.
- Daemon maintains a sidecar SQLite index (`~/.hoopoe/audit.idx.db`) over `audit.jsonl` keyed by (seq, time, actor, action, correlationId, causationId, projectId, outcome). Index rebuilt on daemon start if hash mismatch with audit.jsonl tail; otherwise reused. Lazy index maintenance: append-only inserts on each write.
- Free-text search uses the same SQLite index (`reason` and `actionDescription` stored as TEXT, FTS5 virtual table optional).

REDACTION POSTURE
==================
The audit log is *already* redacted at write time by hp-je1p. The viewer NEVER decrypts/un-redacts: the redaction is one-way. The viewer marks redacted regions visually ('[REDACTED:bearer-token]') so the user knows when content was elided rather than absent. Settings (hp-wg5p) toggle: 'Show redaction reason' (verbose / concise) — purely cosmetic.

UNIT TESTS (10) — `apps/desktop/src/diagnostics/__tests__/auditExplorer.test.ts` + `apps/daemon/internal/audit/__tests__/index_test.go`
=================================================================================================================================================
1. **Filter combination** — multi-filter (actor=agent, action=swarm.halt, time=last 24h, severity=warning) returns expected fixture-set; all-filters-empty returns full set.
2. **Time-range correctness** — 'Last 24h' resolves correctly across DST; custom range respects timezone; future timestamps rejected with structured error.
3. **Correlation chain navigation** — given a fixture chain of 5 entries (causationId tree depth 3), 'Show what this caused' returns the 4 descendants; 'Show what caused this' returns the 1 ancestor; cycles in causation tree (which shouldn't happen but might via bug) are detected and reported.
4. **Free-text search redaction-respect** — search for 'sk-live-' (a known redacted-token pattern) returns 0 results because redaction stripped it; search for the *redaction marker* '[REDACTED:bearer]' returns hits as expected.
5. **Pagination cursor stability** — 1000-entry fixture, page through with cursors, verify no duplicates and no skips even when new entries append during pagination (cursor uses seq, not offset).
6. **Daemon index rebuild** — write 1000 entries to audit.jsonl, delete audit.idx.db, restart daemon → index rebuilt → query results match pre-rebuild expectations. Structured log `audit.index.rebuild_complete` with row_count + duration_ms.
7. **Export redaction-profile correctness** — export a slice, untar, confirm manifest.json captures (range, actor filters, redaction profile sha256, exporter user, exported_at, total_entries). Spot-check that a redacted entry remains redacted in the export.
8. **Export approval gating** — 8-day-range export request returns 'requires_approval' problem+json (per hp-g6sp) when not pre-approved; with approval token, succeeds and writes audit entry 'audit.export_completed'.
9. **Per-row link resolution** — entry with `linkedArtifacts: [{ kind: 'bead', id: 'B-142' }]` renders a clickable pill; click resolves to bead detail drawer (mock router); broken link (artifact deleted) shows greyed pill with tooltip 'Artifact no longer exists'.
10. **Performance smoke** — query of 100k-entry fixture returning 500 rows completes <500ms p95 over 50 warm-cache runs. Logs duration_ms percentiles: p50 / p95 / p99.

E2E TESTS (8) — `apps/desktop/e2e/auditExplorer.e2e.ts`
========================================================
1. **End-to-end happy path** — Start daemon with seeded 200-entry audit log; open Diagnostics → Audit log; verify 200 entries visible; filter by actor=tending_job; verify subset; click a tend-swarm entry; confirm correlationId chain shows the agent ActionPlan + postcondition + audit-pair; export the chain as redacted slice; verify tar opens.
2. **Live-tail behavior** — Open Audit log; trigger a real daemon action (a fixture POST); confirm new entry appears in the timeline within 1s without refresh.
3. **Force-release-stale-reservation correlation** — User clicks 'Force release reservation' from Diagnostics repair tray (hp-6d7); navigate to Audit log; correlationId of the repair action shows: 'repair.force_release_reservation' (actor: repair_action) + 'agent_mail.reservation_released' (actor: agent_mail_adapter); both linked.
4. **Approval-gated export** — Request export covering 30 days; UI prompts for approval; click 'Approve' as user; export completes; downloaded file present in Downloads; audit shows the approval and the export.
5. **Free-text search timing** — Type a 4-char search string into the search box; debounced query fires after 250ms; results render <600ms p95 on 50k-entry fixture; structured log captures search latency.
6. **Group-by-correlation grouping** — Toggle 'Group by correlation chain'; entries with shared correlationId appear under a collapsible header; expand toggles work; counts shown.
7. **Mobile / narrow window** — Resize Diagnostics to 800px wide; filter rail collapses to a button; detail pane stacks below timeline; usable.
8. **Crash recovery** — Kill daemon mid-export; restart; UI shows stale export request with 'resume' affordance; resume completes the export.

DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (per hp-lxs)
==========================================
- `audit.query` { user_id, filter_params, returned_rows, took_ms, cache_hit }
- `audit.export_started` / `.export_completed` / `.export_failed` { range_days, actor_filters, total_entries, redaction_profile, sha256, approval_token? }
- `audit.index.rebuild_complete` { row_count, duration_ms, reason }
- `audit.correlation_chain_traversal` { correlationId, depth, total_entries }

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.4 'Every automation must be inspectable' (the trust contract)
- §10 'Observability and recovery' — audit log schema + replay protocol
- §10.4 'Backup, export, and restore' — audit slice in project export bundle
- §5.3 'Approvals can be viewed from the Activity panel and Diagnostics' (this surface)
- hp-g73 (audit log infra), hp-je1p (redaction), hp-6d7 (Diagnostics repair tray)
- design tokens: cream-on-dark for the page surface

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Diagnostics 'Audit log' tab renders three-pane layout
- Filter rail with all 7 filter dimensions
- Timeline virtualized to 1000 visible rows
- Detail pane with all linked-artifact integrations
- Daemon `/v1/audit/query`, `/v1/audit/correlation/{id}`, `POST /v1/audit/export` endpoints
- Sidecar SQLite index with rebuild-on-mismatch
- p95 <500ms on 100k-entry fixture verified
- 10 unit tests + 8 e2e tests passing
- All structured-log events emitted and verified
- Documentation: 'When something seems off, the Audit log answers what just happened.'

title: Phase 10: Agent runtime + typed ActionPlan executor (validates, dry-runs, approvals, executes, verifies postconditions)
description:
WHAT
====
Layer 3 of §8.1: spawns an agent with the job's declared skills loaded into context + the prompt template interpolated from pre-script payload. Agent may use read-only daemon tools while reasoning. For mutations, emits a typed ActionPlan (§8.3.1). Daemon validates / dry-runs / applies approvals / executes through typed RPCs / verifies postconditions. [SILENT] reply suppresses Activity-panel delivery; audit preserved.

ACTION PLAN CONTRACT (§8.3.1)
================================
{
  schemaVersion: 1,
  jobId, runId,
  summary: <human-readable>,
  evidenceRefs: [refs from context manifest],
  actions: [{
    type: <one of allowed types in tending-actions.yaml>,
    target,
    idempotencyKey,
    preconditions,
    postconditions
  }],
  riskClass: low|medium|high|destructive,
  requiresApproval: bool
}

ALLOWED ACTION TYPES (§8.3.1; live in packages/schemas/tending-actions.yaml)
=============================================================================
- agent.ask_status
- agent.send_marching_orders
- agent.pause
- agent.kill_wedged_process
- reservation.force_release
- caam.switch_account
- casr.resume_session
- git.push_branch
- swarm.halt
- review.propose_flip
- bead.create_blocker

Any action type NOT in the schema is rejected at validation; agent CANNOT escape the typed surface by inventing one.

POSTCONDITION VERIFICATION (§8.3.1)
=====================================
The executor NEVER trusts the action's own stdout or its own daemon RPC return value as proof of effect. ALWAYS re-reads canonical state.

Examples:
- git.push_branch verifies origin's target ref contains the expected commit SHA.
- reservation.force_release verifies Agent Mail no longer reports the reservation AND release notice posted in the bead's thread.
- caam.switch_account verifies CAAM reports target account for the agent AND agent's pane resumes producing output within bounded window.
- agent.kill_wedged_process verifies NTM/tmux no longer reports the killed process AND bead/reservation state handled per policy.
- bead.create_blocker verifies br contains new bead AND links to source bead/finding.

If postcondition verification FAILS, executor emits a new detection with sourceActionId + severity tied to failed action's risk class — half-succeeded intervention becomes a new tending input rather than a silent log entry. This is what makes tending self-healing under partial failures.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §8.3.1 — full ActionPlan contract.
- packages/schemas/tending-actions.yaml — typed action surface.
- approvals queue epic — destructive actions go through this queue.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Agent runtime spawns with skills loaded.
- ActionPlan validation rejects unknown types.
- Each action type has a postcondition verifier wired to canonical state.
- Postcondition failure emits follow-up detection.
- All §8.8 evaluation harness scenarios pass.

title: SBOM + provenance verification flow — checksum / signature / attestation for daemon binary; bootstrap refuses on failure
description:
WHAT
====
The full SBOM + provenance + signature verification flow for the daemon binary, integrated into the bootstrap installer + the in-app upgrade flow. Bootstrap refuses installation when verification fails; explicit insecure-development override is audited + visibly marked in Diagnostics.

WHY (§11)
===========
'Single static Go binary, downloaded via signed release URL, verified by checksum, signature, and provenance attestation. Release metadata includes checksums, signing identity, SBOM, artifact attestation, source commit, builder identity, and minimum compatible desktop/API versions. Bootstrap records the verified metadata in the daemon inventory so Diagnostics can show exactly what is running and where it came from.'

This is currently lumped into hp-4iz (daemon upgrade flow); this bead breaks out the cryptographic verification surface as its own first-class deliverable so it gets the same scrutiny as the upgrade flow's mechanics.

VERIFICATION CHAIN
====================
1. Download daemon binary + signature + SBOM + attestation from signed release URL.
2. Verify SHA-256 checksum matches release manifest.
3. Verify signature against pinned public key (signing identity in code).
4. Verify provenance attestation (SLSA L3 minimum):
   - source commit matches release manifest.
   - builder identity matches expected GitHub Actions workflow.
   - build was reproducible (re-build comparison if user opts in).
5. Verify SBOM: every dependency listed; CVE check against last-known good database.
6. Record verified metadata in daemon inventory (~/.hoopoe/daemon-inventory.json).

FAILURE MODES
==============
- Checksum mismatch → refuse install + clear error.
- Signature invalid → refuse install + clear error.
- Provenance attestation invalid → refuse install + clear error.
- SBOM CVE check fails → warn + require explicit user acknowledgement before install.
- Public key rotation → bootstrap fetches new public key over the SSH-authenticated channel + verifies against signing-identity history.

OVERRIDE (insecure dev mode)
==============================
- Flag --insecure-dev-override on bootstrap.sh + a setting in Diagnostics.
- Override is audited to ~/.hoopoe/audit.jsonl with actor + timestamp + override reason.
- Diagnostics shows a red banner: 'Daemon installed with provenance verification BYPASSED. Production use is unsupported.'
- Override persists per-VPS until explicitly removed (Diagnostics action).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.3 — bootstrap flow step 7 (signed release URL, checksum + provenance verified).
- §11 — daemon distribution.
- §14 — Lifted code carries Codex-shaped assumptions risk (defense in depth).
- hp-4iz — daemon upgrade flow (consumes this verification chain).
- hp-n30 — bootstrap.sh installer (consumes this verification chain).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Verification chain implemented + tested.
- Bootstrap refuses on any failure unless explicit override.
- Override audit + Diagnostics red banner implemented.
- SBOM CVE check wired to a last-known-good database (refresh-on-release).
- docs/security.md documents the chain as authoritative.

title: Phase 4: Desktop local clone — initial clone, clone-state.json, file-watcher, dirty banner, size caps
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the desktop-side local Git clone of every project (§7.7) — a sync-driven mirror of origin used purely for fast file-reading. Storage: ~/Library/Application Support/Hoopoe/projects/<project-id>/repo/. Project metadata in <project-id>/clone-state.json.

WHY (§7.7 'Why it exists')
============================
Reading code is the single most frequent thing the cockpit does. The Beads drawer shows files a bead touches, Health tab renders files with coverage gutters, hotspots want previews, review findings link to specific lines, search is expected to be instant. Doing all of that over the SSH tunnel via daemon RPCs is workable but feels sluggish and fails when the laptop is on a poor connection. A local clone makes file open + diff + blame + ripgrep local-disk fast.

WHAT IT IS (§7.7)
==================
- Storage path: ~/Library/Application Support/Hoopoe/projects/<project-id>/repo/.
- clone-state.json: last-fetched SHA, sync state, size, last-accessed.
- Clone source: ORIGIN (NOT the VPS). Avoids exposing extra surface on the VPS, leverages user's existing Git creds, means desktop+VPS reach canonical state via independent paths.
- Refs fetched: ALL of them. `git fetch --all --tags --prune` on every sync.
- Initial clone: full clone (not partial) for v1 simplicity. Revisit --filter=blob:none later if monorepos make full clones painful.
- The default checked-out branch matches whatever the VPS working tree is on, but UI can render any ref via `git show <ref>:path` without checking it out.

DIRTY BANNER
=============
A file watcher on the local clone detects modifications, untracked files, or local commits. When detected, project header shows yellow banner: 'Local clone has unsaved changes — Hoopoe ignores local edits. Make changes via the VPS (ssh or Cursor/VS Code Remote). [Discard local changes] [Open clone in Finder]'.

The 'Discard' action runs `git reset --hard @{u} && git clean -fd` after a confirmation. Hoopoe NEVER auto-discards.

DISK HYGIENE (§7.7)
====================
- Soft cap: 2 GB per project clone. Crossing → warning in settings card with option to clear blobs older than N days.
- Hard cap: 5 GB per project clone. Crossing → blocks further fetches until user clears or raises cap.
- Caps configurable in ~/.hoopoe/userdata/desktop-settings.json per-project and globally.
- Per-project actions: 'Clear local clone' (deletes dir; next access re-clones), 'Reveal in Finder', 'Open in terminal'.
- Project removal: deleting a project from Hoopoe deletes its local clone (confirmation dialog spells this out).
- Total cache view in global settings: list of clones with size, last-fetched, last-accessed; sortable; multi-select clear.

AUTHENTICATION
===============
Desktop uses user's EXISTING Git credentials — typically SSH keys in ~/.ssh/, or GitHub/GitLab personal access tokens already in the system credential helper. Hoopoe does NOT introduce its own Git auth flow. If a clone fails because creds aren't set up, project header shows clear error with link to docs.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.7 — sync-driven mirrors are allowed.
- §5.3 — narrow exception for local clone reads.
- §7.7 — full local clone spec.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Use simple-git, nodegit, or isomorphic-git — the choice is not load-bearing as long as it's read-only.
- Clone path is treated as untrusted file contents; rendering uses escaping; Markdown preview disables unsafe HTML by default (§5.4).
- Watcher uses chokidar or built-in fs.watch with debounce.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Initial clone from origin works.
- clone-state.json tracks state.
- File watcher detects modifications + dirty banner appears.
- Soft + hard caps enforced + tested.
- Per-project actions implemented (Clear / Reveal / Open in terminal).
- Total cache view in settings.
- Auth fallback warning shown when creds aren't set up.

title: Phase 1.5: Wire CommandPalette into shell and make e2e assert it
description:
Root cause: docs/post-phase1-reality-check.md Top 5 Phase 1.5 gap #2 and review-findings.md line 260 show the CommandPalette primitive/registry exists, but the shell feature is inert: RootLayout's top-bar button does not open a palette and e2e logs dialogCount=0 instead of failing.

Acceptance criteria:
- RootLayout top-bar button and Cmd/Ctrl+K open the renderer command palette from any stage.
- Palette commands are sourced from the command registry/keybinding context rather than a duplicate static list.
- Current project/stage context drives when-clause visibility; unknown context keys remain false/fail-loud per hp-rth/hp-6f4 expectations.
- Executing at least one palette command navigates or performs a visible safe shell action.
- Playwright e2e fails if the dialog is absent, verifies fuzzy search, executes one command, and records structured JSON-line evidence.

Cross-link: docs/post-phase1-reality-check.md:191; review-findings.md "Desktop shell command palette — [HIGH] top-bar command-palette control is inert and the e2e masks it".
notes: Wired CommandPalette into the renderer shell with global Cmd/Ctrl+K + Cmd+Shift+P shortcuts and a top-bar Open command palette button (now with onClick + aria-expanded). Mounted CommandPaletteHost from RootLayout.tsx; added commandPaletteOpen + recentCommandIds to the shell store. Built a renderer command source (commands.ts) that derives stage commands from stageDefinitions (no duplicate static list), gates them on project.active, and exposes activity.toggle / activity.close / project.open-picker. Reused the design-system primitive's pure model functions (getCommandPaletteModel, moveCommandPaletteSelection) for fuzzy ranking + when-clause filtering — fail-loud on unknown context keys per Appendix B #7. e2e (apps/desktop/tests/e2e/command-palette.spec.ts) opens dialog via ControlOrMeta+K, fail-loud asserts visibility, fuzzy-searches 'swarm', presses Enter -> /local-demo/swarm, exercises top-bar button + Escape roundtrip, records structured ndjson evidence. Skips on hosts missing libgbm.so.1 (same skip path as hp-j30 — known reality-check limitation). Verification: typecheck clean; bun test 169/0 across 23 files (+7 new commands.test.ts cases); ubs --files <changed> 0 Critical / 0 Warning / 176 Info.

title: Phase 2.5: Chaos / fault-injection suite (tunnel drop, daemon restart, disk pressure, slow renderer, malformed adapter)
description:
WHAT
====
First chaos / fault-injection test suite (§16 Phase 2.5 task 7). Simulate adverse conditions and verify the daemon + desktop degrade gracefully per §10.1, §10.5, and §14 risk mitigations.

WHY
===
Real users will hit every fault below. Without a chaos suite, each fault is a fresh discovery in production. Fault-injection turns 'mystery production crashes' into 'CI failure that gets fixed.'

REQUIRED FAULTS
===============
1. **Tunnel drop** — kill the SSH tunnel mid-flight. Assert: ConnectionManager FSM transitions through reconnecting; bearer survives; sequence-cursor reconnect closes the gap; replay is idempotent.
2. **Daemon restart** — systemctl restart hoopoe. Assert: jobs persist + are picked up where they left off (or marked interrupted with audit); bearer still valid; WS reconnects under p95 < 5s after process is back.
3. **Desktop crash + restart** — kill -9 the Electron process. Assert: tunnel cleaned up server-side within timeout; daemon does not leak resources; user re-launches and resumes.
4. **VPS reboot** — full VPS power cycle. Assert: daemon comes back via systemd; desktop reconnects automatically; tool inventory re-runs.
5. **Disk pressure on VPS** — fill /tmp or /data to ≥ 95%. Assert: watch-safety-thresholds emits warning event (Phase 10 wires the actuator); writes to /v1/jobs/{id}/log degrade gracefully (truncate-with-warning, not crash); audit log writes continue (high priority for audit retention).
6. **Slow renderer** — renderer subscribes to WS but stalls reads. Assert: daemon emits _lag with offset; daemon RSS bounded; recovery via offset fetch works.
7. **Malformed adapter output** — feed an adapter (br, bv, ntm, agent_mail) malformed JSON. Assert: capability registry marks the capability degraded; UI renders 'degraded' state; audit log records the parse failure with the offending bytes redacted.
8. **Long-running scheduler job** — tending tick runs longer than 10× normal. Assert: tick loop dispatches other jobs; one long run does NOT block unrelated due jobs (§8.3.2 invariant).

ADDITIONAL FAULTS (pulled forward from §10.5 fault-injection list)
====================================================================
- Stuck terminal stream (Phase 8 wires this; the substrate must support it now).
- Rate limit (Phase 8/10 wire CAAM/caut detection; substrate-only here).
- Git push failure (network).
- Missing tool — capability registry handles via degraded mode.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §10.1 — backpressure, lag, replay rules.
- §10.5 — fault-injection list.
- §14 — risks and mitigations.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Chaos suite committed under apps/daemon/test/chaos/.
- All 8 base faults pass against Mock Flywheel + a real research-spike VPS.
- Suite runs in CI nightly; smoke variants run on every PR.
- docs/reconnect-replay.md updated with verified behaviors.

title: Phase 6: Bead conversion + traceability + polish-rounds test suite (br adapter correctness, traceability accuracy, polish-round artifacts, bv robot surfaces)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 6 test suite covering plan-to-bead conversion, the br adapter, the bv adapter, polish rounds, and the plan-to-bead + implementation-evidence traceability ledgers.

UNIT TESTS
============
- br adapter: br --json subcommands (create / update / show / list / dep add/remove/list/cycles / ready / blocked / search / sync); .beads/issues.jsonl read model; sync orchestration (br sync --flush-only + git add .beads/ + git commit + git push); idempotency on retried commands; concurrent br invocations serialized through daemon br_ops_per_project semaphore.
- bv robot adapter: bv --robot-triage / --robot-plan / --robot-insights / --robot-diff parsed correctly; bare bv NEVER invoked from automation (lint rule + runtime guard); fallback if --robot- surfaces are missing.
- Plan-to-beads conversion job: verifies plan locked → loads plan.md + meta.json → spawns conversion agent with beads-workflow skill loaded → agent calls br create through allowed daemon RPCs → polish-rounds prompts available → traceability.json built.
- traceability.json shape: maps each bead to plan sections it implements; coverage status; test obligations; unmapped sections; orphans; oversized beads; duplicate candidates.
- implementation-evidence.jsonl streaming: plan section → bead → branch → commits → files → tests → health deltas → review findings → landing item.
- Polish-rounds jobs (6 rounds): plan coverage / dependency correctness / granularity / test obligations / parallel tracks / fresh-eyes; each tracked as its own job artifact; can be re-run; idempotent.
- Bead-set quality tracker: plan coverage, dependency correctness, granularity, ready-set size, testability, duplicate risk, parallelism, context richness — each computed from br + bv + traceability.
- Conversion agent skill loading: beads-workflow skill loaded via jsm (preferred SHA-256 pin) or jfp (fallback advisory); refuses to start when skill is unavailable.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. Locked fixture plan → conversion → real br beads with dependencies + traceability (Phase 6 acceptance). Verify: every plan section is covered, every bead links back to ≥1 plan section, traceability.json validates against schema.
2. Polish round 1 (plan coverage): identifies missing plan sections, opens revision PRs to br beads.
3. Polish round 6 (fresh-eyes): runs in a new agent session with no prior context; produces independent assessment.
4. Bead-delta on plan amend: plan locked v1 converts → amend to v2 → re-run conversion → delta job lists added/removed/changed beads + impact on existing implementation evidence.
5. bv --robot-triage panel renders top-pick beads + actionable_count + blocked_count + open_count from real .beads/issues.jsonl.
6. br sync round-trip: changes in daemon SQLite → br sync --flush-only → .beads/issues.jsonl updated → git add + commit + push works; subsequent git pull on another machine + br sync --import-only restores.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
========================
- br --json returns malformed entry → adapter capability degrades + UI shows degraded state; conversion job pauses with clear error.
- .beads/issues.jsonl corrupted → daemon refuses operations + Diagnostics offers 'rebuild bead read model' repair (§10.2).
- Concurrent br operations from a swarm + a polish round → daemon serializes; no conflicting writes.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every br invocation logs the exact subcommand, args, exit code, stdout-hash, stderr.
- Every polish-round artifact logs the prompt hash + the model/account used.
- Every traceability.json regeneration logs the input plan hash + bead-graph hash + output diff.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 — Beads stage strategic intent.
- §16 Phase 6 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 6 acceptance row.
- hp-dz8 / hp-pjp / hp-ojh / hp-xi9 / hp-j3x — Phase 6 implementation beads.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.
- skill: beads-workflow (jeffreys-skills.md/skills/beads-workflow).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on br adapter + bv adapter + conversion job + traceability builder.
- All 6 e2e scenarios pass against fixture corpus + real br/bv on research-spike VPS.
- Polish rounds 1-6 each produce a tracked artifact under .hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/polish-round-<N>.md.
- traceability.json schema in packages/schemas/ + JSON-schema validation on every regeneration.

title: Phase 2: Daemon HTTP/WS scaffolding (chi/echo + gorilla/nhooyr WS, /health, /v1/version, /v1/jobs)
description:
WHAT
====
Bootstrap the Hoopoe Go daemon: chi or echo for HTTP, gorilla/nhooyr WebSocket, modernc SQLite for storage, creack/pty fallback. Bind 127.0.0.1 by default. Basic endpoints: /health, /v1/version, /v1/events (SSE + WS), /v1/jobs.

WHY
===
These are the minimum endpoints required to prove tunnel + auth + event delivery work. Subsequent phases extend on this scaffolding.

ENDPOINTS (§2.6 seed contract)
================================
- GET /health — liveness; returns 200 if process is up.
- GET /v1/version — daemon version, build commit, build date, API version.
- GET /v1/jobs — list jobs (will be empty in Phase 2; populated by job registry in Phase 2.5).
- GET /v1/events/sse?channels= — SSE stream of events.
- WS /v1/events/ws?wsToken= — WebSocket event stream.
- GET /v1/events/replay?channel=&sinceSequence= — replay events from sequence cursor.

CONFIGURATION
==============
- Bind address: 127.0.0.1 by default (§2.4). Public bind requires explicit config flag + Diagnostics red warning + runtime confirmation.
- Port: discovered dynamically (find-open-port pattern from t3code, plus port-conflict resolution per Appendix B anti-pattern #5). Reported via stdout 'listening on <port>' line so ServerListeningDetector can parse.
- Storage: ~/.hoopoe/daemon.db (modernc SQLite, no CGO).
- Logs: structured JSON to stdout, parsed by systemd journal; redaction layer applies (audit-log epic).

BOUNDED CHANNELS (Appendix B anti-pattern #1)
==============================================
PubSub.unbounded is REJECTED. Every channel has a bounded buffer. Slow consumers force lag detection (§10.1) — emit _lag with lastPersistedOffset or _gap with sinceSequence repair instructions.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — full seed REST/WS contract.
- §2.7 — daemon process manager and job registry (Phase 2.5).
- §10.1 — backpressure, lag, and replay rules.
- Appendix B — anti-patterns to refuse.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- 'go run ./cmd/hoopoe' starts the daemon, binds 127.0.0.1:<port>, writes 'listening on <port>' to stdout.
- /health returns {ok: true}.
- /v1/version returns version + commit + date + apiVersion.
- WS /v1/events/ws accepts a connection given a valid wsToken (Phase 2 separate task wires the token).
- Bounded channel test: artificially slow consumer triggers _lag emission within bounded latency.
- modernc SQLite tested cross-compiled for arm64 + x64 Linux.
close_reason: Implemented in 0633f49 — daemon HTTP/WS scaffold

title: Phase 5 sub-task: Per-step planning prompts versioned + regression-tested
description:
WHAT
====
Per-step planning prompts for the multi-model pipeline (§7.1) — versioned, regression-tested, hashed, and committed under packages/planning-prompts/. Each step's exact prompt content is the source of planning quality; treating it as 'just a string in a function' is how the planning quality drifts silently.

THE STEPS THAT EACH NEED A PROMPT
====================================
1. clarifying-questions.md (when kickoff mode = 'clarify') — what the model asks the user before drafting.
2. take-first-shot.md (when kickoff mode = 'take-first-shot') — the system prompt for the first-pass candidate.
3. candidate-draft.md — used by each candidate model to produce a candidate plan.
4. comparative-matrix.md — used to build the side-by-side comparison surface.
5. synthesis-best-of-all-worlds.md — used by the primary model as final arbiter.
6. fresh-eyes-critique.md — used in a brand-new primary-model session for independent critique.
7. refinement-round-N.md (one shared template, N as a context variable) — used in fresh primary-model sessions for each refinement round.
8. quality-evaluator.md — used to score the 7 quality dimensions deterministically.
9. lock-readiness.md — used to verify unresolved-decisions.md is empty before lock.

VERSIONING
===========
- Every prompt is committed with a frontmatter block: {id, version, hash, owner, last-edited, applies-to-pipeline-versions}.
- packages/planning-prompts/manifest.json lists every prompt with its current hash.
- Prompts can be edited deliberately; CI fails if the manifest hash doesn't match the file content (drift detection).

REGRESSION TESTING
====================
- For each prompt, a fixture in packages/fixtures/planning-prompt-regression/ with: input shape, expected-output schema, sample acceptable outputs.
- Tests run against a deterministic mock model (NOT real models — too expensive, too non-deterministic).
- Real-model regression tests run nightly on a subset, with manual approval before bumping the prompt's expected hash.

PROMPT-DRIFT DETECTION
========================
- Every plan-job logs the exact hash of every prompt used.
- If two plans use different hashes for the same step, Diagnostics surfaces the drift.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 — Planning workspace.
- §1.4 — every automation must be inspectable (prompts are part of automation).
- hp-al4 — multi-model pipeline (consumer).
- hp-ktn — Phase 5 test bead.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 9 prompts committed with frontmatter + manifest hash.
- Drift detection wired into CI.
- Regression fixtures cover deterministic-mock test for every prompt.
- docs/planning-prompts.md describes the surface so external contributors can propose prompt changes via PR.

title: Phase 11: Health snapshot persistence + snapshot-health tending integration + activity events
description:
WHAT
====
Persist health snapshots as artifacts; trigger via the snapshot-health tending job (Phase 10) on event vps_push_completed | every 10m. Emit health_snapshot_updated events to Activity panel.

SNAPSHOT CADENCE (§7.4.1)
===========================
- On every push to main.
- After each swarm round.
- On demand from the Health tab.
- From snapshot-health tending job: fires on vps_push_completed events; at most once per 10 minutes (cached results reused if no relevant files changed).

SNAPSHOT SHAPE
===============
{
  snapshotId, projectId, commitSha, takenAt, durationMs,
  metrics: {
    avgCoverage, avgComplexity, hotspotCount,
    uncoveredFilesRatio, recentTestFailures
  },
  files: [
    {path, loc, complexity, coverage, churn7d, churn30d, ownerAgent?, linkedBeadId?, hotspotReasons: []}
  ],
  trend: {prev_snapshot_id?, deltas: {...}}
}

ACTIVITY EVENT (§7.4.1)
=========================
{type: 'health.snapshot_updated', summary: 'coverage 64% → 67%, 2 new hotspots, 1 hotspot resolved'}

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.4.1 — snapshot cadence + worktree isolation.
- §8.4 snapshot-health job.
- §10.3 — retention: keep last N full snapshots + compacted trend history.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Snapshots persisted as artifacts (per §2.7 artifact registry).
- snapshot-health tending job fires on vps_push_completed.
- Coalescing: don't double-snapshot same commit SHA.
- health.snapshot_updated events flow to Activity panel.
- Retention: last N snapshots + compacted trend.

title: Phase 9: Agent Mail ingestion into Activity timeline + reservation conflict surfacing
description:
WHAT
====
Subscribe to Agent Mail events (via Phase 8 adapter) and publish them into the Activity panel's WS event stream. Surface reservation conflicts as urgent items.

INGESTION
==========
- Daemon subscribes to Agent Mail (MCP/HTTP).
- Each new mail event becomes an Activity-channel event {type: 'mail.sent'|'mail.received'|'mail.urgent', threadId, from, to, subject, body, beadId?}.
- Reservation events ({reserved, renewed, released, conflicted}) similarly become Activity events.

CONFLICT SURFACING
===================
- Two agents reserve overlapping files within a window → conflict event with both reservations linked.
- Stale reservation (open beyond TTL) → warning event with 'force-release' action.
- Force-release goes through approvals queue + Agent Mail notice (per §8.3.1 reservation.force_release postcondition).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.5 — Activity event categories.
- §8.4 tend-swarm — also detects stale reservations independently.
- approvals queue epic.
- audit log epic.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Mail ingestion publishes Activity events per category.
- Reservation conflicts surface as urgent items.
- Force-release works end-to-end (approval → Agent Mail notice → audit → postcondition verify per §8.3.1).
- Phase 9 smoke: stale reservation appears in Activity panel; user force-releases; audit + Agent Mail notice both verify.

title: Phase 4 / §7.7: Local search infrastructure (ripgrep over local clone + daemon grep RPC fallback)
description:
WHAT
====
Wire local search across the cockpit: ripgrep (rg) over the desktop's local clone (§7.7) is the DEFAULT search backend; the daemon's grep RPC is the fallback for queries that need to see uncommitted-on-VPS work. UI: a search box that returns ranked results with file + line + snippet + context preview, click → 'Open in editor' or in-app preview.

WHY (§7.7 'Local search', §7.7 'What it powers')
==================================================
§7.7 lists 'Local search — ripgrep over the local clone is the default search backend; the daemon's grep RPC is a fallback for uncommitted-on-VPS work' as one of the four cockpit-wide capabilities the local clone enables. The local-clone bead (hp-2n1) implements the clone itself; this bead is the search infrastructure on top.

Without dedicated search:
- Users fall back to OS-level Spotlight or external search → breaks the cockpit's 'one place' premise.
- Beads drawer 'find references to X' / Health tab 'where else is this hotspot used' / Review findings 'what calls this function' all become unanswerable inside Hoopoe.
- The 'instant' UX promise of the local clone is wasted.

ARCHITECTURE
=============

DESKTOP (PRIMARY PATH)
-----------------------
Bundled rg binary per platform (arm64 + x64 macOS for v1; ship rg with the DMG; verify SHA on first run). Lifecycle:
1. App start: detect bundled rg path; verify SHA against manifest; refuse to fall back to system rg unless user explicitly opts in.
2. Search RPC inside main process: spawn rg with --json --max-count + project clone path + query; stream NDJSON results; parse into structured ranked results.
3. Cap results at 5,000 hits (configurable); when capped, UI shows 'showing first 5,000 of N — refine query to see more'.
4. Default flags: --hidden (search hidden files), --ignore-case (smart-case fallback), --vimgrep, --column, with .gitignore + .ignore + project-level .hoopoeignore honored.
5. Sub-1s for typical queries on a 50k-LOC repo on a modern Mac (target SLO).

DAEMON FALLBACK (uncommitted-on-VPS / dirty-tree work)
-------------------------------------------------------
GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/grep?query=&flags=&include_working_tree=1
Daemon runs rg under the VPS clone with the working tree included; results streamed back via SSE or chunked HTTP. UI calls this when:
- User explicitly requests 'include unpushed changes' (default off).
- Or the local clone is missing / out of sync past a threshold (e.g., last_fetched > 5min ago and user typed a query).

UI INTEGRATION
---------------
Search box (⌘P / dedicated icon in stage chrome):
- Free-text query with regex toggle.
- Scope: This file / This bead's files / This stage's files / Whole project (default whole project).
- Filter chips: file-type (ts / py / md / yaml), path glob, modified-since.
- Toggle: 'include uncommitted on VPS' (calls daemon fallback).
- Results pane: ranked grouped by file; per-result mini-snippet with the matched line highlighted + 1 line before + 1 line after.
- Click result → in-app file preview at line OR 'Open in editor' (uses §7.7 open-in-editor link).
- Saved queries (e.g., 'TODO' / 'FIXME' / 'console.log' / 'eslint-disable') — bookmark + run-on-demand.

INTEGRATION CONSUMERS
----------------------
- Beads drawer: 'Find all references to {symbol}' (right-click in code preview → search).
- Health tab: 'Where else is this hotspot used' (one-click search from hotspot row).
- Review findings: 'Find similar' (search by error pattern or function name).
- Plan workspace: 'Find in this codebase' (when chat box is auto-attaching existing-codebase bundle, allow user to search to verify before submitting).
- ⌘P palette: 'Search project' as a global command.

PERFORMANCE
============
- Pre-warm: on project open, pre-spawn rg with empty query to warm OS file cache.
- Throttle: 250ms debounce on user input; cancel in-flight rg if query changes.
- Result streaming: render first 50 results immediately, stream the rest as rg emits them.
- Memory cap: kill rg + show error if it exceeds 500 MB RSS (defensive against pathological queries).

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Bundled rg SHA verification** — at startup, bundled rg path is verified; assert SHA matches manifest; if it doesn't, app refuses to use it. Log: rg path, computed SHA, expected SHA, decision.
2. **NDJSON parser** — feed canned rg --json output (10 fixtures: empty result, single match, multi-match-per-line, binary file skipped, large-file truncated, error line, mixed-encoding); assert structured parse for each. Log: per-fixture input/parsed.
3. **.gitignore honored** — search 'foo' in fixture project where 'node_modules/foo.js' exists; result excludes node_modules. Log: project state, query, results.
4. **.hoopoeignore honored** — same fixture but with .hoopoeignore listing 'docs/'; assert results exclude docs/ matches. Log: ignore file, results.
5. **Result cap enforcement** — fixture project produces 5,001 matches; result list capped at 5,000; UI shows 'refine query' message; rg subprocess killed after cap hit. Log: total matches, cap state, message.
6. **Debounce** — simulate rapid typing 'a→ab→abc→abcd' over 100ms; only 1 rg invocation fires (after debounce); intermediate invocations cancelled. Log: keystroke timeline, invocation timeline.
7. **Cancellation on query change** — slow query in flight; user types new query; assert old rg process SIGTERM'd; new query starts. Log: process IDs + signals.
8. **Memory cap kill** — fixture rg subprocess hits memory cap (synthetic); SIGTERM sent + UI shows error. Log: memory readings, kill event, UI error.
9. **Daemon fallback gating** — local clone last_fetched is 6 minutes ago; user types query; UI prompts 'include uncommitted (slower)' option; if accepted → calls daemon RPC; assert RPC fired with right params. Log: clone state, prompt state, RPC call.
10. **Saved query persistence** — save 5 queries; close + reopen app; assert saved queries reload (via desktop-settings.json). Log: save / load roundtrip.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **End-to-end search happy path** — open fixture project with 50k-LOC repo; type 'function calculateRiskScore' in search box; results render in <1s; click first → file preview at correct line; verify 'open in editor' link works. Log: query, latency per phase (debounce, rg spawn, parse, render), results count.
2. **Cross-bead reference search** — fixture has function 'foo()' used in 5 files across 3 beads; search 'foo()' → results group by file; assert all 3 beads' files appear; click result → bead drawer auto-opens for that file's bead. Log: results, bead-attribution.
3. **Daemon fallback for uncommitted** — fixture has uncommitted-on-VPS file with 'newSymbol'; toggle 'include uncommitted'; daemon RPC fires; result includes the uncommitted hit (which local clone wouldn't see). Log: local clone state, daemon RPC + response, results comparison.
4. **Performance SLO** — 50 different queries on a 50k-LOC repo; assert p95 < 1s. Log: per-query latency histogram.
5. **Pathological-regex defense** — type 'a*' on a 5MB single-file repo; rg memory caps; query terminates with error message; UI recoverable (no crash). Log: memory peak, kill event, recovery.
6. **Search-from-hotspot** — Health tab → click hotspot row's 'find usages'; search auto-fills with hotspot symbol; results in scope. Log: hotspot state, search invocation.
7. **Saved-query smoke** — bookmark 'TODO'; close + reopen app; saved query persists; click → fires search; results render. Log: persist + load + run.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.7 — local clone + 'Local search' line.
- §5.4 — local clone paths treated as untrusted (rendering escape).
- hp-2n1 — local clone substrate (consumed).
- hp-w8m — Git watcher / clone-state staleness signal (consumed).
- hp-l65 — GitAdapter fallback grep RPC source.
- hp-i62 — components (search box, result row, file preview).
- hp-elx — design system (visual).
- hp-z1x — four-stage routed shell (search box in chrome).
- hp-4ya — top-bar search-shortcut.
- hp-0ba — bead drawer consumer.
- hp-gmm — health tab consumer.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Bundled rg per platform + SHA verified at startup.
- Search RPC in desktop main process + NDJSON parser.
- Daemon /v1/projects/{projectId}/grep fallback.
- UI search box (⌘P + chrome icon) with scope/filter/regex/uncommitted toggle.
- Result rendering with snippets + click→preview/editor.
- .gitignore + .hoopoeignore honored.
- Performance: p95<1s on 50k-LOC fixture.
- Saved queries persist via desktop-settings.json.
- Consumer integrations: bead drawer / health tab / palette / saved queries.
- All 10 unit tests + 7 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.

title: Phase 1.5: Make e2e runner real at root and CI-host portable
