title: Phase 3: Onboarding wizard + ACFS bootstrap test suite (preflight, structured-checkpoint resume, raw-log fallback, doctor parsing, daemon upgrade rollback)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 3 test suite covering the onboarding wizard, ACFS bootstrap streaming + parsing, resumable setup checkpoints, the tool inventory + CAAM verification, and the daemon upgrade flow.

UNIT TESTS
============
- First-run wizard UI: 13 canonical steps wrapped + Hoopoe extensions interleaved; structured pass/fail cards per phase; resume-on-failure re-enters at the last successful checkpoint.
- ACFS bootstrap parser (§6.4): phase.start / phase.line / phase.checkpoint / phase.end / phase.fail events emitted on stable markers; falls back to raw-log mode if parsing confidence drops; preserves run + exit-code + resume action.
- Bootstrap fixtures (hp-qq9): clean install / partially-complete install / failed-dependency install / already-installed ACFS — each parsed correctly.
- Doctor parsing: acfs doctor --json structured pass/fail per category renders to UI; failure cards link to repair actions.
- Tool inventory: discovers ntm / br / bv / agent_mail / caam / caut / dcg / casr / pt / srp / sbh / ubs / jsm / jfp / ru / oracle versions; populates capability registry.
- CAAM subscription verification: which CLIs are signed in (Claude Code → Claude Max, Codex CLI → GPT Pro, Gemini CLI → Gemini Ultra); warn (don't block) if zero subscriptions configured.
- Daemon upgrade flow: download → verify checksum + signature + provenance + SBOM policy → stop service → backup config/db → install → start → verify /v1/version → run compatibility checks → on migration failure, rollback to previous binary + database backup.
- Provenance verification: refuses installation when verification fails unless explicit insecure-development override is set; override is audited + visibly marked in Diagnostics.
- Resumable checkpoints: pre-flight-passed / packages-installed / acfs-installed / acfs-doctor-passed / daemon-installed / daemon-paired — each persisted; failures resume rather than restart.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. Phase 3 acceptance: ACFS install/doctor runs through Hoopoe with structured checkpoints; a failed run resumes; raw-log fallback works when markers are missing.
2. Cold VPS → 13-step wizard end-to-end → ready state (with Mock Flywheel substituting actual VPS for CI; real VPS for nightly).
3. Mid-install SSH drop: ACFS install progresses to phase 5/10 → drop SSH → reconnect → wizard resumes at phase 5 not phase 1 (idempotent installer).
4. ACFS marker drift: feed parser an installer log with subtly-changed markers → parser confidence drops → falls back to raw-log mode preserving run + exit code.
5. Daemon upgrade happy path: 0.0.5 → 0.0.6, /v1/version returns new version, compatibility checks pass.
6. Daemon upgrade with provenance failure: simulated bad signature → upgrade refused; user sees clear error.
7. Daemon upgrade migration failure: simulated DB migration error → rollback to 0.0.5 + restored DB backup; daemon back online; user notified.
8. Onboard tutorial link: optional step 13 surfaces 'Launch onboard' button in success screen; click → Diagnostics terminal pane; not gating wizard completion.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
========================
- ACFS one-liner network interruption mid-stream → resume from last completed phase.
- Doctor JSON malformed → fall back to raw-log; user sees diagnostic plus retry button.
- Daemon binary corrupted post-download → checksum mismatch → upgrade refused with clear error.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every wizard step logs the step ID + duration + outcome.
- Bootstrap log streamed to ~/.hoopoe/logs/bootstrap-{runId}.log AND parsed events to the structured logger.
- Daemon upgrade logs include before/after versions, checksum, signature identity, SBOM policy result.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6 — onboarding (canonical 13-step wizard, Hoopoe extensions).
- §11 — packaging and updates.
- §16 Phase 3 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 3 acceptance row.
- hp-o6q / hp-qq9 / hp-wa3 / hp-0ug / hp-4iz — Phase 3 implementation beads.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on wizard + parser + upgrade flow.
- All 8 e2e scenarios pass against fixture corpus + at least one real fresh VPS.
- Bootstrap parser fixtures cover all 4 named install states.
- Provenance + SBOM policy verified in CI.

title: Phase 0: Snapshot script — single command emitting normalized JSON for entire stack
description:
WHAT
====
ONE script (Bash + jq + helpers) that produces a single normalized JSON snapshot covering the full ACFS stack on the research-spike VPS. The output is the seed parser fixture corpus that drives Mock Flywheel Mode (cross-cutting epic) and adapter contract tests (§18.3).

WHY (§12 / §16 Phase 0 deliverable 2 / §18 Phase 0 acceptance)
================================================================
'Exit: one command produces a reliable machine-readable project snapshot.' Without this, each adapter team has to discover output formats ad hoc; with it, they have golden fixtures to develop against.

REQUIRED COVERAGE
==================
The snapshot MUST cover (§16 Phase 0 task 2 + §17 'Phase 0 must verify'):
- git: status, branch, HEAD SHA, working-tree state, unpushed commits, remote URL
- br: br list --json (all issues), br ready --json, br dep cycles --json, .beads/issues.jsonl
- bv: bv --robot-triage, bv --robot-plan, bv --robot-insights, bv --robot-diff (NEVER bare bv — that launches TUI per §1.3 / Appendix C #1)
- ntm: ntm --robot-snapshot, ntm --robot-status, ntm --robot-tail, ntm serve REST/SSE/WS schema
- Agent Mail: messages dump, threads, file reservations
- ru: ru sync --dry-run --json --non-interactive, ru status --no-fetch --json, ru list --paths, ru prune --dry-run, ru robot-docs, ru --schema (the LAST is critical — pins the GitAdapter scope per §2.3)
- Health: lizard JSON, language-native test/coverage runner output (TS/JS, Python, Rust, Go, generic via tokei/cloc/scc)
- caut: per-provider quota usage snapshot
- CAAM: account-list, account-status, switch-account command help (do NOT execute switch in research)
- DCG: a sample verdict format (record blocked/requires_confirmation/allowed)
- UBS: a sample finding format
- jsm + jfp: install/list/verify/update CLI help and one --json output
- pt / srp / sbh: invocation contracts (help + sample non-destructive output)
- oracle: oracle serve status, --remote-host help, --write-output sample artifact

OUTPUT FORMAT
=============
{
  meta: {snapshotVersion: '0.1', capturedAt, vpsId, acfsTag, toolVersions: {...}},
  git: {...},
  beads: {issues: [...], ready: [...], cycles: [...], jsonl: '...'},
  bv: {triage: {...}, plan: {...}, insights: {...}, diff: {...}},
  ntm: {snapshot: {...}, status: {...}, tail: {...}, serve: {schemaUrl}},
  agent_mail: {messages: [...], threads: [...], reservations: [...]},
  ru: {sync_dry_run: {...}, status: {...}, list_paths: [...], prune_dry_run: {...}, schema: {...}, robot_docs: '...'},
  health: {lizard: {...}, native: {...}},
  caut: {usage: {...}},
  caam: {accounts: [...], help: '...'},
  dcg: {sampleVerdict: {...}, help: '...'},
  ubs: {sampleFinding: {...}, help: '...'},
  skills: {jsm: {help, list, verify_sample}, jfp: {help, list}},
  ops: {pt_help, srp_signals, sbh_help},
  oracle: {serve_status, remote_host_help, write_output_sample}
}

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Capture two states: (a) a fresh project right after acfs onboard, (b) an active project mid-swarm. Both feed Mock Flywheel.
- Capture one DELIBERATE FAILURE per surface (br on a broken DB, bv with cycle, ntm with a wedged pane) so the failure-path fixtures exist for adapter contract tests.
- Document command, exit code, time-to-complete, and any flakiness for each invocation in research-spike-notes.md.
- The script lives in scripts/research-spike/snapshot.sh; it must be runnable via 'ssh <vps> bash snapshot.sh > snapshot.json'.

ACCEPTANCE
==========
- Running snapshot.sh on the research-spike VPS produces a single non-empty JSON file conforming to the schema above.
- Output committed to packages/fixtures/phase0-<date>/scenarios/{fresh,active,failure}/snapshot.json.
- Each tool's known gotchas (TUI invocations to never repeat, undocumented flags, version skew) documented in docs/integration-contracts/<tool>.md.
close_reason: Snapshot script + envelope helpers + JSON schema + README + research-spike-notes skeleton. Verified via local --self-test: 11/18 tools captured, 7 absent handled gracefully, 2.6 MB output. Real-VPS captures gated on hp-r7i + hp-jvm (human-only).

title: Phase 2.5: SLO metrics scaffolding (collect §10.5 targets in dev + Mock Flywheel)
description:
WHAT
====
Collect SLO metrics from §10.5 in dev builds and Mock Flywheel Mode so regressions show up as graphs/tests rather than user reports.

SLOs (§10.5)
=============
| Area                                    | Target                                                  |
|-----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| Desktop reconnect after laptop wake     | p95 under 10s after network returns                     |
| Event replay after 10-min disconnect    | no lost state; p95 under 5s for normal channels         |
| Activity event display latency          | p95 under 1s excluding terminal chunks                  |
| Terminal stream attach                  | latest ring visible under 2s                            |
| Local file open from clone              | p95 under 150ms for cached blobs                        |
| Bead Kanban load                        | p95 under 1s for 1,000 beads                            |
| DAG usable                              | 500 visible nodes, clustered beyond                     |
| Job cancellation                        | no orphan child process groups after cancel test        |

INSTRUMENTATION
================
- Daemon emits structured metrics: counter (events_replayed_total), histogram (request_duration_seconds, log_fetch_duration_seconds), gauge (active_ws_connections, in_flight_jobs).
- Desktop renderer emits performance-marks for kanban load, DAG render, file open from local clone.
- Both ship to a local metrics aggregator in dev (e.g., Prometheus textfile collector or an in-process histogram).
- CI test thresholds: regression triggers a CI failure with a clear comparison against the baseline.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §10.5 — SLO list.
- §16 Phase 2.5 task 8.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Metrics collected for every SLO above.
- CI publishes a metrics report per PR.
- Baseline thresholds pinned; PR that regresses any metric beyond a slop threshold fails CI.
- Dev mode UI shows metrics in Diagnostics for live observation.

title: Phase 0: Per-tool integration contracts (docs/integration-contracts/<tool>.md)
description:
WHAT
====
For each adapter listed in §2.3 + §17 reference toolchain, produce one markdown file under docs/integration-contracts/ that pins the exact command shape, expected output schema, capability IDs, error modes, and version compatibility expectations Hoopoe will rely on.

WHY
===
The §2.8 capability registry needs ground truth for every capability ID, and the §18.3 adapter contract tests need golden fixtures. Without a per-tool contract written down, adapter authors will reinvent assumptions and the registry will silently drift.

REQUIRED FILES (one each, with consistent shape)
=================================================
- ntm.md — sessions.list, panes.stream (transports: ws/sse/poll), approvals.list, robot.snapshot/status/tail, swarm.halt
- br.md — issues.read/update, dep.add, sync model (br sync --flush-only ONLY; git is OUR job per beads-br skill)
- bv.md — robot.triage / plan / insights / diff. NEVER bare bv.
- agent_mail.md — messages.read/send, threads.read, reservations.list/release
- git.md — status / diff / push / unpushed.list (per-project, via daemon — desktop only reads its own clone per §5.3 narrow exception)
- ru.md — sync --json, status --no-fetch --json, list --paths, prune --archive (Diagnostics repair, §10.2). DELIBERATELY NOT adopted: ru review, ru agent-sweep, ru ai-sync, ru dep-update — see §17 closing notes for rationale.
- caam.md — accounts.list, account.switch (used by tend-swarm rate-limit recovery)
- caut.md — usage.snapshot (subscription-quota tracker, NOT API tokens)
- dcg.md — verdicts ingestion (blocked / requires_confirmation / allowed) → unified approvals queue
- casr.md — session.resume (cross-CLI context preservation; post-MVP)
- pt.md — kill (deterministic actuator, watch-safety-thresholds)
- srp.md — signals.read (disk/CPU/load)
- sbh.md — cleanup (stale-artifact under disk pressure)
- ubs.md — scan (review round 0 + specialized audits)
- jsm.md / jfp.md — skill install/list/verify/update; SHA-256 vs advisory version-string distinction (§10.3)
- oracle.md — serve, --remote-host/--remote-token, browser-mode auto-reattach, --write-output, cookie/session persistence on Chrome restarts
- health/{ts,python,rust,go,generic}.md — coverage, complexity, churn extraction commands
- rch.md — build offload command/flags

EACH FILE MUST CONTAIN
=======================
- Tool name + canonical repo + version observed in research-spike
- Adapter precedence (per §2.3): which surfaces we use first, which are fallback
- Capability IDs declared (§2.8) with canonical schemas linked
- Sample stdin/argv → stdout/stderr → exit code combinations as tested
- Known gotchas (TUI invocations to avoid, undocumented flags, version skew)
- Failure modes and how the adapter should recover or degrade
- Authentication / credential model (where applicable; reference CAAM)
- Test-fixture references in packages/fixtures/phase0-*/

ACCEPTANCE
==========
Every adapter named in §2.3, §7 (per-stage tools), §8 (tending toolsets) has a contract file. Each file is ≥ 1 page and ≤ 5 pages — concise but pinned. A new adapter author can write the Go/TS code from the contract alone, without re-running the research spike.
close_reason: 23 contract files (README + 17 tool + 5 health) for every adapter named in plan.md §2.3, §17. Each contract pins command shape, capability IDs, failure modes, fixtures path, adapter Go-side notes. Grounded in snapshot.sh self-test where the tool was present locally; placeholders explicitly flagged where VPS pinning required (caut, casr, pt, srp, sbh, jfp, oracle).

title: Phase 0: Research spike + integration contract on a real ACFS VPS
description:
GOAL (§12 Phase 0 / §16 Phase 0)
=================================
Prove the stack can be READ and CONTROLLED from code. Produce one machine-readable JSON snapshot covering Git, beads, bv triage, NTM session, Agent Mail messages/reservations, file reservations, ru multi-repo, and health metrics. Capture parser fixtures for every output format that downstream phases will consume.

WHY THIS PHASE EXISTS
=====================
Plain-text CLI parsing is the LAST resort in the §1.3 integration ladder. Before Phase 1 starts vendoring t3code or scaffolding the daemon, we need confidence that the canonical sources of truth (NTM serve, bv --robot-*, br --json, ru --json, Agent Mail MCP/API, Health adapters) actually emit what the plan assumes — and that we have committed fixtures that drive Mock Flywheel Mode (the cross-cutting epic) so subsequent UI work isn't blocked on real VPS provisioning.

The other reason: 'you cannot automate a flow you haven't completed manually.' The team must run the canonical agent-flywheel.com 13-step wizard end-to-end as a USER would before writing the wizard wrapper.

BUDGET (§16)
============
~3 days, parallel with Phase 1 vendor + scaffold.

DELIVERABLES
============
1. Test VPS with ACFS installed via canonical curl|bash (--mode vibe, pinned --ref <tag>) on Contabo Cloud VPS 50 / OVH VPS-5 / existing VPS.
2. Manual run-through of the canonical 13-step wizard (agent-flywheel.com/wizard/os-selection) with step-by-step notes — this is the basis for §6.4 structured ACFS bootstrap parsing.
3. NTM serve running, sample project with br/bv populated, Agent Mail running.
4. Snapshot script — ONE command that emits a normalized JSON snapshot covering:
   - git status + working-tree state
   - br list --json
   - bv --robot-triage, bv --robot-plan, bv --robot-insights
   - ntm --robot-snapshot, ntm --robot-status, ntm --robot-tail (and what NTM serve REST/SSE/WS exposes)
   - Agent Mail dump (messages + reservations + threads)
   - file reservations
   - lizard / language-native health
   - ru sync --dry-run --json, ru status --no-fetch --json, ru list --paths, ru prune --dry-run, ru robot-docs, ru --schema (for §2.3 GitAdapter scope)
   - caut JSON snapshot shape
   - CAAM account-list/switch CLI shape
   - DCG verdict format
   - UBS finding format
   - jsm / jfp install/list/verify/update CLIs (for §10.3 lock-file design)
   - pt / srp / sbh invocation contracts
   - oracle: oracle serve startup, --remote-host/--remote-token, browser-mode auto-reattach behavior, --write-output artifact format, cookie/session persistence
5. Documented command/API contracts in docs/integration-contracts/ (one markdown per tool).
6. Parser fixtures committed under packages/fixtures/phase0-<date>/ in a form that drives Mock Flywheel Mode (normalized snapshots, event streams, pane-output logs, build/test logs, Agent Mail messages, reservation conflicts, health snapshots, FAILURE cases).

EXIT (§12)
==========
One command produces a reliable machine-readable project snapshot. All §17 'verify Phase 0' tools have fixtures committed. Every gotcha (TUI invocations, undocumented flags, version skew, output-format drift) is documented before adapter coding starts in Phase 2.5+.

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1)
========================
On a real ACFS VPS, one command emits a normalized JSON snapshot for Git, beads, bv, NTM, Agent Mail, reservations, and health; fixtures are committed.

CHILD ROLLUP
============
Sub-tasks: VPS provisioning, manual wizard run-through, snapshot script, per-tool integration contracts, fixture corpus, Mock Flywheel Mode seed.
close_reason: Phase 0 epic — all subtasks closed; capability registry + adapter contract tests now have real-VPS evidence

title: Phase 2: AuthBridge wiring — capture pairing → exchange bearer → persist via safeStorage → JIT WS-token
description:
WHAT
====
The desktop AuthBridge module (built in Phase 1 decompose) is now wired against a real daemon: capture the pairing token from bootstrap stdout (over the SSH session, where the channel is authenticated), exchange it over the tunnel for a 30-day bearer, persist via safeStorage.encryptString. Issue a fresh WS-token just-in-time before each WebSocket connect.

WHY (§5.2 'Bootstrap auth flow', §6.3 step 11-12)
====================================================
The SSH session is the trusted bootstrap channel. The desktop's tunnel manager already opens an SSH session to run bootstrap.sh; when the daemon prints its initial pairing token to stdout, the desktop captures it without any QR code or out-of-band transfer.

FLOW
====
1. Desktop kicks off bootstrap.sh via SSH session.
2. bootstrap.sh runs through Phase 2 install steps, ends by printing pairing token to stdout (captured into a structured event).
3. Desktop opens local tunnel to daemon (Phase 2 tunnel manager).
4. Desktop POSTs /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer over the tunnel with the pairing token.
5. Daemon consumes pairing atomically, returns bearer + expiresAt + serverId.
6. Desktop stores bearer via SecretStore (safeStorage.encryptString); record serverId in client-settings.json.
7. For every WebSocket connect: POST /v1/auth/ws-token (with bearer) → wsToken; connect WS with ?wsToken=<token>.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.2 — full auth model.
- §6.3 — bootstrap flow.
- Phase 1 AuthBridge module — the home for this code.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Pairing-token capture must NOT log the token in plaintext anywhere (audit redaction).
- Bearer refresh: when bearer is within 24h of expiry, proactively refresh (POST /v1/auth/bearer/refresh) so user doesn't see auth errors mid-session.
- WS-token issuance: ALWAYS just-in-time; do not cache (5-min TTL is short enough that caching adds risk for ~no benefit).
- If the SSH session dies between print and capture, bootstrap is RESUMABLE — the user can re-run bootstrap.sh and a new pairing token will be generated (the original was issued but never consumed; daemon should age them out after a reasonable timeout).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- End-to-end smoke: cold VPS → bootstrap.sh runs over SSH → pairing token captured → bearer issued over tunnel → bearer persisted in Keychain → desktop reconnects after restart and resumes session.
- Bearer auto-refresh tested.
- WS-token JIT tested.
- Pairing token never appears in logs; redaction layer covered by fixture.
- Reconnect after Mac sleep (or laptop close+open): bearer still valid, no re-pair required.

title: TLS / mTLS direct-mode (advanced; post-MVP; Phase 13+ defer; tailnet-only listener for v1.x)
description:
WHAT
====
Per §2.5 transport ladder #4: mTLS direct-mode for advanced/team scenarios. Requires: explicit opt-in, firewall restriction, bearer auth on top of mTLS, loud diagnostics if exposed publicly. Tailscale / tailnet mode (§2.5 #3) optional and may use a small tsnet sidecar or already-installed Tailscale daemon.

WHY POST-MVP (§2.4, §13 'Can defer')
======================================
v1 ships SSH-tunnel-only. mTLS adds significant operational complexity (cert distribution, rotation, fingerprint TOFU semantics, public-binding alarming). Tailnet is slightly simpler but still post-MVP.

MTLS DETAIL
=============
- Daemon may expose HTTPS on public or private interface.
- Client certs PINNED during provisioning.
- Bearer token still required on top of mTLS.
- Diagnostics shows red warning for any non-local/tailnet bind.
- TLS/mTLS fingerprints in direct or tailnet mode use TOFU only when fingerprint arrived over an authenticated SSH bootstrap channel.

TAILNET MODE
==============
- Manage tsnet sidecar OR connect to already-installed Tailscale daemon.
- Daemon listener bound only to tailnet interface.
- Attractive for teams + long-lived reconnect reliability.
- NOT a v1 blocker.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.4 — default network posture.
- §2.5 — transport ladder.
- §5.4 — TOFU fingerprint rules.
- §13 — post-MVP defer.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- This bead documents the integration shape; full implementation deferred to v1.x.
- Code paths scaffolded so enabling is a flip rather than a major rewrite.
- Diagnostics red-warning surface implemented (already in §10.2).

title: Cross-cutting: Renderer error UX framework — problem+json renderer + toast/banner/inline-pill/blocking-modal hierarchy + accessibility + telemetry hooks
description:
WHAT
====
Cross-cutting infrastructure for surface-appropriate error UX in the desktop renderer. Covers:

1. **`errorBus`** — pub/sub layer any feature can publish to; renderer subscribes once and routes by severity + actionability.
2. **`ProblemJsonRenderer`** — accepts a §2.6 RFC 7807 problem+json payload, parses + classifies + auto-routes to the correct surface.
3. **Four-surface hierarchy** — toast / banner / inline-pill (capability-degraded marker) / blocking-modal — with consistent design tokens and behavior across all stages.
4. **Accessibility** — WCAG AA contrast, screen-reader announcements via aria-live, full keyboard navigation.
5. **Telemetry hooks (opt-in)** — `error_surfaces.published` events with severity + actionability + problem.type (no PII, no payload values).

WHY (§2.6 problem+json mandate, missing infrastructure bead)
============================================================
§2.6 mandates "all errors use RFC 7807-style problem+json." But the desktop side has no infrastructure bead. Today:
- Errors land as either silent toasts (lost) or alert dialogs (annoying).
- Each feature ad-hocs its own error rendering, creating exactly the inconsistent UX the design system is supposed to prevent.
- Phase 13 polish (hp-8op "Polished empty/loading/error states") arrives too late — error rendering touches every feature from Phase 1 onward.
- `aria-live` announcements + keyboard navigation for errors are not addressed in any existing bead.

This bead establishes the infrastructure as a Phase 1 / cross-cutting deliverable that successive phases consume rather than re-invent.

CONTRACT — errorBus
====================
Single renderer-process pub/sub layer:
```ts
type ErrorPayload = {
  source: string;             // e.g., "swarm-launch", "plan-generate"
  severity: "info" | "warning" | "error" | "critical" | "blocking";
  actionability: "none" | "retry-once" | "configure" | "contact-support" | "restart-required";
  problem: ProblemJson;       // RFC 7807 shape
  context?: Record<string, unknown>;  // e.g., {planId, swarmId} — optional, redacted before telemetry
  hints?: {
    primaryActionLabel?: string;
    primaryActionRoute?: string;
    dismissible?: boolean;
    persistAcrossRoutes?: boolean;
  };
};

errorBus.publish(payload);
errorBus.subscribe(handler);
errorBus.dismiss(errorId);
```

CONTRACT — ProblemJsonRenderer
================================
Accepts problem+json payload + classification + renders:
```ts
type ProblemJson = {
  type: string;       // URL identifying problem class
  title: string;      // user-readable summary
  status: number;     // HTTP-style status (e.g., 422)
  detail?: string;    // user-readable extended description
  instance?: string;  // URI of the specific occurrence
  // domain extensions (Hoopoe-specific):
  hoopoe?: {
    code: string;     // stable machine-readable code
    actionableHints?: string[];
    relatedDocs?: string;
  };
};
```

Classification rules (severity × actionability → surface):
```
                | none          | retry-once     | configure       | contact-support | restart-required
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
info            | toast         | toast          | toast           | toast           | banner
warning         | toast         | toast          | banner          | banner          | banner
error           | banner        | inline-pill    | banner          | banner          | banner
critical        | banner        | banner         | banner          | banner          | modal
blocking        | modal         | modal          | modal           | modal           | modal
```

CONTRACT — FOUR SURFACES
==========================

1. **Toast**:
   - Position: top-right, stacks vertically, max 5 concurrent.
   - Auto-dismiss: 5s default; configurable per-payload up to 30s; persist if `dismissible: false`.
   - Coalescing: identical (source + problem.code) within 2s coalesce; show "+ N more" indicator when stack > 5.
   - aria-role: `status` (info/warning) or `alert` (error+); aria-live: `polite` (info) or `assertive` (error+).

2. **Banner**:
   - Position: directly under top-bar, full-width, dismissible by default.
   - Persistence: per-session by default (re-appears on app restart unless persisted-dismiss flag set).
   - Up to 3 stacked; older banner moves to a "Show all banners" overflow.
   - aria-role: `region`, aria-label: "{severity} — {problem.title}".

3. **Inline-pill (capability-degraded)**:
   - Renders inside the affected UI element (e.g., greyed-out + small badge on a button).
   - Tooltip on hover/focus: "{problem.title}. {problem.detail}. {Open Diagnostics}".
   - Click: opens Diagnostics screen with the relevant capability section anchored.
   - Keyboard-accessible: tab focus + Enter triggers tooltip + Cmd+Enter triggers default action.

4. **Blocking-modal**:
   - Centered, focus-trapped, keyboard navigation, Esc dismisses (unless severity=blocking with no dismiss path), Cmd+Enter triggers primary action.
   - Renders problem.title as heading, problem.detail as body, hints.primaryActionLabel as primary button, "Cancel" as secondary.
   - aria-role: `dialog`, aria-modal: `true`.

CONTRACT — ACCESSIBILITY
==========================
- All surfaces meet WCAG AA contrast (normal text 4.5:1, large text 3:1, UI components 3:1).
- Screen-reader announcements via aria-live regions (polite for info, assertive for error+).
- Keyboard navigation: Esc dismisses toast/banner/modal (per dismissibility); Cmd+Enter triggers primary action; Tab order respects DOM order.
- Focus management: blocking-modal traps focus until dismissed; restoring focus to triggering element on close.
- Reduced-motion: opt out of slide/fade animations when `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.

CONTRACT — TELEMETRY
=====================
- When `desktopSettings.telemetryOptIn === true` (default `false`), publish events to telemetry endpoint:
  ```json
  {
    "type": "error_surfaces.published",
    "ts": "...",
    "severity": "warning",
    "actionability": "configure",
    "problem.type": "https://hoopoe.dev/problems/caam-account-missing",
    "problem.code": "caam.no-default-account-for-claude",
    "source": "swarm-launch"
  }
  ```
- NEVER include `problem.detail`, `problem.instance`, `context`, or any user-data — those may contain project paths, file names, or other PII.
- Telemetry endpoint stub for v1 (logs locally to `~/.hoopoe/telemetry.jsonl`); Phase 13 wires real upload.

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Routing matrix** — for each (severity × actionability) combination, publish to errorBus, verify expected surface mounts. Log: matrix table.
2. **problem+json round-trip** — parse problem+json with all fields → renderer outputs match `title` (heading), `detail` (body), `type` (link rendered via reactive markdown), domain extensions consumed correctly. Log: input + render tree.
3. **aria-live announcements** — info → polite; warning → polite; error → assertive; critical → assertive. Log: announcement queue snapshot.
4. **Keyboard nav** — Esc dismisses toast (yes), banner (yes), inline-pill (no — pill is anchored), blocking-modal (yes if dismissible, no if blocking). Cmd+Enter triggers default action. Log: keyboard event chain.
5. **Toast coalescing** — publish 5 identical errors within 2s → coalesce into one toast with "+ 4 more"; publish 6 distinct → stack of 5 with "+ 1 more" overflow. Log: stack state.
6. **Banner persistence** — banner with `persistAcrossRoutes: true` survives route change; banner without flag dismisses on route change. Log: banner state across navigations.
7. **Modal focus trap** — opening a modal moves focus to first focusable element inside; Tab cycles within; Shift+Tab cycles back; Esc closes (if dismissible) and restores focus to triggering element. Log: focus events.
8. **Telemetry redaction** — publish error with `context: {planId: "secret-plan-name", filePath: "/Users/x/secret"}`; verify telemetry payload contains only `severity / actionability / problem.type / problem.code / source`. Log: pre/post telemetry payload.
9. **Reduced motion** — set `matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches = true` → toast / banner / modal animations disabled. Log: animation property values.
10. **Capability-degraded inline pill** — feature publishes a problem with `severity: warning, actionability: configure` while a button is rendered with `requires-capability="caam"`; pill replaces button when CAAM unavailable. Log: render tree before/after.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Capability-degraded flow** — fixture: CAAM adapter returns `missing` for all accounts; affected swarm-launch button greys out + pill renders + tooltip on hover; clicking pill opens Diagnostics with `?capability=caam` anchor; verify within 200ms. Log: full UI render trace.
2. **Banner dismissal persistence** — banner appears (e.g., "VPS daemon version mismatch"); user dismisses; reload app → banner re-renders (because mismatch persists, per-session dismiss only). Reload after fix → no banner. Log: banner state across reloads.
3. **Modal blocker flow** — user attempts to edit a locked plan → modal "Plan locked. Unlock to edit." with primary action "Unlock plan" + secondary "Cancel"; Esc + Cmd+Enter both work; primary opens unlock dialog. Log: modal events.
4. **Cross-stage consistency** — same problem.type fired from Plans, Beads, Swarm, Hardening renders identical UI (same tokens, same layout, same actions). Log: per-stage render snapshots.
5. **Screen-reader smoke** — VoiceOver running; trigger toast / banner / modal; verify each is announced within 200ms with severity prefix ("Warning: ..."); modal heading announced first; focus on modal trapped. Log: VoiceOver announcement log.
6. **Telemetry e2e** — opt in to telemetry; trigger 10 errors of various shapes; verify `~/.hoopoe/telemetry.jsonl` contains exactly 10 lines with redacted payloads. Log: file dump.
7. **Stress test** — publish 100 errors in 1s; renderer doesn't block UI thread; main render frame stays > 50fps; toast queue handles overflow gracefully. Log: frame timings + queue state.
8. **A11y axe-core scan** — run axe-core against all four surface types in mounted state; assert zero WCAG AA violations. Log: axe-core report.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — RFC 7807 problem+json mandate (all errors).
- §5.4 — renderer hardening (CSP-aware modal rendering).
- §10.2 — Diagnostics screen (capability-degraded pill links here).
- hp-r3i — packages/schemas (problem+json schema source).
- hp-elx — design system (visual tokens consumed).
- hp-i62 — reusable components (Toast / Banner / Pill / Modal building blocks).
- hp-8gg — ApprovalDialog (specific modal pattern; this bead generalizes).
- hp-llpe — renderer hardening (cross-link).
- hp-8op — Phase 13 polished empty/loading/error states (Phase 13 polish layer atop this infrastructure).
- hp-kyr — telemetry / crash reports (Phase 13 wires telemetry endpoint).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- errorBus pub/sub primitive implemented.
- ProblemJsonRenderer parses + classifies + routes per matrix.
- All four surfaces (toast / banner / inline-pill / blocking-modal) implemented.
- WCAG AA contrast verified by axe-core CI.
- aria-live announcements verified by screen-reader smoke.
- Keyboard navigation matches contract.
- Reduced-motion support.
- Telemetry redaction enforced.
- Toast coalescing + banner persistence + modal focus trap implemented.
- All 10 unit tests + 8 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.
- Phase 1 ships the infrastructure; Phase 4/5/8/10/11/13 features consume errorBus instead of re-rolling error UI.

title: Phase 5: Plan editor + artifact rail (CodeMirror 6 + side-by-side comparative-matrix view)
description:
WHAT
====
Desktop-side plan editor (CodeMirror 6, plain markdown) + artifact rail showing the full plan-job artifact tree (rough-idea.md, candidates/*, comparative-matrix.md, synthesis.md, fresh-eyes-critique.md, refinement-round-NNN.md, unresolved-decisions.md, history.jsonl). Comparative-matrix view renders candidates side-by-side.

LAYOUT (matches design/mockups/v1)
====================================
- Left rail: list of plans for the project (with active flag).
- Center: editor for the active plan or active artifact.
- Right rail: artifact tree (rough-idea, candidates folder, synthesis, etc.) with per-artifact status pills (queued / running / completed / failed).
- Top: stage header with plan title, lock state, version.

COMPARATIVE-MATRIX VIEW
========================
- Side-by-side scrollable columns, one per candidate.
- Synced scrolling option.
- Highlight diff regions (strict diff or semantic diff per AI-quality dimensions).
- Header per column: model, harness, CAAM account, latency, completion status.

HISTORY VIEW (§7.1 'history.jsonl')
====================================
Per-plan timeline of meaningful actions: candidate generated, synthesis run, refinement round complete, plan locked. Every entry is mirrored into the daemon audit log.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §3 — CodeMirror 6 for plan editing.
- §7.1 — full Plan workspace spec.
- design/mockups/v1/* — visual reference.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Editor reads the plan from /v1/projects/{id}/plans/{planId}/artifacts (or local clone if plan.md is committed; subtle, see §7.1 'Locking').
- Edits to a locked plan create a new version per §7.1 'Locking'.
- Comparative matrix needs scroll-sync that survives column re-sorting.
- Markdown preview disables unsafe HTML by default (§5.4).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Plan editor renders plan.md, supports save (with lock-aware behavior).
- Artifact rail renders all per-plan artifacts with status pills.
- Comparative-matrix view shows 3-4 candidates side-by-side.
- History timeline visible.
- Storybook stories.
- E2E test: import a fixture plan → view comparative matrix → save edit.

title: Phase 1 component subtask: ApprovalDialog (modal, scope picker, expiry, decision actor, note)

============================

title: Phase 4: Git watcher + local clone test suite (vps_commit_created/vps_push_completed/origin_updated event correctness, clone-sync, WIP overlay)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 4 test suite covering the daemon-side Git watcher, the desktop local clone, the post-commit auto-push hook, daemon RPCs for VPS WIP reads, and the cockpit top-bar wiring.

UNIT TESTS (daemon)
====================
- Git watcher event emission:
  • commit on VPS working tree → vps_commit_created with {ref, sha, author, msg-hash}.
  • push to origin → vps_push_completed with {refs, shas, success: bool}.
  • origin advances (third-party push detected via 'git fetch origin --dry-run' polling) → origin_updated with {refs, new_shas}.
  • coalescing: 50 commits in 10s emit 50 vps_commit_created events but at most one origin_updated batch per push.
- Post-commit auto-push hook: installed at swarm-launch time; respects per-project disable; audit event on every push attempt; surfaces push failures (network, auth, conflict) as urgent Activity events.
- VPS WIP RPCs: getWorkingTreeStatus, getStagedDiff, getUnstagedDiff, getUnpushedCommits — each handles dirty/clean/detached-HEAD/conflict states.
- Project create/import + .hoopoe initialization + readiness checks (Git repo present, branch known, AGENTS.md present, .hoopoe initialized, tool detection done).

UNIT TESTS (desktop)
======================
- Clone-sync subsystem: subscribes to origin_updated → runs git fetch --all --tags --prune; debounces simultaneous events; logs each fetch with triggering eventId.
- 60s safety-net poll: independent of WS events; configurable; respects metered-network setting.
- File watcher on local clone: detects modifications/untracked/local-commits; raises 'local clone has unsaved changes' yellow banner; 'Discard local changes' runs git reset --hard @{u} && git clean -fd after confirmation.
- Disk hygiene: 2GB soft cap warning; 5GB hard cap blocks fetches; per-project + global cache view.
- WIP overlay: combines local-clone refs with daemon RPC WIP state; shows 'Agent X has 3 unpushed commits, 2 modified files' correctly even when origin is behind.
- 'Open in editor' deep-link: opens any file in user's default editor; works because file is on local disk.
- Local search: ripgrep over local clone is the default; daemon grep RPC is fallback for uncommitted-on-VPS work.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. Import a repo with origin → daemon clones to /data/projects/<id>/, desktop initial-clone fetches origin → both ready.
2. Agent commits on VPS → daemon emits vps_commit_created; desktop shows '1 unpushed commit on VPS' indicator without fetching.
3. Auto-push fires → daemon emits vps_push_completed → origin advances → origin_updated fires → desktop fetches → file content visible in clone within @slo file.open.cached.p95.
4. Full chain: agent commit → auto-push → origin_updated → desktop fetch < 3s end-to-end (Phase 4 acceptance test).
5. Third-party push (e.g. teammate pushes to origin from elsewhere) → daemon polling detects within 60s → origin_updated → desktop fetches.
6. WS reconnect after gap → desktop fetches once and reconciles cursor; no duplicate fetches.
7. Top-bar chrome: project/branch/Git status, tool-health dots, swarm-state count, beads-pulse counts, code-health pill (seed adapter), subscription-usage indicator (caut seed), Activity panel toggle — all populate from canonical sources, no polling.
8. User edits file directly in local clone → file-watcher detects → yellow banner shows → 'Discard local changes' restores after confirm.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
========================
- git fetch fails (network) → graceful degrade, retry on next event.
- Disk full during clone → block clone with clear error, no half-state.
- Force-push to origin → desktop detects history rewrite, prompts user.
- Detached HEAD on VPS → top bar warns; agent operations gated.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every fetch logs the triggering eventId, the SHAs before/after, the wall-clock duration.
- Every emitted Git watcher event includes correlationId tracing back to the originating commit/push.
- WIP-overlay computations log the {origin_sha, vps_head_sha, vps_dirty_files} tuple at trace level.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.1 source-of-truth table, code rows.
- §1.7 — sync-driven mirror principle.
- §7.3 — push policy.
- §7.7 — desktop local clone full spec.
- §16 Phase 4 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 4 acceptance row.
- hp-w8m / hp-d51 / hp-2n1 / hp-ind / hp-zks / hp-4ya / hp-ilt — Phase 4 implementation beads (test consumers).
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on Git watcher + WIP RPCs.
- All 8 e2e scenarios pass.
- Phase 4 acceptance (commit → desktop fetch < 3s) measured + logged.
- @slo file.open.cached.p95 ≤ 150ms on cached blobs holds.

title: Phase 13: Polished empty / loading / error states across all stages
description:
WHAT
====
Production-quality empty / loading / error states for every stage and every key view. v1 ships with 'good enough' placeholders in earlier phases; this phase brings them to release quality.

STATES TO POLISH
==================
- Project picker empty (no projects).
- Plan stage empty (no plans).
- Beads stage empty (no beads).
- Swarm stage empty (no active swarm).
- Hardening stage empty (no hardening session active).
- Activity panel empty.
- Health tab empty.
- Findings tab empty.
- Tool inventory empty / partial.
- Connection error state (tunnel down, daemon unreachable).
- Capability degraded states (tool missing, version unsupported).

DESIGN PRINCIPLES
===================
- Always offer the next action (e.g., 'Create your first plan', 'Connect a VPS', 'Import existing repo').
- Failure messages explain WHAT broke + WHAT to do next.
- Loading states have skeletons, not bare spinners.
- Recovery affordances visible (retry / refresh / open Diagnostics).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.6 'first install is brittle' risk.
- design/mockups/v1.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Every empty/loading/error state has a designed implementation.
- Storybook stories per state.
- E2E tests cover the major recovery flows.

title: Phase 1 component subtask: AgentTile (harness icon, CAAM account, current bead, status, time-on-bead, recent decisions; NO terminal)
description:
WHAT
====
AgentTile component used in Swarm dashboard agent grid. Per §7.3 'No-terminal default UX' — explicitly NO terminal output in the tile. Status from NTM/Agent Mail/br/bv events.

ELEMENTS
==========

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================

title: Phase 12: Review round runner (10 rounds, both execution modes, prompts versioned + audited)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the review round runner per §9.2 + §9.2.1. 10 rounds (0-9) selectable + queueable; both execution modes (direct LLM + delegated agent); each round writes an artifact recording: model/tool used (with source stamped on every finding), prompts, agents involved, findings, fixes, new beads, false positives, test/health deltas, cost/time summary.

ROUNDS (§9.2)
===============
0: UBS first-pass scan (auto-triggered).
1: original-agent self-review.
2: cross-agent review (different harness reviews same bead).
3: fresh-eyes new-session review (brand-new agent context).
4: random code exploration.
5: hotspot-targeted review (often re-running UBS scoped).
6: test/coverage hardening.
7: UI/UX polish if applicable.
8: security/performance/deadlock/mock-code specialized skills (§9.5).
9: final landing checklist.

EXECUTION MODES (§9.2.1)
==========================
- **Direct LLM**: daemon gathers context, calls provider directly. Good for cheap fresh-eyes critique.
- **Delegated agent**: daemon sends marching orders to NTM-managed pane. Required for tool use, live test execution, cross-agent coordination.

Both modes write findings into the same ledger.

PROMPTS (versioned, regression-tested)
========================================
- Per-round prompt templates in packages/schemas/prompts/review-rounds/round-N.yaml.
- Versioned alongside planning + bead-conversion + kickoff prompts.
- Regression tests snapshot rendered prompts.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §9.2 — round catalog.
- §9.2.1 — execution modes.
- §1.4 — every round artifact inspectable.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 10 rounds implemented.
- Both execution modes work.
- Prompts versioned + regression-tested.
- Each round writes artifact + audit.
- Per-round findings appended to ledger with source stamp.

title: Phase 8: Build/test queue infrastructure (per §2.7 + §8.5; rch preferred; dedup; failure fingerprints)
description:
WHAT
====
Build/test queue per §2.1 + §8.5 + §2.7. Always-on infrastructure (NOT a tending job). Dedups equivalent commands when safe; throttles by project; records exact command provenance for every result; identical commands reuse recent results when commit + command + environment + declared inputs match; repeated failure fingerprints surfaced as 'known unchanged failure'; rch preferred when configured; UI shows queue + currently running jobs.

WHY (§2.1, §2.7, §8.5, §14 'Agents compete for builds/tests')
================================================================
Without a single queue, multiple agents kick off concurrent builds → contention → flake → frustration. With one queue: dedupe + cache + observable.

PER-RESULT RECORD (§2.7)
==========================
- project ID, worktree path, branch, commit SHA
- command, normalized argv, environment digest
- tool versions and runner profile
- lockfile/package manifest digest
- started/completed timestamps and duration
- stdout/stderr artifact IDs
- parsed test cases, failures, coverage files, exit code
- failure fingerprint
- cache key and cache-hit/cache-miss reason

CACHING RULES
===============
- Reuse a recent result ONLY when commit + command + environment + declared inputs match.
- Repeated failure fingerprints create 'known failure' records — agents stop burning cycles on unchanged failures.
- Suspected flakes marked separately + can trigger flake-hardening bead.

UI
==
- Queue view in Diagnostics or Swarm dashboard side panel.
- Currently-running jobs list with cancel actions.
- Per-bead test-result rollup (used by Beads drawer 'Tests / health' tab from Phase 7).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.1 — build/test execution canonical.
- §2.7 — process manager.
- §8.5 — build/test contention as a tend-swarm detection input.
- §14 — risks: 'Agents compete for builds/tests'.
- §17 rch reference.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Queue runs on the daemon's job substrate.
- Per-project semaphores throttle.
- Dedup tested.
- Failure fingerprints surfaced.
- rch invoked when configured.
- Queue UI visible.
- Per-bead test-result rollup feeds Beads drawer.

title: Phase 13: Contabo provider plugin (listRegions, listSizes, createInstance, destroyInstance, estimateMonthlyCost)
description:
WHAT
====
First provider plugin per §6.2 'existing VPS first, provider automation second.' Implements the abstract provider-plugin contract defined in hp-14zt (packages/schemas/provider-plugin.yaml).

CONTRACT (hp-14zt — packages/schemas/provider-plugin.yaml)
=============================================================
Implements all 5 typed methods from the abstract contract:
- listRegions(): Region[]
- listSizes(region): Size[]
- createInstance(opts: CreateOpts): Instance
- destroyInstance(instanceId): {ok: bool}
- estimateMonthlyCost(opts): {usd, breakdown: LineItem[]}

CONTABO API DETAILS
=====================
- Auth: API token (per Contabo docs). Stored via macOS Keychain on the desktop side; never in daemon SQLite.
- API base: https://api.contabo.com/v1 (or current; pinned per ACFS release).
- Image: Ubuntu 24.04+ LTS (matches §6.2 sizing recommendation).
- Region default: US datacenters (matches agent-flywheel.com canonical guide); user-selectable.
- Size default: Cloud VPS 50 (64GB / 16 vCPU / 400GB SSD / $56/mo) — the agent-flywheel.com top pick.

CANONICAL SIZING (§6.2)
=========================
Surface in the wizard's 'Provision new VPS' screen. Matches the agent-flywheel.com sizing card:
- Recommended (tier: 'recommended'): 64 GB RAM / 12-16 vCPU / 250 GB+ NVMe SSD / ~$40-56/mo
- Workable (tier: 'workable'): 48 GB RAM / 8 vCPU / 100 GB / ~lower
- Minimum (tier: 'minimum'): 32 GB RAM / 8 vCPU / 50 GB

The Contabo plugin maps Contabo's offerings to these tiers and warns (does not block) at the 32GB-minimum bound.

WHY CONTABO FIRST (§6.2)
===========================
Agent-flywheel.com top pick + best-value 64GB VPS at ~$56/mo. Easy onboarding for users following the canonical guide. US datacenter pricing includes ~$10/mo surcharge which is reflected in the cost estimate breakdown.

INTEGRATION POINTS
====================
- First-run wizard 'Provision new VPS' path (currently hidden in v1; this phase exposes for Contabo only).
- Cost estimate card shown in wizard before provisioning (uses hp-14zt LineItem schema).
- Teardown action in project settings (with explicit confirmation; audited as destructive).
- Existing-VPS path remains the default and unchanged (regression test in hp-jqos).

UNIT TESTS
============
- Contract conformance: Contabo plugin implements all 5 methods per hp-14zt schema.
- Cost estimate matches §13 numbers (Cloud VPS 50 = ~$56/mo).
- API error handling: 401 (bad token), 429 (rate limit), 503 (Contabo down) → structured errors with provider response logged.
- Idempotent create: calling createInstance twice with the same name → second returns existing instance, not duplicate.

E2E TESTS (§7.4 chaos suite + Phase 13 acceptance)
=====================================================
- Mock-Contabo-API e2e: full wizard → 'Provision new VPS' → cost preview → provision → SSH connect attempt → destroy.
- Real Contabo e2e: gated by `HOOPOE_E2E_CONTABO_TOKEN` env var; skipped in normal CI; runs nightly on a separate billing-allowed CI runner.
- Existing-VPS regression: provider-plugin loaded but not invoked → existing-VPS bootstrap path unaffected.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every API call logs: { plugin: 'contabo', method, opts (redacted: no API token), durationMs, statusCode, errorClass }.
- Provisioning failures surface a structured error with provider response + retry-suggested action.
- Cost estimates stamped with the cost-catalog version (§13 source) that produced them.
- Audit log entry for every createInstance / destroyInstance call.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.2 — provider rollout strategy (Contabo first).
- §13 — cost-transparency numbers + canonical sizing.
- hp-14zt — abstract provider plugin contract (packages/schemas/).
- hp-mbty — cost transparency UI (consumes the cost estimate).
- hp-jqos — provider-plugin test suite (validates contract conformance).
- agent-flywheel.com/wizard step 4 — Rent VPS canonical UX.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Contabo plugin implements the full hp-14zt contract.
- Wizard 'Provision new VPS' path enabled for Contabo only.
- Cost estimate matches §13 numbers (~$40-56/mo for Cloud VPS 50).
- Teardown audited + tested.
- Existing-VPS path unaffected (regression test).
- Real-Contabo nightly e2e green.

title: Phase 6: Bead conversion + traceability + polish rounds + bv adapter
description:
GOAL (§7.2, §12 Phase 6)
=========================
Land Stage 02 — BEAD. br adapter; plan-to-beads conversion job; br sync --flush-only orchestration; traceability map (plan-section → bead → branch → commits → files → tests → health → review → landing); polish round jobs; bead quality tracker; bv --robot-* adapter.

WHY (§7.2)
===========
The bead stage prevents the plan-bead gap. It must ensure the final plan becomes dependency-aware, self-contained, testable work units that agents can execute without improvising major architecture.

CONVERSION WORKFLOW (§7.2 'Conversion workflow', every step audited)
=====================================================================
1. verify plan is locked or ask for override
2. verify br is initialized
3. create conversion job
4. start a high-reasoning conversion agent/job
5. use beads-workflow instructions
6. create/update beads through br only
7. run br sync --flush-only
8. snapshot .beads/issues.jsonl
9. build plan-to-bead traceability map
10. run bv robot triage/insights
11. show quality score and recommended polish round

PLAN-TO-BEAD TRACEABILITY (§7.2)
==================================
Every conversion produces traceability.json mapping each bead to plan sections it implements, with coverage status, test obligations, unmapped sections, orphans, oversized beads, duplicate candidates.

IMPLEMENTATION-EVIDENCE TRACEABILITY (§7.2)
=============================================
After swarm launch: plan section → bead → branch/worktree → commits → files touched → tests/builds run → health deltas → review findings → landing queue item / PR / merge commit. Produces implementation-evidence.jsonl.

POLISH ROUNDS (§7.2)
=====================
Round 1: plan coverage
Round 2: dependency correctness
Round 3: granularity and split/merge
Round 4: test obligations and acceptance criteria
Round 5: parallel execution tracks
Round 6: fresh-eyes review of bead graph

QUALITY DIMENSIONS (§7.2)
===========================
Plan coverage, dependency correctness, granularity, ready-set size, testability, duplicate risk, parallelism, context richness.

EXIT (§12 Phase 6)
====================
Locked plan converts to br beads; Kanban + traceability match br/.beads; polish metrics + manual curation round-trip through br.

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1 Phase 6)
================================
Locked plan converts to br beads; Kanban and traceability match br/.beads; polish metrics and manual curation round-trip through br.

title: Phase 11: Health adapters per ecosystem (TS/JS, Python, Rust, Go, generic)
description:
WHAT
====
Health adapters per §7.4.1. Each ecosystem has a specific adapter; generic fallback for unknown languages.

ADAPTERS (§7.4.1)
===================
- **TS/JS** — vitest/jest + lcov + lizard / ts-complexity.
- **Python** — pytest + coverage.py + radon / lizard.
- **Rust** — cargo test + cargo llvm-cov + lizard.
- **Go** — go test -cover + gocyclo / lizard.
- **Generic** — configurable shell + lizard / scc / tokei / cloc.

ALWAYS-ON METRICS (§7.4.1)
============================
- Coverage % (line and branch where supported).
- Cyclomatic complexity (per-file + per-function).
- LOC + effective LOC.
- Churn (last 7 / 30 days).
- Hotspot count.
- Test pass/fail counts + durations.
- Ratio of files lacking any test coverage.
- Complexity-to-coverage delta.

Every metric timestamped + snapshotted.

WORKTREE ISOLATION (§7.4.1)
==============================
Health jobs run in a dedicated Git worktree under ~/.hoopoe/work/<project-id>/health/<run-id>/ at the commit being measured. They do NOT run in the active VPS working tree used by agents. If worktree creation is blocked by Git state, Hoopoe reports the reason + schedules a retry rather than running coverage in-place + risking collisions with active edits.

CAPABILITY IDS (§2.8)
======================
- health.coverage
- health.complexity
- health.churn
- health.test_runs

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.4.1 — full health subsystem.
- §2.7 — health adapter precedence.
- §10.5 — terminal stream attach SLO (logs from runs).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All five adapters wired.
- Worktree isolation tested (concurrent-edit safety).
- Capability registry reports health.* per language.
- Hotspot scoring uses default thresholds (complexity ≥ 20, coverage < 60%); configurable per project.
- Each adapter passes its golden-output fixture tests.

title: Phase 2.5: Persisted log offset tests + chunked log API
description:
WHAT
====
Test suite + endpoint contract for the chunked log API. Stream 100 MB of output, disconnect, reconnect, and fetch the missing range by offset (§16 Phase 2.5 task 5).

REQUIRED ENDPOINT
==================
GET /v1/jobs/{id}/log?offset=<bytes>&maxBytes=<n>
- Returns: the requested byte range from the persisted log file.
- Headers: Content-Range, X-Log-Total-Bytes, X-Log-Final (true if job is finished).

PERSISTED LOG STORAGE
======================
- ~/.hoopoe/logs/{jobId}.log (per §2.7).
- In-memory ring buffer is an acceleration layer; the persisted file is the source of truth.
- Logs retained for 30 days by default (§10.3); configurable per project.

TEST SCENARIOS
==============
1. **100 MB stream + range fetch**
   - Stream 100 MB into a job log.
   - Fetch ranges: [0, 1MB], [50MB, 60MB], [99MB, 100MB].
   - Assert: bytes match the stream's content at those offsets.
2. **Disconnect mid-stream + reconnect with offset**
   - Stream begins; renderer disconnects at byte 30 MB.
   - Reconnect: fetch offset=30 MB.
   - Assert: no missed bytes; total received = 100 MB.
3. **Concurrent fetches**
   - Multiple offsets fetched concurrently.
   - Assert: serialized correctly, no corruption.
4. **Partial / streaming response**
   - For active jobs, responses can stream as the log grows.
   - Assert: client can poll-via-offset to catch up incrementally.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.7 — chunked log API + persistent logs.
- §10.5 — terminal stream attach: latest ring visible under 2s.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- /v1/jobs/{id}/log?offset= implemented + tested.
- 100 MB end-to-end test passes within bounded memory (daemon RSS ≤ 256 MB).
- Disconnect/reconnect by offset proven lossless.
- docs/process-manager.md and docs/reconnect-replay.md updated with examples.

title: Daemon migration runner — schema migrations with backup, rollback, and /v1/compatibility wiring (§10.3, §11)
description:
WHAT
====
A schema-migration runner inside the daemon that handles every persisted table/file with a schema version: SQLite tables for the job registry + tending state + read-model cache + audit log + auth bearer table; JSON config files; event-replay-log compaction artifacts.

Daemon startup runs migrations with backup and rollback. Rollback is automatic on migration failure: restore the most recent backup, refuse to start, log a clear error to journald, leave the user a path to escalate (Diagnostics 'Restart with previous version' action).

WHY
===
§10.3: 'Every persisted table/file has a schema version. Daemon startup runs migrations with backup and rollback. /v1/compatibility reports daemon API version, minimum desktop version, event schema versions, migration state, and unsupported client warnings.'

§11: 'If migration fails, the daemon rolls back to the previous binary and database backup.'

Without an explicit runner, migrations get hand-coded into ad-hoc startup hooks + drift between code paths + are impossible to test as a coherent surface.

MIGRATION SHAPE
================
Every migration is a Go function with:
- id: monotonic integer (e.g., 0001_create_jobs_table).
- description: 1-line human-readable.
- up(ctx, tx): apply the migration.
- down(ctx, tx): roll it back (best-effort; some migrations are non-reversible + must declare so).
- pre_check(ctx): assert the assumed schema state before applying.
- post_check(ctx): assert the new schema state after applying.

Backup strategy:
- Before any migration, copy ~/.hoopoe/db/daemon.db → ~/.hoopoe/db/backups/daemon-<old-version>-<timestamp>.db.
- Backup retention: keep last 5 successful migration backups + every release boundary.
- /v1/compatibility reports current schema version, last successful migration ID, last failed migration (if any), backup count.

ROLLBACK FLOW
==============
1. Migration N fails (assertion / SQL error / OOM).
2. Daemon refuses to start.
3. systemd Restart=on-failure kicks in; daemon retries with rollback flag → restores backup + refuses to bind + writes structured error to journald.
4. User restarts via Diagnostics 'Roll back to previous version' which:
   - downloads previous daemon binary (matching backup version).
   - swaps the binary.
   - restarts service.
   - reports rollback success in audit + Diagnostics.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — /v1/compatibility surface.
- §10.3 — retention/compaction/migrations.
- §11 — daemon distribution + upgrade flow.
- §18.4 release smoke check #4 — daemon upgrade backs up config/db.
- hp-4iz — daemon upgrade flow (consumer of this runner).
- hp-o42 — backup/export/restore.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- internal/migrations Go package with the runner + manifest of all migrations.
- Backup strategy implemented + tested with simulated power loss between backup + apply.
- Failed-migration rollback verified in CI.
- /v1/compatibility wired to migration state.
- docs/operations.md describes the rollback procedure as authoritative.

title: Phase 8 / §2.7: Flake detection + known-failure ledger + flake-hardening bead auto-create
description:
WHAT
====
The cognitive layer above the build/test queue (hp-977 + hp-gkk): turn raw test/build failure fingerprints into actionable signal. Distinguish (a) the same real failure repeating — record once, suppress retries until the input changes — from (b) intermittent flakes that need their own remediation, and (c) genuinely new failures that warrant agent attention. Auto-create a 'flake-hardening' bead for any failure crossing the flake threshold so flakiness becomes work the swarm fixes instead of a frustration agents grind around.

WHY (§2.7 'Repeated failure fingerprints', 'Suspected flakes', §8.5 'known unchanged failure', §14 'Agents compete for builds/tests')
====================================================================================================================================
§2.7 says: 'Repeated failure fingerprints create known failure records so agents stop burning cycles on unchanged failures. Suspected flakes are marked separately and can trigger a flake-hardening bead.' §8.5 ties this to tend-swarm: 'repeated failure fingerprints are surfaced to the tending agent as known unchanged failure instead of prompting another blind retry.' Without this layer:

- Agents re-run the same broken test 50× per session. Wastes subscription quota (hp-ier / caut budget) AND wall time. Same root cause: lack of a memoization key for failures.
- Flakes silently rot — every nth run goes red, agents lose trust in the test suite, eventually disable / stop running it. Breaks §1.4 ('every automation must be inspectable') by making test results uninterpretable.
- The build queue (hp-977) DEDUPES commands but treats failures as opaque. The cognitive distinction (real / flake / new) lives ABOVE the queue.

This bead is the cognitive layer. hp-977 / hp-gkk are the substrate (record failure_fingerprint per result + the cache key); this bead consumes those records to drive (a) the 'known unchanged failure' suppression rail in tend-swarm, (b) the flake detector, (c) auto-bead creation, (d) the UI surfacing of these signals.

ARCHITECTURE
=============

LEDGER STORE (daemon SQLite)
----------------------------
Table: failure_ledger
  id                ULID
  project_id        TEXT
  failure_fingerprint  TEXT  (sha256 of normalized error: stack-frame paths normalized, timestamps stripped, addresses zeroed, tool-version-prefixed)
  cache_key         TEXT     (commit + command + env-digest + declared-inputs digest)
  first_seen_at     TIMESTAMP
  last_seen_at      TIMESTAMP
  occurrences       INT
  bead_ids_seen     JSONB    (which beads experienced this failure)
  test_id           TEXT NULLABLE  (parsed test ID if available — e.g., pytest 'test_foo::test_bar', vitest test path)
  status            ENUM('real_unchanged','flake','new','resolved')
  flake_score       FLOAT    (0.0-1.0, see Flake Score below)
  flake_bead_id     TEXT NULLABLE  (linked to br-?? when auto-created)
  resolved_at       TIMESTAMP NULLABLE  (when status moved to 'resolved')
  resolved_by_commit TEXT NULLABLE

FAILURE NORMALIZER
-------------------
Normalize raw failure output into a fingerprint:
- Strip absolute paths → relative project paths.
- Strip timestamps + durations.
- Strip memory addresses, hex IDs.
- Sort multi-error blocks deterministically.
- Tool-version prefix (so a node v18 vs v20 stack delta produces a different fingerprint).
- Test-runner-aware adapters: vitest, jest, pytest, cargo-test, go-test, generic. Each adapter exposes (raw_log) → (test_id, error_type, normalized_signature).

CLASSIFIER (per-failure-fingerprint state machine)
---------------------------------------------------
On a new failed result with fingerprint F:
  IF F not seen before → status=new; tend-swarm gets a 'new failure' detection.
  IF F seen before AND cache_key matches the prior occurrence → status=real_unchanged; tend-swarm gets a 'known unchanged failure' detection (per §8.5); agent skips reattempt unless explicitly directed.
  IF F seen before AND cache_key differs → check passing-runs-since-last-fail; if pass/fail ratio over a 10-run window crosses flake threshold → status=flake; flake_score updated.
  IF F.status=flake AND a successful run on the same cache_key → flake_score decays (see Flake Score).

FLAKE SCORE
------------
flake_score is a sliding-window (last 20 runs of the same test_id):
  flake_score = (red_count_within_window / window_size) * (1 - consecutive_green_runs/window_size)
Threshold to mark status=flake: flake_score >= 0.20 AND red_count >= 2 AND green_count >= 2 within window. Two reds with one green between them = flake. Three consecutive reds = NOT flake (real_unchanged or new).
flake_score decay: every successful run reduces flake_score by 1/window_size; status flips to 'resolved' when flake_score < 0.05 AND last 5 runs all green.

FLAKE-HARDENING BEAD AUTO-CREATE
---------------------------------
On status transition new→flake, automatically:
  1. br create -t bug -p 1 'Flaky test: {test_id} ({occurrences} occurrences over {window_size} runs)'
  2. Bead description includes: failure fingerprint (truncated for readability + full as artifact), test_id, last 5 raw failure logs (linked to log artifacts), failure timeline (gantt-style of red/green per run), suspected cause hints from the failure normalizer's adapter (e.g., 'race condition pattern detected', 'time-dependent assertion'), suggested remediation (rerun, isolate, instrument, add explicit wait, mock external dep).
  3. failure_ledger row's flake_bead_id ← new bead ID.
  4. Activity panel: 'Flake bead {bead_id} auto-created for test {test_id}' urgent event.
  5. Audit: 'flake_bead_auto_created' event with full evidence chain.
On status transition flake→resolved (sustained green), the flake bead gets a comment 'Flake appears resolved; verifying for 10 more runs' and is marked 'in_review' (NOT closed automatically — human / agent review confirms).

TEND-SWARM INTEGRATION (§8.5)
------------------------------
tend-swarm pre-script reads recent failure_ledger entries for the project's active beads. Detection payload includes:
- known_unchanged_failures: [{test_id, occurrences, last_seen_at}]
- new_failures: [{test_id, fingerprint, first_seen_at}]
- active_flakes: [{test_id, flake_score, flake_bead_id}]
The skill (vibing-with-ntm) decides whether to: send marching orders ('skip retrying X, work on a different bead'), prompt the agent to investigate the new failure, or surface flake bead for agent attention.

DAEMON RPCs
-----------
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/failures           → list recent failures, filterable by status / test_id / bead_id
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/failures/{id}      → full failure record + log artifact links
- POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/failures/{id}/mark-resolved   → mark resolved (audited)
- POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/failures/{id}/escalate-bead   → escalate to flake bead manually
The build queue (hp-977) writes to failure_ledger; this bead OWNS the schema + the classifier + the auto-bead-create rule.

UI
==
- Beads drawer 'Tests / health' tab shows failure history per bead with status badges (real / flake / new / resolved).
- Build queue panel shows status pill on each result.
- Activity panel: flake-bead-auto-created urgent events.
- Diagnostics: failure ledger viewer with sort/filter; manual mark-resolved + escalate-bead buttons.

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Failure normalizer determinism** — feed 10 raw test failure logs (vitest, pytest, cargo-test, go-test, generic) WITH timestamp + path + address noise; assert each produces a stable fingerprint over 100 hashing runs. Log: per-failure raw → normalized diff, fingerprint stability matrix.
2. **Classifier new→real_unchanged** — same fingerprint + same cache_key over 5 ticks; assert status stays 'real_unchanged' on 2nd+ occurrence; tend-swarm receives 'known_unchanged_failures' detection. Log: per-tick classifier state.
3. **Classifier new→flake** — alternating red/green over 10 runs (RGGRRGGRGR); flake_score crosses threshold on 4th red; status=flake; flake bead auto-created with test_id + occurrences + log artifacts. Log: flake_score time series, threshold crossing event, auto-bead-creation chain.
4. **Classifier flake→resolved** — flake bead exists; 10 consecutive greens after flake detection; flake_score decays; status moves to resolved at run 10. Log: per-run flake_score, status transition, flake bead 'verifying' comment.
5. **Cache-key change resets** — F seen with cache_key=A; new run with same F and cache_key=B (e.g., user updated dep); status moves new→ verify NOT real_unchanged. Log: cache_key comparison + status outcome.
6. **Flake bead description content** — auto-created bead description contains test_id, occurrences, last-5-logs links, suggested-remediation hints. Log: bead description rendered + content checks.
7. **Tend-swarm detection payload shape** — assert payload includes known_unchanged_failures + new_failures + active_flakes lists with required fields. Log: full payload + schema validation.
8. **Concurrent-write safety** — 10 goroutines simultaneously upsert into failure_ledger for the same fingerprint; only ONE bead is auto-created (idempotency on flake-bead-auto-create); occurrences count is correct. Log: per-goroutine action + final ledger state.
9. **Mark-resolved flow** — manual /mark-resolved endpoint moves status to resolved; audit entry written; tend-swarm subsequent tick removes from active_flakes. Log: full request + response + audit + post-state.
10. **Adapter test_id extraction** — for each runner (vitest, pytest, cargo-test, go-test), feed a known failure log; assert correct test_id extracted. Log: adapter input / output per case.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Real-failure suppression end-to-end** — fixture: project with 1 broken test; 5 agents try to run it across 10 minutes; failure_ledger marks status=real_unchanged after 1st occurrence; subsequent agents get 'known unchanged failure' from tend-swarm and skip retry; verify build queue dedup hits + zero unnecessary subscription budget burn. Log file: e2e-real-failure-{ts}.jsonl with ALL build-queue events, classifier states, agent decisions, budget delta.
2. **Flake auto-bead end-to-end** — fixture: project with 1 timing-dependent flaky test; simulate 20 runs with 4 reds + 16 greens distributed flakily; verify status=flake at 4th red; verify auto-created bead has correct title + description + log artifact links + linked source-bead; verify Activity panel urgent event; verify audit chain. Log: full timeline of runs, flake_score evolution, bead creation event.
3. **Flake resolved end-to-end** — start with auto-created flake bead from #2; agent fixes the test (commits added explicit await); 10 sustained greens; flake bead transitions to 'in_review'; verify post-state. Log: fix commit, post-fix runs, status transition.
4. **Tend-swarm integration end-to-end** — fixture: project with 1 known_unchanged_failure + 1 new_failure + 1 active_flake; tend-swarm tick fires; verify detection payload includes all three categories; vibing-with-ntm skill is loaded; agent ActionPlan reflects the right action per category (skip / investigate / surface). Log: tend-swarm tick + detections + ActionPlan + post-state.
5. **Diagnostics manual escalate** — failure_ledger has 1 'real_unchanged' that user judges as 'actually a flake'; user clicks 'escalate to flake bead'; verify bead created + status moved to flake + audit. Log: full UI + RPC + audit chain.
6. **Cross-bead dedup** — same fingerprint occurs under 3 different beads; failure_ledger has ONE row with bead_ids_seen=[A,B,C]; flake_bead links to all three; verify cross-bead surfacing in beads drawer 'shared with X other beads'. Log: per-bead occurrences + ledger row.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.7 — failure fingerprint + known failure + flake-hardening bead.
- §8.5 — tend-swarm 'known unchanged failure' surfacing.
- §14 — risk: 'Agents compete for builds/tests'.
- hp-977 (build/test queue, writes raw fingerprints).
- hp-gkk (job registry / process manager substrate, ledger persistence).
- hp-fb0 (tend-swarm, consumes detection payload).
- hp-dz8 (br adapter, used to auto-create flake beads).
- hp-r3i (packages/schemas, failure_ledger row schema + RPC types).
- hp-g73 (audit log).
- hp-1r4 (Activity panel for urgent events).
- hp-6d7 (Diagnostics, manual viewer).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- failure_ledger schema + Go store + indexed queries (by project_id + status + last_seen_at).
- Failure normalizer per runner (vitest, pytest, cargo-test, go-test, generic).
- Classifier state machine implemented + tested (new→real_unchanged / new→flake / flake→resolved).
- Auto-bead create on new→flake transition (idempotent + audited).
- Tend-swarm pre-script reads + emits the three-category detection payload.
- Three RPCs implemented + schema-validated.
- UI: per-bead failure history + build-queue status pill + Diagnostics ledger viewer + Activity urgent events.
- All 10 unit tests + 6 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.
- Phase 8 acceptance test extended to cover the flake auto-bead path.

title: Phase 5: Multi-model planning pipeline (candidates → matrix → synthesis → critique → 4-5 refinement rounds → lock)
description:
WHAT
====
The full planning job graph that runs on the VPS daemon as a sequence of jobs. Pipeline shape per §7.1.

PIPELINE (§7.1)
================
rough_idea (chat-box input)
  ├─ candidate_plan_chatgpt_pro      ← default primary, via Oracle
  ├─ candidate_plan_claude_opus      ← via Claude Code
  ├─ candidate_plan_gemini_pro       ← via Gemini CLI (Deep Think when available)
  └─ candidate_plan_third            ← optional: Grok Heavy / Codex / etc.
        ↓
  comparative_matrix                 ← side-by-side review surface in UI
        ↓
  best_of_all_worlds_synthesis       ← primary model (default ChatGPT Pro) is final arbiter
        ↓
  fresh_eyes_critique                ← brand-new session of the primary model
        ↓
  refinement_round_1                 ┐
  refinement_round_2                 │ each round uses a fresh primary-model
  refinement_round_3                 │ session — agent-flywheel 'fresh
  refinement_round_4                 │ conversation prevents anchoring' pattern
  refinement_round_5 (optional)      ┘
        ↓
  lock_or_continue

JOB DAG IMPLEMENTATION
========================
- Each step is a daemon job (§2.7 job substrate).
- Candidate jobs run IN PARALLEL.
- Synthesis depends on all candidates.
- Critique depends on synthesis.
- Each refinement round depends on the previous round.
- User can stop after any round, run extra rounds, or jump straight to lock.

PROMPTS (versioned product surface, regression-tested)
=========================================================
- Per-candidate prompts (versioned in packages/schemas/planning-prompts.yaml or similar).
- Synthesis prompt — agent-flywheel 'best of all worlds' formula.
- Fresh-eyes critique prompt — explicitly 'you have not seen this plan before'.
- Refinement-round prompt — explicitly 'fresh conversation; do not assume context from prior rounds beyond the linked artifact'.

JOB ARTIFACTS (§7.1 layout)
=============================
.hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/
  rough-idea.md
  meta.json
  candidates/
    <model-slug>.md
  comparative-matrix.md
  synthesis.md
  fresh-eyes-critique.md
  refinement-round-001.md
  ...
  refinement-round-005.md
  unresolved-decisions.md
  history.jsonl

meta.json records (§7.1):
- schema version
- source mode (chat-box / import / extend-existing)
- project commit SHA at planning time
- per-candidate (model, harness, CAAM account, Oracle topology) tuples
- input hash
- round IDs
- artifact paths
- linked bead IDs
- lock state
- cost-and-time summary

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 — full pipeline.
- §2.7 — job substrate.
- audit log — per-step entries.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Each refinement round explicitly uses a FRESH primary-model session (per agent-flywheel 'fresh-eyes prevents anchoring' pattern).
- Round count: 4-5 is typical; user can do more if rounds keep producing meaningful changes.
- Convergence: user stops when round produces no meaningful changes (subjective; visible in quality tracker).
- Cost ledger updated per round.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Pipeline runs end-to-end as a job DAG.
- All artifacts persisted under .hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/.
- meta.json + history.jsonl populated.
- Stop / resume / re-run individual steps work.
- Phase 5 acceptance: one-paragraph idea → 3-4 candidates → matrix → synthesis → critique → 4 refinement rounds → all artifacts persisted, locked.

title: Lint rule: refuse provider SDK imports (openai, anthropic-ai/sdk, google/generative-ai)
description:
WHAT
====
ESLint + go vet rule that fails the build if any provider SDK is imported in apps/daemon/ or apps/desktop/. Implements Appendix C #11 'Do not call provider APIs directly' as an enforced CI gate.

WHY (§5.1, §7.1, §13, Appendix C #11)
========================================
Hoopoe is subscription-only by design. Every model reach goes through Claude Code / Codex CLI / Gemini CLI / Oracle. NO direct provider API calls. NO OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY config field anywhere.

This isolation is what keeps §5.1's secrets surface minimal and makes Hoopoe's 'subscription-required' position structurally enforceable rather than honor-system.

FORBIDDEN IMPORTS (TS)
========================
- openai
- @anthropic-ai/sdk
- @google/generative-ai
- @anthropic-ai/anthropic
- (any provider SDK in this category)

FORBIDDEN IMPORTS (Go)
========================
- github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go
- github.com/openai/openai-go
- github.com/google/generative-ai-go
