- (any provider SDK)

ENFORCEMENT
=============
- ESLint rule (no-restricted-imports / custom rule) for TS.
- Go vet check with custom analyzer for Go.
- CI gate: any matching import fails the build with a clear pointer to §13 + Appendix C #11.
- Exception process: explicit allow-list with REQUIRED architecture review + ADR.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.1 — secrets boundary.
- §13 — subscription requirement.
- Appendix C #11 — full guardrail.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Lint rules committed + active in CI.
- Adding a forbidden import to a test file fails the build with the expected message.
- Allow-list mechanism documented (no entries in v1).
close_reason: Added provider SDK/API-key lint gate wired into root lint with unit coverage

title: Phase 8: Swarm launch + abstracted dashboard test suite (NTM mock, composition picker, kickoff prompt rendering, NO-terminal default UI, recovery actions)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 8 test suite covering swarm launch via NTM, the abstracted dashboard, the composition picker, the kickoff prompt template, recovery actions (CAAM switch / casr resume / kill / reassign / send marching orders), and the build/test queue.

UNIT TESTS
============
- Composition picker: manual ratios per harness (Claude Code / Codex CLI / Gemini CLI); 12-cap default (configurable); CAAM-account selection per harness; account-pressure warning when count exceeds available accounts; 'Let Hoopoe choose' auto-select uses agent-flywheel ratios (400+: 4/4/2; 100-399: 3/3/2; <100: 1/1/1).
- NTM adapter: ntm serve REST/SSE/WS preferred; --robot-snapshot/status/tail fallback; tmux capture-pane last; capability registry reflects which transport is active.
- Swarm launch orchestration: reconcile state → verify launch gates → render warnings (dirty Git, stale reservations, no ready beads, low disk, missing Agent Mail, N unpushed commits) → create swarm spec + audit → call NTM spawn/add → stagger ≥30s between agent starts → send kickoff prompt → start event subscriptions → activate tending jobs.
- Kickoff prompt template (versioned): asserts exact template content matches packaged hash; interpolation variables render correctly ({{projectName}}, {{projectPath}}, {{mode}}, {{planTitle}}, {{beadScope}}); regression-tested.
- Abstracted dashboard:
  • Bead board (Kanban or DAG, switchable) — renders bead state from canonical sources, never from terminal scrollback.
  • Agent grid — one tile per agent: harness, CAAM account, current bead, status (working/idle/awaiting-review/wedged/rate-limited), time-on-bead, recent decisions; NO terminal output.
  • Activity panel (lazy-loaded from §7.5).
- Send marching orders / broadcast / interrupt / stop actions: each is an audited daemon RPC; safety presets (supervised/guided/autopilot) gate per the preset's policy bundle; SLB co-signature when enabled (hp-kuh).
- Recovery actions: CAAM switch_account / casr resume_session / pause / kill+reassign / send marching orders — each via typed RPC, each audited, each surfaces as a button + agent-tile context-menu + tend-swarm action.
- PTY plumbing on daemon side: NTM WebSocket preferred / tmux pipe-pane fallback / tmux capture-pane last; ring-buffered byte-addressable; persisted as logs with offsets.
- Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' debug toggle: opt-in per agent, audited on/off, auto-closes after configurable idle window, read-only (no input).
- Build/test queue: dedup by (commit, command, env-digest, lockfile-digest); rch preferred when configured; failure-fingerprint records 'known unchanged failure'; suspected-flake records flake-hardening bead; per-project throttle.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. Launch 4-agent mixed swarm (2 Claude Code + 1 Codex + 1 Gemini) on fixture project with 50 ready beads → all 4 stagger-start ≥30s apart, register with Agent Mail, claim beads, run rch builds (Phase 8 acceptance).
2. Default UI never shows terminal output: assert no TerminalPane in Swarm route DOM tree; agent-tile rendering uses only NTM/Agent Mail/br events.
3. Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' opt-in: open Diagnostics → toggle → xterm renders pane output → audit event fires; close → cleans up; auto-close after idle window.
4. Rate-limit detection: simulate Claude Max quota signal → CAAM has alternate account → tend-swarm proposes switch_account ActionPlan → executor runs → postcondition verifies CAAM reports new account + agent pane resumes within bounded window.
5. Wedged-pane detection: simulate no-output + no-syscall for threshold → watch-safety-thresholds emits agent.kill_wedged_process intent → executor kills via pt → postcondition verifies NTM no longer reports the process.
6. Build queue dedup: 5 agents request the same test command at same SHA → 1 actual run, 4 cache-hits.
7. Build queue 'known failure': test fails with same fingerprint twice → third request returns cached failure with 'known unchanged failure' annotation; agent gets a clear message instead of running again.
8. Disk pressure: srp signals 95% full → watch-safety-thresholds runs sbh cleanup → stale artifacts removed → disk drops below threshold → swarm continues.
9. Push policy: every commit auto-pushes within 5min; failure surfaces as urgent Activity event.
10. Kickoff prompt regression: exact bytes of the template match commit hash; any code change to it requires updating the test fixture deliberately.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
========================
- NTM serve unreachable → adapter falls back to --robot-snapshot polling; capability degraded.
- 12-agent swarm with all 12 hitting rate-limit simultaneously → tending serializes recovery without storm.
- Agent claims a bead then crashes → stalled-bead detection fires within threshold + force-release stale reservation with audit.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every recovery action logs source/target accounts, the limit signal that triggered it, and the postcondition outcome.
- Every kickoff prompt log includes the project name + bead scope + interpolated mode (no other content).
- PTY ring buffers are NOT in evidence files by default; only the Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' tests retain them, and they are bytes-redacted before storage.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 — Swarm stage strategic intent.
- §16 Phase 8 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 8 acceptance row.
- Appendix C #12 — no terminal in default UI.
- hp-vq9 / hp-g1j / hp-pux / hp-cj0 / hp-b9l / hp-byj / hp-yf9 / hp-ier / hp-ay4 / hp-977 — Phase 8 implementation beads.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on launch orchestrator + composition picker + recovery actions + build queue.
- All 10 e2e scenarios pass against fixture corpus + a real research-spike VPS with NTM running.
- Default-UI no-terminal invariant verified (DOM assertion + lint rule on Swarm route imports).
- @slo terminal.attach.first-byte.p95 ≤ 2s holds for the Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' surface.
- Kickoff prompt regression test in CI.

title: Phase 2: Phase 2 acceptance smoke (cold VPS → daemon → bearer → WS → disconnect/reconnect replay)
description:
WHAT
====
End-to-end smoke test that proves the §18.1 Phase 2 acceptance: 'Fresh VPS → daemon installed → pairing consumed → bearer stored → WS connected → simulated disconnect/reconnect replays without state corruption.'

WHY
===
The Phase 2 deliverables are individually testable, but the integration is the actual proof. Without the smoke, Phase 3+ adapters can't trust the foundation.

REQUIRED TESTS
==============
1. Full bootstrap on a real research-spike VPS:
   - From a clean Hoopoe install, click 'Connect to existing VPS', enter host + key.
   - Hoopoe runs bootstrap.sh, daemon installs, pairing captured, bearer issued.
   - VPS lifecycle reaches `daemon_running` then `tools_installed` then `ready`.
2. Stream a remote job log:
   - From the daemon, kick off a long-running 'tool_inventory' job.
   - Desktop subscribes to /v1/jobs/{id}/log; sees streaming output.
3. Disconnect/reconnect replay:
   - Mid-stream, kill the SSH tunnel.
   - Tunnel re-establishes; desktop reconnects with sequence cursors.
   - Replay closes the gap; no UI corruption (events arrive in order, idempotent on duplicates).
4. Mac sleep simulation:
   - Trigger powerMonitor 'suspend'/'resume' programmatically.
   - Tunnel + WS reconnect within p95 < 10s after network returns (§10.5).
5. Daemon restart:
   - systemctl restart hoopoe.service.
   - Job registry survives (Phase 2.5 hardening); jobs picked up where they left off OR explicitly marked as interrupted.
   - Bearer still valid; WS reconnects with new daemon instance.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §10.5 SLO targets.
- §18.1 Phase 2 acceptance.
- §18.4 — release smoke checks.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 5 tests above pass on at least one CI run against a real research-spike VPS.
- Mock Flywheel mode replays equivalent scenarios for daily CI.
- Test logs + output committed to docs/test-evidence/phase2-smoke/.

title: Phase 8: Agent Mail adapter (MCP/HTTP, threads, reservations, force-release)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side Agent Mail adapter per §2.3 AgentMailAdapter precedence:
1. Agent Mail MCP/API surfaces
2. NTM mail/locks surfaces
3. read-only DB/file fallback if documented

WRAPPED SURFACES
=================
- Messages: list, get, send, urgent flag.
- Threads: list, get details (thread_id keyed by bead-ID for swarm coordination).
- Reservations: list, request, release, force-release (audited).
- Inbox: subscribe, mark-read.

CAPABILITY IDS (§2.8)
======================
- agent_mail.messages.read / send / urgent
- agent_mail.threads.read
- agent_mail.reservations.list / release / force_release
- agent_mail.inbox.subscribe

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.3 — adapter precedence.
- §17 — github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail (Python 3.12+, fastmcp + FastAPI). MCP/HTTP regardless of daemon language.
- §7.5 — Activity panel timeline ingests Agent Mail.
- §8.4 — orchestrator-chat consumes mail.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- File reservations are ADVISORY, not hard locks (§7.5). Hoopoe surfaces stale reservations + conflict warnings without pretending the GUI prevents every file edit.
- Force-release is destructive; goes through approvals queue with a reason note + Agent Mail notice posted (§8.3.1 reservation.force_release postcondition).
- Bead-ID-keyed threading: thread_id MUST follow the convention `br-<beadId>` so messages aggregate per bead.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All surfaces wrapped + tested.
- Capability registry reports agent_mail.* correctly.
- Thread-id convention enforced.
- Force-release writes audit + Agent Mail notice.
- Adapter contract tests pass.

title: Phase 8: Abstracted Swarm dashboard (bead board + agent grid + Activity panel; NO terminal in default UI)
description:
WHAT
====
Stage 03 dashboard per §7.3 'No-terminal default UX'. Three abstracted surfaces only — bead board + agent grid + Activity panel. NO terminal output in default UI.

PRIMARY SURFACES (§7.3)
=========================
1. **Bead board** — every bead with current state (ready / claimed / in_progress / in_review / closed / blocked), owner agent, files touched, time on bead, links to per-bead Activity thread. Primary visualization. Kanban or DAG view, switchable. Reuses Phase 7 components.
2. **Agent grid** — one tile per agent: harness icon (Claude / Codex / Gemini), CAAM account, current bead claim, status (working / idle / awaiting-review / wedged / rate-limited), time-on-current-bead, recent decisions ('opened bead B-142', 'ran 3 tests, all passed', 'pushed 2 commits'). NO terminal output. Status from NTM/Agent Mail/br/bv events, not scrollback parsing.
3. **Activity panel (§7.5)** — agent-to-agent mail, file reservations, build/test results, urgent alerts. User can converse with orchestrator agent here.

WHY NO TERMINALS (§7.3)
=========================
Terminal scrollback is observability noise the user shouldn't have to decode. Bead state + agent state + mail + build results — read from canonical sources — answer 'what's happening?' precisely; raw git status output and Codex thinking-token streams do not.

PER-BEAD LIFECYCLE EVENT MAPPING (§7.3 per-bead lifecycle)
============================================================
Each agent runs a tight loop, not a long-lived 'implement everything' session:
1. read AGENTS.md + check Agent Mail inbox
2. ask br ready --json + bv --robot-triage for next ready bead
3. claim the bead (br update --status in_progress) + reserve files
4. implement: edit, test, push commits to origin promptly
5. self-review with fresh eyes
6. run tests via build queue
7. close the bead (br close)
8. release file reservations
9. → loop back to step 2

Lifecycle events ingested into the bead board: claim → in_progress → tests → close → next-bead.

CONTEXT-MENU ACTIONS PER AGENT TILE
=====================================
- Switch account (CAAM)
- Resume session (casr; Phase 10+)
- Pause and notify
- Kill and reassign (audited)
- Send marching orders

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 — full dashboard.
- §7.5 — Activity panel.
- design/mockups/v1 — visual reference.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Bead board + agent grid + Activity panel all rendered.
- NO TerminalPane component in default UI.
- Per-bead lifecycle events drive bead state transitions in real time.
- Agent tile context menu wired with all five actions.
- Phase 8 acceptance: launch a 4-agent swarm; user follows from these three surfaces; never sees terminal in default UI.

title: Phase 8: Send marching orders / broadcast / interrupt / stop actions + safety presets (supervised/guided/autopilot)
description:
WHAT
====
User-initiated swarm actions per §7.3:
- send-marching-orders to one agent
- broadcast to all agents
- interrupt one agent (gentle)
- stop one agent (TERM → KILL escalation)
- halt swarm (all agents)

Safety presets per §7.3 — approval-policy bundles, NOT different code paths:
- supervised: approval for writes/destructive
- guided: approval for destructive/unrecognized — DEFAULT
- autopilot: typed allowed actions proceed; destructive/unrecognized still gated

Optional SLB add-on: any preset can require SLB two-person co-signature for destructive class.

ENDPOINTS (§2.6)
==================
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/swarms/{swarmId}/broadcast {text} — same kickoff-rule reminder header in front, then user message.
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/agents/{agentId}/send {text}
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/agents/{agentId}/interrupt
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/agents/{agentId}/stop
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/swarms/{swarmId}/halt — DESTRUCTIVE, requires approval

SAFETY PRESET BEHAVIOR
========================
- Preset selected at swarm launch; configurable mid-swarm with audit.
- Preset = a list of action-class → approval-required bool mappings.
- approvals queue (cross-cutting epic) is the unified surface.
- The user can inspect exactly which actions each preset gates BEFORE launch.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 — actions + safety presets.
- §5.3 — destructive operations require approval.
- approvals queue epic.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All five action endpoints implemented + audited.
- Three safety presets implemented; preset preview UI shows which actions are gated.
- Halt-swarm goes through approvals queue (destructive class).
- SLB integration optional (Phase 13 wires fully); presence does not block when not configured.
- Phase 8 acceptance: user can send a message to one agent; broadcast to all; halt swarm with approval gate.

title: Per-§14 risk acceptance evidence — one bead per named risk with mitigation owner + verification fixture
description:
WHAT
====
§14 names 15+ specific risks, each with a mitigation strategy in the plan. Currently hp-n5k lumps all of them as 'risk mitigation tasks' — that level of granularity is not actionable.

This bead is the parent for per-risk acceptance evidence: one fixture or test scenario per named risk that proves the mitigation is working. Each risk gets:
1. Fixture / test scenario in chaos suite (hp-2qn) or per-phase test bead.
2. Acceptance criteria: 'risk has been verified mitigated by these named tests.'
3. Owner: the phase epic that owns the implementation.

NAMED RISKS (§14, with verification fixture)
==============================================
1. PTY streaming fidelity fails — chaos: NTM disconnect → tend-swarm degrades; Show raw pane fallback works.
2. Tool output drift breaks adapters — adapter contract tests (hp-8a8); upstream output change → CI fail with diff.
3. Hoopoe cache diverges from canonical state — chaos: corrupt read-model cache → 'reload from tools' restores; reconciliation logged.
4. First install is brittle — Phase 0 manual wizard run + Phase 3 ACFS bootstrap test bead (hp-6us1).
5. Subscription rate-limits exhaust mid-swarm — fixture: rate-limited agent with CAAM available + without (Phase 8 + tending eval harness).
6. Agents compete for builds/tests — fixture: 5 agents request same build → 1 actual run + 4 cache hits (Phase 8 test).
7. Stale agents hold beads/reservations hostage — fixture: stalled-bead detection + force-release with audit (tending eval harness).
8. Unsafe commands accidentally exposed — chaos: renderer attempts privileged op → preload rejects + logs (renderer hardening fixtures).
9. Planning quality is weak — Phase 5 acceptance: 4 candidates → matrix → synthesis → critique → 4 refinement rounds → lock.
10. Users trust subjective scores too much — UI assertion: every quality score has linked underlying evidence + override button.
11. Laptop sleep breaks perception of reliability — Phase 2 acceptance: simulated sleep → reconnect → no state corruption + @slo desktop.reconnect.p95.
12. Lifted code carries Codex-shaped assumptions — Phase 1 lint rule: refuses thread/provider/chat identifiers in non-vendored code; manual scrub checklist in docs/source-provenance.md.
13. Upstream t3code drift — quarterly review process documented in docs/upstream-drift.md; CHANGELOG monitor.
14. PubSub.unbounded patterns leak — chaos: load test with wedged consumer → daemon RSS bounded.
15. (additional risks added here as identified)

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §14 — full risks list.
- hp-n5k — original risk-mitigation bead (this bead is the explicit decomposition).
- hp-2qn — chaos suite.
- hp-8a8 — adapter contract tests.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- For each named §14 risk: a fixture exists, a test asserts the mitigation, an owning phase is identified.
- docs/risks.md cross-references each risk to its acceptance fixture.
- A regression that breaks any named risk's mitigation fails CI loudly with a clear pointer to the risk + fixture.

title: Phase 8: Kickoff prompt template (versioned product surface, regression-tested)
description:
WHAT
====
The §7.3 kickoff prompt template, versioned alongside the planning prompts and bead-conversion prompt. Treated as a real product (per §1.4 'every meaningful automation must be inspectable'): versioned, regression-tested, rollback.

TEMPLATE (§7.3 'Kickoff prompt template')
============================================
Reread AGENTS.md and README.md completely so the operating contract is fresh.

You are part of a Hoopoe-managed Agentic Coding Flywheel swarm.
Project: {{projectName}}
Project path: {{projectPath}}
Current mode: {{mode}}
Plan: {{planTitle}}
Bead scope: {{beadScope}}

Rules:
- Coordinate through Agent Mail.
- Register/check your inbox before starting work.
- Use bv --robot-triage and br ready --json before selecting work.
- Do not run bare bv.
- Claim exactly one bead before editing, then mark it in_progress with br.
- Use bead IDs in Agent Mail thread IDs, subjects, file reservation reasons, and commit messages.
- Reserve files through Agent Mail before editing.
- Avoid duplicate work and avoid stepping on active reservations.
- Use rch for builds/tests when available and respect the shared build queue.
- Push every commit to origin promptly — your bead's branch should never sit unpushed on the VPS for more than a few minutes. Origin is the canonical source of truth; unpushed work is invisible to the user, CI, and other tools.
- Report blockers quickly.
- Do not get stuck in communication purgatory; if unblocked, choose useful ready work.
- When finished, run tests appropriate to the bead, self-review with fresh eyes, then move to review or close according to project policy.

VERSIONING + REGRESSION TESTS
===============================
- Stored in packages/schemas/prompts/swarm-kickoff.yaml (or similar) with semver.
- A regression test renders the template against fixtures and snapshots the output.
- Changes audited; rollback supported.
- Per-agent variant (Claude / Codex / Gemini) hooks for harness-specific phrasing.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 — full template.
- §1.4 — inspectability.
- audit log — every swarm dispatch records the rendered prompt.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Template stored + versioned.
- Renders correctly with all variables.
- Regression test catches accidental changes.
- Per-agent harness variants supported.
- Activity panel surfaces the rendered prompt for forensics.

title: Phase 12: Review rounds + finding tracker + convergence detector test suite (round runners, finding lifecycle, convergence dashboard, specialized audit catalog)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 12 test suite covering hardening-mode swarm launch, the 10-round review pipeline, the finding tracker, the convergence detector, and the specialized-audit catalog.

UNIT TESTS
============
- Review-round runner: 10 rounds (R0 UBS first-pass, R1 self-review, R2 cross-agent, R3 fresh-eyes, R4 random-exploration, R5 hotspot-targeted, R6 test/coverage, R7 UI/UX, R8 specialized audits, R9 final-landing); each round produces an artifact recording model/tool used, prompts, agents, findings, fixes, new beads, false positives, test/health deltas, cost/time.
- Two execution modes: direct LLM review (daemon → provider) vs delegated agent review (NTM-managed agent pane); mode is per-round implementation detail; both write to same finding ledger.
- UBS adapter: scan invocation, finding parsing, source-tool stamping (source: ubs), scoping (full vs changed-files vs hotspots).
- Finding lifecycle: new → triaged → fix_now / new_bead / false_positive / needs_human → closed; each transition audited; finding-to-bead conversion creates a real br bead with link back.
- Convergence detector: tracks findings/round, severe-findings/round, duplicate findings, fixes/round, new beads/round, test failures fixed, coverage delta, complexity delta, cost/time per useful finding; states (not_started → high_yield → medium_yield → low_yield → saturated → final_gate_ready).
- Specialized audit catalog: UBS re-run with stricter rules / scoped to changes; mock-code finder; deadlock/concurrency; security audit for SaaS; performance profiling; project reality check; reasoning-mode analysis; golden-artifact testing; fuzzing; e2e testing with logging/no-mocks; UI polish review. Each audit creates beads (not free-floating todos) and stamps findings with source skill ID.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. Round 0 UBS first-pass: swarm finishes implementation → transition to Hardening → R0 runs automatically → findings populate ledger → user sees convergence start at high_yield (Phase 12 acceptance).
2. Finding lifecycle: a finding moves through new → triaged → new_bead → closed; each transition produces an audit entry; the new_bead conversion produces a real br bead linked back to the finding.
3. Convergence dashboard: round-over-round finding counts visible as trend; saturated state triggers ship-recommendation; user can override and run another round.
4. Specialized audit (security): user opts into security audit → skill-attached job runs → findings stamped with source: skill:security-audit → cross-tool dedup against UBS findings via deduper.
5. Final landing checklist: all gates checked (tests/builds pass or exceptions documented; code health gates pass or follow-up beads exist; Git/beads synced); ship-ready gate fires.
6. Direct vs delegated mode parity: same fixture round runs in both modes → findings ledger differs only in the source-mode field; both pass schema validation.
7. UBS finding source-stamping: every UBS finding carries source: ubs; cross-tool dedup handles ubs vs agent vs skill correctly.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
========================
- Review-round runner crashes mid-round → next start resumes from last persisted finding; partial findings preserved.
- UBS scan times out → capability degrades; round continues with subset; finding ledger records the partial scan.
- Finding-to-bead conversion fails (br unavailable) → finding stays in new_bead-pending state; retry on br recovery.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Each round logs: model/tool, prompt hash, agent IDs, total findings emitted, deduped count, by-source breakdown.
- Each finding transition logs the actor + reason.
- Convergence states logged as state-change events.
- Specialized-audit invocations log the skill ID + version.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.4.2 — review rounds strategic intent.
- §7.4.3 — finding tracker.
- §9 — full hardening detail.
- §16 Phase 12 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 12 acceptance row.
- hp-rmv / hp-8xm / hp-k4j / hp-roq / hp-hr4 — Phase 12 implementation beads.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on round runner + finding ledger + convergence detector.
- All 7 e2e scenarios pass.
- Specialized-audit catalog wired and at least 3 audits (mock-code, deadlock, security) exercised in e2e tests.
- Cross-tool dedup verified (ubs vs agent vs skill source IDs).

title: Phase 4: Post-commit auto-push hook installed at swarm-launch (§7.3 push policy)
description:
WHAT
====
Install a post-commit hook in each project's working tree at swarm-launch time that pushes the bead's branch to origin after every successful test run (or after every commit if test policy is 'commit-fast'). Audit on every push attempt; surface push failures in Activity panel as urgent items.

WHY (§7.3 'Push policy — canonical-state freshness')
======================================================
Origin is the canonical source of truth (§1.1). The VPS working tree is just where commits are CREATED before they become canonical via push. Any commit that lives only on the VPS for more than a few minutes is a durability risk and a visibility gap — the desktop's local clone, CI, code review, and the human user can only see what's reached origin.

INSTALL TIMING
===============
- Installed at SWARM-LAUNCH (not at project import) so users not running swarms aren't surprised by silent auto-push.
- Removed when swarm halts.
- Configurable per swarm spec (the user can override to 'manual push' at swarm-launch).

PUSH POLICY (DEFAULT)
======================
- Agents push their bead's branch after every commit (or after every successful test run, configurable per project).
- Daemon enforces via post-commit hook + audit on every attempt.
- Push failures (network, auth, conflict) surface immediately in Activity panel as urgent items.

HOOK CONTENT
=============
Post-commit hook calls Hoopoe daemon RPC: POST /v1/projects/{id}/git/push (idempotent via Idempotency-Key). The daemon executes the actual push, captures stdout/stderr, writes audit, emits vps_push_completed event.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.1 — origin = canonical.
- §7.3 push policy.
- §5.3 — destructive Git ops require approval (force-push); regular push does not.
- audit log epic — every push audited.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Existing user-installed hooks must be preserved; Hoopoe's hook should chain or be adjacent.
- Push failures retry with backoff but eventually surface to user; never silently retry forever.
- Conflict resolution is a human decision; Hoopoe just surfaces it (§14 risks).
- The hook is project-local (not git config --global) so it doesn't pollute the user's other repos.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Hook installed/uninstalled cleanly with swarm spec lifecycle.
- Push attempts audited: actor, target ref, source ref, result, duration.
- Push failures surface as Activity panel urgent items.
- Test: simulated push failure (no auth) → urgent item appears with retry action.
- Existing hooks preserved (chain or adjacent).

title: Phase 0: Document gotchas and version skew before Phase 2.5+ adapters
description:
WHAT
====
A docs/research-spike/gotchas.md file capturing every surprise from the live research-spike VPS — TUI invocations to NEVER repeat from automation, undocumented flags, version skew between docs and shipping CLIs, and shapes that subtly differ from what plan.md assumes.

WHY (§16 Phase 0 task 4)
=========================
Adapter authors in Phase 2.5+ will hit these problems anyway; the question is whether they hit them once (in Phase 0, fixed) or forty times (per developer per adapter). Writing them down once is the cheapest possible insurance.

CATEGORIES TO DOCUMENT
======================
- 'Bare-TUI' invocations — bv with no flags launches an interactive TUI per §1.3 / Appendix C #1; document any others discovered.
- Undocumented flags or behaviors observed (jq sub-flags, --json output shape differences across versions).
- Version skew — running CLI version vs documented schema; capture in capability registry (§2.8).
- Encoding/locale issues — some CLIs change output by LANG/LC_ALL.
- Output streams — what goes to stdout vs stderr; some tools emit JSON to stderr (treat as untrusted until verified).
- Network surfaces — NTM serve port discovery, defaults, what binds publicly by default.
- CAAM authentication — which CLIs are signed in vs not; how Hoopoe should detect this.
- Oracle on macOS — startup behavior, Chrome profile location, cookie persistence quirks.
- ru — unit-of-work model and state-store path under ~/.local/state/ru/** (so the §17 'narrow adoption' boundary is respected).
- jsm vs jfp — what changes when subscription is configured vs not; how the lock-file design (§10.3) needs to handle each.

ACCEPTANCE
==========
- gotchas.md is current, has cross-references to integration-contract files, and has at least one entry per tool surveyed.
- Each gotcha includes: 'symptom', 'root cause', 'mitigation in Hoopoe', 'fixture-coverage in packages/fixtures/'.
- A new contributor reading gotchas.md before coding an adapter does NOT hit the same problem.
close_reason: Two complementary gotchas docs: docs/integration-contracts/gotchas-and-version-skew.md (integration-contracts-angle, 6 categories per user spec) + docs/research-spike/gotchas.md (workflow + setup catalog, broader). Cross-linked. Each entry: symptom/root-cause/mitigation/fixture/references.

title: Phase 1: Monorepo + desktop shell + lifted t3code scaffolding
description:
GOAL (§12 Phase 1, §16 Phase 1)
================================
Initialize the Hoopoe monorepo, vendor the load-bearing pieces of github.com/pingdotgg/t3code (MIT) — desktop lifecycle, build pipeline, settings, keybindings — and decompose t3code's 2,175-line apps/desktop/src/main.ts on day one into discrete modules. Build the greenfield design system and the four-stage routed shell with the Activity panel drawer. Smoke test against Mock Flywheel Mode.

WHY VENDOR T3CODE (§3 desktop layer source provenance)
========================================================
T3 Code's apps/desktop/ is ~plain TypeScript with only 3 Effect-framework references (vs 5,283 in their server) — cleanly portable. The desktop lifecycle (auth, settings, keybindings, build pipeline) is a solved problem that would take 3-6 months to redo and would carry the same bug surface t3code already shook out. We lift the patterns, not the framework.

Effect framework is NOT adopted. Their server is Effect-everywhere; we adopt their PATTERNS (PubSub change streams, atomic file writes, file-watch debounce, sequence cursors, semaphore-guarded ops) in plain TypeScript at lower cognitive cost.

DECOMPOSE THE MONOLITH ON DAY ONE
==================================
T3code's apps/desktop/src/main.ts is 2,175 lines and is the single biggest anti-pattern (Appendix B 'Anti-patterns to refuse' #4). Hoopoe decomposes on the FIRST commit of the lift into:
- BackendLifecycle — daemon spawn/health/restart/shutdown
- UpdateMachine — auto-update state machine (lifted whole from t3code/updateMachine.ts)
- IpcRegistry — typed IPC handlers, central registry (Appendix B anti-pattern #6: t3code's implicit string-switch dispatch is rejected)
- WindowManager — window lifecycle, multi-window support, reveal/restore
- SettingsBridge — three-store settings hot-reload between renderer and daemon
- AuthBridge — pairing → bearer → WS-token capture and persistence

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §3 tech stack — Electron + TS + React + Vite + Tailwind; TanStack Router, TanStack Query, Zustand; CodeMirror 6, xterm.js (Diagnostics-only), React Flow.
- §11 packaging — Mac-only v1; signed/notarized DMG (arm64 + x64); electron-updater against GitHub Releases; mock-update-server for local testing.
- Appendix B — full file inventory of vendored t3code files, target locations, adaptations.
- Appendix B 'Anti-patterns to refuse' — PubSub.unbounded, terminal history blob, silent client-side message caps, monolith main.ts, no port-conflict resolution, implicit command dispatch, unknown when-clause keys silently false.
- design/mockups/v1/ — pre-Phase-1 visual sketches for the four-stage shell.

EXIT (§12)
==========
- Visual review against design/mockups/v1/ passes.
- App can navigate all four stages.
- bun run dist:desktop:dmg:arm64 produces a signed/notarized DMG.
- Auto-update flow works against scripts/mock-update-server.ts.
- Settings hot-reload demonstrated.
- ⌘K palette opens.
- Mock Flywheel Mode replays at least one fixture project (so stage UI + Activity panel are testable in CI without a VPS).

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1 Phase 1)
================================
Signed/notarized DMG opens, navigates all four stages, hot-reloads settings, opens ⌘K, and runs a desktop unit test suite.

WEEK 1 vs WEEK 2 split (§16)
==============================
Week 1: clone + pin t3code, init monorepo, vendor build pipeline + desktop lifecycle files, decompose main.ts, design tokens, four-stage routed shell, reusable component set.
Week 2: vendor settings system, vendor keybindings + add command registry, wire ⌘K, smoke test (boot, navigate, hot-reload, palette).
close_reason: Phase 1 epic — all subtasks closed

title: Daemon bind safety + Diagnostics public-bind warning (§5.4 lines 6) — public bind requires config flag + runtime confirmation
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon must refuse to bind to a public/non-loopback interface unless BOTH of these conditions are met:
1. Config flag explicitly enables public binding (e.g., daemon-settings.json public_bind: true).
2. Runtime confirmation: at startup, the daemon checks for a confirmation token from a trusted source (Diagnostics-issued single-use token signed by the signing secret, valid for the bind operation only).

If either is missing, the daemon binds 127.0.0.1 only and emits a Diagnostics warning if the user requested public bind.

DIAGNOSTICS WARNING (red banner)
==================================
Whenever the daemon is bound to anything other than 127.0.0.1 or a private tailnet interface, Diagnostics shows a red banner:
'Daemon is bound to <interface>:<port>. Public exposure is high-risk. Verify mTLS is configured, firewall rules restrict access, and that this is intentional.'

The banner is dismissable per-bind-event (Diagnostics records dismissal) but reappears on next daemon start.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.4 — default network posture (127.0.0.1 + SSH tunnel).
- §2.5 — transport ladder (mTLS public mode is post-MVP advanced option).
- §5.4 line 6 — public-bind safety.
- hp-7r4 — TLS/mTLS direct mode (post-MVP; this bead is the gate that protects users from accidentally enabling it).
- hp-rkx — parent renderer hardening epic.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Daemon refuses public bind without config flag + runtime confirmation.
- Diagnostics red banner shown on any non-127.0.0.1/non-tailnet bind.
- Test: simulated config-only bind attempt → daemon refuses + logs structured warning.
- Test: simulated config + runtime confirmation → daemon binds publicly + warning shown.
- Documented in docs/security.md as the canonical guidance for advanced/team modes.
close_reason: Implemented daemon bind safety and Diagnostics bind warning

title: tending-actions.yaml schema (typed action surface for ActionPlan)
description:
WHAT
====
The packages/schemas/tending-actions.yaml file that defines every allowed ActionPlan action type per §8.3.1. Any action type NOT in this schema is rejected at validation; agents cannot escape the typed surface by inventing one.

INITIAL ACTION TYPES (§8.3.1)
================================
- agent.ask_status — read-only; agent posts status into mail thread.
- agent.send_marching_orders — sends a corrective message to one agent.
- agent.pause — pause work assignment without killing process.
- agent.kill_wedged_process — destructive; uses pt; postcondition: NTM/tmux no longer reports the process.
- reservation.force_release — destructive; postcondition: Mail no longer reports the reservation AND release notice posted in bead's thread.
- caam.switch_account — postcondition: CAAM reports target account AND agent's pane resumes within bounded window.
- casr.resume_session — post-MVP; postcondition: target CLI now driving session AND context preserved.
- git.push_branch — postcondition: origin's target ref contains expected commit SHA.
- swarm.halt — destructive; halts all agents in swarm.
- review.propose_flip — non-destructive; posts proposal for user to confirm.
- bead.create_blocker — postcondition: br contains new bead AND links to source bead/finding.

PER-ACTION SCHEMA
===================
{
  type: string (matches actionType enum),
  target: object (agent/bead/branch/file/reservation/swarm),
  idempotencyKey: string (required),
  preconditions: string[] (validated against canonical state before execution),
  postconditions: string[] (validated against canonical state after execution; failure emits follow-up detection),
  riskClass: low | medium | high | destructive,
  requiresApproval: bool (computed from policy + riskClass)
}

EXTENSION RULE
================
Adding a new action type to this schema REQUIRES a fixture in §8.8 evaluation harness exercising its happy path + at least one postcondition-failure path. The test runner enforces this gate.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §8.3.1 — ActionPlan contract.
- §8.8 — fixture coverage gate.
- packages/schemas/ epic.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- tending-actions.yaml committed with all 11 initial action types.
- Schema versioned.
- Validator generated from schema (Go + TS).
- Fixture-coverage gate enforced in CI.

title: Cross-cutting: Mock Flywheel Mode (fixture-backed daemon for dev/CI)
description:
WHAT
====
A fixture-backed daemon (or adapter layer) that replays snapshots, event streams, logs, Agent Mail, reservations, bead changes, health snapshots, build queue state, Git state, pane logs, and tool-degraded states — without requiring a real VPS. NOT a user-facing substitute for the real Flywheel; explicitly required for deterministic UI development, release smoke tests, support reproduction bundles, and the Phase 1 'reviewer/contributor/CI' demo path.

WHY THIS IS REQUIRED FOR v1 (§13 'Must include for development and demos')
============================================================================
- UI work in Phase 1 must not block on real-VPS provisioning.
- CI tests for stages 1–4 need replayable inputs.
- Tending evaluation harness (§8.8) requires fixtures: healthy hour, idle but not stuck, genuinely wedged pane, rate-limited agent (with/without CAAM), stale reservation, commit burst, budget breach, skill drift, missing tool, postcondition failure, action arbitration.
- Adapter contract tests (§18.3) need golden fixtures for every adapter: normal output, missing tool, unsupported version, malformed JSON, timeout, high-volume output.
- Reviewer/contributor onboarding shouldn't require a VPS subscription.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.1 'Local-demo path' — wizard offers a path bypassing real VPS for reviewers/CI.
- §8.8 — tending evaluation harness fixture list (the one we MUST satisfy).
- §13 'walking skeleton order' (also references mock-flywheel work).
- §18.3 — adapter contract tests.

WHAT GOES IN
=============
1. Fixture corpus under packages/fixtures/ (or similar):
   - fixtures/projects/<id>/ — repo metadata, AGENTS.md, README, .beads, .hoopoe state
   - fixtures/snapshots/<scenario>/ — bv triage, br list, NTM session state, Agent Mail dump, reservations, health snapshots
   - fixtures/event-streams/<scenario>.jsonl — replayable WS event sequences with sequence cursors
   - fixtures/pane-logs/<scenario>/ — byte-addressable PTY output samples
   - fixtures/build-logs/<scenario>/ — test/build runner outputs
   - fixtures/tool-degraded/ — capability registries with missing/degraded entries
2. Mock daemon mode flag (--mock-flywheel) that boots the daemon with fixture sources instead of real adapters.
3. Replay engine: time-stepped event delivery, optional acceleration (1×, 10×, 100×).
4. Scenario picker UI (Diagnostics-only) for switching scenarios at runtime.
5. CI smoke test that boots Hoopoe with mock-flywheel mode and exercises each stage.

REQUIRED SCENARIOS (§8.8)
==========================
- healthy-hour: deterministic ticks, wakeAgent:false on every tending job, [SILENT] smoke checks, near-zero LLM cost.
- idle-but-not-stuck: low-confidence detection; escalator suppresses or asks for status.
- genuinely-wedged-pane: deterministic evidence threshold crossed; agent.kill_wedged_process proposed/executed; postcondition verified.
- rate-limited-with-caam: ActionPlan emits caam.switch_account; postcondition verifies new account + pane resumes.
- rate-limited-no-caam: degraded-mode path (capabilities_optional missing); pause-and-notify; agent prompt-shaped to not propose caam actions.
- stale-reservation: reservation.force_release with evidence + approval + Mail notice + postcondition.
- commit-burst: snapshot-health coalesces by SHA; push-stale-commits dedupes via idempotency keys.
- budget-breach: watch-safety-thresholds emits swarm.halt without LLM; latency measured.
- skill-drift: pinned project blocks affected jobs; floating-skill project warns.
- missing-tool: required capability absent; affected job enters block_job; no silent failure.
- postcondition-failure: action executes but state doesn't reflect; follow-up detection emitted.
- action-arbitration: safety + recovery target same swarm; safety wins; recovery superseded_by safety run.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All §8.8 fixtures committed and runnable.
- 'bun run mock-flywheel' (or equivalent) boots Hoopoe end-to-end on fixtures alone.
- CI runs each scenario as a smoke test on every PR.
- Adapter contract tests pass against fixtures + drift detector flags upstream tool changes.
- Documented in packages/fixtures/README.md.
close_reason: Implemented fixture-backed daemon Mock Flywheel Mode in eeb7a31; initial scaffold landed in 949ad1a

title: AGENTS.md authoring + per-project agent operating contract surfacing
description:
WHAT
====
Detect AGENTS.md per project. If missing: surface a 'create AGENTS.md' action with a Hoopoe-recommended template (covers §1.1 source-of-truth discipline + §7.3 swarm rules). Provide an 'open AGENTS.md in editor' link in the project header. Re-read enforced at swarm kickoff (§7.3 'Force AGENTS.md and README reread').

WHY
====
AGENTS.md is the per-project agent operating contract. The kickoff prompt (Phase 8 task) explicitly requires agents to reread it. If the project doesn't have one, the swarm has no contract — Hoopoe should make this visible up-front rather than letting a swarm launch without one.

TEMPLATE CONTENT (Hoopoe-recommended)
========================================
- Project goal + success criteria.
- Coordination rules: Agent Mail, br, bv, file reservations, bead-ID convention.
- Push policy: every commit pushed promptly.
- Test/build conventions: which test runner, which lint rules.
- Stuck-state escalation: when to ask for help vs proceed.
- Cross-cutting hardening expectations.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §4.2 'Project imported' gate — AGENTS.md present.
- §7.3 — kickoff requires AGENTS.md reread.
- §15 #7 — agents read AGENTS.md as part of every loop.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Detection on project import + every reconnect.
- 'Create AGENTS.md' action surfaces template; user can edit before commit.
- Project header link to AGENTS.md in the local clone.
- Swarm-launch gate warns when AGENTS.md is missing.

title: Phase 5: CLI adapters for Claude Code / Codex CLI / Gemini CLI (subscription-backed CLI execution)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side adapters that shell out to the agent CLIs. Each adapter captures stdout/stderr per-candidate; parses model output into the per-candidate markdown files at .hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/candidates/<model-slug>.md. Account auth delegated to CAAM and the CLIs themselves.

CLIs SUPPORTED (§5.1, §7.1, §13)
==================================
- **Claude Code CLI** (drives Claude Max → Opus, Sonnet)
- **Codex CLI** (drives GPT Pro → GPT-5.x AND the GPT-5 Pro API counterpart)
- **Gemini CLI** (drives Gemini Ultra → Gemini 3 Pro with Deep Think)

ADAPTER CONTRACT
=================
Each adapter implements:
- run(prompt: string, opts: {model?: string, accountId?: string, maxTokens?: number, timeout?: duration}) → AsyncIterable<chunk> + final result
- supports(model): bool — capability check from registry
- account(accountId): set CAAM-managed account
- artifacts → captures stdout/stderr to job artifacts

ACCOUNT AUTH (§5.1)
====================
- Hoopoe NEVER stores agent-CLI credentials directly.
- Account inventory, current-account-per-agent, and switch-account actions read from / driven through CAAM.
- caam.account.switch is the recovery action.

ARTIFACT STORAGE (§7.1 'Artifact layout')
===========================================
.hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/
  candidates/
    chatgpt-pro.md     ← from Oracle adapter
    claude-opus.md     ← from Claude Code CLI
    gemini-pro.md      ← from Gemini CLI
    third.md           ← optional third candidate

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.1 — secrets boundary (no direct API keys ever).
- §7.1 — execution modes.
- Appendix C #11 — NO provider SDK imports.
- audit log — every model call recorded with model identity, harness, CAAM account, input hash, artifact hash.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- The daemon NEVER calls api.openai.com / api.anthropic.com / generativelanguage.googleapis.com directly. Linter / CI rule enforced.
- Each call produces a context manifest (§5.5) before sending: source files/artifacts included, redactions applied, provider/model/account, policy rule that allowed the call.
- Long-running calls (extended reasoning, deep think) need streaming stdout capture + intermediate progress events.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Three adapters wired (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI).
- Each can run a smoke prompt against a real subscription on the research-spike VPS.
- Stdout/stderr captured to plan-job artifact stream.
- Per-candidate markdown files written.
- CAAM account routing tested.
- Audit log entry per call (model, harness, CAAM account, input hash, artifact hash; redacted).
- Linter rule blocks any provider SDK import in daemon.

title: Phase 9: Activity panel + Agent Mail + user↔orchestrator chat test suite (timeline correctness, mail ingestion, urgent alerts, cross-stage availability)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 9 test suite covering the cross-stage Activity drawer, Agent Mail ingestion, the user↔orchestrator chat backed by orchestrator-chat tending agent, urgent alerts, and the message-type filtering.

UNIT TESTS
============
- Activity drawer UI: persistent across stages, unread badge, filterable timeline, click-to-pivot (bead pill → bead detail; agent chip → swarm tile; file path → reservation view); compose-as-overseer; broadcast-to-swarm; create-bead-from-message; mark-acknowledged.
- Agent Mail adapter: MCP/HTTP surfaces preferred, NTM mail/locks fallback, read-only DB/file last; threads, reservations, force-release; bead-ID-in-subject convention recognized.
- Activity timeline ingestion: every event category renders (agent registered; mail sent/received/urgent; bead claimed/changed; file reserved/renewed/released/conflicted; build/test events; rate limit detected; pane wedged; orchestrator intervention; review request/finding; commit; health snapshot updated; user→orchestrator; orchestrator→user).
- Filter chips: by category, by agent, by bead, by reservation status, urgent-only.
- User→orchestrator chat input: textarea at panel bottom; submit fires user_message_in_activity_panel event → orchestrator-chat tending job activates → agent runs with vibing-with-ntm + ntm skills loaded → response streams back into the same thread.
- Reservation view: lists active + stale reservations; force-release button gated on approval; posts Agent Mail notice on release; verifies notice presence as postcondition.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. Open Activity drawer from each of the four stages → drawer renders with same content (Phase 9 acceptance).
2. Mail event arrives via WS → drawer updates in real time without manual refresh; unread badge increments.
3. Click bead pill in Activity → bead drawer opens with correct bead.
4. Click agent chip in Activity → Swarm stage opens with that agent's tile focused.
5. User types 'what is agent ag_7 doing?' in chat input → orchestrator-chat agent fires → agent reads pane log + br state + agent_mail → responds with summary; chat thread shows both messages with timestamps.
6. User types 'force-release reservation res_9' in chat input → orchestrator-chat proposes reservation.force_release ActionPlan → approval dialog opens → user approves → executor runs → postcondition verifies Agent Mail no longer reports reservation + release notice was posted → drawer shows the action's audit entry.
7. Stale reservation conflict: agent A holds res_9 past TTL while agent B tries to reserve same file → drawer shows urgent conflict warning; user resolves via force-release.
8. Urgent alert: simulate budget breach → urgent banner appears at top of drawer + audio cue (configurable) + remains until acknowledged.
9. @slo activity.event.display.p95 ≤ 1s holds across 100 simulated events.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
========================
- Agent Mail unreachable → drawer shows 'mail unavailable' degraded state; capability registry updated; orchestrator-chat blocks gracefully.
- WS reconnect after gap → drawer fetches missing events via replayEvents; deduped by eventId; no duplicates rendered.
- Rapid event burst (1000 events in 1s) → drawer coalesces noisy events (e.g. agent.tick) per renderer subscription rule; daemon RSS bounded.
- Slow renderer wedged on Activity stream → daemon emits _lag with offset → renderer fetches from disk on resume.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every chat-input message logs correlationId; the orchestrator-chat tending run inherits it.
- Every render of an Activity entry logs the eventId + render duration at trace level.
- Force-release actions log the original reason + the user note + the postcondition result.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.5 — Activity panel strategic intent.
- §8.4 orchestrator-chat — the literal tending-agent backing the chat.
- §16 Phase 9 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 9 acceptance row.
- §10.5 — activity.event.display.p95 SLO target.
- hp-1r4 / hp-3se / hp-tg6 / hp-ay4 — Phase 9 implementation beads.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on drawer + adapter + chat surface.
- All 9 e2e scenarios pass.
- @slo activity.event.display.p95 ≤ 1s holds.
- Cross-stage availability verified across all four stages.

title: Phase 6: br adapter (br --json subcommands; .beads/issues.jsonl read model; sync orchestration)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side br adapter per §2.3 BrAdapter precedence:
1. br commands with --json where available
2. .beads/issues.jsonl read model
3. read-only SQLite fallback if documented

WRAPPED COMMANDS (with --json)
================================
- br list, ready, blocked, search, show, dep tree, dep cycles, dep list
- br create, update, close, reopen
- br dep add, dep remove
- br sync --flush-only (Hoopoe is responsible for git ops on .beads/)
- br doctor

br SYNC SEMANTICS (beads-br skill)
====================================
- br NEVER runs git commands.
- After br create/update/close, Hoopoe MUST run br sync --flush-only.
- Then Hoopoe MUST git add .beads/ + git commit (per §1.1 source-of-truth: code is canonical via origin; this means beads commits also go through the daemon-managed Git push policy).

CAPABILITY IDS (§2.8)
=======================
- br.issues.read
- br.issues.update
- br.dep.add
- br.dep.remove
- br.sync.flush

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.3 — adapter precedence.
- beads-br skill (loaded into context for this bead).
- §17 reference — github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust.
- audit log — every br write is audited.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- NEVER run bare bv (TUI; §1.3, Appendix C #1).
- Sync orchestration: after a daemon-initiated br write, queue a bounded sync job; coalesce multiple writes within a window.
- Cycle detection: br dep cycles MUST return empty before considering a bead set healthy.
- The git commit on .beads/ goes through the same audit gate as other Git ops.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Adapter wraps every br command listed above.
- Sync orchestration: write → sync → git add + git commit chain works (audit-logged).
- Cycle detection enforced.
- Capability registry reports br.* capabilities.
- Adapter contract tests pass against fixtures.

title: Phase 5: Model-context policy + per-call context manifests (§5.5 privacy)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement .hoopoe/model-context-policy.json + the per-call context-manifest writer that records what reaches each model call.

POLICY FILE (§5.5)
====================
.hoopoe/model-context-policy.json controls:
- whether raw source files may be sent to external model APIs
- whether only summaries/indexes may be sent
- excluded paths and glob patterns
- maximum context size per job
- whether logs may be included
- whether secrets/scanned sensitive strings block the job or are redacted
- provider allowlist
- per-stage defaults for Planning, Beads, Swarm, Tending, Review

PER-CALL CONTEXT MANIFEST (§5.5)
==================================
Before any model call, the daemon records a context manifest:
- source files/artifacts included
- redactions applied
- provider/model/account
- policy rule that allowed the call

UI shows this manifest beside plan/review/tending artifacts so the user can audit what reaches each model.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.5 — privacy and model-context policy.
- §8.3 — context manifest paired with tending-job context_policy.
- audit log — manifests stored alongside tending-run records.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Default policy file generated at project import.
- Editor for policy file in project settings.
- Every model call (planning, beads, tending, review) writes a manifest.
- Manifest visible in artifact viewer.
- Redaction patterns covered: provider tokens, RSA keys, .env contents, etc.
- Policy enforcement test: secret-bearing file → redacted in manifest, NOT included raw.

title: Phase 2: SSH profile manager + tunnel manager (ssh2 in main process; FSM-driven reconnect)
description:
WHAT
====
Desktop-side SSH profile manager (host, port, username, key path/reference) + tunnel manager that opens, monitors, and re-establishes the SSH local tunnel to the VPS daemon. Uses ssh2 in the main process. FSM-driven (§2.5 ConnectionManager FSM): unconfigured → ssh_probing → bootstrapping → tunnel_connecting → authenticating → ready → degraded → reconnecting → ready → disconnected.

WHY (§2.5 transport ladder, §5.3 'one narrow exception')
=========================================================
SSH tunnel is the v1 default transport. The desktop opens a local-port → daemon-127.0.0.1:<port> tunnel and speaks HTTPS/WS to localhost. This keeps the source-of-truth boundary clean (no public daemon port), gives full SSH key authentication for free, and works through every NAT/firewall the user already navigated to ssh in.

The desktop NEVER invokes project-level git, br, bv, ntm, or shell commands directly (§5.3); it ONLY invokes ssh for the tunnel itself, plus read-only git plumbing on the desktop's local clone (§7.7, the narrow exception).

PROFILE MANAGER
================
- VPS connection profiles stored: host, port, username, ssh-key reference, daemon binary URL/version, tunnel local-port preference.
- Profile data is non-secret; secret material (passphrase) goes through SecretStore (Phase 1 Keychain task).
- v1 is single-VPS (ADR-0001); profile manager scales to multi-VPS post-MVP.

TUNNEL MANAGER
===============
- Opens local TCP listener on a chosen port; forwards to VPS:127.0.0.1:<daemon-port>.
- Exposes port via IPC to renderer (TanStack Query keys reference base URL `localhost:<port>`).
- Monitors tunnel health via heartbeat to /health.
- On tunnel death: backoff with jitter, 30s cap; transitions through reconnecting state.

FSM TRIGGERS (§2.5)
====================
- macOS sleep/wake (powerMonitor.on('suspend'/'resume')).
- Tunnel death (ssh2 'close' event).
- Daemon health failure (/health returns non-200 or times out).
- WS heartbeat timeout (no events for >N seconds).
- Version mismatch (/v1/version returns incompatible API version).
- Expired bearer (HTTP 401).
- Network change (Mac network configuration change).

RECONNECT BEHAVIOR (§2.5 'Reconnect always performs')
======================================================
1. Tunnel check — re-establish if dead.
2. Bearer/session check — refresh if expired.
3. WS-token refresh (always).
4. Subscribe with sequence cursors — per-channel from latestSequence.
5. Fetch one snapshot for active channels.
6. Reconcile local clone if project is active (Phase 4).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.5 — full transport ladder + FSM.
- §5.3 — desktop runs SSH for the tunnel + read-only git on its own clone; no other project-level shell.
- §10.5 — desktop reconnect after laptop wake p95 < 10s after network returns.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- ssh2 is mature; document version pin and security posture.
- Use known_hosts TOFU on first connect; verify on subsequent connects; warn on fingerprint changes.
- Tunnel listener must bind 127.0.0.1, NEVER 0.0.0.0.
- Tunnel local port: try preferred first, fall back to ephemeral; document the chosen port in client-settings.json so reconnects can prefer it.
- Mac sleep can break ssh2 sessions silently; powerMonitor is the canonical signal.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- ConnectionManager FSM implemented; transitions logged for debugging.
- Tunnel survives mac sleep+wake (reconnect within p95 < 10s of network return per §10.5 SLO).
- Bearer expiry triggers refresh; WS-token refresh on every WS connect.
- Sequence-cursor reconnect tested via Mock Flywheel disconnect simulation.
- Network-change trigger: switching wifi → tunnel re-establishes within bounded latency.
- Unit + integration tests for each FSM transition.

title: Phase 13: Onboarding tour + demo project + documentation
description:
WHAT
====
Onboarding tour mirroring the agent-flywheel.com 13-step wizard for new users. Demo project shipping with mock fixtures so users can explore each stage without immediately needing a real VPS. Documentation for the v1 release.

TOUR (§12 Phase 13)
=====================
- Optional first-run tour after wizard completes.
- Walks through: top bar pills, opening Activity panel, switching stages, creating a plan, converting to beads, launching swarm.
- Skippable; resumable from Diagnostics.

DEMO PROJECT
==============
- Pre-populated fixture project with realistic plan, beads, recent commits, health snapshots.
- Uses Mock Flywheel scenarios.
- Walks user through 'what does this look like in real use?'.

DOCUMENTATION
===============
- /docs/getting-started.md
- /docs/wizard.md (mirrors agent-flywheel.com canonical guide structure)
- /docs/source-of-truth.md (per Appendix A)
- /docs/security.md (per Appendix A)
- /docs/onboarding.md (per Appendix A)
- /docs/testing.md (per Appendix A)
- /docs/api-seed.md (Phase 2.5 generated)
- /docs/process-manager.md (Phase 2.5 generated)
- /docs/reconnect-replay.md (Phase 2.5 generated)
- /docs/troubleshooting.md
- /docs/upgrade-and-rollback.md

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.1 — canonical 13-step wizard.
- Appendix A — docs new home table.
- §12 Phase 13 — demo project + documentation.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Tour functional + skippable + resumable.
- Demo project ships with v1.
- Documentation covers all major stages + concepts.
- Phase 13 acceptance: a new user can complete the §15 definition-of-success flow guided only by docs + tour.

title: Cross-cutting: packages/design-system — tokens, components, Storybook
description:
WHAT
====
Hoopoe's greenfield design system. Tokens (cream/dark sidebar, agent-family color palette, status pills, priority chips, coverage-bar ramp), reusable components (StageHeader, AgentTile, BeadCard, StatusPill, PriorityChip, CoverageBar, TerminalPane, TimelineRow, HealthKpiCard, ApprovalDialog, CommandPalette), and Storybook for visual review against the reference design.

WHY GREENFIELD
==============
The lifted t3code desktop layer is rebranded but its UI is chat-centric and not a fit for a four-stage cockpit. The visual language Hoopoe needs — STAGE N — VERB framing, deliberate cream-on-dark palette, polished status/priority chips, agent tiles, bead cards — is purpose-built. Mockups exist in design/mockups/v1/ and design choices in design/DECISIONS.md.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- Appendix A: design-system component inventory new home.
- §7.1 design/mockups/v1/wizard.jsx (StepBrief)
- §7.2 design/mockups/v1/beads.jsx (DAGView, RoundsModal)
- §7.6 cockpit chrome top bar — health pill, subscription pill, etc.

WHAT GOES IN
=============
1. Tokens layer (Tailwind config + CSS variables): colors, typography, spacing, radii, shadows. Cream/dark theme is primary; light theme deferred.
2. Foundational components — StageHeader, EmptyState, ErrorState, LoadingState, ConfirmDialog, ApprovalDialog, Drawer, Toast.
3. Domain components:
   - StatusPill (ready / claimed / in_progress / in_review / closed / blocked / paused / queued / running / waiting_approval / canceling / succeeded / failed / interrupted)
   - PriorityChip (P0–P4)
   - CoverageBar (red < 60%, yellow 60–80%, green > 80%; configurable thresholds)
   - AgentTile (harness icon, CAAM account, current bead, status, time-on-bead, recent decisions)
   - BeadCard (Kanban + DAG)
   - TerminalPane (xterm.js, used ONLY in Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' debug toggle, not default Swarm UI)
   - TimelineRow (Activity panel)
   - HealthKpiCard (coverage / complexity / hotspots)
   - CommandPalette (⌘K, backed by a real command registry — t3code's implicit string-switch dispatch is rejected per Appendix B anti-patterns)
4. Top-bar chrome (§7.6) — project/repo/branch, tool health dots, swarm state, beads pulse, code-health pill, subscription-usage pill, Activity panel toggle.
5. Agent-family palette: distinct colors for Claude Code / Codex / Gemini agents.
6. Storybook with stories per component and per state. Visual diff in CI.

DESIGN PRINCIPLES (lifted from design/DECISIONS.md, §1.4)
==========================================================
- Every meaningful automated action must be inspectable; the UI must surface what triggered each event.
- The default Swarm UI is TERMINAL-FREE (§7.3 / Appendix C #12). Terminal scrollback is reachable only behind explicit Diagnostics opt-in.
- Match the rigor of the reference mockups (cream-on-dark, STAGE N — VERB framing).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Storybook builds and CI publishes a static gallery.
- Visual diff against design/mockups/v1/* clears at parity (no regressions; new screens require explicit acceptance).
- Components documented in packages/design-system/README.md with usage examples.
- Tokens shared with Tailwind via plugin + CSS variables — no hand-coded hex values in component code.
- Accessibility pass: keyboard nav works on every interactive component; semantic HTML; AA contrast on all status states.

title: Release smoke checks (§18.4; pre-release gate)
description:
WHAT
====
Pre-release smoke gate per §18.4. Every Hoopoe beta release must pass this checklist before publish; CI runs the full suite as a hard gate. Failures block release.

CHECKS (each is its own automated test)
==========================================
1. **Signed app launches on arm64 + x64 macOS** — DMG opens, app runs, no signing/notarization warnings.
2. **Auto-update mock server** — upgrades stable → beta and beta → stable channel choices; rollback works.
3. **Settings + keybindings survive migration** — start app on N-1 release, upgrade to N, settings + keybindings + custom commands intact.
4. **Daemon upgrade backs up config/db** — daemon vN-1 → vN; config + DB backed up before migrate; /v1/version reports new version; compatibility checks pass.
5. **Local clone cache can be cleared and rebuilt** — Diagnostics 'Clear local clone' deletes + next access re-clones from origin; no orphan files.
6. **Event replay works after simulated laptop sleep** — desktop sleeps 10 min; resumes; sequence-cursor reconnect closes gap; no UI corruption; @slo events.replay.10min.p95 ≤ 5s.
7. **Process manager cancels long-running job without orphan children** — long job → cancel → SIGTERM → SIGKILL escalation completes within timeout; no orphan child process group remains; @slo job.cancellation.no-orphans.
8. **Health worktree cleanup leaves no stale multi-GB directories** — after 100 health snapshots in a fixture project, no stale worktrees > retention window.
9. **Audit log redaction catches every secret class** — bearer tokens, model API keys, SSH passphrases, provider credentials, browser cookies, pairing tokens — none appear in raw form across audit log + structured-log evidence + crash reports + plan-job artifacts.
10. **Project export/restore round-trip** — export bundle includes plan + beads traceability + findings + landing history + artifact hashes; restore produces byte-identical artifact set; no secrets in export.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Each check logs structured outcome (pass/fail) with timing.
- Failure dumps include the gap (expected vs observed) + last 200 log entries.
- Evidence committed to docs/test-evidence/release-<version>/.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §18.4 — release smoke checks.
- §10.5 — SLO targets that map to checks 6 and 7.
- §10.4 — backup/export/restore semantics covered by check 10.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t / hp-5ja — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 10 checks automated.
- CI gate fails release on any single failed check.
- Evidence retention: every released version's evidence kept indefinitely; pre-release runs follow §10.3 retention.
- docs/release-process.md describes the gate.

title: Phase 10: Initial tending job set (tend-swarm, watch-safety-thresholds, push-stale-commits, snapshot-health, drift-check, review-readiness-check, orchestrator-chat)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the initial tending jobs per §8.4. Each ships with its pre-script Go file + prompt; users can edit them post-install.

JOBS (§8.4)
============

1. **tend-swarm** (every 4m)
   - Toolsets: br, bv, ntm, agent_mail, git_read, caam, casr.
   - Pre-script reconciles NTM/br/bv/Mail/Git/CAAM; detects idle/wedged/rate-limited/stalled-bead candidates, duplicate claims, agents not using Agent Mail, agents not updating br; surfaces CAAM-reported account exhaustion as first-class detection. If none → wakeAgent:false.
   - Skills: vibing-with-ntm, ntm.
   - Prompt instructs the agent to read AGENTS.md + vibing-with-ntm and decide actions per the skill.
   - Mutating actions returned as typed ActionPlan; cite evidence refs.
   - Deliver: hoopoe_activity_panel.

2. **watch-safety-thresholds** (every 30s)
   - Toolsets: budget, ntm, caut, srp, sbh, pt.
   - Pre-script checks per-agent + per-swarm subscription-quota caps (caut), hard rate-limit halts, disk pressure (srp), CPU/load (srp), daemon health.
   - HARD THRESHOLD CROSSING emits typed deterministic action intents:
     - budget breach → swarm.halt
     - disk pressure → sbh-driven cleanup of stale artifacts
     - genuinely wedged process → agent.kill_wedged_process
   - Always wakeAgent:false (no judgment needed).
   - audit_always: true.
   - Deliver: hoopoe_activity_urgent.

3. **push-stale-commits** (every 1m)
   - Toolset: git_write.
   - Pre-script detects unpushed commits older than threshold (default 5 min); pushes via daemon (audit; policy).
   - Never wakes agent — push policy is mechanical (§7.3).
   - Deliver: hoopoe_activity_panel only when push happens or fails.
   - audit_always: true.

4. **snapshot-health** (event vps_push_completed | every 10m)
   - Toolset: health_adapter, file_read.
   - Pre-script runs language-appropriate coverage/complexity tools; writes snapshot artifact; emits health_snapshot_updated event.
   - Never wakes agent — measurement is mechanical.
   - Deliver: hoopoe_activity_panel event-style entry only.

5. **drift-check** (every 30m)
   - Toolsets: br, bv, git_read, health_adapter.
   - Pre-script checks 'many commits / few beads closed', 'P0 critical path stale', 'review findings clustering in same domain', 'code health worsens while beads close', 'agents create many new beads without closing old ones'. If none → wakeAgent:false.
   - Skills: vibing-with-ntm.
   - Prompt instructs the agent to summarize drift + propose corrective action OR reply [SILENT].
   - Deliver: hoopoe_activity_panel.

6. **review-readiness-check** (every 15m)
   - Toolsets: br, bv.
   - Pre-script checks if implementation beads mostly closed, P0/P1 ready beads handled, no obvious stuck in-progress, latest health snapshot available — i.e., §9.1 prerequisites. If review-mode threshold not crossed → wakeAgent:false.
   - Skills: vibing-with-ntm.
   - Prompt: per vibing-with-ntm skill, decide whether to propose flip to Debugging/Hardening (§7.4); post proposal or [SILENT].
   - Deliver: hoopoe_activity_panel.

7. **orchestrator-chat** (event user_message_in_activity_panel)
   - Toolsets: br, bv, ntm, agent_mail, git_read, health_adapter.
   - Pre-script gathers user message + recent activity + current swarm state.
   - Skills: vibing-with-ntm, ntm.
   - Prompt: respond as orchestrator. Read-only freely; mutations as typed ActionPlan.
   - Deliver: hoopoe_activity_panel.
   - This is the LITERAL realization of '§7.5 user can chat with orchestrator agent.'

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §8.4 — full job definitions.
- §8.5 — what pre-scripts cover (preserved from §8.3 / §8.4 / §8.5 of old plan).
- §8.7 — tradeoffs explicit.
- §8.8 — evaluation harness fixtures.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All seven jobs running.
- Healthy-hour fixture (§8.8): zero LLM wakes, zero panel noise.
- Stuck-fixture: tend-swarm fires, ActionPlan emitted, postcondition verified.
- Budget-breach fixture: watch-safety-thresholds emits swarm.halt without LLM.
- Push-stale-commits dedupes via idempotency.
- snapshot-health coalesces by SHA.
- drift-check + review-readiness-check trigger correctly.
- orchestrator-chat answers user messages from Activity panel.
- All §8.8 fixtures pass.

title: Phase 6 / Phase 7 / §7.2: Bead Rounds view modal — per-polish-round prompt + action summary + artifact inspection (the §1.4 inspectability surface for bead-conversion polish rounds)
description:
WHAT
====
§7.2 mandates: 'The Rounds view exposes the literal prompt sent to each polish-round model alongside the action summary, making each round's artifact directly inspectable per §1.4 (see design/mockups/v1/beads.jsx RoundsModal).'

The Rounds view is a *bead-conversion* surface (Phase 6 / Phase 7) — distinct from the Plan refinement-round diff viewer (hp-hx12, which inspects per-plan-refinement-round in Phase 5). Bead conversion has its own polish rounds (rounds 1-6: plan coverage / dependency correctness / granularity / test obligations / parallel tracks / fresh-eyes review). This bead implements the inspection UI that lets the user see exactly what each round did.

WHY THIS IS A FOCUSED BEAD
==========================
Existing coverage:
- **hp-xi9** — Phase 6 polish-rounds JOBS (6 rounds, each tracked artifact, beads-workflow polish prompt). Owns the *engine*.
- **hp-9kt** — Phase 6 epic (bead conversion + traceability + polish rounds + bv adapter).
- **hp-ojh** — Plan-to-beads conversion job + beads-workflow skill loading + traceability.json.
- **hp-0ba** — Phase 7 bead detail drawer (overview, context, deps, traceability, mail, files, tests, commits, findings, audit). Does NOT mention the Rounds view (bead-detail drawer is per-bead; Rounds view is per-conversion).
- **hp-5cr** — Phase 7 epic (Kanban / DAG / Force views + bead drawer + dependency editing).

What's missing: the **inspection UI** for the polish-round artifacts hp-xi9 produces. Without it, the user sees the result of the polish (better beads) but cannot see *why* the rounds made the decisions they did, what the prompt was, what the diff in the bead graph looked like before/after the round.

VIEW LOCATION + INVOCATION
==========================
The Rounds view is a **modal** invoked from:
- The Beads stage: 'Polish history' button in the Beads stage header → modal opens.
- The conversion-job artifact rail (when reviewing a finished conversion): 'Open Rounds view' link.
- The bead detail drawer: 'Show polish history affecting this bead' (filtered to rounds that touched the current bead).

Modal width: ~1100px. Modal layout:
- **Round timeline (top, 80px)** — horizontal stepper with the 6 rounds + the conversion job at the start. Active round highlighted.
- **Three-column body**:
  1. **Round detail (left, 360px)** — round number, name, model used (CAAM account), elapsed time, started/completed timestamps, prompt template (versioned, see hp-ojh), input scope summary (which beads were considered).
  2. **Action summary (center, flexible)** — Markdown rendered: 'Round 3 (granularity) made these changes: split bead B-142 into B-142a + B-142b; merged B-201 + B-202; reworded B-50 description...' Each change is a clickable row that opens the relevant bead in a side preview.
  3. **Diff pane (right, 380px)** — When a row is selected, this shows the bead-graph diff for that change (before/after dependencies, before/after fields).

PROMPT EXPOSURE
===============
Per §1.4, the **literal prompt sent to the polish-round model** is exposed verbatim (with redactions per hp-je1p). The user can:
- Read it.
- Copy it.
- Compare to a previous version (when prompt template versioning recorded a change).
- Compare to the upstream beads-workflow skill prompt (so prompt drift from the skill is visible).

This is critical because §17 says 'when the plan and a referenced skill disagree on swarm or tending behavior, the skill wins.' The Rounds view makes such disagreements visible: 'Round 3 used a prompt that differs from beads-workflow v1.2.0 in 3 lines.'

ARTIFACT-LEVEL VS RENDER-TIME
=============================
The polish-round runner (hp-xi9) writes `.hoopoe/conversion-runs/<runId>/round-<N>/{prompt.md, summary.md, beads-before.jsonl, beads-after.jsonl, action-log.jsonl}` for each round. The Rounds view loads these artifacts via daemon RPC (read-through cache). Bead-graph diffs are computed at view-time from the before/after JSONL snapshots; not pre-rendered.

NEW DAEMON ENDPOINTS
====================
- `GET /v1/projects/{id}/beads/conversion-runs/{runId}` — full run metadata + per-round summaries.
- `GET /v1/projects/{id}/beads/conversion-runs/{runId}/rounds/{roundN}` — round detail + prompt + action log.
- `GET /v1/projects/{id}/beads/conversion-runs/{runId}/rounds/{roundN}/bead-diff?beadId={id}` — per-bead diff (before/after fields + dep changes).
- `GET /v1/projects/{id}/beads/conversion-runs/{runId}/rounds/{roundN}/skill-drift` — diff of the round's prompt vs the upstream beads-workflow skill prompt at that pin.

UNIT TESTS (10) — `apps/desktop/src/beads/__tests__/roundsModal.test.ts` + `apps/daemon/internal/beads/__tests__/conversion_artifacts_test.go`
==============================================================================================================================================
1. **Round timeline rendering** — given fixture conversion run with 6 rounds + conversion job, stepper renders 7 steps with correct active state.
2. **Prompt redaction respect** — fixture round prompt contains a redacted token marker → modal renders '[REDACTED:bearer-token]' literally, not the original value.
3. **Action-summary parse** — round-3 summary `{ split: 2, merged: 1, reworded: 5 }` produces 8 clickable rows in the center column with correct labels.
4. **Bead-graph diff** — before-jsonl has B-142, after-jsonl has B-142a + B-142b; diff renderer shows split-into-2 with arrows; dep changes (parent edges from B-200 redirected) shown.
5. **Per-bead filter from drawer** — opening Rounds view from bead B-142 with filter active → only rounds where action-log contains B-142 (or split origins) are visible; structured log records filter applied.
6. **Skill-drift diff** — fixture prompt diverges from beads-workflow skill at pinned digest in 3 lines → skill-drift endpoint returns 3 hunks; UI renders banner 'Round 3 prompt diverges from beads-workflow v1.2.0 in 3 places. Show drift?'.
7. **Copy-prompt** — click 'Copy' on the prompt block → clipboard contains the prompt text (verified via stub clipboard).
8. **Empty round (no actions)** — round-N with empty action log → modal shows 'This round produced no graph changes' and skill-drift banner if applicable; prompt still inspectable.
9. **Failed round** — round-N status=failed → modal shows error message + last-N-lines of stderr from the job + 'Retry' affordance (links to hp-xi9 retry).
10. **Concurrent open** — two windows open the modal simultaneously for the same conversion run → both render correctly; daemon serves them from cache; structured log records cache_hits.

E2E TESTS (6) — `apps/desktop/e2e/roundsModal.e2e.ts`
========================================================
1. **End-to-end happy path** — Run a real plan-to-beads conversion + 3 polish rounds via fixture; click 'Polish history' in Beads stage; verify timeline + action summary + per-bead diff all render correctly; click a row → bead preview side-panel.
2. **Per-bead filter** — Open bead detail drawer for B-142; click 'Show polish history affecting this bead'; modal opens filtered; only rounds 2 + 3 visible (the ones that touched B-142 per fixture).
3. **Skill-drift visible** — Fixture pins beads-workflow v1.2.0; round prompt diverges in 4 lines; banner appears; click 'Show drift' → diff modal opens with the 4 lines highlighted.
4. **Copy + paste prompt** — Copy round-3 prompt; paste into a Notes app (or a stub textarea); verify content matches.
