5. **Failed round retry** — Conversion run had round 4 fail; open Rounds view; round-4 step shows error state with stderr; click Retry → hp-xi9 retry RPC fires; structured log captures `rounds_modal.retry_invoked`.
6. **Audit chain integration** — Open Rounds view → click on a specific round-3 action 'Split B-142 into B-142a/B-142b'; navigate via 'Show in audit log' to hp-1wg8; chain shows the conversion-job → round-3 fire → split action → resulting bead-create + bead-archive entries.

DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (per hp-lxs)
==========================================
- `rounds_modal.opened` { project_id, conversion_run_id, source (beads-stage|drawer|artifact-rail), round_filter? }
- `rounds_modal.round_selected` { conversion_run_id, round_n, filter_bead_id? }
- `rounds_modal.skill_drift_detected` { conversion_run_id, round_n, skill_id, skill_pin_digest, drift_line_count }
- `rounds_modal.bead_diff_rendered` { conversion_run_id, round_n, bead_id, change_kind (split|merge|reword|dep_change|status_change|other), compute_ms }
- `rounds_modal.retry_invoked` { conversion_run_id, round_n }

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 'Views' — Kanban, DAG, Force, drawer, Rounds view (this surface)
- §1.4 'Every automation must be inspectable' (the trust contract; this is a primary surface for it)
- §17 'When the plan and a referenced skill disagree, the skill wins' — drift surfacing here makes that visible
- design/mockups/v1/beads.jsx `RoundsModal`
- hp-xi9 (polish-rounds jobs that produce the artifacts), hp-ojh (plan-to-beads conversion + skill loading), hp-9kt (Phase 6 epic), hp-5cr (Phase 7 epic), hp-0ba (bead detail drawer; Rounds view is a sibling, not nested), hp-r3i (schemas), hp-elx (design system), hp-i62 (component set: ApprovalDialog-shaped modal), hp-je1p (redaction in prompts), hp-1wg8 (audit log explorer cross-link), hp-g73 (audit log infra)

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Rounds view modal with timeline + 3-column layout
- Daemon endpoints for conversion-runs / rounds / bead-diff / skill-drift
- Action summary renderer with clickable rows
- Bead-graph diff render at view-time from before/after JSONL
- Skill-drift banner + drift diff modal
- Per-bead filter (when invoked from drawer)
- Failed-round retry integration with hp-xi9
- 10 unit + 6 e2e tests passing
- Structured logging events emitted
- All round prompts redaction-respecting
- Documentation: 'Click any polished bead to see why and how it was polished.'

title: Phase 13 final-gate landing checklist UI (ship-ready gate per §4.2)
description:
WHAT
====
The §4.2 'Ship ready' gate UI per §9.4 'final_gate_ready' state. Final landing checklist surfaced at the end of Stage 04.

CHECKLIST (§4.2 Ship ready)
=============================
- tests/builds pass or exceptions documented
- code health gates pass or follow-up beads exist
- Git/beads synced

AUTO-DERIVED ITEMS
====================
- ALL implementation beads closed (or intentionally deferred).
- Convergence detector reports final_gate_ready.
- Latest health snapshot meets project thresholds (or follow-up bead exists).
- No blocking review findings.
- VPS unpushed-commit count = 0.
- Implementation-evidence ledger covers every plan section.

UX
===
- Each item has status: pass / fail / waived (with reason note).
- Per-item evidence link (which bead, which test, which finding).
- 'Confirm ship' action gated until all items pass or are waived.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §4.2 gate invariants.
- §9.4 convergence state machine.
- §15 #14 — definition of success: 'session lands with synced beads, clean Git, passing tests or documented exceptions, restartable audit trail.'

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Final-gate UI implemented.
- Checklist auto-populates from project state.
- Waiver flow audited.
- 'Confirm ship' writes a 'project completed' audit entry + transitions project lifecycle (§4.1).

title: Phase 8: NTM adapter (ntm serve REST/SSE/WS preferred; --robot-snapshot/status/tail fallback; tmux capture last)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side NTM adapter per §2.3 NTMAdapter precedence:
1. ntm serve REST/SSE/WS
2. ntm --robot-snapshot / --robot-status / --robot-tail
3. tmux capture-pane fallback

WHY (§2.3, §3 'NTM is the largest integration surface')
=========================================================
NTM is the swarm orchestration layer Hoopoe wraps. As the LARGEST integration surface (and same-language Go), the adapter has the highest leverage. Use the official surfaces first; never parse human CLI output unless explicitly fallback.

WRAPPED SURFACES
=================
Sessions: list, get details, spawn, terminate, attach.
Panes: list, stream output (WS preferred), send input, capture snapshot.
Approvals: list, approve, deny.
Robot mode: --robot-snapshot, --robot-status, --robot-tail, --robot-spawn, --robot-send, --robot-wait, --robot-activity, --robot-interrupt.

ROBOT-MODE STATE MAPPING (per ru reference, §17)
==================================================
NTM emits IDLE / TYPING / THINKING / TOOL_USE / COMPLETE / ERROR; Hoopoe maps these to a unified state vocabulary used by AgentTile + tend-swarm.

CAPABILITY IDS (§2.8)
======================
- ntm.sessions.list / spawn / terminate
- ntm.panes.stream (transports: ws / sse / poll)
- ntm.approvals.list / approve
- ntm.robot.snapshot / status / tail
- ntm.swarm.halt

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.3 — adapter precedence.
- §1.3 — robot/API surfaces first.
- §3 — NTM is Go (1.25+, charmbracelet). Same-language adapter writing means we can read NTM source as canonical reference.
- §17 ntm reference.
- §17 ru reference — port the unified-state mapping.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- WS transport preferred for streaming pane output (lowest latency).
- SSE fallback when WS not available.
- tmux capture-pane is LAST resort for any features ntm serve doesn't expose.
- PTY transport ladder: NTM WebSocket/robot-tail preferred; tmux pipe-pane fallback; tmux capture-pane polling last; never one-SSH-per-pane and never aggressive renderer polling.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All wrapped surfaces tested against the research-spike VPS.
- Capability registry reports ntm.* capabilities correctly.
- Pane output streamed via WS to daemon (with byte offsets persisted; renderer never sees raw bytes by default).
- ru-reference unified-state mapping ported.
- Adapter contract tests pass.

title: Phase 2.5 / §2.6: RFC 7807 problem+json producer infrastructure (problem-type registry + error mapping + redaction + contract tests)
description:
WHAT
====
The daemon-side load-bearing infrastructure that turns every error response into RFC 7807 problem+json. §2.6 says: 'all errors use RFC 7807-style problem+json' — but the implementation is more than 'set Content-Type'. It needs a problem-type registry (so each error has a stable URI like https://hoopoe.io/problems/auth/pairing-token-expired), a routing layer that maps Go error → problem+json structure, redaction (so sensitive fields don't leak through error.detail), and contract tests that prove EVERY endpoint emits problem+json on every error path. Paired with hp-8dym (renderer error UX framework) which CONSUMES this format.

WHY (§2.6, §5.4 redaction, hp-8dym consumer)
==============================================
§2.6 is explicit: 'all errors use RFC 7807-style problem+json'. RFC 7807 specifies five core fields (type, title, status, detail, instance) plus extension fields. Without a producer subsystem:
- Each handler hand-writes error responses → drift over time → renderer (hp-8dym) breaks because some endpoint emits {error: 'foo'} and some emit {message: 'foo'}.
- Sensitive data leaks through error.detail (stack traces with paths, parameter values, internal IDs that reveal volume of data).
- Problem-type URIs are not stable → renderer can't switch on them to pick the right surface (toast vs banner vs inline pill vs blocking modal per hp-8dym's matrix).
- Contract tests are bypassable; CI doesn't catch regressions.

This is foundational backend infrastructure. Every endpoint in §2.6 is a consumer.

ARCHITECTURE
=============

PROBLEM-TYPE REGISTRY
----------------------
packages/schemas/problem-types.yaml
  - id: auth.pairing.token-expired
    type_uri: https://hoopoe.io/problems/auth/pairing-token-expired
    title:    'Pairing token has expired'
    status:   401
    surface:  blocking_modal      # hp-8dym surface routing hint
    actionability: re-pair
    user_message: 'The pairing token expired. Re-pair to continue.'
  - id: auth.bearer.invalid
    ...
  - id: project.not-found
    ...
  - id: bead.cycle-detected
    type_uri: https://hoopoe.io/problems/bead/cycle-detected
    title:    'Dependency cycle detected'
    status:   422
    surface:  banner
    actionability: edit-deps
    user_message: 'Adding this dependency would create a cycle: {{cycle_path}}'
  ... (estimated ~150 problem types across the daemon's surface)

Each problem type has machine-readable surface + actionability hints so hp-8dym's renderer matrix can pick the right UI without hand-coding per error type.

GO ERROR → PROBLEM MAPPING
---------------------------
Go errors implement an optional ProblemError interface:
  type ProblemError interface {
    error
    ProblemTypeID() string
    ProblemExtensions() map[string]any   // for {{cycle_path}}, etc.
  }

Standard error wrappers (auth.ErrPairingExpired, beads.ErrCycle, project.ErrNotFound, etc.) implement this. Handlers that return a plain Go error fall through to a default '500 internal' problem type with detail redacted.

ROUTING LAYER
--------------
chi/echo middleware that catches handler errors:
1. If error implements ProblemError → look up registry entry by ID → render problem+json with status, type URI, title, detail (redacted), extensions.
2. Else if error is HTTPStatusErr (e.g., 404 for unknown route) → standard problem+json for that status.
3. Else → 500 with type=hoopoe.io/problems/internal-error and detail='internal error; see correlation_id'.

REDACTION LAYER (cross-references hp-je1p)
-------------------------------------------
problem.detail and problem.extension values pass through the daemon's redaction layer (hp-je1p) before serialization. Patterns redacted: bearer tokens, pairing tokens, model API keys (defensive — should not exist anywhere but belt-and-suspenders), SSH passphrases, paths under user home (substituted with ~), credential helper output. The redaction layer applies regardless of whether the developer remembered to scrub.

CORRELATION IDs
----------------
Every problem+json response carries a 'correlation_id' field (extension) matching the audit log entry written for that request. Lets users / support correlate UI errors back to audit log entries.

UI HINTS (hp-8dym integration)
-------------------------------
Two custom RFC 7807 extensions:
  surface:        'toast' | 'banner' | 'inline_pill' | 'blocking_modal'
  actionability:  'reload' | 're-pair' | 'edit-deps' | 'switch-account' | 'open-docs' | 'manual'
hp-8dym's renderer reads these to drive the surface routing matrix. Without these hints the renderer falls back to severity-only routing (less precise).

CONTRACT TESTS
===============
Auto-generated per-endpoint test:
  for endpoint E in §2.6:
    for known error path P in E:
      response = call(E, fixture for P)
      assert response.status == registry[P].status
      assert response.headers['Content-Type'] == 'application/problem+json'
      assert json.parse(response.body).type == registry[P].type_uri
      assert json.parse(response.body).title == registry[P].title
      assert 'correlation_id' in json.parse(response.body)

Negative test: planted endpoint that returns plain error (not ProblemError) → response is still problem+json (default 500 mapping); regression test that hand-rolled errors are caught.

OBSERVABILITY
==============
- Metrics: problem_type counter per type_uri (Prometheus or daemon-internal counters); request_duration histogram by status class.
- Diagnostics screen: 'recent problems' table with type_uri, count, last seen, sample correlation_id.

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Registry loader** — registry parses; every entry has required fields (id, type_uri, title, status, surface, actionability, user_message). Log: registry validation results.
2. **ProblemError interface mapping** — auth.ErrPairingExpired returns the correct problem+json shape with status 401 + correct type URI + title. Log: error → response shape.
3. **Plain Go error fallback** — fmt.Errorf('something') returns 500 + hoopoe.io/problems/internal-error + redacted detail. Log: input error string + response detail.
4. **Extension interpolation** — bead.ErrCycle returns problem+json with extensions.cycle_path filled from ProblemExtensions(); user_message rendered with {{cycle_path}} substituted. Log: input cycle path + rendered message.
5. **Redaction kicks in** — error.Error() returns 'token=abc123def...'; response.detail has 'token=[REDACTED]'. Log: pre-redaction + post-redaction strings.
6. **Correlation_id present** — every problem+json response has correlation_id; matches the audit log entry's correlation_id. Log: response + audit chain.
7. **404 routing** — unknown route returns problem+json with status 404 + standard type URI + title 'Not Found'. Log: request + response.
8. **Schema validation against registry** — all 150 entries' status codes match HTTP standards (401 for auth, 404 for not-found, 422 for unprocessable, etc.). Log: schema-vs-registry mismatch report.
9. **Surface hint correctness** — auth.ErrPairingExpired's registry entry has surface='blocking_modal'; assert hp-8dym's matrix-test consumer would pick blocking modal. Log: surface lookup.
10. **Backwards compat** — adding a new problem-type entry does NOT change behavior for existing endpoints; load registry v1, then v2; assert v1 endpoints unchanged. Log: registry diff + behavior diff.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Per-endpoint contract test (auto-generated)** — for every endpoint in §2.6 + every known error path, hit endpoint with fixture trigger; assert response is application/problem+json with correct shape. Log file: e2e-problem-contract-{ts}.jsonl with per-endpoint pass/fail.
2. **Planted plain error caught** — handler that returns fmt.Errorf instead of ProblemError → middleware still emits problem+json + 500. Log: handler return + middleware emission.
3. **Real auth flow problem** — expired pairing token sent to /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer; response is 401 problem+json with the right type URI; renderer (hp-8dym) shows blocking modal. Log: full request → response → render chain.
4. **Real bead cycle problem** — POST /v1/projects/{id}/beads/{id}/deps with a cycle; response is 422 problem+json with cycle_path extension; renderer shows banner with the cycle. Log: full chain + UI render.
5. **Redaction in real path** — failed request with bearer token in error string; response has [REDACTED]; audit log retains the same redaction. Log: pre + post + audit.
6. **Diagnostics 'recent problems' table** — generate 10 distinct problems over 5 minutes; Diagnostics shows type_uri counts + correlation IDs. Log: diagnostics view dump.
7. **Cross-version compatibility** — daemon v0.5 emits a problem type renderer (v0.4 desktop) doesn't recognize; renderer falls back to severity-based routing per hp-8dym's compatibility behavior. Log: type unknown + fallback rendering.
8. **Schema-evolution test** — add new field to a problem type's extensions; older renderers ignore + render; newer renderers consume the new field. Log: pre/post extension fields + render outcomes.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — RFC 7807 mandate for all errors.
- §5.4 — redaction layer.
- §10 — audit log correlation IDs.
- hp-1ry — seed REST contract (consumer; every endpoint).
- hp-8dym — renderer error UX framework (CONSUMES this).
- hp-je1p — daemon redaction layer (used by problem detail/extensions).
- hp-r3i — packages/schemas (problem-types.yaml + Go/TS types).
- hp-g73 — audit log (correlation_id linkage).
- hp-6d7 — Diagnostics 'recent problems' table.
- hp-9xtt — daemon migration / schema evolution.
- hp-ngq — idempotency-key tests (pairs with this for retry semantics).
- hp-k4h — Phase 2.5 epic parent.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- packages/schemas/problem-types.yaml with ~150 entries (full coverage of §2.6 endpoints' error paths).
- Go ProblemError interface + standard error wrappers per subsystem (auth, beads, projects, plans, swarm, mail, health, reviews, tending, approvals).
- chi/echo middleware that converts ProblemError → problem+json.
- Default 500 mapping for plain errors.
- Redaction integration with hp-je1p.
- Correlation_id extension on every response.
- Surface + actionability hints per problem type.
- Auto-generated per-endpoint contract test.
- Diagnostics 'recent problems' panel.
- All 10 unit tests + 8 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.
- CI fails if a new endpoint adds an error path without a corresponding registry entry.

title: Cross-cutting: audit log infrastructure (~/.hoopoe/audit.jsonl + redaction)
description:
WHAT
====
Append-only audit log at ~/.hoopoe/audit.jsonl. Every meaningful daemon action writes an entry: actor, project, action, reason, command preview, result, artifact pointers. Each entry carries a monotonic seq number per project (not just time) so multi-process actors — tending jobs + user actions + adapter callbacks all writing concurrently — order deterministically under clock skew.

WHY THIS IS LOAD-BEARING (§1.4 'Every automation must be inspectable')
========================================================================
Hoopoe should never feel like hidden magic. Every meaningful automated action produces an event capturing who/what triggered it, which command or API call ran, why, what input scope, what output, and which artifact stores the evidence. This makes the tool trustworthy when it reopens a stalled bead, kills a wedged pane, force-releases a stale reservation, queues a build, or asks agents to re-read AGENTS.md. Without this, the cockpit can't be trusted with autopilot or automated tending.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.3 — destructive operations require audit
- §5.4 — daemon logs/audit pass through a redaction layer before persistence and streaming
- §8.3 — every tending tick writes audit entries even when wakeAgent:false or [SILENT]
- §8.3.1 — postcondition verifications recorded
- §10 — observability section header
- §10.1 — events carry causationId + correlationId
- §10.3 — audit retained indefinitely unless user exports/prunes

REDACTION LAYER (§5.4)
======================
- Bearer tokens, SSH passphrases, provider credentials, OPENAI/ANTHROPIC/GEMINI key-shaped strings (sk-*, ghp_*, AKIA*, BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY, xox*) are NEVER written to audit log unredacted.
- Patterns lifted from ru's secret-scan list (§17 reference).
- Redaction runs BEFORE persistence and BEFORE streaming to clients.
- Redaction is testable — fixture inputs assert no leakage in stored or streamed bytes.

CONTRACT
========
Each audit entry:
{
  seq: monotonic int per project,
  time: RFC3339,
  projectId: ULID | global,
  actor: {kind: user|tending_job|agent|repair_action|pre_script|adapter, id, runId?},
  action: typed action ID,
  reason: free text,
  commandPreview: redacted command or RPC call,
  result: success|failure|partial|approval_required,
  artifactRefs: [...],
  causationId: cmd_*,
  correlationId: swarm_*|trun_*|approval_*,
  approvalId?: appr_*
}

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- audit.jsonl writer is concurrency-safe (file lock + append-only, fsync on each entry).
- Per-project seq numbers are monotonic even under concurrent writes (single-writer or distributed counter).
- Redaction passes a fixture suite covering the lift list above plus payloads with embedded JSON containing keys.
- Schema-versioned and migrable per §10.3.
- Surfaced in Diagnostics (§10.2) recent-audit-events panel.
- Indexed for efficient queries by project / time-range / actor / action.
- Retention: indefinite unless exported (per §10.3).

title: Phase 2.5: Job registry + process manager invariants (§2.7)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the daemon's job registry per §2.7. One registry keyed by sortable IDs; one process group per child; bounded semaphores per resource; persistent logs ~/.hoopoe/logs/{jobId}.log; chunked log API; structured status; artifact registry; idempotency keys on every write endpoint.

REQUIRED INVARIANTS (§2.7)
============================
- ONE job registry keyed by sortable IDs (ULID). Jobs survive HTTP requests and desktop disconnects; interrupted jobs visible after daemon restart.
- ONE process group per child. Cancellation: SIGTERM → grace period → SIGKILL escalation. Long-running children must NOT become orphans.
- Bounded semaphores per resource: llm_calls, git_ops_per_project, br_ops_per_project, health_runs_per_project, swarm_spawns_global, terminal_streams_per_client.
- Persistent logs under ~/.hoopoe/logs/{jobId}.log (in-memory rings only as acceleration).
- Chunked log API with byte offsets, NOT 'one huge terminal string'. Terminal attach sends latest ring + current offset.
- Structured status: queued | running | waiting_approval | canceling | succeeded | failed | interrupted (stored in daemon SQLite).
- Artifact registry for plan outputs, conversion traces, health snapshots, review findings, bootstrap logs.
- Idempotency keys on every write endpoint that can be retried by reconnecting desktop.

BUILD/TEST QUEUE (§2.1, §8.5; this bead's substrate)
======================================================
The build/test queue is implemented ON TOP of this job substrate. It dedupes equivalent commands when safe, throttles by project, records exact command provenance for every result. Source of truth for test/build evidence used by landing, reviews, health snapshots, convergence.

Each result records (§2.7):
- project ID, worktree path, branch, commit SHA
- command, normalized argv, environment digest
- tool versions and runner profile
- lockfile/package manifest digest
- started/completed timestamps and duration
- stdout/stderr artifact IDs
- parsed test cases, failures, coverage files, exit code
- failure fingerprint
- cache key and cache-hit/cache-miss reason

Reuse a recent result only when commit + command + environment + declared inputs match. Repeated failure fingerprints create 'known failure' records. Suspected flakes marked separately and trigger a flake-hardening bead.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.7 — full spec.
- §8.5 — build/test contention as detection input for tend-swarm.
- §10.5 — 'Job cancellation: no orphan child process groups after cancel test.'

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Job registry persisted in daemon SQLite; survives daemon restart with jobs marked interrupted.
- Process group invariant: cancel test asserts no orphan children remain (matches §10.5 SLO).
- Semaphore tests: requesting more concurrent ops than the cap blocks new requests until others complete.
- Chunked log API: stream 100 MB of output, fetch arbitrary byte ranges with offset, latency p95 within target.
- Idempotency-key test: retry same write through simulated tunnel drop, get same result.
- docs/process-manager.md generated from invariants + tests.
close_reason: Implemented job registry and process manager restart invariants

title: Phase 11: Health tab UI (KPI cards, sortable file table, hotspot scoring, trend sparklines, create-bead-from-hotspot)
description:
WHAT
====
The Stage 04 Health tab UI per §7.4.1 'Where the metrics show up — Debugging / Hardening — Health tab'. KPI cards (written files, avg coverage, avg complexity, hotspot count, uncovered-files ratio, recent test failures); sortable file health table (path, LOC, complexity, coverage bar, churn, owner agent, linked bead, hotspot reasons); trend sparklines per metric; quick action 'create bead from hotspot.'

FILE HEALTH TABLE COLUMNS
============================
- path
- LOC
- complexity (cyclomatic)
- coverage (CoverageBar component)
- churn (last 7d / 30d)
- owner agent (last touched by which agent)
- linked bead (if files-touched record exists)
- hotspot reasons (badges: high complexity, low coverage, high churn, failed tests, near-bead)

HOTSPOT SCORING (§7.4.1)
==========================
Weighted sum of:
- high complexity
- low coverage
- high churn
- recent agent changes
- failed tests nearby
- review findings nearby
- critical-path bead linkage

Default thresholds: complexity ≥ 20, coverage < 60%. Configurable per project.

CREATE BEAD FROM HOTSPOT
==========================
Quick action: 'Create bead' from any hotspot. Pre-fills bead with: file path, complexity / coverage / churn metrics, hotspot reasons. User edits + creates via br adapter.

PER-BEAD 'FILES TOUCHED' ROLLUP (§7.4.1)
==========================================
Beads drawer (Phase 7) 'Files touched' section now shows: for each file the bead is expected to modify, current coverage + complexity + delta vs last snapshot once work begins.

PER-AGENT DELTA (§7.4.1)
==========================
Swarm agent tile (Phase 8) shows code-health-delta since the agent started its current bead — did they raise or lower coverage? add complexity?

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.4.1 — full Health tab spec.
- §7.6 — top-bar pill (separate task lights up).
- design/mockups/v1 — visual reference.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Health tab UI matches mockups.
- All KPI cards + table + sparklines work.
- Create bead from hotspot works end-to-end.
- Beads drawer + Swarm agent tile pick up health data.
- Phase 11 acceptance: health scan runs in worktree, top-bar pill updates, bead created from hotspot.

title: Phase 7: Kanban view (status columns, drag-to-claim disabled by default, filter chips, bulk select)
description:
WHAT
====
Beads stage Kanban view using BeadCard components. Columns by status (ready / claimed / in_progress / in_review / closed / blocked). Filters (priority, owner agent, plan section, recent activity, has-test-failures, etc.). Bulk select for batch operations.

DESIGN
======
- Columns scrollable horizontally; cards scrollable within.
- Card: ID, title, status pill, priority chip, owner agent, files-touched count, age.
- Filter chips at top: priority, owner agent, plan section, label, has-failure.
- Bulk select for: 'reopen all', 'mark in-review', 'change priority', 'unassign'.
- Drag-to-claim DISABLED by default — claiming a bead is an agent action; the user can override but it's an audited destructive class.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 'Views' — Kanban.
- design/mockups/v1/beads.jsx — visual reference.
- BeadCard component — design system.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Performance: 1,000 beads should load p95 < 1s (§10.5 SLO 'Bead Kanban load').
- Virtualization for column scroll (react-window or TanStack Virtual).
- Filtering is client-side over the cached bead list (TanStack Query keyed by /v1/projects/{id}/beads).
- WebSocket events update bead state in place; cards animate state transitions for clarity.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Kanban renders bead status columns.
- 1,000-bead test passes SLO (p95 < 1s).
- Filter chips work; bulk select wires to PATCH /v1/projects/{id}/beads/{beadId}.
- Drag-to-claim disabled by default, configurable per project.
- WS-driven updates animate without page reload.

title: Phase 12: Specialized audits catalog (§9.5 mock-code, deadlock, security, perf, fuzzing, reasoning, golden-artifact, e2e, UI polish)
description:
WHAT
====
Specialized audits catalog per §9.5. When review saturation is reached but the user wants further hardening, Hoopoe offers targeted skills/workflows. Each audit creates beads (not free-floating todos) and stamps each finding with source skill so post-audit triage can see 'the deadlock skill produced 12 findings, 8 were duplicates of UBS findings already filed.'

CATALOG (§9.5)
================
- UBS re-run with stricter rules or scoped to recently-changed files
- Mock-code finder
- Deadlock / concurrency finder
- Security audit for SaaS
- Performance profiling
- Project reality check
- Reasoning-mode analysis
- Golden artifact testing
- Fuzzing
- E2E testing with logging / no mocks
- UI polish review

INTEGRATION
=============
- Each audit is a delegated-agent review (§9.2.1) using a specific skill (loaded via jsm/jfp).
- Findings flow into the §9.3 ledger with source: skill:<name> stamped.
- Cross-tool dedupe handles overlap with UBS (Round 0/5).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.4.2 — specialized skills layer on top of UBS.
- §9.5 — catalog.
- §17 — UBS + specialized skills references.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Each audit in catalog is a registered skill + delegated-agent runner.
- Findings stamped with source skill.
- Cross-tool dedupe surfaces overlap.
- Specialized-audit picker UI in Review tab.

title: Phase 9 / §7.5: macOS Notification Center + dock unread badge integration for urgent events when Hoopoe is unfocused (rate-limit, swarm halt, approval requested, plan round complete, build failure, urgent mail) with quiet-hours + per-category opt-out + audit
description:
WHAT
====
§7.5's Activity panel emits 'urgent alerts' as a category. §7.3 surfaces rate-limit events; §8.4 `watch-safety-thresholds` halts swarms; §5.3 produces approval requests; §7.4 emits health snapshots and review findings. When Hoopoe is unfocused, minimized, or backgrounded, the user *currently* has no signal that any of these happened — they must surface the app and click into Activity to discover it.

This bead adds **macOS Notification Center delivery + dock-icon unread-badge updates** for urgent events, with per-category user control, quiet-hours, and an audit trail.

WHY THIS IS A FOCUSED BEAD
==========================
Existing coverage:
- **hp-1r4** — Activity drawer UI 'persistent unread badge, filterable timeline'. The unread badge is *inside* the app, on the Activity panel toggle. Not the dock.
- **hp-v6n** — Phase 9 Activity panel + Agent Mail epic.
- **hp-3se** — Agent Mail ingestion produces urgent events.
- **hp-tmo7** — auth-rotation forced re-pair (one-off urgent surface).

Nothing wires urgent Activity events into the **OS-level surface** (Notification Center + dock). For a desktop cockpit of a long-running operation, OS-level notifications are essential to user trust ('did the swarm halt while I was at lunch?').

NOTIFICATION CATEGORIES
=======================
Each category is opt-out individually in Settings (hp-wg5p). Defaults reflect 'urgency you'd want to know without unlocking your laptop':

| Category                     | Default | Trigger                                                 | Body fields                                            | Action buttons              |
|------------------------------|---------|----------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| swarm.halted                 | ON      | watch-safety-thresholds halts swarm; user-initiated halt | project, reason, agent_count                           | View, Resume, Dismiss       |
| approval.requested.destructive | ON    | Hoopoe-policy + DCG + SLB urgent approvals               | project, action, requester, expiry                     | Review, Dismiss             |
| rate_limit.subscription_exhausted | ON | caut crosses hard threshold OR provider returns 429    | project, provider, percent_used                        | Switch account, Pause, Dismiss |
| plan.round_complete          | OFF     | refinement-round-N completed                              | plan, round_id, indicator (substantive/light/marginal) | Open plan, Dismiss          |
| build.test_failed            | OFF     | build-queue records new failure-fingerprint              | project, command, fingerprint, count                   | View, Dismiss               |
| mail.urgent                  | ON      | Agent Mail urgent flag                                   | project, sender, subject                               | View thread, Dismiss        |
| pane.wedged                  | ON      | tend-swarm detects wedged + kill_wedged_process fires    | project, agent, bead, reason                           | View, Pause swarm, Dismiss  |
| daemon.connection_lost       | ON      | Tunnel down + reconnect failure beyond grace             | host, last_event_seq                                   | Diagnostics, Dismiss        |
| project.switched             | OFF     | (mostly informational; default off)                      | from, to                                               | Dismiss                     |

QUIET HOURS
===========
Per-day schedule (default off, configurable in Settings): e.g., 22:00-07:00 → only `approval.requested.destructive` and `daemon.connection_lost` fire; others are queued and delivered as a digest 'You missed N events overnight' on next focus.

DOCK BADGE
==========
Counts **unacknowledged urgent events** for the active project. Resets when:
- User clicks the Activity panel toggle (badge already in hp-1r4 → propagate to dock via main-process IPC),
- User opens Hoopoe from the dock (`activate` event from `app.on('activate')`),
- User explicitly clicks 'Mark all read' in Activity panel.
The dock-badge count is the **sum across all projects**; clicking the dock surfaces the app focused on the project with the highest urgent count.

ELECTRON IPC + IMPLEMENTATION
==============================
- IPC handler `notifications.dispatch` in main process:
  ```ts
  notifications.dispatch({
    category: 'swarm.halted',
    title: 'Hoopoe — Swarm halted in {project}',
    body: '{reason} (auto-recovery in {N}s)',
    actions: [{ id: 'view', label: 'View' }, { id: 'resume', label: 'Resume' }],
    correlationId: 'evt_…'
  })
  ```
- Uses `new Notification` (Electron renderer-side), but only main process invokes it via IPC, so renderer can't fire arbitrary notifications.
- Click on notification routes to the relevant deep link (project + stage + drawer).
- macOS-only for v1 — Linux/Windows use `Notification` API as a graceful fallback when the lift's CI matrix re-enables those targets later.
- When user has 'Do Not Disturb' on, system suppresses delivery; we still write audit + still increment badge.

AUDIT POSTURE
=============
Every dispatched notification writes an audit entry: `{actor: 'system', action: 'notification.dispatched', category, project, correlationId, suppressed_by_quiet_hours: bool, suppressed_by_user_setting: bool, focused_at_dispatch: bool, click_action_taken?: 'view'|'resume'|'dismiss'|null}`. Click-action gets a follow-up audit entry tying the click to the source event via correlationId.

UNIT TESTS (10) — `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/notifications.test.ts`
=============================================================================
1. **Category opt-out respected** — user disables 'plan.round_complete'; dispatch with that category → no native notification fired; badge not incremented; structured log `notification.suppressed_by_setting`.
2. **Quiet hours filtering** — clock=23:00, schedule 22-07; `mail.urgent` is suppressed (queued); `approval.requested.destructive` is delivered.
3. **Quiet-hours digest** — at 07:01 the digest fires; payload includes the queued event count; structured log `notification.digest_delivered` with `queued_count`.
4. **Dock badge increment + reset** — dispatch increments badge; user clicks Activity panel toggle → badge resets via IPC; structured log `dock_badge.reset`.
5. **Cross-project badge sum** — Project A has 2 urgent, Project B has 1 → dock badge = 3; switching to A does NOT reset (Project A is just active); explicit 'mark all read' resets to 0.
6. **Click routing** — notification click with action='view' on swarm.halted in project X → IPC navigates to /projects/X/swarm; structured log `notification.click_routed` with target route.
7. **Action-button result audit** — notification dispatched + clicked 'Resume' → click-action audit entry written linked via correlationId to the source.
8. **Renderer-can't-fire-arbitrary** — call `new Notification()` directly from renderer (without IPC) → fails (CSP / preload blocks); main process unaffected.
9. **DND state read** — when macOS DND is on (stub system query), notification is logged as 'os_suppressed: true' in audit but Hoopoe still increments badge.
10. **Focused-at-dispatch suppress** — when app window is focused AND active project matches event project, the notification is NOT dispatched (in-app Activity already shows it); badge still increments; structured log `notification.suppressed_focused`.

E2E TESTS (6) — `apps/desktop/e2e/notifications.e2e.ts`
=========================================================
1. **Real macOS notification delivery** — Hoopoe in background; trigger swarm-halt fixture event → macOS Notification Center receives a notification with title 'Hoopoe — Swarm halted in fixture-A'; click → app focuses, route correct.
2. **Per-category disable workflow** — open Settings; disable 'rate_limit.subscription_exhausted'; trigger that event → no notification; re-enable → next event fires.
3. **Quiet hours flow** — set quiet hours via Settings UI; advance system clock to a quiet-hours window via fixture clock; fire 3 events; advance to outside-window; digest delivers with body matching '3 missed events.'
4. **Dock badge sync** — verify dock badge updates correctly across multiple unread events; CI/Playwright reads NSDockTile via OS shell scripting.
5. **Approval-notification end-to-end** — agent ActionPlan kicks off destructive action requiring approval (fixture); notification fires; click 'Review' → app routes to /approvals/{id}; user approves; second notification 'Approval granted' (default off, so verify suppression with default settings).
6. **Audit-trail correlation** — Notification dispatched, clicked, action-taken; navigate to Audit log explorer (hp-1wg8); confirm correlationId chain links source event → notification dispatched → notification clicked → resulting action.

DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (per hp-lxs)
==========================================
- `notification.dispatched` { category, project_id, correlation_id, suppressed_by_quiet_hours, suppressed_by_user_setting, suppressed_focused, os_dnd_suppressed }
- `notification.click_routed` { category, action_id, route_target, correlation_id }
- `notification.digest_delivered` { queued_count, oldest_event_age_s, queued_categories (set) }
- `dock_badge.set` { count, source_categories (set) }
- `dock_badge.reset` { reason (activity_panel_open | mark_all_read | app_activate), prev_count }

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.5 Activity panel — urgent alerts category
- §7.6 Top bar — Activity-panel toggle with unread count (in-app counterpart)
- §1.4 'Every automation must be inspectable' — notifications are surfaces for automation outcomes
- §10 audit log + hp-1wg8 audit explorer
- hp-1r4 (Activity drawer with unread badge), hp-3se (Agent Mail urgent ingestion), hp-tmo7 (urgent re-pair flow), hp-zir (main.ts decompose; NotificationManager goes in apps/desktop/src/main/), hp-wg5p (Settings UI for category toggles + quiet hours), hp-r3i (notification payload schema)

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- NotificationManager class in apps/desktop/src/main/ with IPC `notifications.dispatch`
- 9 categories implemented with default-on/off matrix
- Per-category opt-out + quiet-hours UI in Settings
- Dock-badge IPC integrated with Activity panel
- macOS Notification Center happy-path + DND handling
- 10 unit + 6 e2e tests passing
- Structured logs for 5 events
- Audit entries for every dispatch and click
- Documentation: 'Hoopoe pings you when something needs attention; quiet otherwise.'

title: Phase 5 / §7.1: Plan refinement-round diff viewer (per-round semantic diff + quality-dimension delta + 'meaningful changes' indicator) for the during-pipeline refinement-round-N → refinement-round-N+1 comparison
description:
WHAT
====
§7.1 says: 'The user can stop after any round, run extra rounds, or jump straight to lock if a round produces no meaningful changes.' That choice is impossible to make well without a diff between consecutive refinement rounds. This bead implements the **per-round refinement diff viewer** so the user can see exactly what changed between `refinement-round-N.md` and `refinement-round-N+1.md` (and also vs the synthesis baseline) and judge whether to continue.

WHY THIS IS A FOCUSED BEAD
==========================
Existing coverage:
- **hp-x3z** covers plan **locking** + **post-lock amendment** diff (between locked plan version v1 and v2 after the user re-amends). That is a different surface — it operates on amended *versions* of a *locked* plan, not on the rounds *during* the planning pipeline.
- **hp-8ec** is the plan editor + side-by-side comparative-matrix view (for the *candidates → matrix* step of §7.1). That's the candidate-comparison view, not the round-to-round refinement diff.
- **hp-al4** owns the planning pipeline end-to-end (candidates → synthesis → critique → refinement rounds → lock); it produces the artifacts but not the diff UI.
- **hp-s00** is the plan quality tracker (intent / architecture / workflow / implementation / testing / risk / bead-readiness). It records dimension scores per round but does not render a diff.

The gap: while §7.1's pipeline DAG covers `refinement_round_1 → … → refinement_round_5 → lock_or_continue`, the *human* decision-point at `lock_or_continue` requires a per-round diff that today no bead owns. Without it the user is reading two markdown blobs side-by-side from disk.

DIFF DIMENSIONS
===============
Two diff axes layered together:

1. **Textual diff** — Standard Myers-style line diff between rounds N and N+1, per file: `refinement-round-001.md` vs `refinement-round-002.md`. Rendered with CodeMirror 6 diff extension (already vendored from t3code → hp-8ec). Color-coded by type: addition (cream-green), deletion (cream-red), unchanged (muted), moved-block (cream-blue with arrow).

2. **Semantic diff** — Per plan-section heading (`#`/`##`/`###` parse), summarize round-over-round:
   - **Section added** (e.g., '## 7.5 Activity panel' newly introduced at round 3)
   - **Section removed**
   - **Section grew** (>20% larger) / **shrank** (>20% smaller)
   - **Section reworded substantially** (`difflib.SequenceMatcher` ratio <0.7) without adding/removing
   - **Section unchanged**
   - **Decision flipped** (an explicit decision flagged with the planning template's `> Decision:` syntax changed)
   - **Risk added / closed**

3. **Quality-dimension delta** — Pulled from hp-s00's per-round scores. Surface as small bar deltas next to the round-N+1 header: 'intent +0.2, architecture +0.1, testing -0.05, …'.

VIEW LAYOUT
===========
Plan workspace gains a new sub-route: `/projects/{id}/plans/{planId}/rounds`. Three columns:

1. **Round list (left, 240px)** — `candidate_chatgpt_pro → comparative_matrix → synthesis → fresh_eyes_critique → refinement_round_1 → … → refinement_round_5`. Each row has the round name, model, completed-at, and a 'meaningful changes' indicator (green = substantive, yellow = light edits, grey = essentially identical). Click selects the round.

2. **Diff pane (center, flexible)** — Side-by-side or unified textual diff (toggle). When semantic diff is active, sections are collapsible; an 'Outline' rail in the column gutter shows the section tree of round N+1, color-coded by change type.

3. **Right rail (320px)** —
   - 'Round summary': model used, harness (Claude Code / Codex CLI / Gemini CLI / Oracle), CAAM account, elapsed time, cost-class summary (caut subscription window).
   - Quality-dimension deltas: bar chart with 7 dimensions, before/after.
   - 'Decision changes' list: any `> Decision:` flips between rounds.
   - Action: 'Run another round' / 'Lock this round'.

The 'meaningful changes' indicator uses a heuristic combining textual delta size, number of sections changed, and quality-dimension max-delta:
- **Substantive (green)**: >50 lines changed OR ≥1 section added/removed OR any quality dimension changed by >0.1.
- **Light (yellow)**: 10-50 lines changed AND no section structural changes.
- **Marginal (grey)**: <10 lines changed AND no quality-dimension change >0.05 — STRONG hint to lock.

Once 'marginal' fires twice in a row, the lock_or_continue panel surfaces a default suggestion: 'Round N produced negligible change vs N-1 and N-2. Lock?' — per agent-flywheel guidance ('the suggestions become incremental').

ARTIFACT-LEVEL VS RENDER-TIME
=============================
The diff is **rendered live, not pre-computed**, so no new artifact is written. `.hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/round-summary.json` is updated by the planning pipeline (hp-al4) with the indicator + dimension deltas; the textual diff is computed on view-open against the two source markdown files. This keeps the artifact set in §7.1 unchanged.

When the *renderer* compares two round files, it does so via daemon RPCs (fetch + diff) so the local clone stays a strict origin mirror (§1.7). New endpoint `GET /v1/projects/{id}/plans/{planId}/rounds/{roundId}/diff?vs={baseRoundId}&granularity={textual|semantic|both}`.

UNIT TESTS (10) — `apps/desktop/src/planning/__tests__/refinementDiff.test.ts`
=================================================================================
1. **Textual diff correctness** — fixture pair (round-001 + round-002 with known edits) produces expected hunk count + line-level addition/deletion counts.
2. **Semantic-diff section detection** — Markdown with sections `## A`, `## B`, `### B.1`, `## C` parsed; round N+1 with `## A`, `## B` (modified), `## D` produces `{section_added: ['D'], section_removed: ['C'], section_modified: ['B'], section_unchanged: ['A']}`.
3. **'Substantive' threshold** — exactly 51 lines changed, no section change, no dimension delta > 0.1 → indicator='substantive' (line-count branch wins).
4. **'Marginal' threshold** — 8 lines changed, max dimension delta 0.04 → 'marginal'; with sec dimension delta 0.06 → 'light'.
5. **Decision-flip detection** — a `> Decision: Use Postgres` in round N becomes `> Decision: Use SQLite` in round N+1 → `decisions_flipped: [{ section: 'X', from: 'Use Postgres', to: 'Use SQLite' }]` returned.
6. **Quality-dimension delta** — `round-summary.json` round 1 has `{intent: 0.7, …}` and round 2 has `{intent: 0.8, …}` → dimension delta returns `{intent: +0.1, …}` mapped to bar-chart props.
7. **Empty-round handling** — round N+1 file is empty (failed run) → diff renderer returns `status: 'skipped', reason: 'round_failed'` and the indicator is rendered greyed-out with a 'failed' badge.
8. **Same-round edge case** — vs is itself the same round → diff returns `status: 'identity'` (handled gracefully by the daemon, not a server error).
9. **Large round handling** — round files >1MB each diff in <800ms (acceptable for planning); structured log captures `refinement_diff.compute_ms`.
10. **Outline rail color-coding** — given multi-section semantic-diff result, outline rail props match (additions=green, removals=red, modifications=yellow, unchanged=grey) per design tokens.

E2E TESTS (7) — `apps/desktop/e2e/refinementDiff.e2e.ts`
=========================================================
1. **Real refinement run** — Start fixture plan with rough idea + 2 candidates → run synthesis + critique + 3 refinement rounds → navigate to /rounds → all rounds visible in left list with indicators set per the artifacts.
2. **Substantive vs marginal across rounds** — Round 1→2 fixture is substantive (large architectural addition); 2→3 is light (wording polish); 3→4 is marginal (typo); UI shows green / yellow / grey appropriately.
3. **Two-marginal-in-a-row prompt** — After round 4 marginal, round 5 also marginal → 'Lock?' suggestion banner appears at top of /rounds; clicking 'Lock' transitions to lock flow (hp-x3z).
4. **Decision-flip surfacing** — Fixture has decision flip in round 3 → /rounds detail rail shows it; click drills into the section in the diff pane with the relevant lines highlighted.
5. **Switch between textual and semantic diff** — Toggle 'Textual' to 'Semantic' to 'Both'; the right rail and outline update; structured log captures `refinement_diff.granularity_changed`.
6. **Outline scroll-sync** — Click an outline entry → main diff pane scrolls to that section; vice versa (scroll diff → outline highlights current section).
7. **Quality-dimension delta from caut + per-round-summary.json** — Complete a real fixture round → round-summary.json updated → /rounds dimension delta bar chart updates within 1s.

DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (per hp-lxs)
==========================================
- `refinement_diff.opened` { plan_id, base_round_id, target_round_id, granularity }
- `refinement_diff.compute_ms` { plan_id, base_round_id, target_round_id, line_count_added, line_count_removed, sections_added, sections_removed, sections_modified, compute_ms }
- `refinement_diff.indicator_set` { plan_id, round_id, indicator (substantive|light|marginal|skipped), reason }
- `refinement_diff.granularity_changed` { plan_id, from, to }
- `refinement_diff.lock_suggested` { plan_id, after_round_id, marginal_streak } — fires when 2-marginal-in-a-row triggers lock prompt

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 'Pipeline (from rough idea)' — refinement_round_1 → … → refinement_round_5 → lock_or_continue
- §7.1 'Quality dimensions' — drives the dimension delta bar chart
- §7.1 'Locking' — 'Run another round' / 'Lock this round' actions integrate with hp-x3z
- §1.5 'Build for restartability' — diff is rendered live, never written as a fragile artifact
- design/mockups/v1/wizard.jsx + plan workspace mocks
- hp-al4 (planning pipeline writes the input artifacts), hp-8ec (plan editor + comparative-matrix view), hp-s00 (quality tracker), hp-x3z (lock + post-lock amendment), hp-r3i (schemas)

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- /rounds sub-route renders three-column layout
- Textual diff via CodeMirror 6 diff extension
- Semantic diff with section-level change taxonomy
- Quality-dimension delta bar chart wired to round-summary.json
- 'Meaningful changes' indicator with substantive / light / marginal logic
- 'Marginal x2' lock suggestion banner
- Daemon endpoint `GET /…/rounds/{roundId}/diff`
- 10 unit tests + 7 e2e tests passing
- Structured logging for all 5 events
- Documentation: 'When refinement rounds get marginal, Hoopoe nudges you to lock.'

title: Phase 1: Build reusable component set (StageHeader, AgentTile, BeadCard, StatusPill, PriorityChip, CoverageBar, TerminalPane, TimelineRow, HealthKpiCard, ApprovalDialog)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the reusable component library in packages/design-system/components/, each with Storybook stories covering all relevant states. These are the visual primitives every later stage composes.

REQUIRED COMPONENTS
====================
1. **StageHeader** — STAGE N — VERB framing; breadcrumb; right-side action slot.
2. **AgentTile** — harness icon (Claude Code / Codex / Gemini), CAAM account label, current bead claim, status pill, time-on-bead, recent decisions list (max ~3 items). NO terminal output (§7.3 'No-terminal default UX').
3. **BeadCard** — used in both Kanban and DAG. Shows bead ID, title, status pill, priority chip, owner agent, files-touched count, age. Compact variant for DAG nodes; expanded variant for Kanban columns.
4. **StatusPill** — covers all status states from the design tokens task. Variant prop `kind: 'bead'|'job'|'approval'|'capability'|'tool'`. Default size + small variant.
5. **PriorityChip** — P0–P4 with the color ramp from tokens.
6. **CoverageBar** — horizontal bar with percentage label; color shifts via the coverage ramp tokens; gridlines at threshold values.
7. **TerminalPane** — wraps xterm.js. USED ONLY in Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' debug toggle (§7.3, Appendix C #12); explicitly NOT a default Swarm UI surface. Component should make this constraint visible (label, audit-on-toggle hook).
8. **TimelineRow** — Activity panel item: timestamp, actor avatar/icon, event-type label, summary text, optional pill row, optional inline preview, click target. Multiple variants per event type (mail, reservation, build, approval, audit, agent-decision, user-message, orchestrator-reply).
9. **HealthKpiCard** — title, big number, delta indicator, sparkline placeholder.
10. **ApprovalDialog** — modal with: requested action summary, source rule (Hoopoe-policy / DCG / SLB), evidence chips, scope picker (once / this bead / this swarm / this project session), expiry, risk-class label, decision actor input, note textarea, [Approve] / [Deny] / [Extend] buttons. Used by the unified approvals queue epic.
11. **CommandPalette (⌘K)** — backed by the IpcRegistry command registry from the main.ts decomposition; fuzzy-search over registered commands.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- design/mockups/v1/* — visual reference.
- §7.3 — agent tile visual design.
- §7.5 — Activity panel timeline.
- §7.6 — top bar pills (separate components; this bead doesn't include the pill bar wiring, just the inputs needed for it).
- Design tokens bead — depends on tokens being in place.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Every component is keyboard-navigable and AA-contrast on every state.
- Avoid `any` types; props are fully typed with TypeScript.
- Components compose via children where possible; avoid huge prop matrices.
- TerminalPane wraps xterm.js — keep the dependency isolated in this one component so the rest of the renderer doesn't pull xterm into its bundle.
- ApprovalDialog must NOT execute the action; it only collects decision + note. Execution happens elsewhere (typed action executor, §8.3.1).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All components above implemented in packages/design-system/components/.
- Storybook stories covering every state variant.
- Unit tests for components with logic (CommandPalette fuzzy match, TerminalPane attach/detach, ApprovalDialog scope/expiry).
- Used in at least one stage (will mostly be empty wiring at Phase 1 exit; full wiring in later phases).
close_reason: Added story coverage for the remaining design-system primitives; component models and tests already landed

title: Phase 8: CAAM + caut adapters (account inventory, switch, subscription-quota usage)
description:
WHAT
====
CAAM (Cross-Agent Account Manager) + caut (coding agent usage tracker) adapters. CAAM is the SOLE credential pathway; caut is the SOLE source of subscription-window usage telemetry.

CAAM (§5.1, §17)
==================
- accounts.list — inventory of provider accounts.
- account.status — auth state per account.
- account.switch — rotate an agent to a different account (recovery action).
- Surfaces in: tool inventory (Phase 3), composition picker (Phase 8), tend-swarm rate-limit recovery (Phase 10), top-bar subscription pill.

CAUT (§5.1, §17, §7.6)
========================
- usage.snapshot — per-provider subscription-window usage (Claude Max, GPT Pro, Gemini Ultra, ChatGPT Pro).
- Per-CLI status messages.
- Backs the top-bar subscription-usage pill (§7.6) and the watch-safety-thresholds tending job (§8.4).
- IF caut is unavailable for a provider, UI labels the cell 'unmeasured' rather than fabricating a number.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.1 — secrets boundary.
- §7.3 — subscription-quota and rate-limit guardrails.
- §7.6 — top-bar pill.
- §13 — subscription-only.
- §8.4 watch-safety-thresholds — uses caut.

CAPABILITY IDS (§2.8)
======================
- caam.accounts.list / account.switch
- caut.usage.snapshot

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Both adapters wrapped + tested against research-spike VPS.
- Capability registry reports caam.* + caut.* correctly.
- Top-bar pill (Phase 4 seed) gets full data here.
- Composition picker reads CAAM account counts.
- 'unmeasured' fallback when caut is silent.
- Adapter contract tests pass.

title: Phase 8 / §7.3 / §10: Pane snapshot capture into audit log on agent.kill_wedged_process action (post-mortem evidence)
description:
WHAT
====
When the daemon executes an agent.kill_wedged_process action (per §8.3.1's typed ActionPlan + §7.3 'Post-mortem investigation tools may capture pane snapshots into the audit log when the pt-driven kill action fires'), the executor MUST capture the killed agent's pane scrollback ring buffer + structured agent state INTO an audit-log artifact BEFORE the kill, so post-mortem investigation has the evidence the action eliminated. This bead implements that capture pipeline.

WHY (§7.3 'Post-mortem investigation tools', §10 audit, §8.3.1 typed actions)
==============================================================================
§7.3 explicitly mentions: 'Post-mortem investigation tools may capture pane snapshots into the audit log when the pt-driven kill action fires.' Without this:
- The kill destroys the evidence that justified it.
- Future debugging of 'why was agent X killed?' has no access to the agent's last words.
- watch-safety-thresholds tending job (§8.4) and tend-swarm 'kill_wedged_process' decisions become unreviewable — the user cannot tell if a kill was warranted.
- Postcondition verification (§8.3.1 'pre-condition: agent still running'; 'postcondition: NTM no longer reports the process') has no input for the 'why was this state present' question.

ARCHITECTURE
=============

CAPTURE PIPELINE (BEFORE KILL)
-------------------------------
The action executor (§8.3.1, hp-209) wraps agent.kill_wedged_process with a pre-execution snapshot:
1. **Pane scrollback** — read the agent's pane ring buffer (latest N=5000 lines) via NTMAdapter.captureRing(agentId). Persist as artifact ~/.hoopoe/audit/artifacts/kill-snapshot/{killActionId}/pane-scrollback.txt.
2. **NTM session snapshot** — ntm --robot-snapshot for the agent's session: pane state, last activity time, last input received. Persist as pane-meta.json.
3. **Agent state from canonical sources** — current bead claim (br get), file reservations (Agent Mail), recent commits (git log on its branch), build queue last-result for its commands. Persist as agent-state.json.
4. **Subscription state** — caut snapshot for the agent's CAAM account (was it rate-limited? near quota? recent error rate?). Persist as subscription-state.json.
5. **Pre-script detection chain** — the tend-swarm or watch-safety-thresholds detection that led to this kill action; the full pre-script payload + the agent's ActionPlan if applicable. Persist as detection-chain.json.

ARTIFACT STORE LAYOUT
----------------------
~/.hoopoe/audit/artifacts/kill-snapshot/{killActionId}/
  manifest.json         # links to all artifacts + their sha256 hashes + timestamps
  pane-scrollback.txt   # raw bytes
  pane-scrollback.ansi  # original ANSI (rendering-faithful for forensics)
  pane-meta.json        # ntm session snapshot
  agent-state.json      # canonical-state snapshot
  subscription-state.json
  detection-chain.json
  audit-correlation.json   # links: kill action ID → audit event seq → correlation/causation IDs

ATOMIC SEQUENCING
------------------
The capture MUST complete (or at least its critical fields written + flushed) BEFORE the kill is dispatched. Order:
1. Capture artifacts → flush.
2. Write 'kill_snapshot_captured' audit event with manifest link.
3. Dispatch the kill (pt or NTM kill).
4. Verify postcondition (agent process gone).
5. Write 'agent_kill_executed' audit event with snapshot ref + postcondition outcome.

If step 1 fails (NTM unreachable, disk full):
- snapshot-capture-failure audit event written.
- Action proceeds anyway with status='snapshot_failed' on the audit event so the user sees the kill happened blind.
- A follow-up detection emitted to tend-swarm so the issue gets visibility.

FAILURE-MODE GUARANTEES
========================
- Disk-full at capture time → degraded artifact (capture only the highest-priority items: pane scrollback + agent-state.json); record 'partial_capture' marker.
- NTM down → skip pane-meta.json; capture what's available; mark partial_capture.
- Capture timeout (5s default) → write what's already captured; emit 'capture_timed_out'; proceed.

UI / DIAGNOSTICS
=================
- Audit log entry for 'agent_kill_executed' has 'View kill snapshot' link.
- Diagnostics 'kill-snapshot viewer': renders pane-scrollback.ansi with terminal colors (xterm.js, read-only); shows agent-state.json + detection-chain.json side-by-side.
- 'Open in editor' link to the artifact directory.

PRIVACY / RETENTION
====================
- Snapshot artifacts pass through the same redaction layer (hp-je1p) as audit log entries — bearer tokens, model API keys, SSH passphrases, paths under user home all redacted.
- Default retention: 30 days (configurable per project under model-context-policy.json).
- 'Export kill snapshot' bundles the directory into a tar.zst for sharing externally (with the same redactions applied).

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Pane scrollback capture** — mock NTM with a pane that has 10000 lines of scrollback; capture pulls latest 5000 lines; persists as plain text + ansi variants. Log: line counts, file sizes, sha256.
2. **Agent state snapshot** — mock canonical-source clients (br, Agent Mail, git, build queue); capture builds agent-state.json with all four sections; schema-validate. Log: per-source returned data + composed state.
3. **Capture before kill ordering** — mock kill executor; capture writes artifact then writes audit then dispatches kill; assert order via timestamps. Log: per-step timestamps.
4. **Snapshot failure path** — mock NTM unreachable; capture proceeds with NTM section skipped; partial_capture marker present. Log: failure detection + partial-capture record.
5. **Disk full handling** — mock disk full at write step 3; capture writes high-priority items only; degraded marker on manifest. Log: write attempts, success/fail per item.
6. **Capture timeout** — mock pane scrollback read takes 8s (over 5s default); capture writes what's available + emits 'capture_timed_out'. Log: timeout event + recovery.
7. **Redaction applied** — pane scrollback contains 'BEARER abc123def...'; persisted file has '[REDACTED]'; original ANSI variant ALSO redacted. Log: pre/post redaction.
8. **Manifest hashing** — manifest.json contains sha256 of every artifact; tampering with an artifact triggers hash-mismatch audit event. Log: manifest dump + tamper test.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Wedged-agent kill end-to-end** — fixture: agent stuck on bead B-142 with 0 output for 15 min + reservation expired; tend-swarm proposes agent.kill_wedged_process; executor captures snapshot; kills; verifies NTM no longer reports process; audit chain has snapshot ref + postcondition outcome. Log file: e2e-killsnapshot-{ts}.jsonl with full timeline + capture artifacts paths.
2. **watch-safety-thresholds budget-breach kill** — fixture: agent in pathological loop spending budget; watch-safety-thresholds emits typed deterministic kill intent (pre_script actor); same capture pipeline runs; audit shows pre_script:watch-safety-thresholds + snapshot ref. Log: full chain.
3. **Capture failure resilience** — fixture: NTM down at the moment of kill; capture skips NTM section; partial_capture marker; kill proceeds; postcondition verification still fires. Log: NTM down injection + capture outcome + kill outcome.
4. **Diagnostics kill-snapshot viewer** — open Diagnostics; navigate to kill snapshot from #1; viewer renders pane-scrollback.ansi with colors; agent-state.json sidebar populated. Log: viewer state.
5. **Export bundle** — click 'Export kill snapshot'; tar.zst created; verify archive contents + redactions preserved + sha256 manifest. Log: export archive listing + redaction verification.
6. **Retention** — run kill capture, advance time 31 days, run retention sweep; snapshot artifacts deleted; manifest record retained for 1 year (linked from audit log forever). Log: pre/post sweep state.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 — 'Post-mortem investigation tools may capture pane snapshots'.
- §8.3.1 — typed action executor + postcondition verification.
- §10 — audit log artifact registry.
- §10.3 — retention.
- hp-209 — agent runtime + ActionPlan executor (this bead extends).
- hp-yf9 — daemon-side PTY plumbing + 'Show raw pane' (capture source).
- hp-g1j — NTM adapter (snapshot source).
- hp-g73 — audit log infrastructure.
- hp-je1p — redaction layer.
- hp-6d7 — Diagnostics screen.
- hp-6bj — pt / srp / sbh adapters (kill executor).
- hp-fb0 — tend-swarm + watch-safety-thresholds (action sources).
- hp-o42 — backup/export/retention.
- hp-m9w — Phase 8 epic parent.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Capture pipeline implemented (pane scrollback + ANSI variant + meta + agent-state + subscription + detection-chain + manifest with sha256).
- Atomic ordering (capture → audit → kill → postcondition → audit) verified.
- Failure-mode degraded paths (NTM down, disk full, timeout) tested.
- Redaction integration.
- Manifest hash verification.
- Diagnostics kill-snapshot viewer with ANSI rendering.
- Export bundle action.
- Retention sweep.
- All 8 unit tests + 6 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.

title: wakeAgent:false / [SILENT] healthy-hour cost+noise instrumentation — assert §8.6 invariants (zero LLM cost, zero panel noise on healthy hour)
description:
WHAT
====
§8.6 invariant: 'a healthy hour of swarm operation produces near-zero LLM cost (wakeAgent:false on every pre-script) and near-zero panel noise ([SILENT] on agent runs that decide no action is needed).' This bead is the test infrastructure that makes that invariant measurable and enforceable, not just aspirational.

WHY THIS MATTERS
==================
The §8 tending architecture's core economic argument is that a healthy swarm costs ~zero LLM tokens because deterministic pre-scripts shortcut most ticks. If the wakeAgent:false rate drops below threshold (e.g., a regression makes tend-swarm always wake), the user pays unbounded subscription quota for nothing. Making this MEASURABLE in CI catches the regression before the user experiences a quota burn.

§8.6 says it. §8.7 calls cost ceilings 'ultimately set by the user' but only if the deterministic floor holds. This bead enforces the floor.

INSTRUMENTATION
================
The scheduler (hp-5s4) records per-tick:
- jobId
- tickAt
- preScriptDurationMs
- preScriptOutcome: 'wakeAgent:false' | 'wakeAgent:true' | 'deterministic-action' | 'error'
- agentRunOutcome (when wakeAgent:true): 'silent' | 'spoke' | 'action-plan' | 'timeout' | 'error'
- approvalsRequested
- approvalsGranted
- tokensConsumed (from CLI/Oracle output)

These rows persist in daemon SQLite under `scheduler_metrics` per §8.3.2.

ASSERTIONS (over a 'healthy hour' fixture)
============================================

**Healthy-hour fixture definition** (extends §8.8 'healthy hour'):
- 60 minutes of mocked canonical state with no detections.
- All 7 default tending jobs running per their schedules (tend-swarm 4m, watch-safety-thresholds 30s, push-stale-commits 1m, snapshot-health 10m, drift-check 30m, review-readiness-check 15m, orchestrator-chat event-only).
- No user messages → orchestrator-chat does not fire.
- VPS pushes once → snapshot-health fires once.
- No commits stale beyond push threshold → push-stale-commits never pushes.

**Cost assertion (LLM)**:
- Total `agentRunOutcome != null` count over 60 minutes ≤ **1** (allow snapshot-health's optional drift check).
- Total `agentRunOutcome == 'spoke'` ≤ **0** (silent or no run).
- Aggregate tokensConsumed ≤ **5,000** (small budget for the optional drift check).

**Noise assertion (Activity panel)**:
- Total Activity events emitted (excluding system heartbeats and `health_snapshot_updated` event) ≤ **1**.
- Zero `hoopoe_activity_urgent` deliveries.
- Zero force-release / kill-process / swarm-halt actions.

**Audit-on-every-tick assertion**:
- Audit log contains EXACTLY one entry per tick (about 720 audit entries in 60 min: 15 tend-swarm + 120 watch-safety-thresholds + 60 push-stale-commits + 6 snapshot-health + 2 drift-check + 4 review-readiness-check ≈ 207 actually, double-check).
- Per §8.3.2 'audit-on-every-tick regardless of wake/silence.'

UNIT TESTS
============
- Pre-script unit tests for each of the 7 default jobs: a no-detection input → wakeAgent:false output.
- Mock agent runtime: when wakeAgent:true with no-action-warranted scenario → response begins with [SILENT] → delivery is suppressed BUT audit entry is written.
- Scheduler unit test: every tick writes an audit entry whether or not the agent woke and whether or not it spoke.

E2E TEST (HEALTHY-HOUR FIXTURE)
==================================
1. Boot Mock Flywheel Mode with healthy-hour fixture loaded.
2. Run scheduler for 60 simulated minutes.
3. Collect scheduler_metrics rows.
4. Assert cost + noise + audit invariants above.
5. Emit `docs/test-evidence/healthy-hour/<runId>.json` with the rollup.

REGRESSION TRIPWIRES
======================
- A code change that breaks tend-swarm's no-detection shortcut should fail this test.
- Specifically test: introduce a fake detection-always pre-script → assertion fails loudly with 'wakeAgent:false rate dropped from 100% to 0%, regression in tend-swarm pre-script.'

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
==========================
- Disk-pressure injection during healthy hour → watch-safety-thresholds emits one disk-pressure detection + one sbh cleanup action; cost should remain bounded.
- Skill-cache miss during healthy hour (jsm/jfp unavailable) → degraded_mode block_job kicks in for tend-swarm; cost stays at zero (job blocked, not silently running with bad skill).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Healthy-hour fixture run logs: `{ runId, fixture: 'healthy-hour', startAt, endAt, llmWakes, panelEvents, urgentEvents, tokensConsumed, auditEntries }`.
- Test failure logs the FIRST tick that broke the invariant, with the full pre-script output.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §8.6 — invariant statement.
- §8.7 — tradeoff: cost ceilings ultimately user-set, but the floor must hold.
- §8.8 — healthy-hour fixture definition.
- hp-5s4 — scheduler infrastructure (instrumentation host).
- hp-rnn — tending evaluation harness (parent).
- hp-fb0 — initial tending job set (per-job pre-scripts).
- hp-q3t — fixture-replay harness.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- The healthy-hour fixture is explicitly NOT 'every job paused' — that would be trivially zero. It's all jobs RUNNING per schedule, just no detections.
- Define the cost+noise THRESHOLDS in packages/slo-targets.yaml (hp-5ja owns the file) so they're tuneable without touching test code.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- scheduler_metrics table populated per tick.
- Healthy-hour fixture in §8.8 corpus + assertion harness.
- Cost + noise + audit invariants asserted in CI.
- Regression tripwire test (deliberately break tend-swarm) demonstrates assertion fires.
- packages/slo-targets.yaml carries the threshold numbers.

title: Phase 4: Project create/import flows + .hoopoe initialization + readiness checks
description:
WHAT
====
Project registry: import existing repo (with origin remote required), clone fresh repo, OR create new project from scratch. Projects live on the VPS at /data/projects/<project>. Hoopoe's metadata lives under <project>/.hoopoe/. Readiness checks gate progression.

PROJECT IMPORT (§7.1 'Project entry')
======================================
- User picks: 'Import existing repo' / 'Create new project' / 'Clone repo'.
- For import: user provides a path on the VPS (existing checkout) or a remote URL.
- Hoopoe:
  1. Validates Git remote exists (origin required for v1; projects without external remote are NOT supported per §7.7).
  2. Initializes .hoopoe/ in the project root.
  3. Detects AGENTS.md, README.md, language manifests (package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, go.mod).
  4. Initializes br if .beads/ is missing.
  5. Adds project to /v1/projects.
  6. Triggers initial local clone (§7.7) on desktop.

PROJECT LIFECYCLE (§4.1)
=========================
imported → planning → plan_finalized → beads_created → beads_finalized → swarm_running → hardening_rounds → quality_gates → completed

GATE INVARIANTS (§4.2)
=======================
- Project imported: Git repo present, branch known, AGENTS.md present, .hoopoe initialized, tool detection done.
- Subsequent gates land in later phases.

.hoopoe LAYOUT
===============
.hoopoe/
  project.json          — metadata (id, name, branch, languages, tooling, lifecycle state)
  plans/<plan-id>/      — plan artifacts (Phase 5)
  skills.lock.json      — skill SHA-256 pins (Phase 10)
  model-context-policy.json  — privacy/context policy (§5.5)

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.1 — origin required for v1.
- §4 — domain model.
- §7.1 'Project entry' — single project flow before plan stage.
- §7.7 — desktop local clone.
- ru list --paths — VPS-side project enumeration (§2.3 GitAdapter).

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Multiple projects per VPS (v1 is single-VPS but multi-project per ADR-0001).
- Project switching is a top-bar action (§7.6).
- ru robot-docs exposes project lookup for VPS-side multi-project ops.
- Concurrent project creation: serialize via project-creation lock per VPS to avoid race conditions.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- POST /v1/projects creates a project (idempotent via Idempotency-Key).
- Project registry persisted in daemon SQLite.
- AGENTS.md detection + editor link.
- br init (in .beads/) runs if missing.
- Tool detection captures package manifests.
- Readiness check returns the gate-invariant result for 'imported'.
- ru list --paths integration for project enumeration.
- Project switching tested in top bar.

title: Phase 4: Clone-sync subsystem — subscribe to origin_updated, 60s safety-net poll, on-demand refresh, fetch-on-reconnect
description:
WHAT
====
Desktop-side sync subsystem keeping the local clone aligned with origin. Subscribes to origin_updated events from the daemon (Phase 4 watcher); runs `git fetch --all --tags --prune` on event; renders 'N unpushed commits on VPS' indicator from vps_commit_created / vps_push_completed events without fetching; 60s safety-net poll; on-demand refresh button; fetch-on-WS-reconnect.

WHY (§7.7 sync model)
=======================
The DESKTOP side of the §7.7 sync model. Daemon emits the events; this bead consumes them and keeps the local clone fresh.

SYNC TRIGGERS (§7.7)
=====================
1. **origin_updated** event → fetch within 1-2s.
2. **vps_commit_created** event → render 'N unpushed commits' indicator; do NOT fetch (nothing in origin yet).
3. **vps_push_completed** event → fetch (downstream origin_updated will also trigger).
4. **60s safety-net poll** — background `git fetch` per active project (configurable).
5. **On-demand 'Refresh'** action in project header.
6. **Reconcile on reconnect** — when WS reconnects after a gap, fetch once + reconcile sync cursor.

UNPUSHED OVERLAY
=================
The cockpit composes both views:
- File history + current pushed contents → from local clone.
- 'VPS WIP' overlay → from daemon RPCs (next bead): getWorkingTreeStatus, getStagedDiff, getUnstagedDiff, getUnpushedCommits.
The split is what lets the local clone stay a strict origin mirror without losing live-agent visibility.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.7 — sync model.
- §10.1 — events use sequence cursors.
- §5.3 — desktop runs git fetch + read-only plumbing on its own clone.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Fetch operations bounded by a per-project semaphore (don't fan out 100 fetches simultaneously).
- 60s poll configurable per project + globally; can be disabled to save bandwidth on metered connections.
- Each fetch is logged with the triggering event ID for auditability (audit log epic).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- origin_updated event triggers fetch within bounded latency.
- 60s safety-net poll runs in background.
- 'Refresh' button works.
- Fetch-on-reconnect tested.
- 'N unpushed commits' indicator renders correctly without spurious fetches.
- Phase 4 acceptance flow: VPS commit → auto-push → origin_updated → desktop fetch → local clone shows commit, end-to-end < 3s.

title: Cross-cutting: Appendix B anti-pattern compliance audit — verify all 7 anti-patterns are absent in shipped code
description:
WHAT
====
Cross-cutting compliance bead that explicitly verifies all 7 anti-patterns from Appendix B 'Anti-patterns to refuse' are absent in the shipped Hoopoe codebase. Some are covered by individual implementation beads; this bead is the single audit/test surface that asserts compliance and fails CI if any regression appears.

THE 7 ANTI-PATTERNS (Appendix B)
==================================

**#1 PubSub.unbounded everywhere → bound all channels**
- Implementation owner: hp-q3p (Bounded-channel + slow-consumer load tests).
- This audit asserts: grep `PubSub.unbounded` in apps/daemon/internal/** returns zero hits; grep `make(chan T)` (unbuffered) in event-fanout paths is justified by comment.

**#2 Terminal history as one big string blob on terminal.open → chunk it**
- Implementation owner: hp-9vg (chunked log API) + hp-yf9 (PTY plumbing).
- This audit asserts: PTY-attach handler sends a bounded ring (default 256 KB) + offset + 'fetch older via /v1/jobs/{id}/log?offset='; never the full scrollback as one string; renderer never receives `terminalHistory: <megabytes>` payload.

**#3 Silent client-side message caps → virtualize or show 'showing latest N'**
- This audit asserts: Activity timeline, Agent Mail timeline, audit log viewer, finding ledger, and all message-list surfaces use a virtualizer (TanStack Virtual or similar) OR display an explicit 'showing latest N of M' indicator with a 'load older' affordance. NEVER a silent `MAX_LIST_LENGTH` slice that hides messages without telling the user.

**#4 2,175-line main.ts monolith → decompose on day one**
- Implementation owner: hp-zir (decompose t3code main.ts).
- This audit asserts: apps/desktop/src/main/*.ts each < 800 lines (soft) / < 1200 lines (hard CI fail); every BackendLifecycle/UpdateMachine/IpcRegistry/WindowManager/SettingsBridge/AuthBridge module exists as a separate file; no `apps/desktop/src/main.ts` contains > 200 lines of orchestration logic.

**#5 No port-conflict resolution → findOpenPort(preferred) in BackendLifecycle.ts**
- This audit asserts: BackendLifecycle.startDaemon spawns the daemon with `HOOPOE_PORT=<findOpenPort(preferred=8765)>`; if preferred is busy, daemon binds to next free port; daemon emits 'listening on :<port>' and BackendReadinessDetector parses the actual port from stdout (not assumes a fixed one); test asserts that two desktop instances on the same Mac can run side-by-side with different ports.

**#6 Implicit string-switch dispatch → real command registry**
- Implementation owner: hp-rth (keybindings + command registry).
- This audit asserts: no `switch (commandName) { case 'X': ... }` in main process; every command goes through `commandRegistry.invoke(id, payload)`; new commands register at module-load time, not via a switch statement edit.

**#7 Unknown when-clause keys evaluate to false → validate at parse time, fail loudly**
- Implementation owner: hp-rth (when-clause parser).
- This audit asserts: when-clause AST parser has a known-context-keys set; unknown identifier in a when-clause emits a parse error (not silent false); test fixture: `{key:'cmd+x', command:'foo', when:'stage == swrm'}` (typo) fails to load and surfaces a Diagnostics entry.

CI INTEGRATION
