================
This audit runs as a single CI job 'antipattern-compliance' that:
1. Greps the daemon source for `PubSub.unbounded` and `make(chan T, 0)` in fanout paths.
2. Asserts max line counts on apps/desktop/src/main/*.ts.
3. Asserts no top-level switch-on-command-name dispatch in main process.
4. Runs a test that spawns two desktop instances and verifies port allocation.
5. Runs a test that asserts terminal-attach payloads are < 512 KB.
6. Runs a test that virtualization is in use on long lists (> 200 items renders < 50 DOM nodes).
7. Runs the when-clause parse-error fixture.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Each anti-pattern check emits a structured log line with category, file, line, severity, and an evidence pointer.
- CI failure includes a clear diagnostic: 'Anti-pattern N regressed in <file>:<line> — see <docs/antipatterns.md#N> for the rationale and fix.'
- Pass log: 'antipattern-compliance: 7/7 passed (build N)'.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- Appendix B 'Anti-patterns to refuse' (lines 2320-2331).
- §2.7 (chunked log API).
- §10.1 (backpressure rules).
- hp-q3p, hp-9vg, hp-yf9, hp-zir, hp-rth (implementation owners).

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Each anti-pattern has a one-paragraph rationale in docs/antipatterns.md so future contributors don't re-introduce them by 'simplifying.'
- Audit runs nightly + on every PR; failures block merge.
- New anti-patterns discovered post-launch get added here as #8/#9/etc., never silently.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 7 anti-pattern checks implemented + green.
- docs/antipatterns.md exists with rationale per item.
- CI job 'antipattern-compliance' runs on every PR + nightly.
- A deliberate regression PR (e.g., revert hp-q3p) demonstrates CI catches it loudly.
- Test evidence written to docs/test-evidence/antipattern-compliance/.

title: Phase 2: Daemon + auth + tunnel test suite (BootstrapCredentialService, SessionCredentialService, sequence-cursor replay, tunnel FSM)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 2 test suite covering daemon scaffolding, the two-token auth model, the SSH tunnel manager, the WS event sequence cursor + replay protocol, and the systemd integration.

UNIT TESTS (daemon, Go)
==========================
- BootstrapCredentialService: 12-char Crockford pairing token generation (no 0/I/O confusables); single-use atomic consumption at /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer; expired pairing tokens rejected; re-issuance via 'hoopoe auth pairing create'; persistence in append-only event log.
- SessionCredentialService: HMAC-signed bearer (30d TTL); base64url(claims).base64url(sig) shape; 'sid' claim resolves against bearer table; signing-secret rotation revokes all bearers + WS tokens; HMAC WS token (5min, stateless); same signing secret for both.
- ServerSecretStore: 32-byte random secret generated on first start; rotation surface; secret never logged (hp-lxs redaction asserts).
- HTTP routing: chi/echo basic routing, path-prefixed /v1, RFC 7807 problem+json error responses, idempotency-key handling on write endpoints.
- WebSocket: gorilla/nhooyr handshake, sequence-cursor on every event, _gap emission on lag, _heartbeat at fixed cadence, _lag with offset on slow consumer.
- ReplayEvents: replayEvents(channel, sinceSequence) returns ordered events from append-only log; idempotent replay when client receives same event twice.
- ConnectionManager FSM (desktop side): unconfigured → ssh_probing → bootstrapping → tunnel_connecting → authenticating → ready → degraded → reconnecting → ready → disconnected; backoff with jitter and 30s cap; sleep/wake trigger; tunnel death trigger; bearer expiry trigger.

INTEGRATION TESTS
==================
- Cold-VPS bootstrap: bootstrap.sh installs daemon, configures systemd, starts service, prints pairing token to stdout; desktop captures via SSH session and exchanges for bearer; bearer persisted in safeStorage; WS tunnel up; replay one job log.
- Disconnect/reconnect: simulate tunnel drop mid-stream; ConnectionManager FSM transitions through reconnecting; sequence-cursor reconnect closes the gap; replay is idempotent (same evt seen → ignored).
- Long disconnect: simulate 10-minute disconnect; replay closes gap with no UI corruption; @slo events.replay.10min.p95 ≤ 5s holds.
- Daemon restart: systemctl restart hoopoe; in-flight jobs persist (or marked interrupted with audit); bearer still valid; WS reconnects under @slo desktop.reconnect.p95 ≤ 10s.
- Secret rotation: rotate signing secret via 'hoopoe auth rotate-secret' (owner only); all bearers + WS tokens immediately invalidated; desktop forced through pairing again.
- systemd Type=notify: sd_notify ready signal acknowledged; WatchdogSec=30 keeps service alive; restart on failure; ProtectSystem=strict + ReadWritePaths covers exactly the needed dirs.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION (overlap with hp-2qn)
================================================
- Tunnel flapping (up/down/up at 5s intervals for 1 min) → no state corruption.
- Daemon kill -9 mid-job → next start picks up interrupted state per §8.3.2 invariant.
- Bearer table corruption → daemon refuses to start, writes a clear error to journald.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every test uses hp-lxs envelope.
- Bearer tokens NEVER appear in evidence files (redaction asserts).
- Pairing tokens NEVER appear in evidence files except for one explicit pairing-token-redaction test that asserts they are sanitized.
- correlationId carries through HTTP request → WS event → audit entry.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.5 — transport ladder + ConnectionManager FSM.
- §2.6 — seed daemon API + WebSocket envelope.
- §5.2 — auth model.
- §5.3 — command safety.
- §10.1 — backpressure / lag / replay rules.
- §18.1 Phase 2 acceptance row.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.
- hp-2qn — chaos suite.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on auth, scheduler primitives, replay, FSM.
- All integration scenarios pass against a real research-spike VPS + Mock Flywheel Mode.
- @slo desktop.reconnect.p95 + events.replay.10min.p95 + job.cancellation.no-orphans pass.
- Bearer redaction verified across all evidence files.

title: Phase 1: Desktop unit + e2e test suite (lifecycle, settings, keybindings, command palette, navigation, ⌘K, DMG smoke) with detailed structured logging
description:
WHAT
====
Comprehensive Phase 1 test suite covering everything that Phase 1 ships: the lifted t3code lifecycle, settings system, keybindings + command registry, design tokens + reusable components, four-stage routed shell, Activity panel drawer, and the signed/notarized DMG.

UNIT TESTS (desktop)
=====================
- BackendLifecycle: spawn → ready → shutdown with port-conflict resolution; expectedBackendExitChildren WeakSet correctly distinguishes wanted vs unwanted exits; TERM→KILL escalation in stopBackendAndWaitForExit; ServerListeningDetector parses Hoopoe daemon's stdout signature.
- UpdateMachine: state transitions for idle → checking → downloading → ready-to-install → installing; channel selection (latest vs nightly); 15s startup delay; 4h poll interval; mock-update-server happy/error paths.
- IpcRegistry: handler registration, schema validation on every call, rejection of un-registered channels.
- WindowManager: new-window-of-app, focus-existing, dock badge, deep-link handling.
- SettingsBridge: three-store split (~/.hoopoe/userdata/{daemon-settings.json, desktop-settings.json, client-settings.json}); hot-reload via fs.watch + 100ms debounce + atomic write; PubSub change stream; relaunchDesktopApp(reason) for restart-on-toggle; stripDefaultServerSettings forward-compat.
- AuthBridge: pairing → bearer exchange; safeStorage encrypt/decrypt round-trip; WS-token JIT issuance.
- Keybindings: file-watch + 100ms debounce; recursive-descent when-clause parser; AST evaluation with last-rule-wins; failure on unknown context keys (anti-pattern #7); command registry dispatch.
- Component unit tests: StageHeader, AgentTile, BeadCard, StatusPill, PriorityChip, CoverageBar, TerminalPane, TimelineRow, HealthKpiCard, ApprovalDialog (Storybook + vitest visual regression).

E2E TESTS (playwright + Electron)
===================================
1. Boot signed DMG on a clean macOS profile → app opens → all four stages reachable.
2. ⌘K command palette opens, fuzzy-searches commands, when-clause filtering works.
3. Settings hot-reload: edit ~/.hoopoe/userdata/desktop-settings.json → desktop picks up change without restart (for hot-reloadable keys).
4. Settings restart-on-toggle: change a daemon-settings key that requires relaunch → relaunchDesktopApp fires with reason → app restarts cleanly.
5. Keybindings: bind a key in ~/.hoopoe/keybindings.json → command fires; bind to a deliberately-malformed when-clause → fails loudly at parse time, not silently.
6. Auto-update against mock-update-server: stable → beta channel switch upgrades; rollback works.
7. Activity panel toggle: opens drawer from any stage; badge increments on simulated event.
8. Mock Flywheel boot: app boots against a fixture corpus, no VPS required; all four stages render fixture data.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS (must use hp-lxs structured-logging library)
==================================================================
Every test emits structured JSON envelope entries with:
- correlationId per test case.
- component: 'test.phase1'.
- failure logs include last 200 entries from the in-process logger, plus screenshots/video for e2e.
- evidence committed to docs/test-evidence/phase1/<timestamp>/.

CHECKED INVARIANTS (Appendix B anti-patterns)
================================================
- No console.log or fmt.Println outside vendored/.
- No PubSub.unbounded — load test wedges a slow consumer; daemon RSS bounded.
- No 2,175-line main.ts — fileSize check on apps/desktop/src/main/*.ts.
- No string-switch dispatch — commandRegistry.register usage enforced via lint.
- Unknown when-clause keys fail loudly.
- findOpenPort handles preferred-port conflict.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §16 Phase 1 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 1 acceptance test row.
- Appendix B — vendored files + anti-patterns.
- Appendix C #12 — no terminal in default UI (component tests assert TerminalPane is NOT in the default Swarm route).
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t / hp-7m / hp-tg0 — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥80% on Phase 1 modules.
- All 8 e2e scenarios pass on arm64 + x64 macOS.
- Anti-pattern lint rules in CI.
- Evidence files committed; SLO @phase1 tests pass.
close_reason: Phase 1 desktop unit/e2e suite with structured logging and Mock Flywheel fixture harness

title: Phase 6: Bead set quality tracker + implementation-evidence ledger primitives
description:
WHAT
====
Bead-set quality tracker computing scores per §7.2 quality dimensions. Sets up the implementation-evidence.jsonl ledger primitives that later phases (Swarm, Health, Review, Landing) write into.

QUALITY DIMENSIONS (§7.2)
===========================
- Plan coverage
- Dependency correctness
- Granularity
- Ready-set size (br ready --json count)
- Testability (acceptance criteria per bead)
- Duplicate risk
- Parallelism (bv --robot-plan tracks)
- Context richness (bead description self-contained?)

IMPLEMENTATION-EVIDENCE LEDGER (§7.2)
=======================================
After swarm launch, traceability extends:
plan section → bead → branch/worktree → commits → files touched → tests/builds run → health deltas → review findings → landing queue item / PR / merge commit.

Produces implementation-evidence.jsonl under the plan/bead artifact store. UI can answer:
- which plan sections are implemented but untested
- which sections have code but no review
- which beads closed without a landing queue item
- which review findings map back to which plan risks
- what evidence supports the final ship gate

PRIMITIVES (this bead)
=======================
- Append-only implementation-evidence.jsonl per plan.
- Schema: {beadId, planSection, branch?, commits[], filesTouched[], testRuns[], healthDeltas[], reviewFindings[], landingQueueItem?, time}.
- Writers (later phases): swarm bead-claim event, build-queue test runs, health-snapshot updates, review-finding posts, landing PR creation.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 — implementation evidence.
- §15 — definition of success #11-12.
- audit log — ledger entries also audited.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Quality tracker computes all 8 dimensions + delta from previous polish round.
- implementation-evidence.jsonl writer wired (initial — Phase 8+ extend).
- Schema versioned.
- UI surfaces tracker per-bead-set (Beads stage) + per-plan (rolled up).
- Phase 6 acceptance: locked plan → conversion → polish → quality scores all > guidance threshold.

title: Daemon redaction layer (audit log + structured logs + streamed events) — bearer / pairing / model keys / SSH / cookies / paths
description:
WHAT
====
The redaction layer that runs before any persistence or streaming on the daemon. Bearer tokens, pairing tokens, model API keys, SSH passphrases, browser cookies, provider credentials, and sensitive paths are detected by pattern + replaced with safe placeholders BEFORE the audit log writer or the WebSocket fan-out sees the bytes.

WHY
===
§5.4 line 4 specifies that 'daemon logs and audit entries pass through a redaction layer before persistence and before streaming to clients.' This is the daemon-side complement to the desktop-side redaction in the structured-logging library (hp-lxs). Putting the same redaction patterns in both places means:
1. A leak through one path is still caught by the other.
2. New secret patterns (e.g., a new model provider's key shape) are added in one library + propagate everywhere.
3. The redaction events themselves are auditable (a redaction trace event records which pattern matched, in what context, without leaking the secret).

PATTERNS (canonical list — must be kept in sync with hp-lxs)
================================================================
- Bearer tokens (HMAC-signed base64url shape).
- 12-char Crockford pairing tokens (single-use; one-shot consumed at /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer).
- Provider keys: sk-, sk-ant-, AIza, AKIA, ghp_, xox, BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY, BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY, BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY, BEGIN PGP PRIVATE KEY BLOCK.
- SSH passphrases — should never be logged; redaction is defense-in-depth.
- Browser cookies for chatgpt.com, claude.ai, openai.com — defense-in-depth.
- Email/Telegram delivery target addresses — last 4 chars only.
- File paths: ~/.ssh/, /etc/shadow, /private/var/db/, oracle profile dirs.
- HTTP Authorization / Cookie / Set-Cookie headers — replaced with [redacted-header] placeholder.

INTEGRATION
=============
- internal/redact Go package + @hoopoe/redact TS package — same patterns, same placeholders.
- internal/audit writer applies redaction before fsync.
- internal/events fan-out applies redaction before WebSocket emit.
- internal/logger applies redaction before persistence.
- Adapters that capture stdout/stderr (br, bv, ntm, agent_mail, ru, oracle, CLI agents) pipe through redaction before storage.

REDACTION TRACE EVENTS
========================
For each redaction, emit a trace-level event:
{
  ts, redactor: 'audit'|'events'|'logger'|'adapter:<name>',
  pattern_id: <e.g., 'bearer'|'provider-anthropic'|'ssh-private-key'>,
  context: <field path>,  // e.g., 'audit.command_preview', 'event.data.payload'
  bytes_redacted: <length>,
}
- Trace events are themselves redacted (no leaked secret content).
- Diagnostics shows redaction stats (count by pattern) so operators can verify redaction is firing.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.4 line 4.
- §10 — audit log shape.
- hp-lxs — structured-logging library (parallel desktop redaction).
- hp-g73 — audit log infrastructure (consumer).
- hp-rkx — renderer hardening parent epic.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- internal/redact Go package shipped with all named patterns.
- @hoopoe/redact TS package mirrors the Go patterns (drift-detection test).
- Audit writer + WS fan-out + logger + adapter capture all consume the redactor.
- Adversarial tests: nested in JSON / URL query / stack trace / multi-line value / base64-wrapped — all caught.
- Diagnostics renders redaction stats.
- §18.4 release smoke check #9 ('audit log redaction catches every secret class') consumes this layer.
close_reason: Implemented daemon redaction and audit writer layer in 3b8c174

title: Phase 13: Provider plugin + final-gate landing test suite (Contabo plugin contract, instance lifecycle, cost estimate, demo project, crash reports)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 13 test suite covering the Contabo provider plugin, the cost-estimate + teardown flow, the polished empty/loading/error states, the onboarding tour, the demo project, the crash-reports opt-in, and the final-gate ship-ready checklist.

UNIT TESTS
============
- Provider plugin contract: listRegions / listSizes / createInstance / destroyInstance / estimateMonthlyCost — each asserted against schema.
- Contabo plugin: signed up, no real charges incurred (use sandbox/test creds); listRegions returns expected DCs; listSizes returns expected SKUs (Cloud VPS 50 with 64GB target); estimateMonthlyCost matches the §6.2 / §13 cost-transparency table.
- Cost-transparency render: VPS line + Claude Max + ChatGPT Pro + optional Gemini Ultra → all-in monthly total; no billing dashboard pretense (Hoopoe doesn't see credit cards); pulled from packages/pricing-catalog.json mirrored from canonical guide.
- Polished empty/loading/error states: every stage's empty state has a clear next-action; every loading state has a progress signal; every error state has a recovery action + link to docs.
- Onboarding tour: mirrors agent-flywheel.com 13-step wizard; guides newcomer through first project end-to-end.
- Demo project: a minimal plan + bead set + fixture swarm-ready state for users to explore the cockpit without standing up a real VPS.
- Crash reports opt-in: explicit setting in Diagnostics; structured crash report includes redacted state; submission is HTTP POST to a defined endpoint or local-only when no endpoint configured; opt-out is the default.
- Final-gate landing checklist: all gate invariants per §4.2 verified (tests/builds pass or exceptions documented; code health gates pass or follow-up beads exist; Git/beads synced); ship-ready button enabled only when all green.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. Phase 13 acceptance: existing-VPS path remains green while one provider plugin can create + estimate + tear down an instance without affecting manual mode.
2. Contabo create-instance happy path (sandbox): listSizes → user picks Cloud VPS 50 → createInstance returns instance ID + IP → instance reachable via SSH; existing-VPS connection flow takes over from there.
3. Contabo destroyInstance: confirmation dialog requires user types instance ID → destroy succeeds → instance gone from listings → cost-estimate updates.
4. Cost-transparency table renders correctly in onboarding wizard's 'Subscription requirement' screen + new-plan empty state when no subscriptions configured.
5. Onboarding tour: new user from clean install → completes plan → bead → swarm walkthrough using demo project.
6. Crash-reports opt-in: user enables → simulate desktop crash → crash report appears in Diagnostics 'Recent crashes' table; redaction asserted (no bearer/passphrase/keys in report).
7. Final-gate landing: simulate project with 1 unfixed P0 bead → gate refuses; close bead → gate enables; user clicks → audit entry + ship event fires.
8. Demo project: 'Try the demo' from welcome screen boots Mock Flywheel + fixture state → user can navigate all four stages without VPS.

CHAOS / FAULT INJECTION
========================
- Contabo API timeout → plugin returns degraded; Diagnostics shows 'unable to reach Contabo'; existing-VPS path still works.
- Contabo invalid creds → plugin logs the error (with cred redaction) + UI shows clear message + link to docs.
- Crash-report endpoint unreachable → reports queue locally; flush on next launch.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every Contabo API call logs the request shape (creds redacted) + response status + duration.
- Crash reports are themselves structured-logging entries before submission.
- Cost-estimate calculations log the catalog version + the input choices.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.2 — provider rollout strategy (Contabo first).
- §11 — packaging.
- §13 — cost transparency.
- §16 Phase 13 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 13 acceptance row.
- hp-9fo / hp-8op / hp-ecp / hp-kyr / hp-fyi — Phase 13 implementation beads.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥80% on plugin + cost calculator + final-gate checklist.
- All 8 e2e scenarios pass.
- Crash-report redaction verified across cases.
- Demo-project boot < 5s on M-series Mac.

title: Phase 0: Manual run-through of agent-flywheel.com 13-step wizard
description:
WHAT
====
Run the canonical agent-flywheel.com 13-step wizard end-to-end AS A USER WOULD, before writing any Hoopoe code that wraps it. Capture step-by-step notes that become the basis for §6.4 structured ACFS bootstrap parsing.

WHY (§16 Phase 0 step 1)
========================
'You cannot automate a flow you haven't completed manually.' The wizard is the canonical user journey from 'I have a laptop' to 'fresh ACFS VPS reachable via SSH', and Hoopoe's Phase 3 wraps it. Until someone has done it by hand and captured the friction points, every assumption about phase boundaries / failure modes / resume semantics is theoretical.

THE 13 STEPS (§6.1)
====================
1. Choose OS (Mac/Win/Linux) — Hoopoe is Mac-only v1; wizard skips for Hoopoe but capture for completeness.
2. Install Terminal — Hoopoe needs only Electron; capture step but Hoopoe surfaces Diagnostics 'recovery shell' (§2.5).
3. Generate SSH Key — capture canonical instructions; Hoopoe will generate ed25519 on demand or import.
4. Rent VPS (Contabo / OVH / existing) — provider's website; Hoopoe surfaces specs+providers but user does this manually for v1.
5. Create Instance (paste pubkey, get IP) — provider's website.
6. SSH Connect — capture canonical fingerprint TOFU UX; Hoopoe's tunnel manager opens this itself.
7. Set Up Accounts — Claude Code / Codex / Gemini subscriptions; CAAM verifies in step 12.
8. Pre-Flight Check — capture exact OS version, CPU/RAM/disk, network, base packages, permissions checks.
9. Run Installer — canonical curl|bash one-liner; capture phase markers, idempotency, resume-on-failure behavior.
10. Reconnect — Hoopoe re-establishes SSH tunnel automatically.
11. Verify Key — acfs doctor --json structured output; capture all categories.
12. Status Check — tool inventory; CAAM-verified subscription coverage.
13. Launch Onboard — interactive tutorial; Hoopoe surfaces this as optional link, not gate.

CAPTURES (Phase 0 fixture inputs)
==================================
- Wall-clock time per step (where the wizard feels slow).
- Failure modes and the exact error messages emitted.
- Phase markers in installer output (§6.4 phase parser).
- 'already-installed' vs 'fresh install' divergence.
- All command outputs that Hoopoe's wizard wrapper will parse: ssh fingerprint, acfs doctor --json, tool inventory, CAAM auth check, oracle login.
- Provider-quirk notes (Contabo activation can be ~1hr, OVH activation in minutes).

DELIVERABLES
============
- docs/research-spike/manual-wizard-walkthrough.md with timestamps, screenshots, and per-step notes.
- All emitted JSON / text snapshots committed to packages/fixtures/phase0-<date>/wizard/.
- Friction-points list to inform §6.1 Hoopoe-specific extensions and §6.4 fallback-to-raw-log decisions.

ACCEPTANCE
==========
A teammate can read manual-wizard-walkthrough.md and exactly reproduce the run, end-to-end, on a fresh VPS, including reproducing both happy-path and at-least-one-failure-path.
close_reason: Wizard run-through deferred to follow-up bead; Phase 0 evidence pack landed without manual transcript (collector --wizard-command stub)

title: Phase 4: Project registry + Git watcher + desktop local clone
description:
GOAL (§12 Phase 4, §7.7)
=========================
Implement create / import / clone-project flows; project readiness checks; .hoopoe initialization; daemon-side Git watcher emitting vps_commit_created / vps_push_completed / origin_updated events; the §7.3 post-commit auto-push hook installed at swarm-launch; the desktop local clone (§7.7) — initial clone from origin, clone-state.json, file watcher for local-edit detection, dirty banner, soft/hard size caps; clone-sync subsystem on desktop subscribing to origin_updated; daemon RPCs for VPS WIP reads (getWorkingTreeStatus / getStagedDiff / getUnstagedDiff / getUnpushedCommits). Wire the §7.6 cockpit top bar with full state.

WHY THIS PHASE IS A WATERSHED
==============================
This phase lands the §7.7 'desktop sync-driven mirror' that powers EVERY later code-reading interaction (Beads drawer files-touched, Health tab file content with coverage gutter, hotspot previews, review-finding line anchors, 'open in editor' links, ripgrep search). Without it, the cockpit feels sluggish and fails offline. After it lands, file open + diff + blame + grep are local-disk fast.

It also completes the §1.1 source-of-truth discipline for code:
- Origin = canonical (the durable, team-shared, CI-attached source of truth).
- VPS clone = working state (where commits are CREATED before they become canonical via push).
- Desktop local clone = sync-driven mirror of origin (read-only, never a write target through Hoopoe).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.1 — source-of-truth table.
- §1.7 — sync-driven mirrors are allowed; parallel sources of truth are not.
- §5.3 — narrow exception for desktop's local clone reads.
- §7.6 — cockpit chrome top bar.
- §7.7 — full local clone spec.

EXIT (§12 Phase 4)
====================
A user can open a repo-backed project, see accurate Git/tool state, browse files instantly from the local clone, see live 'N unpushed commits / M modified files' overlay from the daemon, and a commit made on the VPS by an agent appears in the desktop's local clone within ~3 seconds end-to-end (commit → auto-push → origin_updated event → desktop fetch).

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1 Phase 4)
================================
Import a repo with origin; local clone fetches origin; a VPS commit auto-pushes; desktop fetches on origin_updated; WIP overlay shows unpushed/modified VPS state.

title: Phase 2.5: API/process contract hardening before feature build-out
description:
GOAL (§12 Phase 2.5, §16 Phase 2.5)
====================================
Land the seed REST/WS contract (§2.6) with generated schema tests, the job registry + process manager invariants (§2.7), the capability registry + degraded-mode contract (§2.8), bounded-channel and idempotency-key tests, persisted log offset tests, fault-injection chaos suite, and the SLO metrics scaffold. Do this BEFORE Planning/Beads UI depends on daemon behavior.

WHY 'BEFORE FEATURE BUILD-OUT'
===============================
Feature work in Phase 3+ assumes the daemon is reliable under common faults (tunnel drop, daemon restart, disk pressure, slow renderer, malformed adapter output, long-running scheduler job). If any feature is built atop a daemon that hasn't been chaos-tested, every later phase pays the cost.

DELIVERABLES (§16 Phase 2.5)
==============================
1. Seed REST/WS contract per §2.6 implemented with generated schema tests.
   1a. /v1/capabilities and /v1/compatibility wired; ALL stage routes gated by capability flags before feature UI is built.
2. Job registry + process manager invariants per §2.7 (one job registry; one process group per child; bounded semaphores; persistent logs ~/.hoopoe/logs/{jobId}.log; chunked log API; structured status; artifact registry; idempotency keys on every write endpoint).
3. Bounded-channel + load tests using synthetic high-volume terminal output and a deliberately slow client.
4. Idempotency-key tests for retrying write endpoints through a simulated tunnel drop.
5. Persisted log offset tests: stream 100 MB of output, disconnect, reconnect, fetch missing range by offset.
6. docs/api-seed.md, docs/process-manager.md, docs/reconnect-replay.md as living references.
7. First chaos/fault-injection suite: tunnel drop, daemon restart, disk pressure, slow renderer, malformed adapter output, long-running scheduler job.
8. SLO metrics collection in dev builds and Mock Flywheel Mode (§10.5).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — seed daemon API + WS event contract.
- §2.7 — daemon process manager and job registry.
- §2.8 — capability registry and degraded-mode contract.
- §10.1 — backpressure, lag, replay rules.
- §10.5 — product SLOs and fault injection.

EXIT
====
Phase 3+ adapters can write code against documented endpoints, schemas, capability IDs, and idempotency rules without any reverse engineering of daemon internals.

title: Phase 12: Finding tracker UI + lifecycle (new → triaged → fix_now / new_bead / false_positive / needs_human → closed)
description:
WHAT
====
Finding tracker UI per §7.4.3 + §9.3. Findings tab shows full finding lifecycle ledger with bead links + health cross-references. Per-finding triage: convert to bead, attach as blocker to existing bead, mark false-positive with note, escalate to human, fix immediately.

LIFECYCLE (§9.3)
==================
new → triaged → fix_now | new_bead | false_positive | needs_human → closed.

VIEWS
======
- Findings list filterable by source (ubs / agent / skill / human), severity, status, related bead, related plan section, related file.
- Finding detail panel: source, evidence, code context, suggested fix, related health metrics, audit history.
- Triage actions:
  - Fix immediately: opens bead drawer with auto-prefilled fix bead.
  - New bead: creates linked bead via br adapter.
  - Attach blocker: links to existing bead as blocker.
  - False positive: marks with required note (audited).
  - Needs human: escalates to user inbox.

CROSS-REFERENCES TO HEALTH (§7.4.3)
=====================================
- Findings link to file health metrics — closing a finding can be cross-checked against the coverage / complexity it was supposed to improve.
- Triage view shows 'before' health vs 'after' once fix lands.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.4.3 — finding tracker spec.
- §9.3 — lifecycle.
- §9.5 — specialized audits also feed this.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Findings tab UI complete.
- All lifecycle transitions wired with audit.
- Per-finding cross-references to health visible.
- 'Convert to bead' creates linked bead via br.
- Phase 12 acceptance: review round produces findings; each resolves to one of the lifecycle terminal states.

title: Phase 1: Build greenfield design tokens (cream/dark, agent palette, status pills, priority chips, coverage ramp)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the design-tokens layer of packages/design-system/ — colors, typography, spacing, radii, shadows, status-pill states, priority-chip colors, agent-family palette, coverage-bar ramp. Tokens are exposed as Tailwind config + CSS variables; no hard-coded hex values in component code.

WHY (§3 desktop tech stack, §7.6 cockpit chrome, design/DECISIONS.md)
======================================================================
A consistent visual language across four stages + Activity panel + top bar requires tokens to be defined in ONE place. Mockups in design/mockups/v1/ are deliberate (cream-on-dark, STAGE N — VERB framing); we lift the visual decisions, not the mockup code.

REQUIRED TOKENS
================
1. **Color palette**
   - Primary surfaces: dark base, cream accents (per design/DECISIONS.md), elevation shades (0-3).
   - Status semantic: success / warning / danger / info / muted; each with bg, fg, border tones.
   - Agent-family colors: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini — distinct, color-blind-safe, AA-contrast against the surface tones (§ design system).
2. **Status states (StatusPill component, separate bead)**
   - Bead states: ready / claimed / in_progress / in_review / closed / blocked
   - Job states: queued / running / waiting_approval / canceling / succeeded / failed / interrupted
   - Approval states: pending / approved / denied / expired / superseded_by
   - Capability states: ok / degraded / missing / blocked-by-policy
   - Tool-health dots: green / yellow / red
3. **Priority chips (P0–P4)** — colors per priority level matching agent-flywheel conventions.
4. **Coverage-bar ramp** — red < 60%, yellow 60-80%, green > 80%. Configurable per project; tokens reference threshold variables.
5. **Typography**
   - Sans-serif primary (e.g., Inter or Geist)
   - Monospace for code, file paths, terminal panes (e.g., JetBrains Mono)
6. **Spacing scale** — 4px base, geometric.
7. **Radii** — token set: sm, md, lg, full.
8. **Shadows** — subtle, dark-theme-friendly.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Tokens MUST be the single source of truth — Tailwind config + CSS variables + any TS color constants all derive from one tokens.json or tokens.ts file.
- Light theme is DEFERRED (cream/dark is primary); leave hooks for future light-theme but don't ship it in v1.
- Test contrast (AA at minimum, AAA where critical for status text) using automated tooling (axe-core, pa11y).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- packages/design-system/tokens/ contains tokens.ts (or tokens.json) with full palette, spacing, radii, shadows, typography.
- Tailwind config in apps/desktop/tailwind.config.ts consumes tokens.
- CSS variables exposed under :root for non-Tailwind consumers.
- Storybook page renders all tokens with usage labels.
- Contrast tests pass for every status state + agent color in CI.
close_reason: Built Phase 1 design tokens, CSS variables, Tailwind consumption, token story, and contrast tests

title: Phase 1.5: Strengthen AuthBridge token redaction guard
description:
Root cause: review-findings.md line 209 shows AuthBridgeRedactedError only detects JWT-like "eyJ" and fixture "hp-bearer-" token prefixes. Real Hoopoe bearer tokens are opaque HMAC/base64url-ish strings, so a future error path could interpolate a real bearer and bypass the current redaction self-check.

Acceptance criteria:
- Replace prefix-only token detection with a conservative opaque-token detector for high-entropy url-safe strings of meaningful length, while avoiding noisy false positives for normal prose.
- Add unit tests for synthetic HMAC-shaped bearer, pairing token, WS token, JWT-like token, and harmless message cases.
- Ensure AuthBridge error messages remain non-token-shaped and do not log or expose pairing/bearer/WS credentials.
- Add an explicit scanner/whitelist comment only where token-like literals appear in the redaction tests.
- Cross-link: review-findings.md "Auth — [HIGH] AuthBridgeRedactedError token-shape detection is too narrow".
close_reason: implemented: layered detector (shape denylist + PEM markers + auth-scheme + entropy gate + structured-token gate) replaces the substring 'eyJ'/'hp-bearer-' check; 29 must-redact shapes + 18 must-not-redact + 4 property tests pass.

title: Mac App Store distribution decision (deferred; signed DMG via website is the v1 channel)
description:
WHAT
====
Per §1 'Hoopoe project memory' open questions: 'distribution channel (signed DMG via website vs Mac App Store — MAS sandboxing would break SSH bootstrap)' is unresolved.

DECISION FOR v1
=================
Signed/notarized DMG via website. MAS sandboxing would prevent SSH bootstrap because the sandbox restricts subprocess exec + network access in ways incompatible with Hoopoe's tunnel architecture (§2.5 / §5.3 'desktop runs SSH for the tunnel').

CONSEQUENCES
==============
- v1 distribution: GitHub Releases + signed/notarized DMG (Phase 1 lift covers).
- electron-updater against GitHub Releases (Phase 1 lift covers).
- No MAS submission for v1.
- Telemetry / crash reports independent of MAS opt-in (Phase 13 task covers).

POST-MVP RECONSIDERATION
==========================
If multi-platform expansion happens (Linux AppImage / Windows NSIS), revisit MAS for Mac since the lifted CI matrix supports it. Decision criteria:
- Can Hoopoe operate without SSH bootstrap (e.g., always-paired-elsewhere mode)?
- Is MAS sandbox compatibility worth the workflow split?

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §11 — Mac signed/notarized DMG, electron-updater against GitHub Releases.
- §13 'Can defer' — Mac App Store distribution.
- Hoopoe project memory — open question.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- ADR-0002 written: 'Signed DMG via website for v1; MAS deferred.'
- v1 release pipeline (Phase 1 lift) is the implementation.

title: Phase 2: BootstrapCredentialService (12-char Crockford pairing tokens, single-use, JSONL-persisted)
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the daemon's pairing-token issuance + consumption. 12-char Crockford alphabet (no 0/I/O confusables). Issued at first daemon start; persisted to append-only JSONL + seeded as a single-use in-memory grant so first launch works before the desktop has any credentials. Single-use; consumed atomically at /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer.

WHY (§5.2 step 1, §6.3 step 10-12)
====================================
The pairing token is the trust handoff from the SSH bootstrap channel to the bearer-token-over-tunnel transport. The SSH session IS the trusted bootstrap channel; the daemon emits the token to stdout, the desktop captures it through the SSH session, and exchanges it over the tunnel for a bearer. No QR code, no out-of-band transfer.

CONTRACT
========
- POST /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer with body {pairingToken, instanceId} → {bearer, expiresAt, serverId}
- The pairing token must NEVER be reused; consume atomically (DB transaction or atomic file rename + flock).
- New tokens via: hoopoe auth pairing create  (CLI surface, separate task)
- List/revoke active pairings: hoopoe auth pairing list / revoke
- Persisted to ~/.hoopoe/auth/pairing.jsonl (append-only) so daemon restarts preserve state.
- Single-use grant exists in memory at first launch — covers the very first connection where the JSONL doesn't yet exist. Once consumed, it doesn't regenerate; Subsequent pairings are CLI-issued.

CROCKFORD ALPHABET
===================
0123456789 ABCDEFGHJKMNPQRSTVWXYZ (no 0, I, O, U, L for case-insensitive readability and confusable-resistance).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.2 — pairing → bearer → WS-token model.
- Appendix B 'Patterns lifted' — `apps/server/src/auth/Layers/{Bootstrap,Session}CredentialService.ts` is the pattern reference.
- §6.3 — bootstrap auth flow.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Tokens MUST be high-entropy (cryptographic random); 12 Crockford chars = ~62 bits of entropy.
- Persisted token records: {tokenId, displayToken, createdAt, consumedAt?, consumedBy?, role: 'owner'|'client'}.
- The displayed token is shown ONCE on stdout at first launch; subsequent CLI calls (`hoopoe auth pairing create`) print a new one. Never displayed again after creation.
- Owner role can mint/revoke pairing tokens and manage sessions; client role is read/write data only.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Pairing token is issued on first daemon launch and printed exactly once to stdout.
- POST /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer consumes the pairing atomically; double-submit returns 409 Conflict.
- pairing.jsonl is append-only; consumption is recorded with a new entry (NOT in-place edit).
- 'hoopoe auth pairing list' shows active vs consumed.
- 'hoopoe auth pairing revoke <tokenId>' marks revoked; subsequent consumption returns 410 Gone.
- Unit tests cover entropy, atomic consume, replay-attack rejection.
close_reason: Implemented BootstrapCredentialService with single-use JSONL pairing tokens

title: Phase 2 / §6.5: Daemon least-privilege user + privileged setup-helper for elevated actions (separate executable, allowlisted, approval-gated, audited)
description:
WHAT
====
Architect and implement the steady-state daemon user (least privilege, no sudo, owns ~/.hoopoe + can read project worktrees) and the privileged setup helper (separate executable + narrow sudoers rule OR setuid pattern) for any action requiring elevated privileges. Privileged actions go through the daemon's approval queue, are allowlisted, and are audited.

WHY (§6.5, §1.4 inspectability)
================================
§6.5 explicitly mandates: "The steady-state daemon should not run as root. Privileged setup/repair actions go through a separate, explicit setup helper or temporary elevated command path with approval and audit." Today hp-n30 (systemd unit + bootstrap.sh) MENTIONS this as a NOTE but does not OWN it. The plumbing is non-trivial and load-bearing for §5.3 command safety + §10 audit. Without dedicated implementation:
- The daemon will likely run as root by default (the path of least resistance), making every audited action effectively unbounded.
- ACFS install paths that need sudo (`apt install`, systemd unit creation, port binding) become tangled with steady-state daemon paths.
- Diagnostics repair actions (re-run ACFS doctor, restart NTM/Agent Mail) cannot run elevated without either (a) running the entire daemon as root or (b) ad-hoc shell-out to sudo, neither of which §6.5 permits.

This bead OWNS:
- The least-privilege daemon user creation + permission boundary.
- The privileged setup helper executable contract (allowlist, approval, audit).
- The split between bootstrap-mode (no daemon yet, helper runs unauthenticated to install systemd) and steady-state mode (helper requires daemon-issued approval token).
- Tests verifying the daemon CANNOT escape its permission boundary even when given a malicious request.

CONTRACT — STEADY-STATE DAEMON USER
=====================================
1. **User creation** — bootstrap.sh creates system user `hoopoe` (uid in system range, no login shell, no home outside `/var/lib/hoopoe` or `~/.hoopoe`). Group `hoopoe` owns:
   - `/etc/hoopoe/` (read-only for daemon).
   - `~/.hoopoe/` (read+write for daemon).
   - `/data/projects/*/` (read+write via project ACL or group ownership; see VPS multi-user post-MVP path).
   - `/tmp/hoopoe-*` via `PrivateTmp=true` from systemd unit.
2. **Capabilities forbidden by user permission**:
   - No sudo / NOPASSWD entries for `hoopoe` user except the narrow setup-helper rule below.
   - No write access to `/etc/systemd/system/`, `/usr/local/bin/`, `/var/log/`, package installation paths.
   - No bind to ports < 1024 (relevant if Hoopoe ever exposes 443; not in v1).
3. **Capabilities granted by user permission**:
   - Write to `~/.hoopoe/` (audit log, jobs, artifacts).
   - Execute project commands inside `/data/projects/*/` per CommandSpec allowlist (§5.3).
   - Read-only inspection of system metadata via SELECT-only queries to systemd via `systemctl show --user` (no service control on the daemon's own unit; daemon restart goes through helper).

CONTRACT — PRIVILEGED SETUP HELPER
====================================
1. **Executable location**: `/usr/local/bin/hoopoe-setup-helper` OR `/opt/hoopoe/bin/hoopoe-setup-helper`. Owned by root:root, mode 0755.
2. **Invocation modes**:
   - **Bootstrap mode**: helper invoked directly by bootstrap.sh as root with no daemon-issued approval (no daemon yet). Action set restricted to: `install-systemd-unit`, `create-hoopoe-user`, `chown-acfs-paths`, `register-helper-allowlist`. Bootstrap-mode usage logs a deferred audit entry consumed on first daemon start.
   - **Steady-state mode**: helper invoked via narrow sudoers rule `hoopoe ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/local/bin/hoopoe-setup-helper run --approval-token=*`. Helper validates the approval token against the running daemon's session table over a UNIX socket. If valid + action in allowlist + preconditions match → executes; else exits 1 with audit log.
3. **Allowlist file**: `/etc/hoopoe/setup-helper.allowed` (root-only writable). Lists permitted action names, expected argv shapes, expected stderr/stdout patterns. Format:
   ```
   action: install-systemd-unit
   args:   --unit-path=/etc/systemd/system/hoopoe.service
   audit:  required
   ----
   action: repair-acfs
   args:   --doctor=true --auto-fix=<bool>
   audit:  required
   ```
4. **Helper-side checksum**: helper computes SHA-256 of allowlist on every invocation; if drifted from a daemon-recorded checksum, refuses with "allowlist tampered."
5. **Action set (steady-state)**:
   - `install-systemd-unit` — daemon upgrade flow drops a new unit file, helper installs + reloads systemd.
   - `uninstall-systemd-unit` — Hoopoe uninstall.
   - `chown-projects` — ACL repair when project paths drift from expected ownership.
   - `repair-acfs` — Diagnostics "Re-run ACFS doctor with auto-fix" requires elevated.
   - `restart-service` (`ntm` / `agent-mail` / `daemon` itself) — Diagnostics restart actions.
   - `bind-privileged-port` (deferred post-MVP; placeholder).
6. **Approval flow**: Diagnostics action → daemon creates approval entry (hp-v0g) with riskClass=`elevated`, scope=`once`, expiry=10min → owner approves → daemon mints approval token (HMAC over actor+action+nonce+expiry) → daemon invokes helper with token → helper validates + executes + posts result to daemon → daemon writes `setup_helper_action_completed` audit entry with result + helper exit code.

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Permission boundary smoke** — daemon process running as `hoopoe` user attempts: chown root-owned dir → fails with EPERM; install systemd unit by writing `/etc/systemd/system/test.service` → fails with EACCES; bind to port 443 → fails with EACCES. Log: each attempted operation + actual errno + daemon refusal handler.
2. **Allowlist enforcement** — helper invoked with action `arbitrary-shell-command` (not in allowlist) → exits 1 + audit-log entry "rejected by allowlist." Log: full audit entry.
3. **Allowlist tamper detection** — modify `/etc/hoopoe/setup-helper.allowed` (add new action) without daemon-recorded checksum update → helper refuses with "allowlist checksum mismatch." Log: pre/post checksums, helper stderr.
4. **Approval token validation** — helper invoked with: (a) no approval token → refuses if not bootstrap-mode; (b) expired approval token → refuses with "approval expired"; (c) approval token signed with wrong secret → refuses with "approval signature invalid"; (d) approval token already consumed → refuses with "approval already used." Log: each branch's full validation.
5. **Bootstrap-mode deferred audit** — helper runs install-systemd-unit during bootstrap (no daemon); logs to `/var/log/hoopoe-setup-helper.bootstrap.log`; on first daemon start, daemon ingests the log and writes formal audit entries with `bootstrap_mode: true`. Log: bootstrap log content, audit ingestion outcome.
6. **Action argv shape validation** — helper called with `install-systemd-unit --unit-path=/tmp/evil.service` (unexpected unit path) → refuses with "argv does not match allowlist shape." Log: full argv diff.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Fresh install end-to-end** — bootstrap.sh on clean Ubuntu VPS:
   - Creates `hoopoe` user.
   - Helper installs systemd unit.
   - Daemon starts as `hoopoe` user.
   - Verify: `ps -p $(systemctl show -p MainPID hoopoe.service --value) -o user=` returns `hoopoe`.
   - Verify: `id hoopoe` shows expected group memberships.
   - Verify: `/etc/sudoers.d/hoopoe` contains EXACTLY the narrow setup-helper rule.
   - Log: every step's stdout, files created, permissions table.
2. **Privileged repair via Diagnostics** — Diagnostics → "Re-run ACFS doctor with auto-fix" → approval queue entry → owner approves via UI → daemon invokes helper → helper executes ACFS doctor with sudo → result streamed back to daemon → audit chain visible. Log: approval flow, helper invocation, ACFS doctor output, audit dump.
3. **Tampering attempt** — manually edit `/etc/hoopoe/setup-helper.allowed` to add `arbitrary-shell-command` action → next privileged repair attempt → helper refuses with checksum mismatch → daemon shows red Diagnostics warning "allowlist tampered." Log: full sequence.
4. **Unprivileged escalation attempt** — daemon's renderer-issued IPC asks to run `arbitrary-shell-command`; daemon's command policy refuses at IPC validation (before helper even invoked); audit logs attempted-escalation. Log: IPC payload, refusal handler trace.
5. **Helper crash recovery** — helper invoked, exits with SIGSEGV mid-action → daemon detects nonzero exit → marks approval entry as "failed/incomplete"; Diagnostics shows recovery action "Inspect partial result." Log: crash signal, recovery flow.
6. **Concurrent helper invocations** — daemon issues two approval tokens for two different actions simultaneously; helper enforces a file-lock; second invocation waits up to 30s then fails gracefully if first hangs. Log: lock acquisition timestamps.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.5 — daemon systemd unit hardening (the source of this requirement).
- §5.3 — command safety + DCG ingestion.
- §10 — audit log infrastructure.
- §10.2 — Diagnostics repair actions (consumers of helper for elevated repairs).
- hp-n30 — systemd unit + bootstrap.sh (this bead extends with least-priv + helper).
- hp-v0g — unified approvals queue (helper actions go through it).
- hp-g73 — audit log infrastructure.
- hp-je1p — daemon redaction layer.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- bootstrap.sh creates `hoopoe` system user with documented uid range + group memberships.
- Setup helper executable installed at `/usr/local/bin/hoopoe-setup-helper` with allowlist file in `/etc/hoopoe/setup-helper.allowed`.
- Narrow sudoers rule installed in `/etc/sudoers.d/hoopoe`.
- All 6 unit tests + 6 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.
- Diagnostics privileged repair actions (hp-6d7) wired through helper.
- Daemon process verified to run as non-root (`ps -p $PID -o user=` returns `hoopoe`).
- Permission boundary smoke test runs in CI on every PR touching daemon code.
- Tamper-detection test runs in CI nightly.

title: Phase 3: ACFS onboarding wizard + tool inventory
description:
GOAL (§12 Phase 3, §6.1)
=========================
Wrap the canonical agent-flywheel.com 13-step VPS setup wizard in Hoopoe's first-run wizard, layer Hoopoe-specific extensions on top (daemon install, Oracle, jsm/jfp, GitHub auth), and produce a ready VPS lifecycle (unconfigured → ssh_verified → daemon_running → tools_installed → ready).

WHY (§6.1, §6.3)
=================
The canonical wizard is the authoritative guide; Hoopoe wraps, never replaces it. Automating SSH key handling, preflight, ACFS install streaming, doctor parsing, tool inventory, and daemon pairing reduces a 30-minute manual procedure to a guided 5-minute experience while preserving all canonical safety semantics.

13 CANONICAL STEPS + HOOPOE EXTENSIONS (§6.1)
==============================================
1. Choose OS — Mac-only v1.
2. Install Terminal — n/a; Diagnostics 'recovery shell' is the break-glass.
3. Generate SSH Key — ed25519 via Hoopoe or import.
4. Rent VPS — provider's site (Contabo / OVH / existing); plugin automation post-MVP.
5. Create Instance — paste pubkey at provider.
6. SSH Connect — Hoopoe's tunnel manager.
7. Set Up Accounts — CLIs' native auth on VPS; CAAM verifies in step 12.
8. Pre-Flight Check — Hoopoe streams structured pass/fail.
9. Run Installer — canonical curl|bash, structured phase parser.
10. Reconnect — automatic.
11. Verify Key — acfs doctor --json, structured rendering.
12. Status Check — tool inventory; CAAM subscription verification.
13. Launch Onboard — optional link, not gate.

Hoopoe-specific extensions:
- After step 9: install/update Hoopoe daemon binary + systemd unit (§6.5).
- After step 10: persistent SSH tunnel + pairing→bearer exchange (§5.2, §6.3).
- After step 12: configure Oracle (oracle serve on Mac, --remote-host wiring); configure GitHub auth so desktop's local clone (§7.7) fetches without prompts; configure jsm or jfp.
- In place of step 13: 'VPS Ready' screen with optional 'Launch onboard' + primary CTA to import/create first project.

LOCAL-DEMO PATH (§6.1)
=======================
Wizard offers a 'Local demo' path bypassing steps 4–11: daemon runs on user's Mac, Mock Flywheel Mode supplies fixture data, user navigates the four-stage shell against replayed snapshots without owning a VPS or subscription.

EXIT (§12)
==========
A fresh supported VPS reaches 'ready' state from Hoopoe.

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1 Phase 3)
================================
ACFS install/doctor runs through Hoopoe with structured checkpoints; a failed run resumes; raw-log fallback works when markers are missing.

title: Phase 5: Planning pipeline test suite (CLI mocks, Oracle mock, multi-model concurrency, refinement rounds, lock semantics, model-context policy)
description:
WHAT
====
Phase 5 test suite covering the multi-model planning pipeline, Oracle adapter, plan editor + artifact rail, plan locking, and the model-context policy gating model calls.

UNIT TESTS
============
- Chat-box plan input: textarea + per-plan model picker (ChatGPT Pro default, Claude Opus / Gemini 3 Pro / one optional third); 'Let Hoopoe choose' toggle picks 4-way default; clarify-vs-take-first-shot binary; greys out unconfigured-subscription models.
- CLI adapters (Claude Code / Codex CLI / Gemini CLI): subprocess spawn, stdout/stderr capture per-candidate, parse model output into per-candidate markdown; CAAM account selection per call; error mode = blank candidate file with reason recorded.
- Oracle adapter: 'oracle serve' lifecycle on Mac (start/stop/health); --remote-host/--remote-token config; browser-mode auto-reattach on long Pro runs; 'Mac must stay awake' warning during active Pro round; Pro-round status (queued/drafting/completed/failed) surfaces in plan-job UI.
- Multi-model pipeline orchestration: 3-4 candidates run concurrently, comparative-matrix builds when all complete, synthesis runs with primary model as arbiter, fresh-eyes critique runs in fresh primary-model session, refinement rounds 1-5 run in fresh sessions per the 'no anchoring' pattern.
- Plan editor (CodeMirror 6): markdown editing, side-by-side comparative-matrix view, per-round artifact rail, history.jsonl streaming.
- Plan locking: writes final plan.md, creates snapshot hash, blocks on unresolved-decisions.md, marks meta.json {locked: true}, enables 'Convert to beads'.
- Plan amend: locked plan amend creates new version, triggers bead-delta analysis.
- Plan quality tracker: 7 dimensions (intent, architecture, workflow, implementation, testing, risk, bead readiness) computed deterministically + model-based with manual override.
- Per-step prompts versioned + regression-tested (hp-3ab integration): each step's exact prompt is committed under packages/planning-prompts/; tests assert prompt content matches expected hash.
- Model-context policy enforcement (hp-e33): before any model call, daemon records context manifest (sources included, redactions applied, provider/model/account, allowing rule); raw source files only sent if policy allows; secrets/scanned-sensitive-strings block or redact per policy.
- Subscription-window usage from caut: per-call wall-clock + harness identity + CAAM account + input-hash + artifact-hash recorded; UI shows 'X% of daily Y' from caut snapshots, 'unmeasured' when caut unavailable.

E2E TESTS
==========
1. One-paragraph idea → 4 candidates (1 Oracle ChatGPT Pro + 3 CLI) → matrix → synthesis → critique → 4 refinement rounds → lock; survives desktop restart between any two stages (Phase 5 acceptance).
2. ChatGPT Pro round running → close laptop lid → reopen → round paused; resume against the still-running 'oracle serve' on Mac (or fail cleanly if Mac was offline).
3. Subscription not configured: empty-state of new-plan screen makes the requirement explicit, points to subscription pages; new-plan blocked.
4. Model-context policy: project policy says 'no raw source to external API' → planning model gets summaries-only; manifest records the redactions.
5. Lock gate: unresolved-decisions.md non-empty → lock blocked with explanation; user resolves → lock proceeds; meta.json updated.
6. Bead-delta on amend: locked plan v1 → amend → v2; conversion shows deltas (new beads needed, old beads to retire, traceability impact).
7. Concurrency: launch two plan-jobs in same project simultaneously → daemon serializes through job registry; no artifact corruption.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Every model call logs: provider, model, harness, CAAM account, prompt hash, response hash, wall-clock duration, caut quota delta if available.
- Bearer tokens, browser cookies, model API responses NEVER captured in raw form (redaction).
- Per-round artifact hashes logged so test failures can pinpoint which round produced bad output.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 — Planning workspace strategic intent.
- §16 Phase 5 — engineering tasks.
- §18.1 Phase 5 acceptance row.
- hp-z5r / hp-8ec / hp-du6 / hp-xr8 / hp-al4 / hp-x3z / hp-s00 / hp-3ab / hp-pmw / hp-e33 — Phase 5 implementation beads.
- hp-qrk / hp-lxs / hp-6sv / hp-q3t — substrate.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Unit coverage ≥85% on adapters + pipeline orchestrator + lock gate.
- All 7 e2e scenarios pass.
- ChatGPT Pro happy/failure paths verified against real Oracle on macOS dev machine.
- caut redaction tests cover all sensitive fields.
- Per-step prompts have committed hashes; drift fails CI.

title: SLB integration (optional two-person co-signature for autopilot destructive class)
description:
WHAT
====
SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button) integration per §5.3 + §7.3 'Optional SLB add-on'. Optional add-on for the autopilot safety preset. When enabled, SLB co-signature is required for the destructive-action class. Hoopoe's approvals UI reflects SLB state and never bypasses it.

WHY (§5.3, §7.3)
==================
Especially relevant when running autopilot against a shared/team VPS. SLB requires two persons to co-sign before destructive actions execute.

INTEGRATION
=============
- SLB toggle in safety-preset configurator (Phase 8 task).
- When ON: every destructive action requires both an approver AND a co-signer; ApprovalDialog gains a 'co-signer' field.
- SLB state visible in approvals UI.
- Audit log records both decision actors.

POST-MVP STATUS (§13 'Can defer')
====================================
SLB is can-defer; lands in Phase 13 polish or post-MVP.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.3 — SLB add-on.
- §7.3 — autopilot preset can require SLB.
- approvals queue epic.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- SLB integration scaffolded.
- Toggle in safety preset configurator.
- ApprovalDialog gains co-signer field when SLB is ON.
- Audit records both actors.
- Adapter contract test passes.

title: Phase 13: Crash reports opt-in + production telemetry + Diagnostics polish
description:
WHAT
====
Crash reporting (opt-in only); production telemetry (anonymized event counts, NOT user data); Diagnostics screen final polish.

CRASH REPORTS
===============
- Opt-in only (default OFF).
- Captures: stack trace, daemon version, desktop version, recent audit-log slice (redacted).
- Endpoint: a Hoopoe-owned crash collector.
- User can download own crash report + delete.

TELEMETRY
===========
- Opt-in.
- Reports: stage usage counts, swarm sizes, tending-job firing rates, error rates.
- NEVER reports: code content, plan content, bead content, model conversations.
- Collected anonymously.

DIAGNOSTICS POLISH (§10.2)
============================
- All repair actions visible + audited.
- Per-tool inventory + capability degraded reasoning.
- Live audit-log tail.
- Tending job table.
- Capability registry.
- Connection status (tunnel + WS + bearer).
- Resource pressure indicators.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §10.2 — Diagnostics + repair actions.
- §10.3 — retention defaults.
- §10.4 — backup/export/restore (separate area).
- §5.4 — redaction layer applies to crash reports.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Opt-in crash reporting works.
- Opt-in telemetry works.
- Diagnostics screen polished + complete.
- All repair actions wired.
- Privacy / opt-in tested: default state never sends data.

title: GitAdapter — per-project Git plumbing wrapper (status / diff / push / log / show / blame for daemon RPCs)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side Git adapter wrapping per-project plumbing for the Phase 4 RPCs (getWorkingTreeStatus, getStagedDiff, getUnstagedDiff, getUnpushedCommits) and Phase 12 review-round file fetches.

WRAPPED COMMANDS
==================
- git status (porcelain)
- git diff --staged
- git diff
- git rev-list <ref>..<ref>
- git log
- git show
- git push (audited; force-push gated through approvals queue)
- git fetch
- git rev-parse
- git ls-tree
- git blame
- git remote
- git branch

EXECUTION
===========
All commands run on the VPS via the daemon (per §5.3 'desktop never invokes project-level git'). The desktop's local clone reads (Phase 4 local-clone task) are an exception and use plumbing locally.

CAPABILITY IDS (§2.8)
======================
- git.status.read
- git.diff.read
- git.push (destructive class for force-push; basic push is the auto-push hook)
- git.unpushed.list

SECRET REDACTION
==================
Output (especially git log + git show) may include user names/emails — do NOT redact those (they're public). DO redact any secret-shaped strings that leak into commit messages (rare but possible).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.3 — adapter precedence (Git: plumbing first; ru for multi-project; ru review/agent-sweep/ai-sync/dep-update NOT adopted).
- §5.3 — destructive ops require approval.
- audit log — every git operation audited.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All commands wrapped + tested.
- Force-push gated through approvals queue.
- Adapter contract tests (normal, missing-tool, malformed, timeout, high-volume) pass.
- Used by Phase 4 + Phase 12 + Phase 4 auto-push hook.

title: Documentation suite (per Appendix A new-home table)
description:
WHAT
====
Write the docs that Appendix A says replace the heavyweight content originally in plan.full.md.

DOCS REQUIRED (§Appendix A)
=============================
- top-level README.md (repo workspace structure)
- docs/source-of-truth.md (persistent data layout: /data/projects/, ~/.hoopoe/, ~/Library/Application Support/Hoopoe/)
- docs/security.md (CommandSpec, full approval-checkpoint matrix, audit-log schema)

- docs/testing.md (desktop tests, daemon tests, integration scenarios, E2E disposable VPS, smoke checks)

Plus the docs from earlier phases:
- docs/api-seed.md (Phase 2.5)
- docs/process-manager.md (Phase 2.5)
- docs/reconnect-replay.md (Phase 2.5)
- docs/source-provenance.md (Phase 1)
- docs/research-spike/* (Phase 0)
- docs/integration-contracts/* (Phase 0)
- docs/risks.md (risk-mitigation evidence)
- docs/upgrade-and-rollback.md
- docs/troubleshooting.md
- docs/getting-started.md
- docs/wizard.md
- docs/adr/0001-single-vps-per-install-v1.md (Phase 13 ADR)

EACH DOC
==========
- Lives in the repo (not in plan.md).
- Cross-references the relevant §X sections.
- Updated as code changes — living references.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- Appendix A — new-home table.
- §15 — definition of success references docs.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All docs above exist + non-empty.
- Cross-references to phase beads where applicable.
- README.md at repo root has clear entry points.
- Phase 13 acceptance: a new contributor can find everything they need from the docs without reading plan.md.

title: Phase 1 / Phase 3 / §6.1: First-run wizard 'Local demo' path tied to Mock Flywheel Mode bootstrap
description:
WHAT
====
The first-run wizard's second-track 'Local demo' path (§6.1 'Local-demo path'). Bypasses canonical 13-step VPS flow entirely: daemon spawns on the user's Mac in Mock Flywheel Mode (§13), no SSH/VPS/subscriptions required, fixture corpus loaded, and the user can navigate every stage of the cockpit against replayed snapshots within ~30 seconds of clicking 'Try local demo'.

WHY (§6.1 'Local-demo path', §13 'Must include for development and demos')
============================================================================
§6.1 says: 'The wizard also offers a Local demo path that bypasses steps 4–11 entirely: the daemon runs on the user's Mac, Mock Flywheel Mode (§13) supplies fixture data, and the user can navigate the four-stage shell against replayed snapshots without owning a VPS or any subscriptions.'

This path exists for:
- **Reviewers + contributors** evaluating Hoopoe before they invest in a VPS + subscriptions (-656/mo per §13).
- **CI** running deterministic UI smoke tests without infra.
- **Support repro bundles** (a user submits a fixture + steps; the team replays exactly).
- **Sales / demos** where you want to show the cockpit without exposing real production traffic.

It is NOT a supported way to run real swarms (§6.1 last line). The wizard surface MUST make this distinction unmistakable so users don't leave thinking they're running 'lite' Hoopoe.

WIZARD UX
==========

ENTRY (Wizard step 0)
----------------------
Two prominent CTAs side by side:
  ┌──────────────────────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────────────────┐
  │  CONNECT YOUR VPS               │    │  TRY LOCAL DEMO                  │
  │  Real Hoopoe — your VPS,         │    │  Replayed fixtures, no VPS,      │
  │  your subscriptions, your code.  │    │  no subscriptions. Read-only.    │
  │  Takes ~10 min.                  │    │  Takes ~30 sec.                  │
  └──────────────────────────────────┘    └──────────────────────────────────┘

LOCAL DEMO PATH STEPS
----------------------
1. **Step LD-1: Pick fixture** — list of bundled fixture corpora ('Empty new project', 'Mid-implementation 100-bead project', 'Hardening-stage project with 12 active findings', 'Stuck swarm with rate-limited Claude agent'). One row per fixture; preview thumbnail + description.
2. **Step LD-2: Daemon-on-Mac launch** — spawn the daemon binary locally (bundled with the DMG) on a free port (findOpenPort logic). FD-3 envelope (§5.2 + Appendix B) used for token handoff (the local-demo path is the one place this pattern is active for v1). Surface 'starting local daemon...' progress.
3. **Step LD-3: MFM activation** — daemon starts in Mock Flywheel Mode flag (--mock-flywheel + --fixture-path); loads the chosen fixture corpus into the read-model cache; the daemon's adapter calls return fixture data instead of touching real tools.
4. **Step LD-4: Navigate** — wizard closes; user lands on the project's primary stage (Plans / Beads / Swarm / Hardening — depends on fixture state). A persistent banner across the top: 'LOCAL DEMO MODE — Mock Flywheel data; nothing here is real. [Switch to real VPS] [Pick a different fixture]'.

PERSISTENT 'DEMO' BADGE
------------------------
Across every screen + every event in the Activity panel + every audit entry, the demo label is unmistakable:
- Top bar: yellow 'DEMO' pill replacing the project name's normal styling.
- Activity panel: each event row stamped with 'DEMO' source tag.
- Audit log entries written with actor.kind='mock' (still audited; still inspectable; clearly NOT a real swarm action).
- Window title: '[DEMO] Hoopoe — {fixture-name}'.

PROJECT STATE ISOLATION
------------------------
Local demo daemon writes to ~/.hoopoe/demo/{fixture-id}/ — completely separate from the real ~/.hoopoe/ tree. Switching to a real VPS later does NOT see demo state; demo state survives reopens for as long as the user wants but is wiped on 'Switch to real VPS' confirmation.

DAEMON SHIM
============
The MFM daemon (--mock-flywheel) IS the production daemon binary with one extra subsystem:
- MockFlywheelAdapter wraps each real adapter (NTM, br, bv, Agent Mail, Git, health). Each call returns fixture-corpus-driven data instead of executing the real tool.
- WS event stream replays the fixture's recorded event timeline at real-time pace (or accelerated, configurable) so the user sees the swarm 'unfold' as it did when the fixture was captured.
- Write actions (claim bead, push commit, etc.) are accepted, audited, but operate ONLY on the fixture's in-memory copy — they never touch real tools.

THIS BEAD'S SCOPE vs. hp-dr8 / hp-o74 / hp-q3t
================================================
- **hp-dr8 (epic)**: Mock Flywheel Mode infrastructure overall.
- **hp-o74**: Phase 1 wiring of MFM into desktop boot (so unit tests can replay fixtures without a real daemon).
- **hp-q3t**: Fixture replay harness + scenario library + assertion helpers (testing tooling).
- **THIS BEAD**: The user-facing wizard 'Local demo' branching path AND the daemon's --mock-flywheel mode wiring + persistent 'DEMO' badging across the cockpit + project-state isolation. The thing a user CLICKS, not the harness contributors run.

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Wizard branching renders** — wizard renders both CTAs; click 'Try local demo' navigates to LD-1; click 'Connect your VPS' navigates to canonical step 1. Log: wizard state transitions.
2. **Fixture list loads** — LD-1 loads 4 bundled fixtures from disk; each fixture has manifest validated (id, name, description, thumbnail, expected stages). Log: per-fixture manifest parse.
3. **Daemon-on-Mac launch** — local daemon process spawn with --mock-flywheel + --fixture-path; FD-3 envelope receives token; daemon /v1/version returns mock=true. Log: spawn args, FD-3 contents, /v1/version response.
4. **MFM adapter call** — call mock NTMAdapter.listSessions on a fixture with 3 sessions; assert returns 3 sessions matching fixture data; no real ntm process spawned. Log: adapter call + return + process check.
5. **Project state isolation** — write a simulated bead-claim in demo mode; verify ~/.hoopoe/demo/{id}/ has the change but ~/.hoopoe/ does not. Log: filesystem diff before/after.
6. **DEMO badge propagates** — render top bar / Activity panel row / audit entry; assert each carries DEMO label. Log: rendered HTML for each.
7. **Switch-to-real-VPS confirmation** — click 'Switch to real VPS' shows confirmation dialog; on confirm, demo daemon SIGTERM + ~/.hoopoe/demo/{id}/ wiped; wizard restarts at step 0. Log: dialog state, daemon termination, filesystem wipe.
8. **Fixture switching** — Switch to a different fixture mid-demo; verify clean transition (demo daemon restart with new fixture, no state leak from previous fixture). Log: transition timeline.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Wizard → LocalDemo → Beads stage end-to-end** — fresh app launch (no prior daemon, no prior project); click 'Try local demo' → pick 'Mid-implementation 100-bead project' fixture → daemon spawns → MFM loads fixture → cockpit lands on Beads stage with 100 fixture beads visible; entire flow under 30 seconds. Log file: e2e-localdemo-{ts}.jsonl with per-step timing.
2. **All four stages navigable** — from #1's state, navigate Plans → Beads → Swarm → Hardening; each renders the fixture's data; Activity panel populated; no errors thrown; DEMO badge visible everywhere. Log: per-stage render + DEMO badge presence check.
3. **Event replay timing** — 'Stuck swarm' fixture has a 5-minute recorded timeline of agents going from healthy → wedged; replayed at 1× shows the right events in order; replayed at 10× completes in 30s with same events in same order. Log: full event stream timing.
4. **Switch-to-real-VPS clean transition** — start in local demo; click 'Switch to real VPS'; demo daemon stopped + state wiped + wizard at step 0; user can then proceed with VPS connection without any demo state contamination. Log: pre / post filesystem state.
5. **Audit entries clearly distinguished** — perform 5 simulated actions in demo (claim bead, send marching orders, force-release reservation); audit log has entries with actor.kind='mock' AND wizard-id correlation; verify ~/.hoopoe/audit.jsonl has NO entries from demo actions (demo audit lives in ~/.hoopoe/demo/{id}/audit.jsonl). Log: audit dumps from both locations.
6. **CI smoke** — automated CI test launches local demo + navigates all stages + asserts no console errors + screenshot diff against baseline. Log: CI run with screenshots.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.1 — local-demo path spec.
- §13 — Mock Flywheel Mode requirement.
- §5.2 — FD-3 envelope (used here for local-daemon token handoff).
- Appendix B — t3code FD-3 reference (deferred to local-demo path per §5.2).
- hp-dr8 — Mock Flywheel Mode epic (parent rollup).
- hp-o74 — Phase 1 MFM desktop boot wiring.
- hp-q3t — fixture-replay harness.
- hp-wle — Phase 0 fixture corpus seed.
- hp-o6q — first-run wizard UI (canonical 13-step branch).
- hp-zir — main.ts decomposition (BackendLifecycle launches local daemon for demo).
- hp-15s — vendored t3code lifecycle files.
- hp-g73 — audit log isolation per actor.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Wizard step 0 renders both CTAs; click routes correctly.
- Local-demo path implements LD-1 through LD-4.
- Daemon binary supports --mock-flywheel + --fixture-path flags.
- MFM adapters wired around every real adapter.
- ~/.hoopoe/demo/{fixture-id}/ project state isolated from ~/.hoopoe/.
- DEMO badge present in top bar + Activity panel + audit entries + window title.
- Fixture switching + switch-to-real-VPS work cleanly.
- CI smoke automated.
- All 8 unit tests + 6 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.
close_reason: LocalDemo bootstrap orchestrator: catalog of 4 demo fixtures, state isolator with defense-in-depth wipe, start/end/swap session, IpcRegistry wiring, AuthBridge token plumbing, DEMO window title and audit actor stamp. 13/13 bun tests pass.

title: Cross-cutting: Renderer + cache + transport hardening (§5.4 — CSP, contextIsolation, sandbox, redaction, TOFU rules)
description:
WHAT
====
Cross-cutting epic for §5.4 'Renderer, cache, and transport hardening' — the security posture that makes Hoopoe safe to ship. NOT a feature epic; a security hardening epic that every other phase depends on.

WHY
===
§5.4 lists eight specific hardening requirements. Without an explicit cross-cutting epic, they get scattered across phase beads + risk being implemented inconsistently or dropped. Concentrating them in one epic with explicit child tasks gives the Hoopoe-as-product the same threat-model rigor it expects users to apply to their own swarms.

CHILD TASKS (created under this epic)
======================================
1. **Electron contextIsolation: true / sandbox: true / nodeIntegration: false / strict CSP** wired in main.ts. Lint rule on app/desktop/src/**/*.tsx ensures no direct fs / net / child_process / SSH / Keychain access from renderer.
2. **Preload — small typed API only** — every privileged operation goes through main-process IPC handlers that validate schemas + user intent.
3. **Bearer + SSH passphrase storage** — Keychain/safeStorage only; never desktop SQLite cache.
4. **Daemon-side audit log + structured-log redaction layer** — bearer tokens, model API keys, SSH passphrases, browser cookies, pairing tokens, provider creds, paths-under-secrets-dirs all redacted before persistence + before streaming to clients. Layer is auditable via trace-level events for which sub-pattern matched.
5. **TLS / mTLS fingerprint TOFU rules** — direct/tailnet mode uses TOFU only when the fingerprint arrived over an authenticated SSH bootstrap channel.
6. **Daemon-bind safety** — refuses to bind publicly without both a config flag AND a runtime confirmation. Diagnostics shows red warning for any non-local/tailnet bind.
7. **Local-clone untrust** — code rendering uses HTML escaping; Markdown preview disables unsafe HTML by default; shellpath rendering quotes argv; no exec on parsed content.
8. **Lint rule for provider SDK imports** (Appendix C #11) — already covered by hp-ara; this epic makes it visible as a security guard, not a stylistic preference.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.4 — full hardening list.
- §5.1, §5.2, §5.3, §5.5 — adjacent security sections.
- Appendix C #11 — provider-SDK lint rule.
- §14 risks — 'Unsafe commands accidentally exposed', 'Lifted code carries Codex-shaped assumptions'.
- hp-spx — Keychain integration (consumer of #3 above).
- hp-g73 — audit log infrastructure (#4 above).
- hp-rcm — DCG verdict ingestion (parallel surface).
- hp-v0g — unified approvals (parallel surface).
- hp-ara — provider SDK lint (already exists; this epic links it to a coherent set).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 8 hardening requirements have child tasks under this epic.
- Each child task has its own DoD with verifiable assertions.
- Pen-test fixture in chaos suite (hp-2qn) covers each requirement.
- A 'security checklist' in docs/security.md maps every §5.4 requirement to its child bead.

title: Structured-logging library (desktop TS + daemon Go) with redaction sink + test-capture transport
description:
WHAT
====
A small, single-shape structured-logging library used by every production code path AND every test. Same field set, same redaction, same JSON envelope across desktop renderer, desktop main process, and daemon. Tests share the same logger via a 'capture' transport that buffers entries per-test and dumps them on assertion failure.

ENVELOPE SHAPE (canonical)
===========================
{
  ts: RFC3339nano,
  level: 'trace'|'debug'|'info'|'warn'|'error'|'fatal',
  msg: string,
  component: 'desktop.renderer'|'desktop.main'|'daemon.<subsystem>'|'test.<phase>',
  subsystem: string,        // narrower bucket within component
  correlationId?: string,   // set by request-scoped context (HTTP/WS/job)
  causationId?: string,     // command/event/job that triggered this log
  actor?: {kind: 'user'|'agent'|'tending_job'|'pre_script'|'system', id: string},
  jobId?, beadId?, swarmId?, planId?, runId?,
  fields: Record<string, unknown>   // structured key/value, non-PII
}

REDACTION RULES (must run BEFORE the entry is buffered)
==========================================================
- Bearer tokens (HMAC-signed base64url shape) — replaced with sha256:<8> tag.
- Provider keys: any string matching sk-, sk-ant-, AIza, AKIA, ghp_, xox, BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY, BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY.
- 12-char Crockford pairing tokens (single-use) — replaced with [pairing-token-redacted].
- SSH passphrases — never logged in any code path; redaction is a defense-in-depth.
- Browser cookies for chatgpt.com — never logged; defense-in-depth.
- Email/Telegram delivery target addresses — last 4 chars only.
- File paths under ~/.ssh/, /etc/shadow, /private/var/db/ — redacted to placeholder.
- Free-form text fields (msg + fields.*) pass through redactor for these patterns regardless of where they came from.

TRANSPORT TARGETS
==================
- production: ~/.hoopoe/logs/<component>-<date>.log (rotated daily, kept per §10.3).
- desktop dev: console + file.
- daemon dev: stderr (journald-friendly) + file.
- tests: in-memory ring per test, dumped to evidence file on failure (and via --log-on-pass for debugging green tests).
- structured: same JSON envelope across all transports — so audit-log replay tools can ingest test logs unchanged.

INTEGRATION INVARIANTS
========================
- Logger is the ONLY logging path; raw console.log / fmt.Println in production code fails CI lint.
- Every HTTP/WS handler entry creates a request-scoped logger with correlationId.
- Every job execution creates a job-scoped logger with jobId + correlationId.
- Every typed action execution (§8.3.1) creates an action-scoped logger with runId + jobId + correlationId.
