- The redaction layer is auditable: redaction events are themselves logged at trace level (which sub-pattern matched) so we can verify redaction is firing without leaking the secret.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.4 — every meaningful action is inspectable; logs are half of that surface.
- §5.4 — daemon logs and audit entries pass through a redaction layer before persistence and streaming.
- §10 — audit log shape; this library uses the same envelope.
- §10.3 — retention/compaction (logs follow the same defaults).
- hp-g73 — audit log infrastructure (must share envelope + redaction with this library).
- hp-qrk — parent epic.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- @hoopoe/logger TS package + internal/logger Go package shipped.
- Redaction unit tests cover every named pattern + adversarial inputs (e.g. nested in JSON, in URL query strings, in stack traces).
- Lint rule: console.log / fmt.Println fails build outside vendored/ and tests.
- Test-capture transport dumps last 200 entries on assertion failure.
- Audit log writer (hp-g73) consumes the same envelope.
- docs/observability.md documents the envelope and redaction list as authoritative.
close_reason: Implemented in 949ad1a — structured logger (TS + Go) with redaction + test capture + CI lint. apps/daemon/internal/logger/, apps/desktop/src/shared/logger/, scripts/loggerlint/, docs/observability.md. 76 tests green; lint wired into bun run lint + bun run test. Closing via JSONL workaround due to BR DB FK / malformed-page issue (matches recovery in 77abef9).

title: Per-stage detailed unit + e2e test suites with high-quality logging (cross-phase test scaffolding)
description:
WHAT
====
Comprehensive unit + e2e test suites with detailed logging across every phase. 'So we can be sure everything is working perfectly after implementation.'

UNIT TEST COVERAGE TARGETS
=============================
- Desktop renderer: ≥80% statement coverage on core modules (Settings, Auth, Activity, Beads view, Plan view, Swarm view, Health view).
- Daemon Go: ≥85% statement coverage on adapters, scheduler, action executor, audit log, redaction, Git watcher.
- packages/schemas: 100% (generators ensure no hand-written code).

E2E TEST COVERAGE
====================
Per phase acceptance test (§18.1):
- Phase 0: snapshot script reproducibility.
- Phase 1: signed DMG + 4-stage navigation + ⌘K + settings hot-reload.
- Phase 2: cold-VPS bootstrap + bearer round-trip + disconnect/reconnect replay.
- Phase 3: ACFS install with structured checkpoints + failed-run resume + raw-log fallback.
- Phase 4: VPS commit → auto-push → origin_updated → desktop fetch < 3s; WIP overlay correct.
- Phase 5: one-paragraph idea → candidates → synthesis → critique → refinement → lock; survives desktop restart.
- Phase 6: locked plan → br beads + traceability; polish round-trip.
- Phase 7: 500-node DAG renders with critical path + ready frontier highlight.
- Phase 8: 4-agent swarm launch via NTM + staggered kickoff + agent registers; user follows from bead board + agent grid + Activity panel; Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' opt-in works.
- Phase 9: Activity drawer shows mail + reservations + bead changes + urgent alerts + user↔orchestrator chat from any stage.
- Phase 10: healthy-hour fixture: zero LLM cost + zero panel noise; stuck-fixture triggers skill-driven intervention + audit.
- Phase 11: health scan in worktree + top-bar pill updates + create bead from hotspot.
- Phase 12: review round → findings; convergence dashboard updates.
- Phase 13: existing-VPS path remains green; provider plugin creates + estimates + tears down.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- All test runs emit structured JSON logs.
- Failure: full repro context (git SHA, daemon version, fixture scenario, exact step).
- Success: timing + resource usage so SLO regressions are visible.
- Test evidence committed to docs/test-evidence/<phase>/<timestamp>/ for audit.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §18 — full verification matrix.
- §10.5 — SLO targets.
- packages/fixtures — test inputs.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Coverage targets met across desktop + daemon + schemas.
- Every Phase N acceptance test runs in CI.
- Test logs structured + queryable.
- Test evidence committed per release.

title: Phase 5 / §7.1: Import-existing-plan flow (markdown paste OR .md file picker from local clone) — store under .hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/, run quality review, optionally trigger refinement rounds before lock or convert
description:
WHAT
====
§7.1 names two plan-entry modes: 'Import an existing plan' and 'Create a new plan from a rough idea.' hp-z5r owns the **chat-box create-from-rough-idea UX** with primary picker, competing-candidate selector, and clarify/take-first-shot toggle. The **import flow** does NOT have a focused bead — yet the plan is explicit: 'paste markdown or pick a .md file from the local clone. Hoopoe stores it under .hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/plan.md, runs a quality review, and offers to optionally run one or more refinement rounds before conversion.'

This bead implements that import flow end-to-end so users with an existing plan markdown can adopt it without retyping into the chat box.

WHY THIS IS A FOCUSED BEAD
==========================
Existing coverage:
- **hp-z5r** — Chat-box plan-input UX (the *create* path).
- **hp-al4** — Multi-model planning pipeline (candidates → matrix → synthesis → critique → 4-5 refinement rounds → lock).
- **hp-x3z** — Plan locking + version history + bead-delta-on-amend.
- **hp-s00** — Plan quality tracker (the dimensions used in the post-import review).
- **hp-rsly** — Existing-codebase context bundle (attached when project has a populated repo).
- **hp-2n1** — Local clone (where the user picks the .md from).
- **hp-3xzu** — Local search (helps user locate plan candidates).

What's missing: the focused **import surface** with file picker + paste textarea + provenance recording + post-import quality review + optional-refinement-rounds UI. Without it, a user with an existing plan must paste it into the chat-box-for-rough-ideas, which is the wrong shape: rough ideas are seeds for synthesis from N candidates, while existing plans are *already* the synthesis result and should skip straight to fresh-eyes critique + refinement (or directly to lock if quality is high).

ENTRY UI
========
Plan workspace empty state has two CTAs (per §7.1 'Plan entry'):
1. **Create a new plan** → hp-z5r flow.
2. **Import an existing plan** → this flow's modal/route.

Import flow has 3 sub-paths surfaced as tabs in the import modal:
- **Paste markdown** — textarea with a preview pane (CodeMirror 6 + Markdown rendering); minimum 200 words to enable Continue; max 200KB.
- **Pick a file** — file picker scoped to the local clone (hp-2n1) by default; user can also pick an arbitrary local file. Picker uses the local search (hp-3xzu) for fast 'find that .md' UX. Files <50 KB load instantly; larger files show a confirmation step ('This is a 180KB plan; large plans take longer to refine. Continue?').
- **Re-import a previous Hoopoe plan** — list of locked plans from this OR other Hoopoe projects (read from `.hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/plan.md` on the VPS daemon). Useful when user wants to fork a plan template.

PROVENANCE
==========
`meta.json` records:
- `source_mode: 'import'` (vs 'chat-box' / 'extend-existing')
- `source: { kind: 'paste' | 'file' | 'reimport', source_uri?: string, source_sha256: string, source_bytes: number, imported_at: RFC3339, imported_by: user-id }`
- The `candidates/` directory contains a single 'imported.md' file with the raw input — kept for audit even after refinement rounds modify the working plan.

POST-IMPORT QUALITY REVIEW
==========================
After import, hp-s00's quality tracker runs immediately:
- Score across 7 dimensions (intent / architecture / workflow / implementation / testing / risk / bead-readiness).
- Render dimension bars + 'Suggested next steps':
  - All dims ≥0.7 → 'Looks ready. Lock or convert directly.'
  - 1-2 dims <0.7 → 'Run a targeted refinement round to improve {dim, dim}.'
  - 3+ dims <0.7 OR any dim <0.5 → 'Run full refinement (recommended): synthesis pass + 2-3 refinement rounds.'

User picks: **Lock now** / **Refine then lock** / **Edit before refining** / **Cancel**.

REFINEMENT-AFTER-IMPORT
========================
If the user picks 'Refine then lock', the flow short-circuits hp-al4's pipeline:
- Skip the candidates step (we already have a plan).
- Skip the comparative-matrix step.
- Treat the imported plan as the synthesis output → run fresh-eyes critique + 1-N refinement rounds (user picks count).
- Lock-or-continue gate uses hp-hx12's diff viewer.

This keeps the §7.1 pipeline DAG intact — just enters at the synthesis-output point with provenance `pipeline_entry: 'import-as-synthesis'` recorded in `history.jsonl`.

EXISTING-CODEBASE BUNDLE FOR REFINEMENT
=========================================
When the project has a populated repo (hp-rsly's bundle applicable), the bundle is attached to the refinement-round prompts even on the import path. The user sees a checkbox 'Include codebase context (recommended)' default-on; uncheck for projects where they want a context-free refinement.

UNRESOLVED-DECISIONS DETECTION
================================
On import, parse the markdown for explicit decision markers (`> Decision:`, `> TODO:`, `> RESOLVED:`, `## Open questions`/`## Unresolved` heading) and pre-populate `unresolved-decisions.md` (per hp-x3z). User sees them surfaced before the lock gate.

UNIT TESTS (10) — `apps/desktop/src/planning/__tests__/importPlan.test.ts`
=============================================================================
1. **Paste-and-store** — paste 1500-word markdown → `.hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/plan.md` written + `candidates/imported.md` mirror; `meta.json` provenance shows `source.kind='paste'` + sha256 matches.
2. **File-picker-and-store** — pick a 12KB .md from local clone → stored at the canonical path; sha256 captured; structured log captures `source_uri`.
3. **Re-import previous plan** — list locked plans across all Hoopoe projects → user picks one → store at canonical path; `source.kind='reimport'`; cross-project re-import succeeds (verifies daemon RPC path).
4. **Min-length validation** — paste 100 words → 'Continue' button disabled; tooltip shows minimum.
5. **Max-size validation** — paste 250KB → modal shows 'plan too large; consider splitting.'
6. **Quality review trigger** — import completes → hp-s00 quality tracker runs once; 7 dimensions populated; 'suggested next steps' message rendered per the 3 thresholds.
7. **Skip-pipeline routing on 'Lock now'** — user picks Lock now → hp-x3z lock action invoked directly; pipeline entry = 'import-direct-lock'; structured log captures.
8. **'Refine then lock' pipeline entry** — user picks Refine then lock + 2 rounds → hp-al4 invoked at synthesis-output entry point; pipeline entry recorded; round 1 + round 2 artifacts written under canonical names.
9. **Decision-marker parser** — markdown with 3 `> Decision:` lines + 1 `## Unresolved` section → unresolved-decisions.md pre-populated with 4 entries.
10. **Existing-codebase-bundle toggle** — project has populated repo → 'Include codebase context' checkbox visible + default-on; unchecking and proceeding does NOT attach the bundle (verified in refinement-round prompt manifest).

E2E TESTS (7) — `apps/desktop/e2e/importPlan.e2e.ts`
=======================================================
1. **Paste path end-to-end** — Open plan workspace empty state; click 'Import existing plan'; paste fixture plan; quality review runs; pick 'Refine then lock' with 1 round; verify lock succeeds and Convert-to-beads enabled.
2. **File-picker-from-local-clone** — Pick `docs/spec.md` from local clone via the file picker (hp-2n1 + hp-3xzu); plan stored; quality review runs; pick 'Lock now'.
3. **Re-import previous plan** — Project A has 2 locked plans; switch to Project B; click 'Import' → 'Re-import a previous Hoopoe plan' → pick A's plan-1 → it imports into B with provenance 'reimport'; refinement runs; original A plan unchanged.
4. **Quality-driven recommendation** — Fixture plan with mediocre testing dimension → quality review highlights testing dim and recommends 'targeted refinement'; user accepts → only the testing-targeted prompt fires.
5. **Decision pre-population** — Fixture plan with 5 `> Decision:` markers → unresolved-decisions.md shows 5 entries; lock attempt blocks until user resolves; resolve all → lock succeeds.
6. **Codebase-context attachment** — Populated repo project; import a plan; refinement round 1 fires; round prompt manifest (per hp-rsly) shows existing-codebase-bundle attached.
7. **Audit chain** — Import → quality review → refinement round → lock; navigate to Audit log explorer (hp-1wg8); confirm chain via correlationId tying all events together.

DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (per hp-lxs)
==========================================
- `plan.import.opened` { project_id, available_modes (paste|file|reimport) }
- `plan.import.completed` { project_id, plan_id, source_kind, source_bytes, source_sha256, decisions_detected }
- `plan.import.quality_review` { plan_id, dim_intent, dim_architecture, dim_workflow, dim_implementation, dim_testing, dim_risk, dim_bead_readiness, recommendation_class }
- `plan.import.refinement_routed` { plan_id, pipeline_entry (lock|targeted|full), rounds_planned }
- `plan.import.codebase_bundle_toggle` { plan_id, attached, bundle_size_tokens }

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 'Plan entry' two modes
- §7.1 'Pipeline (from rough idea)' — pipeline DAG entry points
- §7.1 'Quality dimensions' for the post-import review
- §7.1 'Locking' for the lock gate
- hp-z5r (chat-box create flow), hp-al4 (planning pipeline), hp-x3z (lock + version history), hp-s00 (quality tracker), hp-rsly (existing-codebase bundle), hp-2n1 (local clone), hp-3xzu (local search), hp-8ec (plan editor), hp-hx12 (refinement diff viewer for the lock_or_continue gate), hp-r3i (schemas), hp-elx (design system)

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Import modal with 3 tabs (Paste / File / Re-import)
- Provenance + sha256 captured in meta.json
- Post-import quality review wired to hp-s00
- 'Lock now' / 'Refine then lock' / 'Edit before refining' / 'Cancel' branching
- Decision-marker pre-population
- Codebase-context bundle toggle
- 10 unit + 7 e2e tests passing
- Structured logging events emitted
- Documentation: 'Already have a plan? Bring it.'

title: Phase 8: Swarm launch MVP (composition picker, NTM launch, abstracted dashboard, NO terminal default UI)
description:
GOAL (§7.3, §12 Phase 8)
=========================
Land Stage 03 — SWARM. Composition picker (manual ratios or 'let Hoopoe choose' auto-select); NTM launch integration; staggered kickoff (≥30s between starts); kickoff prompt renderer wired to §7.3 template; abstracted agent grid (harness, CAAM account, current bead, status, time-on-bead, recent decisions — NO terminal output); bead board parallel with agent grid; per-agent state from NTM/Agent Mail/br events (no scrollback parsing); send-marching-orders / broadcast / interrupt / stop actions; PTY plumbing on daemon side for tending + Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' debug toggle ONLY (not default UI); per-bead lifecycle event mapping ingested into bead board.

WHY 'NO TERMINAL DEFAULT UX' (§7.3, Appendix C #12)
=====================================================
Terminal scrollback is observability noise the user shouldn't have to decode. Bead state + agent state + mail + build results — read from canonical sources — answer 'what's happening?' precisely; raw git status output and Codex thinking-token streams do not. The agent-flywheel methodology already says terminal content is not canonical state; we just take that to its conclusion in the UI.

PTY plumbing still EXISTS on the daemon side — orchestrator-chat tending agent (§8.4) reads recent pane output to answer 'what is agent X doing right now?' without exposing bytes; tend-swarm uses long-no-output + pattern-matched stuck-state heuristics; Diagnostics exposes opt-in 'Show raw pane' for forensics (audited).

EXIT (§12 Phase 8)
====================
Launch + observe a mixed small swarm (e.g., 2 Claude Code + 1 Codex CLI + 1 Gemini CLI per agent-flywheel <100-bead recommendation) against a real bead set; user can follow swarm from bead board + agent grid + Activity panel without ever seeing a terminal pane in default UI; opening Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' toggle for one agent reveals scrollback for forensic inspection + writes audit entry.

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1 Phase 8)
================================
A small mixed swarm launches through NTM, receives staggered kickoff, registers with Agent Mail, claims beads, and streams logs (logs visible in tending forensics, NOT default agent grid).

title: Subscription requirement + cost transparency UI — wizard step + new-plan empty state + project-settings docs page (§13 cost-transparency, ~$440-656/mo)
description:
WHAT
====
Hoopoe is subscription-only (§13). Users discover the requirement at three discrete UI surfaces, each pulling from a small JSON catalog mirroring the agent-flywheel.com pricing table. This bead is the explicit UI surface; the lint-rule enforcement of 'no provider SDK imports' is hp-ara.

THREE SURFACES (§13)
======================

**1. Onboarding wizard step 'Subscription requirement' (post-step 12 Status Check, pre-success)**
- Card layout matching the agent-flywheel.com cost-breakdown table.
- Renders the cost catalog rows: VPS ($40-56), Claude Max ($200), ChatGPT Pro ($200), Gemini Ultra (variable), all-in monthly ($440-656).
- Sub-line: 'For comparison, a junior developer is $5,000+/month; under $700 buys a 10+-agent swarm running 24/7.'
- Detected coverage: shows green checkmarks next to subscriptions CAAM has detected (from §3.0 Status Check) and grey 'not configured' next to missing ones.
- Warn-but-don't-block: zero subscriptions shows a yellow banner 'Planning, swarm, and tending will be disabled until at least one is configured. [Open subscription docs]'.

**2. New-plan empty state (Phase 5 Planning)**
- Triggered when the user opens Planning and no subscriptions are configured.
- Renders the cost-breakdown card (same component as wizard).
- CTA: 'Configure a subscription'  → opens Diagnostics / Subscription Settings.
- Secondary: 'Continue in Mock Flywheel Mode' (only visible when `mockMode: true` env / setting active; otherwise hidden).

**3. Project Settings → Subscription docs tab**
- Reachable from project settings sidebar.
- Static reference page rendered from the catalog.
- Links to provider sign-up pages (canonical agent-flywheel.com cost breakdown referenced).
- Live status: which subscriptions CAAM currently sees as signed-in for this project.

CATALOG SOURCE
================
- packages/schemas/cost-catalog.json (single source of truth).
- Rows: { product, monthlyUSD, displayName, providerUrl, requiredFor: ['planning' | 'swarm' | 'tending'], tier: 'core' | 'recommended' | 'optional' }.
- Refreshed alongside ACFS releases; version stamped in Diagnostics.

NO BILLING DASHBOARD
======================
Hoopoe DOES NOT see the user's credit card. The numbers are reference, not live billing. Per §13: 'These costs are not in Hoopoe's UI as a billing dashboard — Hoopoe doesn't see the user's credit card.'

NO TOKEN-COST THEATRE
=======================
Subscription pills (top bar §7.6) and plan-job UI (§7.1) show 'X% of daily Claude Max quota' / 'remaining Pro Deep Research uses', NEVER fabricated USD-per-call numbers. If `caut` doesn't have data for a provider, the cell says 'unmeasured' (per §1.4 inspectability).

UNIT TESTS
============
- Cost catalog schema validates.
- Cost-breakdown card renders from catalog (snapshot tests for VPS, Claude Max, ChatGPT Pro, Gemini Ultra rows).
- Subscription-coverage detection: mock CAAM with 0/1/2/3 subscriptions; assert green-check + grey-not-configured rendering.
- Zero-subscription warning banner: appears when `subscriptions.length === 0`; is dismissable per-session but re-appears on next launch.

E2E TESTS
===========
- Wizard happy path: 0 subscriptions → see warning + can proceed.
- Wizard happy path: 1 subscription (Claude Max) → wizard shows partial coverage + can proceed.
- New-plan empty state: open Planning with 0 subscriptions → see cost-breakdown + 'Configure subscription' CTA.
- Project-settings docs page renders cost catalog + live subscription status.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
======================
- Surface name (`wizard.subscription-requirement`, `planning.empty-state`, `settings.subscription-docs`) tagged on every render event.
- Subscription-detection logs: { provider, status, source: 'caam' | 'cli-status' | 'unmeasured' }.
- Cost catalog version stamped in any rendered card.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §13 — full cost transparency, subscription requirement.
- §5.1, §7.1, Appendix C #11 — no BYOK / no provider SDK; subscription-only is structurally enforced.
- hp-ara — lint rule that enforces no-API-key path at build time.
- hp-0ug — Phase 3 Tool Inventory + CAAM subscription verification (data source).
- hp-pmw — Phase 5 plan-job UI subscription-window usage (downstream).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Cost catalog JSON exists in packages/schemas/.
- Wizard step 'Subscription requirement' renders cost breakdown + detected coverage.
- New-plan empty state renders cost breakdown when 0 subscriptions configured.
- Project Settings docs tab renders catalog + live status.
- All three surfaces use the SAME card component (single render path).
- Zero subscription scenario tested end-to-end (warn-but-don't-block flow).

title: Local-clone untrust posture — markdown preview disables unsafe HTML; rendered code uses HTML escaping (§5.4 line 7)
description:
WHAT
====
The desktop's local clone (§7.7) contains arbitrary file content fetched from origin. Hoopoe must treat that content as untrusted: rendering uses HTML escaping; Markdown preview disables unsafe HTML by default; shellpath rendering quotes argv; no exec on parsed content.

WHY (§5.4 line 7)
===================
'Local code clone paths are treated as untrusted file contents. Rendering code uses escaping; Markdown preview disables unsafe HTML by default.' Without this, a malicious or compromised origin remote could push a markdown file containing inline HTML, JS, or stylesheet that compromises the renderer when previewed.

REQUIREMENTS
==============
- Code rendering: monaco / codemirror with no markdown-html-injection; tokenized highlighting only.
- Markdown preview: react-markdown with rehype-sanitize using a safe-allowlist (no scripts, no on* attrs, no javascript: URLs); raw HTML disabled by default. Setting to enable raw HTML exists but requires explicit opt-in per project + warning banner.
- Shell path rendering: any rendered shell command quotes argv; no rendering of unparseable shell as 'click to run'.
- 'Open in editor' deep links: validate path is inside the local clone root before launching; no traversal.
- ripgrep wrapper: spawn rg with explicit args; no shell interpolation of user input.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §5.4 line 7.
- §7.7 — desktop local clone.
- §1.7 — sync-driven mirror principle (clone never trusted as canonical).
- hp-rkx — parent renderer hardening epic.
- hp-2n1 — local clone implementation (consumer of these rules).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Markdown preview component uses rehype-sanitize allowlist; covered by tests with adversarial markdown payloads (XSS, javascript: URLs, inline scripts, on* attrs, data: URIs with HTML).
- Code rendering tested against adversarial code payloads (long lines, nested-bracket bombs, unicode-confusable identifiers).
- Path-traversal tests for 'Open in editor' deep links.
- Lint rule: any new render component imports must go through the sanitized markdown wrapper.
- docs/security.md documents the trust boundary.

title: Phase 12: Review rounds + finding tracker + convergence detector
description:
GOAL (§7.4.2, §7.4.3, §9, §12 Phase 12)
==========================================
Second subsystem of Stage 04. Hardening-mode swarm launch; review round jobs; fresh-eyes prompts; cross-agent review; finding tracker; finding-to-bead conversion; convergence dashboard; final landing checklist.

WHY (§7.4.2-3, §9.4 'Convergence is the success criterion')
=============================================================
Per-bead self-review (§7.3 step 5) is local quality control on the code that agent just wrote, while context is fresh. Stage 04 is the CROSS-CUTTING pass: whole-project concerns that only emerge once many beads have landed together. Hotspots that span files, architectural drift, integration bugs at module seams, security/perf issues, UI polish across screens, de-slopification across all generated code.

The success criterion is CONVERGENCE — not 'every bug is fixed.' A round is 'saturated' when new useful findings are low relative to cost + effort, and remaining findings are mostly duplicates / low severity / already tracked as beads.

REVIEW ROUNDS (§9.2)
======================
Round 0: UBS first-pass scan (deterministic, fast, ACFS-installed) — runs automatically when entering Debugging/Hardening.
Round 1: original-agent self-review.
Round 2: cross-agent review.
Round 3: fresh-eyes new-session review.
Round 4: random code exploration.
Round 5: hotspot-targeted review (often re-running UBS scoped to hotspots).
Round 6: test/coverage hardening.
Round 7: UI/UX polish if applicable.
Round 8: security/performance/deadlock/mock-code specialized skills.
Round 9: final landing checklist.

EXECUTION MODES (§9.2.1)
==========================
1. **Direct LLM review** — daemon gathers plan, bead context, recent diffs, health hotspots, AGENTS.md, target files, calls a provider directly. Good for cheap fresh-eyes critique, random exploration, targeted hotspot review where tool use NOT required.
2. **Delegated agent review** — daemon sends marching orders to NTM-managed agent pane, usually orchestrator or fresh agent, streams output into review topic. Required for reviews that need tool use, live test execution, cross-agent coordination, UBS scans, project-specific commands.

Both modes write findings into the same finding ledger; user sees a consistent Review tab.

FINDING LIFECYCLE (§9.3)
==========================
new → triaged → fix_now | new_bead | false_positive | needs_human → closed.

Every finding records source tool (ubs / agent / skill / human) for cross-tool deduping.

CONVERGENCE DETECTOR (§9.4)
=============================
not_started → high_yield → medium_yield → low_yield → saturated → final_gate_ready.

EXIT (§12 Phase 12)
=====================
Completed implementation transitions into structured debugging/hardening + reaches final gate.

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1 Phase 12)
==================================
Review round produces findings; each finding resolves to fix/new bead/false positive/human escalation; convergence dashboard updates.

title: Cross-cutting: §4.2 Gate invariant enforcement state machine + /v1/projects/{id}/readiness wiring
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the §4.2 gate-invariant matrix as a typed state machine in the daemon, surfaced through
GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/readiness (already declared in §2.6). Every stage transition the
daemon performs (or refuses to perform) is checked against this table; missing preconditions
return a structured 422 problem+json explaining which precondition is missing and what evidence
would satisfy it. Every transition attempt — successful or refused — writes an audit entry.

WHY THIS DESERVES ITS OWN BEAD
================================
§4.2 names 8 gates with concrete preconditions:
  1. VPS ready                — SSH verified, daemon reachable, ACFS installed, tool versions recorded.
  2. Project imported         — Git repo present, branch known, AGENTS.md present, .hoopoe initialized.
  3. Plan locked              — plan self-contained, decisions explicit, testing strategy present, unresolved decisions listed/accepted.
  4. Beads created            — br contains beads linked to plan, .beads/issues.jsonl flushed, conversion artifacts saved.
  5. Beads finalized          — plan-to-bead coverage checked, dependencies checked, ready set sufficient, bead clarity acceptable.
  6. Launch ready             — NTM healthy, Agent Mail healthy, bv robot surfaces healthy, br ready --json nonempty (or intentionally scoped), build queue policy set.
  7. Hardening ready          — implementation beads closed or intentionally deferred, no obvious stuck in-progress, review prompts available.
  8. Ship ready               — tests/builds pass or exceptions documented, code health gates pass or follow-up beads exist, Git/beads synced.

These preconditions are currently distributed across phase-implementation beads (hp-ilt covers
"Project imported"; hp-x3z covers "Plan locked" lock action; hp-pux covers Phase 8 launch
sequence). What is *missing* is a single load-bearing enforcement layer that:
- Knows the full 8-gate table.
- Refuses to advance lifecycle state without preconditions.
- Returns a uniform problem+json shape so every UI surface ("Convert to beads," "Launch swarm,"
  "Flip to hardening," "Ship") gets the same explanation.
- Audits every refusal so the user can later answer "why couldn't I launch?"
- Produces a typed readiness payload the UI can render as a checklist.

CONTRACT
========

Schema (lives in packages/schemas/readiness.yaml):

  GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/readiness?gate=<gate-id>?
  → 200 {
      gate: "launch_ready",
      satisfied: bool,
      preconditions: [
        { id: "ntm_healthy",       satisfied: true,  evidence: { capabilityId: "ntm.sessions.list", lastCheckedAt: "..." } },
        { id: "agent_mail_healthy", satisfied: false, evidence: null, missingExplanation: "Agent Mail MCP not registered; run `acfs doctor`" },
        { id: "bv_robot_healthy",   satisfied: true,  evidence: { capabilityId: "bv.robot.triage" } },
        { id: "br_ready_nonempty",  satisfied: true,  evidence: { count: 47, sample: ["hp-...","hp-..."] } },
        { id: "build_queue_policy_set", satisfied: false, evidence: null, missingExplanation: "..." },
      ],
      auditTrail: [ { time, actor, attemptedTransition, result } ]
    }

  POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/transitions
  body: { from: "imported", to: "planning" }
  → 200 if all preconditions met (writes audit entry, advances state).
  → 422 with problem+json:
    {
      type: "https://hoopoe.dev/problems/gate-not-satisfied",
      gate: "launch_ready",
      missingPreconditions: [ ... ],
      auditId: "aud_..."
    }

Internal state machine layout:
  apps/daemon/internal/lifecycle/
    vps_state.go            — VPS lifecycle states + transitions
    project_state.go        — Project lifecycle states + transitions
    gates.go                — the 8-gate table + precondition functions
    enforcer.go             — the policy that wraps every transition RPC
    readiness_endpoint.go   — the GET /readiness handler
    audit.go                — transition-attempt audit emission

Each precondition function is a pure Go func(ctx, projectId, deps) (Satisfied, Evidence, Missing) tuple.
Implementing each precondition is a small task; the enforcement layer is what unifies them.

UI INTEGRATION
================
- Wizard "Subscription requirement" + "Status check" steps: read GET /readiness?gate=vps_ready.
- Plan-stage "Lock plan" button: disabled if /readiness?gate=plan_locked is not satisfied; tooltip
  shows the first missing precondition.
- Beads-stage "Convert to beads" button: gated on plan_locked.
- Swarm-stage "Launch swarm" button: gated on launch_ready; the precondition list renders as a
  checklist in the launch dialog (warnings inline).
- Debug/Hardening-stage flip: gated on hardening_ready; review-readiness-check tending job
  (hp-fb0) reads /readiness?gate=hardening_ready and proposes the flip when satisfied.
- Final-gate landing checklist (hp-fyi): consumes /readiness?gate=ship_ready as its primary
  data source.

UNIT TESTS
===========
- One test per precondition function: positive case, negative case, evidence-shape correctness.
- One test per gate: all preconditions satisfied → satisfied: true; one failing → satisfied: false
  with that precondition's missing explanation populated.
- Audit emission test: every transition attempt produces an audit entry with the request's
  causationId and the result (satisfied/refused).
- Concurrent transition test: two clients attempting the same transition simultaneously — exactly
  one succeeds, the other gets a 409 conflict with the actor of the winning transition.

E2E TESTS
==========
- "wrong-order transition refused": attempt POST /transitions { from: "imported", to: "swarm_running" }
  bypassing planning — server returns 422 with all intermediate gate failures listed.
- "happy path through all stages": fixture project walks imported → planning → plan_finalized →
  beads_created → beads_finalized → swarm_running → hardening_rounds → quality_gates → completed;
  asserts each transition writes one audit entry.
- "stuck-at-gate diagnosis": fixture where launch_ready is unsatisfied because Agent Mail is
  missing; UI checklist correctly highlights the missing item; clicking it opens Diagnostics with
  the relevant repair action (hp-6d7).
- Detailed structured logging: every transition attempt logs
  event=lifecycle_transition project=<id> from=<state> to=<state> gate=<id> result=<satisfied|refused> missing=[...] audit_id=<id>.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §4.1 lifecycle states (VPS + Project).
- §4.2 gate invariants (THE table this bead implements).
- §2.6 daemon API: GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/readiness (this bead's primary endpoint).
- §1.4 inspectability — every refusal audited.
- hp-ilt — Phase 4 readiness checks (covers gate "Project imported"; this bead generalizes to all 8).
- hp-x3z — Plan locking (covers gate "Plan locked"; this bead enforces the lock from outside).
- hp-pux — Phase 8 swarm launch (consumes gate "Launch ready").
- hp-fyi — final landing checklist (consumes gate "Ship ready").
- hp-r3i — packages/schemas (this bead adds readiness.yaml).
- hp-g73 — audit log (consumer of every transition attempt).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- packages/schemas/readiness.yaml committed; TS + Go bindings generated.
- All 8 gates implemented in apps/daemon/internal/lifecycle/.
- Per-precondition unit tests cover both branches with structured logging.
- E2E test asserts the happy-path walk and at least 3 wrong-order refusals.
- UI wires the readiness payload into wizard, plan-lock, convert-to-beads, launch-swarm, flip-to-hardening, ship buttons.
- docs/lifecycle.md documents the gate table + precondition catalog with a per-gate troubleshooting section.
- All gate refusals visible in Diagnostics > Recent audit events filter "transition_refused".

title: Phase 2: systemd unit (Type=notify) + bootstrap.sh installer over SSH
description:
WHAT
====
The systemd unit that runs the Hoopoe daemon as a Type=notify service, plus the bootstrap.sh script that the desktop streams over SSH to install the daemon binary, install the unit, start the service, and print the initial pairing token to stdout.

WHY (§6.3, §6.5)
=================
- Type=notify so systemd knows when the daemon is actually ready (sd_notify ready signal), not just 'process started.'
- ProtectSystem=strict, ReadWritePaths scoped, NoNewPrivileges=true, PrivateTmp=true (§6.5 hardening target).
- Watchdog so a wedged daemon is restarted.
- bootstrap.sh handles install, upgrade, and the pairing-token exchange so users never have to run scp/systemctl by hand.

SYSTEMD UNIT (target — exact unit may evolve)
==============================================
[Service]
Type=notify
Restart=on-failure
WatchdogSec=30
KillMode=mixed
TimeoutStopSec=20
LimitNOFILE=65536
ProtectSystem=strict
ReadWritePaths=%h/.hoopoe /data/projects /tmp
NoNewPrivileges=true
PrivateTmp=true

NOTES
=====
- ProtectSystem=strict may need relaxation during early ACFS setup; setup mode and steady-state mode use different units or drop-ins.
- Steady-state daemon should NOT run as root. Privileged setup/repair actions go through a separate, explicit setup helper or temporary elevated command path with approval and audit.
- Diagnostics screen shows the active unit, last restart reason, watchdog status.

BOOTSTRAP.SH (§6.3)
====================
1. verify OS and basic dependencies (preflight)
2. install missing base packages
3. verify bootstrap source pins (ACFS commit/tag, install.sh checksum)
4. stream the canonical curl|bash one-liner with --yes --mode vibe --ref <pinned-tag>
5. run acfs doctor --json
6. record exact ACFS/tool versions, source URLs, commits, checksums into tool inventory
7. install Hoopoe daemon binary (via signed release URL, verify checksum + signature + provenance)
8. create daemon config + signing secret (32 bytes random, ServerSecretStore)
9. install systemd unit + start daemon as the least-privileged service user compatible with project paths
10. daemon emits initial pairing token to stdout (captured by SSH session)
11. desktop opens SSH tunnel, exchanges pairing token → 30-day bearer
12. bearer persisted in macOS Keychain via safeStorage
13. version handshake; print machine-readable result JSON
14. (optional) surface 'Launch onboard' link

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.3 — bootstrap flow.
- §6.5 — systemd unit hardening.
- §11 — daemon distribution: signed release URL, verified by checksum + signature + provenance attestation; SBOM, artifact attestation, source commit, builder identity, min compatible desktop/API versions.
- §6.4 — structured ACFS phase parser is implemented in Phase 3 (uses fixtures from this phase).

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- bootstrap.sh is IDEMPOTENT: re-running skips already-installed phases. Phase 0 fixtures cover this.
- Provenance verification on daemon binary download: refuse install when verification fails unless user explicitly enables an insecure development override (audited, visibly marked in Diagnostics per §11).
- Runs under user mode systemd (--user) for the user's project paths; root-mode systemd is post-MVP.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- bootstrap.sh runnable via 'ssh <vps> bash bootstrap.sh' end-to-end on a fresh VPS.
- Idempotent: re-running ends in the same state.
- systemd unit hardened per the target above; sd_notify ready signal works.
- Daemon binary download verifies checksum + signature + provenance.
- Diagnostics shows active unit, last restart reason, watchdog status.
- Audit entry written for install / upgrade / rollback.

title: Risk mitigation tasks (§14 named risks tracked individually with owner + acceptance evidence)
description:
WHAT
====
For each §14 named risk, ensure the codebase has a verifiable mitigation in place + an acceptance test pinning the mitigation's behavior. Track each risk individually so 'risk closed' has explicit evidence.

§14 RISKS
===========
1. PTY streaming fidelity fails — mitigation: prototype pane early; NTM stream/robot first; tmux capture fallback; ring buffers + reconnect cursors; treat terminal output as observability not canonical state. Verifies: §14 'PTY fidelity failures degrade tending and forensics gracefully (§8.2 degraded-mode warning) rather than crashing swarm dashboard.'
2. Tool output drift breaks adapters — mitigation: prefer robot/API/JSON; pin versions; golden tests; tool inventory + compatibility warnings; user-controlled updates.
3. Hoopoe cache diverges from canonical — mitigation: periodic reconciliation; canonical wins; explicit stale-cache indicators; 'reload from tools' action; source-of-truth table in docs.
4. First install brittle — mitigation: existing-VPS first; checkpointed setup; clear logs + structured steps; one provider only after manual works; diagnostics + resume.
5. Subscription rate-limits exhaust mid-swarm — mitigation: caut-driven telemetry; per-provider alert thresholds; CAAM switch; casr cross-CLI resumption (post-MVP); stop/pause policies; usage as % of subscription budget not dollars.
6. Agents compete for builds/tests — mitigation: build queue; rch preference; dedupe repeated commands; operator warnings; disk pressure cleanup.
7. Stale agents hold beads/reservations hostage — mitigation: stalled bead detection; stale reservation detector; forced release with audit; reopen/reassign workflows.
8. Unsafe commands accidentally exposed — mitigation: typed command specs; allowlist; path sandboxing; approval gates; DCG/NTM safety checks; audit log; no arbitrary shell API.
9. Planning quality is weak — mitigation: competing model candidates; synthesis; quality tracker; fresh-eyes review; lock gate; bead traceability.
10. Users trust subjective scores too much — mitigation: label scores as decision aids; show underlying evidence; allow override; keep canonical artifacts visible.
11. Laptop sleep breaks perception of reliability — mitigation: VPS owns jobs/loops; event replay; pane ring buffers; reconnect UI; no swarm dependency on Electron process.
12. Lifted code carries Codex-shaped assumptions — mitigation: scrub aggressively in Phase 1, integration tests against Hoopoe's own flows, refactor anything saying thread/provider/chat after week 2.
13. Upstream t3code drift — mitigation: pin commit at lift; quarterly review their CHANGELOG; cherry-pick deliberately.
14. PubSub.unbounded patterns leak through — mitigation: bound channels at design time; load tests with wedged consumer.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Each risk has at least one acceptance test pinning the mitigation's behavior.
- §14 risks list mapped to bead/test references in docs/risks.md.
- Phase 13 release smoke verifies all 14 risks have green evidence.

title: Phase 1.5: Harden preload and IPC command/topic contract
description:
Root cause: review-findings.md lines 203-212 identify three related HIGH gaps: renderer-supplied daemon.request(method, body) and daemon.subscribe(topic, listener) flow through preload without an allowlist, and IpcRegistry.dispatch<Input, Output>() lets callers pick unchecked generic types until hp-r3i generated schemas land.

Acceptance criteria:
- Preload rejects unknown daemon request methods and subscription topics before main-process IPC sees them.
- The allowlist is generated from packages/schemas when hp-r3i is available; if hp-r3i is not closed yet, use a local const allowlist with a parity test and a clear TODO to replace it with generated shapes.
- IpcRegistry dispatch stops exposing unconstrained caller-selected Input/Output generics for privileged daemon methods; replace with a typed command/topic map or document and test the interim boundary explicitly.
- Unit tests prove unknown request methods/topics are refused, known commands still pass, and type-level fixtures fail for mismatched payload/result shapes.
- Cross-link: review-findings.md "Renderer preload — [HIGH] no allowlist gate on daemon.request", "Renderer preload — [HIGH] no allowlist gate on daemon.subscribe", and "IPC — [HIGH] dispatch type-safety is illusory pending hp-r3i schemas".
close_reason: shipped

title: macOS sleep/wake fault-injection during active swarm + planning + build (§14 risk #11, §10.5 SLO p95<10s reconnect)
description:
WHAT
====
Dedicated fault-injection suite for **macOS sleep/wake during live work**, distinct from the
generic chaos suite (hp-2qn). Asserts the §10.5 SLO target (desktop reconnect after laptop wake:
p95 < 10s after network returns) and the §14 risk-#11 mitigation invariants (VPS owns jobs/loops;
event replay closes gaps without state corruption; pane ring buffers reattach; the swarm itself
never depends on Electron being alive).

WHY THIS DESERVES ITS OWN BEAD (not folded into hp-2qn)
=========================================================
The generic chaos suite (hp-2qn) covers "tunnel drop" and "daemon restart" as discrete events.
macOS sleep/wake is *not* either of those:
- The kernel may suspend wall-clock time, freeze sockets WITHOUT closing them, and resume the
  process minutes-to-hours later mid-syscall. TCP keepalives may not fire, so the daemon side
  may not know the desktop is "gone" until much later. The desktop side may resume with a
  half-open WS that reads bytes from a frozen kernel buffer.
- The ConnectionManager FSM (hp-s2cg) handles "reconnecting" but the wake transition is
  triggered by a macOS power-management notification, not by a normal disconnect event.
- Active subsystems (planning round running on Mac-resident Oracle, build streaming logs,
  Activity panel showing live mail) all need distinct recovery semantics on wake.
- §14 names laptop-sleep specifically as a perception-of-reliability risk — getting it wrong
  is a brand-defining bug, not a generic fault.

REQUIRED FIXTURES (each is a deterministic scenario in Mock Flywheel + a real research-spike VPS run)
======================================================================================================
1. **Sleep during active swarm, no Mac-resident dependencies.**
   - Setup: 4-agent swarm running on VPS (Claude Code + Codex + Gemini); user has WS subscriptions
     to project, swarm, activity channels. Push events queued during sleep.
   - Action: macOS pmset sleepnow → 90s sleep → wake.
   - Assertions:
     a. Swarm continues uninterrupted on VPS (NTM panes show continuous output across the sleep
        window; agents claim/work/close beads while desktop is asleep).
     b. Daemon's append-only event log captures every event during sleep (no event lost).
     c. On wake, ConnectionManager FSM transitions: ready → reconnecting → tunnel_connecting →
        authenticating → ready. p95 wall-clock < 10s after networkStatusChanged: online.
     d. Sequence-cursor replay (via /v1/events/replay) returns missed events; client merges
        idempotently; UI shows accurate state within 2s of WS reconnect.
     e. No duplicate event IDs applied to UI state (verified via state-snapshot diff before/after).
     f. Top-bar pill (§7.6) refreshes within 2s of reconnect (push-not-poll).
     g. Activity drawer unread badge accurate; clicking a sleep-period event opens the correct pivot.
   - Logging: detailed structured logs at every transition with event=sleep_wake_test phase=<x>
     elapsed_ms=<y>. Log capture asserts on phase ordering.

2. **Sleep during planning round with Mac-resident Oracle.**
   - Setup: ChatGPT Pro refinement round in flight; Oracle running on the user's Mac; VPS daemon
     mid-call to the Mac via --remote-host.
   - Action: macOS pmset sleepnow → 60s sleep → wake.
   - Expected behavior (§7.1 "MVP topology" tradeoff):
     a. The active Pro round PAUSES; UI showed "this will pause the active ChatGPT Pro round"
        warning BEFORE sleep (assertion: warning was rendered when round started).
     b. On wake, Oracle reconnects to its WebDriver session; the round resumes from its last
        checkpoint, OR the round is marked failed-with-resume-option (whichever the Oracle adapter
        guarantees per hp-xr8).
     c. Other planning candidates (Claude Opus / Gemini Pro / Codex CLI) running on the VPS continue
        unaffected — assert their per-candidate artifacts grew during the sleep window.
     d. Plan-job UI renders Oracle round as "paused-by-sleep" state distinct from "failed" or
        "running"; user can resume manually.
     e. No partial markdown artifact corruption; .hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/refinement-round-N.md is
        atomically written or absent.

3. **Sleep during build/test stream.**
   - Setup: agent ran `bun test`; logs streaming to /v1/jobs/{id}/log via WS chunked transport.
   - Action: pmset sleepnow → 30s sleep → wake mid-stream.
   - Assertions:
     a. Daemon's persisted log file under ~/.hoopoe/logs/{jobId}.log is intact; offset cursor
        unchanged (daemon never received an EOF that wasn't real).
     b. On reconnect, desktop resumes at last-acked offset via /v1/jobs/{id}/log?offset=<n> and
        renders the missing range without flicker or duplication.
     c. If the build completed during sleep, on wake the desktop shows final status correctly
        (no "still running" stale UI).

4. **Sleep with rate-limit recovery in flight.**
   - Setup: tend-swarm (hp-fb0) just emitted an `agent.kill_wedged_process` ActionPlan; daemon was
     mid-execution waiting for postcondition verification.
   - Action: pmset sleepnow → 45s → wake.
   - Assertions:
     a. The action either fully completed (postcondition verified before sleep) or is in
        pending_postcondition_verification state on wake; never "completed but unverified."
     b. On wake, daemon resumes postcondition verification; if it fails, a follow-up detection
        is emitted (per §8.3.1 self-healing).
     c. Audit log shows the sleep gap explicitly (a host_lifecycle: suspended audit entry from
        the desktop) so post-mortem can correlate.

5. **Sleep across the 5-minute Anthropic prompt cache TTL boundary.**
   - Setup: planning candidate is using Claude Code; cache hot.
   - Action: pmset sleepnow → 6 minutes → wake.
   - Assertions:
     a. The candidate job either finished before sleep or was checkpointable (assert artifacts
        are atomic — no half-flushed candidate markdown).
     b. UI does not show stale "running" state on wake.

6. **Repeated short sleeps (close laptop briefly, multiple times in 10 minutes).**
   - Setup: WS active, Activity panel open.
   - Action: pmset sleepnow → 15s → wake → 30s steady → repeat 5 times.
   - Assertions:
     a. WS reconnect cost is bounded — daemon does not exhaust connection slots, sequence-cursor
        replay is amortized (no full re-snapshot on every wake).
     b. Bearer/session tokens are not re-minted on every wake (30-day bearer survives sleep cycles
        per §5.2).
     c. UI does not show flapping reconnect spinners; idle state stays calm.

INSTRUMENTATION
================
- macOS power-management notifications wired into apps/desktop/src/main/sleepWakeMonitor.ts (uses
  Electron's powerMonitor events: 'suspend', 'resume', 'lock-screen', 'unlock-screen').
- Each notification emits a host_lifecycle IPC event to the renderer + an audit entry through
  the bearer.
- Test harness uses `pmset sleepnow` + a delayed `caffeinate` to wake, with per-test deterministic
  durations.
- CI runs the suite on macOS-only runners (macos-latest with arm64 + x64); skipped on Linux/Windows
  CI; smoke variants (1, 2) gate every PR; full suite (1-6) runs nightly.
- Logging: every assertion failure prints (a) the FSM transition trace, (b) the sequence-cursor
  trace, (c) the audit-log slice spanning the sleep window, (d) the persisted-log offset diff.
- Failure artifacts uploaded under apps/desktop/test/artifacts/sleep-wake/<scenario-id>/<run-id>/.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §14 risk #11 ("Laptop sleep breaks perception of reliability") — this bead is the verification.
- §10.5 SLO targets (reconnect p95 < 10s; event replay p95 < 5s; pane ring reattach < 2s).
- §2.5 ConnectionManager FSM — the state machine under test.
- §7.1 Mac-resident Oracle MVP topology — the planning-round sleep tradeoff lives here.
- hp-2qn — generic chaos suite (this bead is its sleep/wake-specific complement).
- hp-s2cg — ConnectionManager FSM (the FSM whose transitions this asserts).
- hp-wqm — sequence cursor + replayEvents (the protocol this exercises).
- hp-q3p — bounded-channel/slow-consumer (this asserts no leak after repeated sleep cycles).
- hp-rnn — tending evaluation harness (sleep-during-action-execution feeds into §8.8 fixtures).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 6 fixtures pass deterministically against Mock Flywheel.
- Fixtures 1, 2, 3 also pass on a real macOS arm64 + x64 against a real research-spike VPS, with
  results logged under apps/desktop/test/evidence/sleep-wake/<scenario>/<host>.jsonl.
- p95 reconnect < 10s asserted in CI by aggregating 100 runs of fixture 1.
- docs/reconnect-replay.md gains a "macOS sleep/wake" section with the verified behavior.
- The §10.5 SLO target line includes a cross-link to this bead.

title: Phase 12 / §9.2.1: Delegated-agent review mode infrastructure (NTM marching orders + agent output → finding extraction → finding ledger)
description:
WHAT
====
The infrastructure for §9.2.1's 'Delegated agent review' execution mode — distinct from 'Direct LLM review'. The daemon dispatches review marching orders to an NTM-managed agent pane; streams agent output into the review topic; runs a finding-extraction normalizer that turns free-form agent prose into structured Finding records; lands them in the same finding ledger as direct-LLM findings. Both modes write into the same ledger via the same Finding schema (verified by hp-v9hc parity test).

WHY (§9.2.1, §7.4.2 'review *process*', §7.4 'cross-cutting hardening')
========================================================================
§9.2.1 names two execution modes for review rounds:
  1. **Direct LLM review** — daemon gathers the plan, bead context, recent diffs, health hotspots, AGENTS.md, and target files, then calls a provider directly. (= cheap fresh-eyes critique, random exploration, targeted hotspot review WITHOUT tool use.)
  2. **Delegated agent review** — daemon sends marching orders to an NTM-managed agent pane, usually an orchestrator or fresh agent, and streams its output into the review topic. (= REQUIRED for reviews that need tool use, live test execution, cross-agent coordination, UBS scans, project-specific commands.)

Direct-LLM is straightforward (provider API call → structured response). Delegated-agent is harder: the agent's response is unstructured prose mixed with tool-use traces; we need to normalize it into the same Finding schema as direct-LLM mode so the finding ledger doesn't fork into 'two kinds of findings' with different fields. hp-8xm (review round runner) declares that both modes exist; this bead is the focused implementation of the delegated path.

ARCHITECTURE
=============

DISPATCH SURFACE
-----------------
ReviewMarchingOrder struct {
  RoundId           ReviewRoundID
  Mode              'delegated_agent'
  TargetAgentSpec   { kind: 'fresh' | 'orchestrator' | 'specific:<agentId>', subscription: 'claude-max' | 'gpt-pro' | 'gemini-ultra' }
  Topic             ReviewTopicID                   // internal bus topic; unique per round
  Skills            [vibing-with-ntm, ubs, security-audit, ...]   // round-dependent
  Prompt            string                           // versioned, regression-tested per hp-cj0
  ContextRefs       []ArtifactRef                    // the same plan/bead/diff/health refs direct-LLM mode also uses
  ExpectedOutputs   []OutputContract                 // 'findings_jsonl' | 'narrative_summary' | 'patch'
  Timeout           Duration
  IdempotencyKey    string
}

NTM LAYER
----------
The daemon uses NTMAdapter (hp-g1j) to either spawn a fresh agent in a new pane or send to an existing orchestrator pane:
1. ntm spawn (or send-to-existing) with the prompt rendered.
2. Subscribe to the agent's pane output stream (NTM WebSocket preferred per §7.3 PTY plumbing ladder).
3. Stream pane bytes → review topic.

OUTPUT STREAMING
-----------------
ReviewTopic is an in-daemon SSE/WS topic per round. Subscribers:
- The Review tab UI (live finding ticker as they arrive).
- The finding-extraction normalizer (consumes raw bytes + emits structured findings).
- The audit log (raw output + structured findings linked).

FINDING-EXTRACTION NORMALIZER
------------------------------
Two paths, in order of confidence:

PATH A: Structured contract (preferred)
The prompt template instructs the agent to emit findings as JSONL fenced blocks:
'''json-finding
{ severity, location: { file, line }, category, summary, suggested_fix?, evidence_refs? }
'''
The normalizer scans the streamed bytes for these fenced blocks; each parses into a Finding record. Reliable when the agent obeys.

PATH B: Prose extraction (fallback)
When path A yields fewer findings than expected (heuristic: agent declared 'I found X issues' but emitted Y < X JSONL blocks), run a follow-up structured pass: feed the agent's full raw output back through a deterministic 'extract findings' LLM call (model = small fast model on the same subscription) with output schema constraint. This is essentially a one-shot post-processor.

PATH C: Manual fallback
If both fail (agent crashed / produced nothing parseable / timeout), the round status moves to 'manual_review' with raw output preserved as artifact + UI prompt: 'Round produced no parseable findings; review raw output manually'.

POSTCONDITION VERIFICATION
---------------------------
Each Finding extracted has its location verified against the local clone (file exists, line in range). Mismatches downgrade the finding's confidence ('location_unverified' tag) but do NOT discard it — the Review tab surfaces these for human triage.

INTEGRATION WITH FINDING LEDGER
--------------------------------
Each Finding is upserted into the ledger (hp-k4j) with source=delegated_agent + agent_id + round_id + raw_artifact_ref. The ledger's finding-lifecycle state machine handles new → triaged → fix_now / new_bead / false_positive / needs_human → closed (per §9.3) regardless of source.

CONCURRENCY
============
Multiple delegated review rounds can be in flight simultaneously (e.g., security audit on round 5, performance audit on round 6). Each round has a unique ReviewTopicID + IdempotencyKey. Rounds CANNOT use the same agent pane simultaneously — the dispatcher serializes per-pane.

CROSS-MODE PARITY (hp-v9hc test)
=================================
The same plan section + same diffs run through direct-LLM mode AND delegated-agent mode MUST produce findings whose schema validates identically. Common Finding shape lives in packages/schemas. hp-v9hc verifies this; this bead implements the delegated side.

UI
==
- Review tab: live finding ticker as findings stream in.
- Per-finding source pill: 'delegated:claude-code-fresh' or 'direct:gpt-5'.
- 'Manual review fallback' state when extraction fails: shows raw output + 'extract findings' button (manual path C trigger).
- Round detail: agent pane log link (Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' integration for forensics).

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Path A structured extraction** — feed mock agent output with 5 JSONL-finding fenced blocks; normalizer extracts 5 findings; each parses correctly. Log: input bytes, fenced-block extraction, parsed findings.
2. **Path B fallback trigger** — agent declared 'I found 7 issues' but emitted only 3 JSONL blocks; normalizer triggers path B; mock the post-processor LLM call returning 4 more findings; total 7 findings extracted. Log: path A count, mismatch detection, path B invocation, total.
3. **Path C manual fallback** — agent crashed mid-output; normalizer detects 0 parseable findings + 'agent_terminated' signal; round status = manual_review; raw artifact preserved. Log: termination detection, status transition, artifact link.
4. **Postcondition file check** — finding cites 'src/foo.ts:42'; local clone has src/foo.ts with 100 lines → verified. Log: verification check.
5. **Postcondition file missing** — finding cites 'src/missing.ts:5'; file does not exist → finding tagged 'location_unverified'. Log: verification + tag.
6. **Per-pane serialization** — dispatch 2 rounds to the same orchestrator pane simultaneously; round 2 queues until round 1's NTM marching orders complete. Log: dispatch timeline.
7. **Idempotency-key collision** — dispatch same ReviewMarchingOrder twice; 2nd is no-op (idempotency hit). Log: requests + outcomes.
8. **Schema parity** — direct-LLM Finding + delegated-agent Finding pass the same Finding schema. Log: schema-validation runs for both.
9. **Topic stream backpressure** — agent emits 10MB of output in 5s; topic stream applies backpressure (hp-9vg chunked log API); UI receives in-order without OOM. Log: stream events, backpressure signals.
10. **Audit linkage** — round + agent + dispatch + findings + raw-artifact all chained by correlationId in audit log. Log: full audit chain dump.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Delegated-agent UBS scan end-to-end** — fixture project with 3 known UBS-detectable bugs; round 0 dispatches UBS scan via delegated-agent (NTM ubs invocation in a fresh pane); 3 findings flow into ledger; each finding has location_verified + source='delegated:ubs'. Log file: e2e-delegated-ubs-{ts}.jsonl with full timeline + findings + audit chain.
2. **Cross-agent review round** — round 2 (cross-agent review per §9.2) dispatches fresh Claude Code agent to review code written by Codex agent; findings extracted; each with source='delegated:claude-code-fresh:r2'. Log: dispatch + agent output + extracted findings.
3. **Path A → B → C cascade** — fixture has agent that emits 0 fenced blocks but 'I found 5 issues' in prose; path A extracts 0; path B extracts 5; round status = ok with 5 findings. Log: per-path execution + outcome.
4. **Manual review fallback** — agent crashes mid-output; round_status = manual_review; raw artifact link in UI; user clicks 'Extract findings' → triggers manual path B run; 4 findings extracted. Log: crash event, fallback UI, manual extract.
5. **Concurrent rounds** — round 5 (hotspot-targeted) + round 6 (test/coverage hardening) dispatched concurrently to two different agents; both stream into ReviewTopics; both extract findings; ledger correctly attributes per round. Log: parallel dispatch, ledger state.
6. **Cross-mode parity in real workflow** — plan + bead + diff fed through both direct-LLM AND delegated-agent paths; assert ledger contains findings with same Finding schema validation; per-finding source pill differentiates origin. Log: both paths' findings + side-by-side schema check.
7. **Tend-swarm integration** — review round in flight; tend-swarm tick sees 'review_in_progress' marker; doesn't propose flip-mode actions; verify suppression. Log: tend-swarm pre-script payload + decision.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §9.2.1 — review execution modes spec.
- §7.4.2 — review process.
- §9.3 — finding lifecycle.
- §10.1 — backpressure rules.
- hp-8xm — review round runner (parent — covers both modes; this is the delegated half's deep impl).
- hp-v9hc — direct-LLM ↔ delegated-agent parity test (consumes this).
- hp-rmv — UBS adapter (path A consumer for round 0/5).
- hp-hr4 — specialized audits (delegated-agent path frequent consumer).
- hp-k4j — finding ledger (where extracted findings land).
- hp-g1j — NTM adapter (delegation substrate).
- hp-9vg — chunked log API / persisted log offsets.
- hp-cj0 — kickoff prompt template.
- hp-fb0 — tend-swarm review-in-progress marker.
- hp-mzg — Phase 12 epic parent rollup.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- ReviewMarchingOrder schema in packages/schemas.
- NTM dispatch (spawn / send-to-existing) per agent spec.
- ReviewTopic SSE/WS bus (per-round-isolated).
- Finding-extraction normalizer with paths A → B → C cascade.
- Postcondition verification against local clone.
- Per-pane dispatch serialization.
- Idempotency on ReviewMarchingOrder.
- Audit chain linkage.
- UI: live finding ticker + source pills + manual-fallback state.
- All 10 unit tests + 7 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.

title: Phase 2.5: Idempotency-key tests for retried writes through simulated tunnel drop
description:
WHAT
====
Test suite asserting every write endpoint accepts an Idempotency-Key header and treats retries with the same key as the same request — same response (or 409 if mismatched body) — surviving a simulated tunnel drop mid-request.

WHY (§2.7 'Idempotency keys on every write endpoint that can be retried by reconnecting desktop')
==================================================================================================
A reconnecting desktop will re-send writes that 'might' have succeeded; without idempotency, the user sees duplicate beads, double-pushed commits, doubled approvals, etc. Idempotency keys are how distributed systems safely retry.

WRITE ENDPOINTS REQUIRING IDEMPOTENCY (§2.6)
=============================================
- POST /v1/auth/bootstrap/bearer (once-only by definition; idempotency at HTTP-level handles retry mid-success)
- POST /v1/projects (create)
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/activate
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/git/push
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/plans
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/plans/{planId}/rounds
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/plans/{planId}/lock
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/beads/conversion-runs
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/beads/polish-runs
- PATCH /v1/projects/{id}/beads/{beadId}
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/swarms
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/swarms/{swarmId}/broadcast
- POST .../agents/{agentId}/send|interrupt|stop
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/mail/messages
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/reservations/force-release
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/health/snapshots
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/reviews
- PATCH /v1/projects/{id}/findings/{findingId}
- POST .../tending/jobs/{jobId}/run
- POST /v1/projects/{id}/approvals/{approvalId}/approve|deny|extend

BEHAVIORS TO ASSERT
====================
1. Same key + same body twice → second response is identical to first (cached server-side for at least 24h or until job result expires).
2. Same key + different body → 409 Conflict 'idempotency-key mismatch'.
3. Different keys + same body → two independent operations.
4. Tunnel drop mid-request → retry with same key succeeds (no double-execute).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — idempotency keys mentioned.
- §2.7 — invariant.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Test suite covers every endpoint above.
- Tunnel-drop simulation: kill the desktop's TCP socket mid-write; retry succeeds with same key.
- Idempotency-key cache TTL configurable; default 24h.
- Mismatched-body case returns 409 with a problem+json body.

title: Backup, export, restore, retention, compaction (§10.3, §10.4)
description:
WHAT
====
Project export bundle + restore. Audit log retention. Schema migrations with backup + rollback. Skill-lock-file inclusion.

EXPORT (§10.4)
================
hoopoe export project <project-id> --out hoopoe-project-<date>.tar.zst

Bundle includes (§10.4):
- daemon project metadata
- audit log slice for the project
- event replay checkpoints
- plan artifacts
- bead conversion traces + traceability maps
- health snapshots
- review findings
- landing queue history
- artifact refs + selected blobs
- capability/tool inventory
- skill lock file (.hoopoe/skills.lock.json) with SHA-256 pins (jsm) or advisory version strings (jfp)
- redacted diagnostics

Secrets EXCLUDED by default. Optional encrypted backup targets later.

RESTORE
=========
- Validates schema versions, artifact hashes, compatibility before rehydrating.
- Refuses incompatible bundles with clear error.

RETENTION (§10.3)
====================
- audit log: indefinite unless user exports + prunes
- terminal/build/bootstrap logs: 30 days, configurable per project
- model raw artifacts: 30 days private retention default
- health snapshots: keep last N + compacted trend history
- event replay log: enough to cover recent reconnects, then compact into snapshots
- skill installs: pinned via .hoopoe/skills.lock.json (verified or advisory)

SCHEMA MIGRATIONS (§10.3)
===========================
Every persisted table/file has schemaVersion. Daemon startup runs migrations with backup + rollback. /v1/compatibility reports daemon API version, min desktop, event schema versions, migration state, unsupported client warnings.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §10.3 — retention + migrations.
- §10.4 — backup + export + restore.
- §18.4 — release smoke checks include export/restore preservation.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Export + restore tested end-to-end.
- Retention defaults applied + configurable.
- Schema migration test runs in CI.
- Phase 13 acceptance: project export + restore preserves plan, beads traceability, findings, landing history, artifact hashes WITHOUT secrets.

title: Phase 3: First-run wizard UI — wraps canonical 13-step wizard with structured checkpoints
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the desktop first-run wizard (Stage 0 — Connect) that wraps agent-flywheel.com/wizard/os-selection. Each step has a structured checkpoint card with resume-on-failure semantics. UI matches design/mockups/v1/wizard.jsx.

REQUIRED STEPS (Hoopoe wrapping per §6.1)
============================================
1. Choose path: 'Connect existing VPS' / 'Local demo' (skip steps 4-11) / 'Provision new VPS' (post-MVP, hidden in v1).
2. Generate or import SSH key — Hoopoe creates ed25519 on demand or imports from ~/.ssh/.
3. (Provider-side instructions for Rent VPS / Create Instance — read-only docs cards.)
4. Enter VPS host + port + username; Hoopoe's tunnel manager opens SSH connection; fingerprint TOFU.
5. Pre-Flight check (calls /v1/bootstrap/preflight) — streamed pass/fail cards.
6. Stream ACFS installer (calls /v1/bootstrap/acfs/start) — structured phase parser (separate task).
7. Reconnect — automatic.
8. Verify Key — acfs doctor --json structured rendering.
9. Status Check — tool inventory + CAAM subscription verification.
10. Hoopoe-specific extensions (daemon install, Oracle, jsm/jfp, GitHub auth).
11. 'VPS Ready' success screen.

CHECKPOINT BEHAVIOR
====================
- Each step writes a checkpoint event (audit log + UI history).
- Failed steps surface a 'Resume' button rather than restarting from scratch.
- Step state persisted in client-settings.json under wizard.<runId>.checkpoints[].
- On daemon side, /v1/bootstrap/acfs/resume picks up from the last completed phase.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.1 — full step table + Hoopoe extensions.
- §6.3 — bootstrap sequence.
- §6.4 — structured phase parser (separate task).
- design/mockups/v1/wizard.jsx — visual reference.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Wizard runs on first launch + when a VPS profile is removed; user can re-enter via Diagnostics → 'Reconnect VPS'.
- Local-demo path is a clean shortcut, NOT a degradation; the user can switch back later.
- VPS spec recommendations (§6.2) shown in step 3 docs cards; numbers come from a JSON catalog mirroring upstream.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Wizard UI complete, matching mockups; passes accessibility + keyboard nav.
- Resume tested: kill desktop mid-wizard → re-launch → wizard resumes from last checkpoint.
- Local-demo path tested: full happy-path on Mock Flywheel without any VPS.
- Existing-VPS path tested against the research-spike VPS.
- Step events written to audit log + visible in Diagnostics.

title: Phase 1: Mock Flywheel Mode wiring — desktop boots against fixture corpus, no VPS required
description:
WHAT
====
Wire Mock Flywheel Mode INTO the desktop renderer / main process so the four-stage shell can be exercised against the Phase 0 fixture corpus alone. Required for Phase 1 exit ('Mock Flywheel Mode can replay at least one fixture project without connecting to a VPS, so stage UI and Activity panel behavior are testable in CI').

WHY (§13 'Must include for development and demos')
====================================================
Stage UI work in Phase 1 must not block on real-VPS provisioning. CI tests need replayable inputs. Reviewer/contributor onboarding shouldn't require a VPS subscription. The cross-cutting Mock Flywheel epic owns the fixture corpus; this bead wires it into the desktop.

DESIGN
======
1. Mock daemon mode (--mock-flywheel CLI flag on the desktop) — substitutes a fixture-replay client for the real daemon RPC client. The renderer cannot tell the difference.
2. Scenario picker UI in Diagnostics — list all packages/fixtures/scenarios/<scenario-id>/, click to load.
3. Replay engine (renderer-side or main-process): time-stepped event delivery from events.jsonl with optional acceleration (1×, 10×, 100×).
4. Sequence-cursor handling — fixture replay must respect the sequence cursor + snapshot-on-reconnect protocol so testing reconnect behavior works against fixtures.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- Mock Flywheel epic — owns the fixture format and corpus.
- §6.1 'Local-demo path' — the wizard offers this path; this bead implements it.
- §13 'walking skeleton order' — Phase 1 exit requires Mock Flywheel.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- The mock daemon should still emit redacted events (so contributor scenarios don't accidentally include real tokens).
- A clearly visible 'MOCK MODE' indicator must be visible in the top bar so users never confuse a fixture with a real VPS.
- Scenarios are SELECTED by the user from Diagnostics; default scenario is `healthy-hour`.
- Mock Flywheel must NOT replace daemon authentication — a mock daemon still issues mock pairing tokens / mock bearers so the AuthBridge code path is exercised.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- 'bun run dev:mock' launches Hoopoe with mock-flywheel enabled.
- Default scenario boots; the Planning, Beads, Swarm, and Harden stages render fixture data.
- Activity panel shows fixture events.
- Scenario picker in Diagnostics swaps scenarios at runtime.
- CI smoke test: 'bun run dev:mock' + Playwright through stages passes.
- 'MOCK MODE' indicator visible in top bar.
close_reason: Mock Flywheel Mode wiring: packages/fixtures/replay/{scenario-source,event-stream,mock-daemon-client}.ts + apps/desktop/src/main/MockFlywheel{Mode,Client,Mode.test}.ts. 16/16 bun tests pass; typecheck clean both packages. Files landed in commit f1511d3 (peer hp-rflj sweep included staged files; functional work intact). Implements per-channel sequence cursors, configurable replay speed, mock auth dance, IpcRegistry-gated commands with WHEN_MOCK_FLYWHEEL when-clause.

title: Phase 6: Plan-to-beads conversion job + beads-workflow skill loading + traceability.json
description:
WHAT
====
The plan-to-beads conversion job per §7.2 'Conversion workflow'. Verifies plan is locked, ensures br is initialized, kicks off a high-reasoning conversion agent loaded with the beads-workflow skill, creates/updates beads through br only, runs br sync --flush-only, snapshots .beads/issues.jsonl, builds traceability.json, runs bv robot triage/insights, surfaces a quality score and recommended polish round.

WHY (§7.2)
===========
This is the FUNDAMENTAL plan→bead transition that prevents the plan-bead gap. If the conversion produces oversized / incoherent / dependency-broken beads, every subsequent stage suffers. Hoopoe's value here is making the conversion ROUTINELY high-quality, not just possible.

WORKFLOW (§7.2 audited)
========================
1. Verify plan locked (else 'override?' prompt).
2. Verify br is initialized (else br init).
3. Create conversion job in daemon job registry.
4. Start high-reasoning conversion agent — model = primary plan model, skill = beads-workflow.
5. Agent reads plan + creates/updates beads through br only (typed RPCs go through approvals).
6. Run br sync --flush-only.
7. Snapshot .beads/issues.jsonl.
8. Build plan-to-bead traceability map.
9. Run bv --robot-triage and --robot-insights.
10. Show quality score + recommended polish round.

TRACEABILITY.JSON (§7.2)
==========================
Each bead links to:
- plan section IDs implementing
- coverage status (covered / partial / missing)
- test obligations
- unmapped sections (orphans)
- oversized bead candidates
- duplicate candidates

BEADS-WORKFLOW SKILL
=====================
Per §17 reference, the beads-workflow skill is the authoritative methodology. Hoopoe loads it into the conversion agent's context (via jsm-pinned SHA-256 when configured; jfp fallback otherwise). The skill is the SPEC; agent applies it.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 — full conversion workflow.
- §17 — beads-workflow skill reference.
- §8.1 — skills are pinned per-project in .hoopoe/skills.lock.json.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Conversion is one-shot per plan version; re-running on an amended plan triggers bead-delta analysis (Phase 5 amend flow).
- The conversion agent runs through the same daemon-RPC + approvals pipeline as tending agents.
- All bead creates/updates audited.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Conversion job runs end-to-end.
- traceability.json produced + viewable.
- bv --robot-triage + --robot-insights surface graph health.
- Quality score + recommended polish round shown.
- Test: locked plan → 'Convert to beads' → real br beads created with deps → bv triage clean.

title: casr adapter (cross-agent session resumption; post-MVP recovery action)
description:
WHAT
====
casr (Cross-Agent Session Resumer) adapter for the §7.3 / §8.4 recovery action 'Resume the session via casr'. Converts in-flight sessions to a different CLI/provider when account-switching alone isn't enough.

WHY (§7.3 'Recovery actions when an agent rate-limits or wedges')
====================================================================
For example, the user's Claude Max is exhausted but they have GPT Pro. casr converts the in-flight session to a different CLI/provider, preserving context rather than restarting from scratch.

POST-MVP STATUS (§13 'Can defer')
====================================
casr is post-MVP — CAAM-only account switching ships first; casr follows when the cross-CLI conversion path stabilizes. This bead documents the integration shape so when the time comes, wiring it is mechanical.

INTEGRATION
=============
- casr.session.resume capability ID.
- Used as casr.resume_session ActionPlan type (§8.3.1).
- Postcondition: target CLI is now driving the session AND original session context preserved.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 — recovery actions.
- §8.4 — tend-swarm uses casr in rate-limit recovery.
- §13 — post-MVP defer.
- §17 casr reference.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Adapter scaffolded with capability ID.
- Skeleton implementation (returns 'not yet implemented' until post-MVP enable).
- ActionPlan type registered.
- Adapter contract tests for the post-MVP enable path.

title: Phase 6: bv --robot-* adapter (triage/plan/insights/diff; never bare bv)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side bv adapter per §2.3 BvAdapter:
1. bv --robot-triage
2. bv --robot-plan
3. bv --robot-insights
4. bv --robot-diff

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT (§1.3, Appendix C #1)
============================================
NEVER invoke bare bv from automation — it launches an interactive TUI. Hoopoe uses ONLY bv --robot-* surfaces for machine reads.

ENDPOINT MAPPING
=================
Wires into:
- /v1/projects/{id}/beads/graph (graph data: nodes + edges + critical path + ready frontier from bv --robot-triage / --robot-plan).
- Tending pre-scripts read bv --robot-insights to detect graph-health issues (§8.4 tend-swarm).

CAPABILITY IDS (§2.8)
=======================
- bv.robot.triage
- bv.robot.plan
- bv.robot.insights
- bv.robot.diff

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.3 — adapter precedence.
- §1.3 — bv constraints.
- §7.2 — bv robot triage/insights used after conversion.
- Phase 0 fixtures — bv --robot-* shapes.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Robot output is JSON; pin the schema via Phase 0 fixtures.
- Insights includes graph metrics (cycles, bottlenecks, branch factor) that downstream views use.
- Plan view shows parallel-execution tracks.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All four --robot-* commands wrapped.
- Adapter contract tests against fixtures.
- Capability registry reports bv.* capabilities.
- Cycle detection feeds into bead-quality dashboard.
- Used by /v1/projects/{id}/beads/graph endpoint.

title: Phase 0 fixture-quality test bead — verify each scenario covers happy + at least one failure path, deterministic boot, parser-fixture coverage
description:
WHAT
====
