title: Phase 4: Daemon-side Git watcher emitting vps_commit_created / vps_push_completed / origin_updated events
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side Git watcher that watches VPS-local + origin state and emits three event types on the WebSocket stream:
- vps_commit_created — new commit in VPS working tree, not yet pushed.
- vps_push_completed — VPS pushed N commits to origin.
- origin_updated — origin's refs advanced (fired by vps_push_completed AND by external pushes detected via git fetch origin --dry-run polling).

WHY (§7.7 'Sync model — Event-driven primary')
================================================
Origin-updated events drive the desktop's local clone fetch. Without them, the desktop relies entirely on the 60s safety-net poll (§7.7), which is slow and bandwidth-wasteful. Event-driven sync gets new code into the local clone within ~1-2s of being pushed.

EVENT PAYLOADS
===============
vps_commit_created:
  {projectId, commitSha, branch, parentSha, authorName, authorEmail, message: <truncated>, filesChanged: <count>, time}

vps_push_completed:
  {projectId, branch, commitsPushed: [...shas], remote: 'origin', time, durationMs, ok: true|false, reason?}

origin_updated:
  {projectId, refs: [{name: 'refs/heads/main', oldSha, newSha}, ...], source: 'vps_push' | 'external_push', time}

POLLING
========
Daemon polls origin (`git fetch origin --dry-run` or refspec ls-remote) every N seconds (configurable, default 60s) per project so external pushes by other contributors are detected. This is in addition to vps_push_completed.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 push policy — agents push every commit; auto-push hook installed at swarm-launch.
- §7.7 — event-driven sync model.
- §1.1 — origin = canonical.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Watching VPS-local commits requires a post-commit hook (or filesystem watcher on .git/refs/heads/).
- Watching origin requires periodic fetch + hook on incoming pushes (post-receive on origin if user-controlled, otherwise polling).
- Event sequence cursor (Phase 2) applies — these are normal channel events.
- Dedupe: if the same push is detected twice (e.g., post-commit hook fires + ls-remote sees the new SHA), emit once.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All three event types wired and emitted with stable schemas.
- Smoke test: create commit on VPS → vps_commit_created event in <1s; push → vps_push_completed + origin_updated within bounded latency.
- External-push test: push to origin from another machine → origin_updated detected within poll interval.
- Dedupe tested.
- Events flow through the §10.1 sequence-cursor + replay protocol.

title: Phase 3: Resumable setup checkpoints + Diagnostics repair actions
description:
WHAT
====
Make the entire wizard resumable from any failure point. Surface repair actions in Diagnostics for failures discovered after wizard completion. Each step writes a structured checkpoint that's queryable.

CHECKPOINT MODEL
=================
- Wizard.runId is assigned on first launch.
- Each step creates a Checkpoint{runId, stepId, status: pending|running|succeeded|failed|skipped, startedAt, completedAt?, evidenceRefs[], failureReason?, resumeHint?}.
- Persisted in daemon SQLite; survives daemon restart.
- Diagnostics renders the wizard timeline with per-step status; failed steps have 'Resume' / 'Skip' / 'View logs' actions.

FAILURE-RESUME FLOW
====================
- Failure → wizard freezes at the failed step + shows resumeHint.
- User clicks 'Retry' → reruns the step from its last successful checkpoint (e.g., installer phase).
- User clicks 'Skip' → marks skipped + warns about reduced functionality (capability registry shows blocked-by-policy).
- User clicks 'View logs' → opens raw bootstrap log (§6.4 fallback path).

REPAIR ACTIONS (§10.2)
=======================
After wizard completes, certain failures may surface in Diagnostics with safe repair buttons:
- Re-run ACFS doctor — read-only unless user approves fixes.
- Re-install daemon (rare).
- Refresh tool inventory.
- Update / verify skills (jsm or jfp) — separate task in Phase 10 lands the full skill loader.
- Restart Oracle (separate task in Phase 5 lands Oracle adapter).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §6.3 — bootstrap is idempotent; resume is a property of upstream installer + wizard wrapper.
- §10.2 — Diagnostics repair actions full table.
- §6.4 — phase parser exposes resume points.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Every wizard step writes structured checkpoints.
- Failure-resume tested for each step.
- Diagnostics renders wizard timeline + per-step actions.
- Repair actions implemented for the items listed above.
- Audit log records every checkpoint transition.

title: Phase 1 / cross-cutting: User-facing Settings screen UI (Global / Project / Accounts / Skills / Diagnostics / About; search; live preview; reset; per-key audit on security-relevant changes)
description:
WHAT
====
The actual user-facing Settings screen in Hoopoe Desktop. Distinct from:
- hp-4bt — three-store *persistence* layer (daemon-settings.json, desktop-settings.json, client-settings.json with hot reload + atomic write + PubSub).
- hp-zir — `SettingsBridge` IPC module decomposed from t3code main.ts.
- hp-rth — keybindings AST + last-rule-wins.
- hp-mbty — subscription/cost transparency UI in wizard + new-plan empty state.

This bead OWNS the Settings screen as a PRODUCT SURFACE: where users configure project clone caps, push policy, build queue policy, model-context policy, default model picker; global settings (update channel, telemetry opt-in, mock-flywheel-mode toggle, log retention, keybindings overrides); per-tool settings (CAAM account management, jsm subscription, oracle Mac/VPS topology, skill version pinning + manual upgrade); per-account/CAAM management.

WHY (multi-section: §7.7, §10.3, §11, §13, §1.1, §8.1, §8.2)
==============================================================
Plan calls out user-configurable settings across many sections:
- §7.7: local clone soft/hard caps configurable per-project + globally.
- §10.3: log retention, model artifact retention, audit retention configurable per-project.
- §11: update channel selection (latest / nightly).
- §13: telemetry opt-in (Phase 13 polish), mock flywheel mode toggle (dev only).
- §1.1: jsm vs jfp skill installer preference; CAAM account-per-CLI defaults.
- §8.1: skill version pinning (`.hoopoe/skills.lock.json`) with manual upgrade flow.
- §8.2: per-job tending overrides (cadence, paused state, max_concurrency) editable from Diagnostics.

Today these surfaces are scattered across Phase 13 polish (hp-8op, hp-kyr, hp-ecp), the wizard (hp-o6q), and several feature beads. There is NO dedicated bead for the user-facing Settings screen. As a result:
- Scope is unclear; the Settings screen will arrive in Phase 13 as polish — too late, because Phase 4/5/8/10/11 features depend on user-configurable knobs.
- Each feature ad-hocs its own settings surface (e.g., a per-feature gear icon menu), creating exactly the inconsistent UX the design system is supposed to prevent.
- Audit-on-change for security-relevant settings (channel, telemetry, skill pin) has no central enforcement point.

This bead establishes the Settings screen as a **Phase 1 deliverable** (skeleton) that successive phases extend.

CONTRACT
========
1. **Layout** — left-rail navigation + right pane:
   ```
   ┌──────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┐
   │ Global       │  ⌘F search                         │
   │ Project      │  ─────────────────────────────────│
   │ Accounts     │  Section heading                   │
   │ Skills       │                                    │
   │ Diagnostics  │  Setting label   [control]    [↺] │
   │ About        │  Description / docs link           │
   │              │  Source: default | global | proj   │
   └──────────────┴───────────────────────────────────┘
   ```
2. **Sections**:
   - **Global**: update channel (latest / nightly), telemetry opt-in, mock-flywheel-mode toggle (dev-only), log retention windows (audit, terminal, model artifacts, health snapshots), keybindings file path + reload, default editor command for "Open in editor" links.
   - **Project**: per-project local-clone soft cap + hard cap (§7.7), default push policy (auto-push every commit / every successful test / manual), build queue policy (concurrent N, timeout), model-context policy (loaded from `.hoopoe/model-context-policy.json` with editor view), default model picker for plans (primary + competing candidates), default safety preset for swarm launches (supervised / guided / autopilot).
   - **Accounts**: CAAM account inventory (per-provider list with "active for" indicator, switch-default actions per agent harness, add new account, revoke); jsm subscription status + login.
   - **Skills**: skill catalog (vibing-with-ntm, ntm, ubs, custom) with current pinned SHA-256 (jsm) or advisory version (jfp); per-skill "Update" action (audited config change); installer preference (jsm preferred / jfp only).
   - **Diagnostics**: tending job table (read-only summary, links to full Diagnostics page hp-6d7); Show raw pane toggle policy (per-agent default off); recovery shell access toggle.
   - **About**: Hoopoe version, daemon version, ACFS version, OS version, license/MIT attributions.
3. **Per-setting widgets**:
   - Each setting carries: typed schema (Zod / similar), default value, validation, source resolution chain (default ← global ← project ← env override), reset-to-default action.
   - Live preview where applicable (e.g., changing keybindings → current binding map renders inline; changing default model picker → preview of new-plan UI in a small inline frame).
   - Validation errors render inline beneath the control with an actionable message; restart-required settings (e.g., channel switch) show a banner: "Restart Hoopoe to apply."
   - Tooltips with short docs + link to full docs.
4. **Search (⌘F)** — fuzzy search over setting names + descriptions + section titles. Highlights matches inline; dims non-matches.
5. **Import / export** — per-section export to JSON (excluding secrets); per-section import with schema validation; project settings can be cloned to another project.
6. **Reset to defaults** — per-key, per-section, or global-reset (last with confirmation modal).
7. **Audit on change** — security-relevant settings (channel, telemetry, mock mode, skill pin, push policy, safety preset, model-context policy) write a `setting_changed` audit entry with old + new + actor + timestamp. Routine settings (e.g., editor command) do not.
8. **Accessibility** — full keyboard nav, screen-reader announcements via aria-live, contrast meets WCAG AA, Esc closes Settings, Cmd+Comma opens (standard macOS shortcut).

UNIT TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
==============================================
1. **Schema validation** — invalid input rejected inline with actionable error per setting type (number, enum, path, regex). Log: per-setting (input, error message).
2. **Source precedence** — for a setting with default A, global B, project C, env D: with all set, resolves D; with env unset, resolves C; etc. Log: precedence resolution table.
3. **Reset-to-default** — per-key reset clears project + global override; per-section reset cascades; global reset confirms via modal. Log: pre/post settings tree.
4. **Search** — keyword "telemetry" matches Global → Telemetry opt-in; keyword "clone" matches Project → Local clone caps; ⌘F focuses search field. Log: query → matches.
5. **Audit-on-change** — toggle telemetry opt-in writes audit entry with old/new/actor; verify no audit for editor-command change. Log: audit entries dump.
6. **Schema-versioned settings migration** — load old schema-v1 settings file, verify v1 → v2 migration runs, settings preserved with new defaults filled in for new fields. Log: pre/post settings.
7. **Live preview rendering** — change default model picker to "Claude Opus" → live preview new-plan inline frame updates within 100ms. Log: timing + before/after rendered DOM snapshots.
8. **Import / export round-trip** — export project settings to JSON, modify one value externally, re-import → schema validation catches drift OR accepts with confirmation; verify secrets excluded. Log: export blob, post-import settings.
9. **Restart-required banner** — change update channel to nightly → banner appears with "Restart" button → click triggers `relaunchDesktopApp("channel-changed")`. Log: banner state, relaunch call.
10. **Accessibility smoke** — Esc closes Settings, Cmd+Comma opens, screen-reader announces section navigation, focus visible at every focused element, contrast ratios checked. Log: keyboard event chain, aria-live announcements.

E2E TESTS (with detailed structured logging)
=============================================
1. **Open + navigate full surface** — Cmd+Comma opens Settings; navigate Global → Project → Accounts → Skills → Diagnostics → About via keyboard; every section renders within 200ms; ⌘F search opens. Log: per-section render time.
2. **Update channel switch end-to-end** — Settings → Global → toggle "Update channel: nightly" → restart-required banner → click "Restart" → app relaunches → /v1/version reports nightly channel. Log: full state machine.
3. **Local clone cap → enforce** — Settings → Project → "Local clone soft cap = 1 GB" → /clone-state.json reflects new cap → trigger 2 GB clone → header banner "Soft cap exceeded" appears. Log: cap propagation.
4. **CAAM account management** — Settings → Accounts → see CAAM inventory loaded from `/v1/caam/accounts` → click "Switch default for Claude Code" → audit entry written → next swarm launch uses new default account. Log: CAAM API exchange, audit entry, swarm launch evidence.
5. **Skill update flow** — Settings → Skills → see vibing-with-ntm pinned SHA-256 → click "Update" → confirmation modal → daemon refreshes pin → `.hoopoe/skills.lock.json` updated → next tending tick uses new SHA. Log: skills.lock.json diff, tending tick verification.
6. **Audit chain on telemetry toggle** — toggle telemetry opt-in 3 times rapidly → audit log shows 3 distinct setting_changed events (each with previous + new + actor + timestamp); no debounce-induced merging. Log: audit dump.
7. **Search performance** — index 200 settings, search "x" yields < 50ms response. Log: timing histogram.
8. **Multi-project settings clone** — Project A has clone cap 1 GB + push policy "every commit"; Settings → Project → "Clone settings to another project" → pick Project B → Project B's settings updated; verify NOT global. Log: pre/post both projects.
9. **Restart-required banner persistence** — change channel, dismiss banner without restarting; reopen Settings → banner re-renders (because channel still pending). Log: banner state across screen reloads.
10. **Settings export / import round-trip** — export Global to JSON; modify externally to invalid value; re-import → import dialog shows validation error. Log: import flow.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.7 — local clone caps.
- §10.3 — retention / compaction.
- §11 — update channels.
- §13 — telemetry opt-in.
- §1.1 — installer preferences.
- §8.1 — skill pinning.
- §8.2 — per-job tending overrides.
- hp-4bt — three-store persistence layer (consumed).
- hp-zir — SettingsBridge module (consumed).
- hp-rth — keybindings (consumed for Keybindings sub-section).
- hp-mbty — subscription / cost UI (cross-link from About / wizard).
- hp-z1x — four-stage routed shell (Settings is a non-stage screen accessed via Cmd+Comma).
- hp-i62 — reusable components (Settings rows, toggles, etc.).
- hp-elx — design system (visual + interaction).
- hp-4d7 — skill loader (skills section consumes).
- hp-ier — CAAM adapter (Accounts section consumes).
- hp-6d7 — Diagnostics screen (Diagnostics sub-section links).
- hp-g73 — audit log (setting_changed events flow here).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Settings screen renders all 6 sections.
- Cmd+Comma opens; Esc closes; ⌘F search works; full keyboard navigation.
- Schema validation + source precedence + reset-to-default per setting.
- Audit-on-change for security-relevant settings (channel, telemetry, mock, skill pin, push policy, safety preset, model-context).
- Live preview for picker-style settings.
- Restart-required banner for channel switch + similar.
- Import / export per-section round-trip works.
- Multi-project settings clone works.
- Accessibility: WCAG AA contrast, screen-reader announcements, full keyboard nav.
- All 10 unit tests + 10 e2e tests pass with detailed structured logs persisted as artifacts.
- Settings screen ships with Phase 1 skeleton (sections render even when unwired); Phase 4/5/8/10/11/13 progressively fill in their respective sub-sections.
close_reason: shipped in 5e50d3c — Settings UI + audit trail

title: Phase 13: Provider automation (Contabo first), production polish, demo project, docs, crash reports
description:
GOAL (§12 Phase 13)
=====================
One provider plugin (Contabo first, matching §6.2 rollout — agent-flywheel.com top pick + easiest 'just works' path); cost estimate + teardown wired to §13 cost-transparency numbers; polished empty / loading / error states; onboarding tour mirroring agent-flywheel.com 13-step wizard; diagnostics screen final polish; crash reports opt-in; daemon upgrade system end-to-end (Phase 3 lands skeleton); documentation + demo project.

NOTE: Signed/notarized DMG and auto-update infrastructure are already in place from Phase 1's lift — this phase is about POLISH, ERROR UX, and PROVIDER AUTOMATION, not building the release pipeline.

EXIT (§12 Phase 13)
=====================
A new user can install Hoopoe, connect/provision a VPS, import a project, create a plan, convert beads, launch agents, monitor review, and land a small project.

ACCEPTANCE TEST (§18.1 Phase 13)
==================================
Existing-VPS path remains green while one provider plugin can create, estimate, and tear down an instance without affecting manual mode.

title: Phase 0: Seed Mock Flywheel fixture corpus
description:
WHAT
====
Convert the §16 Phase 0 snapshot outputs into a Mock Flywheel fixture corpus that the cross-cutting Mock Flywheel epic consumes: normalized snapshots, replayable event streams, byte-addressable pane logs, build/test logs, Agent Mail messages with reservation-conflict edge cases, health snapshots with multi-language coverage, and explicit failure-case fixtures.

WHY (§13 'Must include for development and demos', §8.8 evaluation harness)
=============================================================================
Mock Flywheel Mode is the entire reason UI work in Phase 1 isn't blocked on a real VPS, and the Phase 10 tending evaluation harness explicitly requires fixtures for: healthy hour, idle but not stuck, genuinely wedged pane, rate-limited (with/without CAAM), stale reservation, commit burst, budget breach, skill drift, missing tool, postcondition failure, action arbitration. Without those, the tending substrate can't be tested.

INPUTS
======
- Phase 0 snapshot.json captures (fresh / active / failure scenarios)
- Manual wizard run-through outputs (§Phase 0 wizard task)
- Per-tool integration-contract markdown
- Real PTY captures from the research-spike VPS (NTM + tmux capture-pane)

DELIVERABLES
============
1. packages/fixtures/scenarios/<scenario-id>/ — one directory per §8.8 scenario, each containing:
   - bv-triage.json, br-list.json, ntm-snapshot.json, agent-mail-dump.json, reservations.json
   - events.jsonl — replayable WS events with sequence cursors per channel
   - pane-logs/<agentId>.bin — byte-addressable PTY captures
   - build-logs/<runId>.txt — test/build output samples
   - capabilities.json — capability registry snapshot (some scenarios degrade specific capabilities)
   - tools-degraded.json — for missing-tool scenario specifically
   - expected-outcome.json — what the tending harness should see (detections emitted, wake/no-wake, ActionPlan shape, approvals requested, postconditions verified)

2. packages/fixtures/golden-outputs/ — one file per adapter with:
   - normal output
   - missing-tool case
   - unsupported-version case
   - malformed-JSON case
   - timeout case
   - high-volume case
   (per §18.3 'Adapter contract tests')

3. packages/fixtures/README.md — fixture catalog, version, refresh cadence, and how to regenerate from a live VPS.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Fixtures should NOT contain real bearer tokens, SSH passphrases, or provider credentials. The redaction layer (audit log epic) must scrub anything sensitive at capture time.
- Scenarios that depend on time progression (commit burst, budget breach over a window) need event-stream timestamps that mock-flywheel can replay at 1×/10×/100× speeds.
- Fixtures versioned (fixturesVersion: 'phase0-2026-04-30' or similar — referenced in the capability registry per §2.8).

ACCEPTANCE
==========
- Mock Flywheel Mode boots Hoopoe end-to-end against the corpus alone (no VPS).
- Each §8.8 scenario plays through and the expected-outcome.json assertions match.
- Each adapter contract test passes against its golden-outputs/ files.
close_reason: Seed corpus: 5 §8.8 scenarios fully populated (healthy-hour, idle-but-not-stuck, wedged-pane, rate-limited-no-caam, rate-limited-with-caam), 17 adapters × 6 golden-output states = 102 contract fixtures, kinds.ts taxonomy + loader.ts stub, deterministic seeder scripts. 144 JSON files validate; typecheck + bun test green. Real-VPS phase0-2026-05-02/ placeholders pending hp-r7i + hp-jvm.

title: Phase 2: WS event sequence cursor + replayEvents endpoint + reconnect protocol
description:
WHAT
====
Wire sequence cursors into every WebSocket event the daemon emits; implement /v1/events/replay so the desktop can fetch missed events after a tunnel drop or sleep. Pattern lifted from t3code's OrchestrationRecoveryCoordinator (Appendix B 'Patterns lifted').

WHY (§2.5, §2.6, §10.1, §10.5 'event replay after 10-minute disconnect: no lost state; p95 under 5s')
=======================================================================================================
This is the protocol that makes laptop sleep, daemon restart, and tunnel re-establishment work without state corruption. Without sequence cursors, every reconnect requires a full snapshot of every channel, which is slow and inconsistent.

CONTRACT
========
Every WS event:
{
  eventId: 'evt_01...',
  schemaVersion: 1,
  channel: 'project:abc' | 'swarm:sw-123' | 'activity:abc' | '_system',
  type: 'bead.changed' | ...,
  sequence: NonNegativeInt (per-channel monotonic),
  time: RFC3339,
  actor: {kind: 'agent', id: 'ag_123'},
  causationId: 'cmd_01...',
  correlationId: 'swarm_01...',
  data: {...}
}

System events:
{channel:'_system', type:'heartbeat', sequence, time}
{channel:'project:abc', type:'_gap', from, to, repair:'replayEvents'}
{channel:'project:abc', type:'_lag', lastPersistedOffset}

WS subscribe envelope:
{op:'subscribe', channels:['project:abc','swarm:sw-123','activity:abc'], cursors:{'project:abc':182}}

Subscribe behavior:
- Snapshot first (returned on the same stream, marked as snapshot).
- Then live deltas.
- If client cursor < server's lastPersistedOffset for the channel and the in-memory ring no longer has that range, emit _gap with repair instructions; client calls /v1/events/replay.

REPLAY ENDPOINT
================
GET /v1/events/replay?channel=<id>&sinceSequence=<n>
Returns events in order; idempotent per eventId.

DESKTOP RECONCILE PROTOCOL (§2.5 reconnect logic)
===================================================
Reconnect always: tunnel check → bearer/session check → WS-token refresh → subscribe with sequence cursors → fetch one snapshot for active channels → reconcile local clone if project is active.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — WS envelope shape.
- §10.1 — backpressure, lag, replay rules.
- §10.5 — SLO targets.
- Appendix B — t3code/apps/web/src/orchestrationRecovery.ts pattern.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Sequence numbers are PER-CHANNEL, not global. Daemon must persist per-channel cursors.
- High-volume streams (terminal output, bootstrap logs, build logs) are persisted as byte-addressable logs; events carry offsets and summaries, not the entire text. The replayEvents path covers events; for byte-addressable logs, separate /v1/jobs/{id}/log?offset= endpoint serves chunked log fetches.
- Renderer subscriptions coalesce noisy events (agent.tick, pane.output) by agent/pane at fixed cadence (Phase 8+; this bead just provides the primitive).
- Replay is IDEMPOTENT — duplicate eventIds are ignored client-side; server must use stable eventIds.
- Schema changes are versioned (event.schemaVersion); mismatched daemon-newer-than-client emits compatibility warning.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Every WS event carries sequence + eventId + schemaVersion + channel + type + time.
- /v1/events/replay returns missed events in order.
- Subscribe with cursor → snapshot + live deltas, all on same stream.
- _gap emission tested by overflowing the in-memory ring with a slow consumer.
- Disconnect/reconnect test: 10-minute disconnect → reconnect → no events lost (matches §10.5 SLO).
- Schema-version mismatch test: client subscribes with older schema → server emits compatibility warning event.
close_reason: Implemented WS per-channel cursors, replay, gap, and reconnect protocol tests

title: §7.6 Top-bar push-not-poll cadence assertion + p95<2s top-bar update latency SLO test
description:
WHAT
====
Test bead asserting two §7.6 invariants for the cockpit top bar:
1. **Push, not poll** — the renderer's only path to top-bar values (project/branch/clean state,
   tool health dots, swarm count, beads pulse, code-health pill, subscription pill, Activity
   unread badge) is the WebSocket event stream. The renderer issues NO recurring HTTP polls for
   top-bar data.
2. **Update latency** — when canonical state changes (a new commit, a tool capability flips
   degraded, a new health snapshot lands, a subscription-window update from caut), the top-bar
   reflects the change with p95 < 2s end-to-end (canonical state change → daemon detects
   via reconciler → emits WS event → renderer applies → DOM updated).

WHY THIS DESERVES ITS OWN BEAD
================================
The plan §7.6 closing paragraph says: *"Top-bar values come from the daemon's reconciled cache
(§2.2) and are pushed over the WebSocket event stream — no polling from the renderer. Health
pill updates within seconds of a new snapshot landing."* This is two distinct testable claims —
"push not poll" (a structural assertion: zero polls) AND "within seconds" (a latency SLO) — and
neither is currently asserted explicitly anywhere in the bead graph. hp-4ya covers wiring the
top-bar elements; hp-ry6 covers Phase 11 health-metrics tests; hp-j30 covers Phase 1 desktop
unit/e2e tests. None of those currently checks that the renderer issues zero recurring polls for
top-bar data, and none has an end-to-end p95 latency assertion across the canonical-change →
DOM-update pipeline.

This is the kind of invariant that decays silently: a new top-bar feature gets added, a
developer adds `setInterval(refresh, 5000)` because it's faster than wiring an event channel,
and now the cockpit polls. The polling works, the test for "the top-bar shows the right value"
passes, and the user never knows. By the time someone investigates "why is the top-bar laggy
on a stale tunnel" or "why does the daemon log show 50 GET /v1/health calls per minute," the
behavior is so embedded that fixing it is risky. Catch this in CI by structural assertion, not
later in production by intuition.

INVARIANT 1 — STRUCTURAL ASSERTION (push, not poll)
====================================================
Test mechanism (apps/desktop/test/topbar/no-poll.test.ts):
- Boot the desktop in a test harness with a recording HTTP/WS proxy in front of the daemon.
- Subscribe to all top-bar channels via WS.
- Run the desktop for 120 seconds in the steady state (no user interaction; one project active;
  no events emitted from the daemon side).
- Assert: ZERO HTTP requests to /v1/projects/*, /v1/health, /v1/projects/{id}/health/summary,
  /v1/projects/{id}/git/status, /v1/projects/{id}/beads, or any top-bar-relevant endpoint
  during that window. (Initial subscribe-phase fetches are allowed and counted separately.)
- Assert: WS subscriptions are open for the relevant channels: project:<id>, swarm:<id>,
  activity:<id>, plus the system heartbeat channel.
- Allowlist: the safety-net `git fetch` for the local clone (§7.7) is NOT a top-bar poll —
  it's the local-clone sync subsystem (hp-ind). It's filtered out of this assertion via URL
  pattern.

Lint-time complement: a custom ESLint rule
(`hoopoe/no-topbar-poll`, in `packages/eslint-config-hoopoe/`) refuses `setInterval` /
`setTimeout`-with-recursion / `useQuery({refetchInterval})` patterns inside any file under
`apps/desktop/src/renderer/topbar/**`. The lint rule has a one-line escape hatch
(`// hoopoe/no-topbar-poll: explicit-reason — the explicit reason the polling is intentional`)
so legitimate exceptions are documented, not silently bypassed.

INVARIANT 2 — LATENCY SLO (p95 < 2s end-to-end)
================================================
Test mechanism (apps/desktop/test/topbar/push-latency.test.ts):
For each top-bar element, run 100 iterations of:
1. Start with steady-state UI; record DOM snapshot of the element.
2. Trigger the canonical state change (commit/tool-flip/snapshot/usage update).
3. Record wall-clock timestamp of the canonical state change at its origin (daemon perspective).
4. Wait for DOM to reflect the new value (via MutationObserver).
5. Record wall-clock timestamp of the DOM update.
6. Latency = step 5 − step 3.

Aggregate p95 across 100 runs per element; assert each is < 2s. Per-element targets:
- project / branch / Git clean state: triggered by a `vps_commit_created` or `git.status.changed`
  event; expect p95 < 1.5s (no heavy computation).
- tool health dots: triggered by capability flip; expect p95 < 1s.
- swarm count: triggered by an agent registration / departure; expect p95 < 1.5s.
- beads pulse: triggered by br state change; expect p95 < 2s.
- code-health pill: triggered by a new health snapshot; expect p95 < 2s. (This is the explicit
  "within seconds of a new snapshot landing" claim from §7.6 / §7.4.1.)
- subscription pill: triggered by a caut usage snapshot; expect p95 < 2s.
- Activity unread badge: triggered by a new mail/event arrival; expect p95 < 1s.

These per-element p95 targets are added to packages/slo-targets.yaml (hp-5ja) so the SLO
assertion library can fail CI if any are missed.

INVARIANT 3 — ON RECONNECT (linked to hp-nd42 sleep/wake)
==========================================================
After a sleep/wake or tunnel-drop reconnect, the top-bar must re-sync within p95 < 2s of WS
reconnect (not 2s of network return). Test reuses fixture 1 from hp-nd42 (sleep 90s) and asserts
the top-bar pill DOM updates within 2s of the WS reconnect event.

INSTRUMENTATION
================
- Each top-bar element instruments its render path with a `window.__hoopoeTestHooks.topbar`
  observer (only present in test builds) that records (eventId, renderTime).
- The recording HTTP/WS proxy logs every request with a stable test-run ID.
- Failure artifacts include: the full HTTP request log for the run window, the full WS event log,
  the DOM snapshot diff, and the per-element latency samples.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.6 cockpit chrome (the spec being asserted).
- §2.2 daemon API facade — reconciled cache that pushes top-bar data.
- §10.1 backpressure — coalesced events arrive on a fixed cadence; this bead lives within those
  cadence rules.
- §10.5 SLO targets (this bead adds 7 new per-element targets to packages/slo-targets.yaml).
- hp-4ya — Phase 4 cockpit top bar wiring (consumer of these tests; pre-implementation gate).
- hp-ry6 — Phase 11 code health metrics test suite (overlap on health-pill latency; this bead
  enforces the cross-element invariant).
- hp-5ja — packages/slo-targets.yaml + assertion library (this bead extends it).
- hp-wqm — sequence cursors / replayEvents (the protocol that delivers top-bar data).
- hp-nd42 — sleep/wake fixtures (Invariant 3).
- hp-j30 — Phase 1 desktop test suite (this bead's tests live in the same harness).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- apps/desktop/test/topbar/no-poll.test.ts passes with the structural assertion (0 polls in 120s).
- apps/desktop/test/topbar/push-latency.test.ts passes with all 7 per-element p95 targets.
- ESLint rule hoopoe/no-topbar-poll committed; existing top-bar code passes; one fixture file
  exercises the rule (positive + negative case).
- packages/slo-targets.yaml extends with topbar.* targets; hp-5ja's assertion library reads them.
- docs/observability.md gains a "Top bar push-not-poll" subsection with the assertion shape.
- Reconnect-after-sleep top-bar recovery test passes (Invariant 3 fixture).

title: Phase 1 / Phase 4 / §1.5 / §7.6: Top-bar project switcher + multi-project state restoration on app launch (last-active project, last-active stage, last-open bead drawer, scroll positions, Activity-panel state)
description:
WHAT
====
§7.6 names the top-bar 'Project / repo / branch — clickable to switch project'. §1.5 mandates restartability: 'A user can close Hoopoe, reopen it, and understand exactly where the project is.' This bead implements both surfaces in one focused piece of work because they share storage, IPC, and UX shape.

WHY THIS IS A FOCUSED BEAD
==========================
Existing coverage:
- **hp-z1x** — Phase 1 four-stage routed shell + Activity panel drawer. The shell exists.
- **hp-spx** — Phase 4 project activate flow. Single project at a time.
- **hp-ilt** — Phase 4 project create/import. Adds projects.
- **hp-4bt** — Settings store (atomic write, PubSub change stream). Storage exists.
- **hp-jxe** — Phase 4 epic.

What's missing:
1. The **top-bar project picker** itself (a switcher with N>1 projects, last-active highlighting, search).
2. **Active-swarm-confirmation-on-switch** — switching while a swarm is running should prompt: 'Swarm in project X is still running. Continue? (Yes / No / Pause swarm first / View swarm).'
3. **State restoration on launch** — last-active project, last-active stage, last-open bead drawer + scroll, last Activity panel filter + scroll, last DAG zoom/pan. §1.5 names this; today nothing implements it.
4. **Project list ordering** — most-recently-active first, plus pinned section.

UI SHAPE (TOP-BAR)
==================
The leftmost top-bar element from §7.6 ('Project / repo / branch') becomes a button: `{projectName} ▾ — {branch} — {clean|dirty status}`. Click opens a 380px-wide popover:

- **Search box** — fuzzy match on project name, slug, repo URL, branch.
- **Pinned section** — projects the user marked pinned (right-click → pin). Pinned ordering preserved.
- **Recent section** — non-pinned projects, ordered by lastActivatedAt desc, top 8.
- **Per-project row** —
  - Project name + slug
  - Current branch + clean/dirty/unpushed badges
  - Tool-health dots (mini, 3 dots: VPS / NTM / Mail)
  - Swarm-running pill if applicable: 'X agents running'
  - Last-activated timestamp (e.g. '2h ago')
- **Footer** — '+ Add project' (opens Phase 4 import/create flow), 'Manage projects' (opens Settings → Projects).

Keyboard: ⌘P opens the picker (scoped to project switching, distinct from a command palette ⌘K which is hp-6f4). ↑/↓ to navigate, Enter to select, Esc to close.

ACTIVE-SWARM-CONFIRMATION
==========================
When the user selects a different project AND the current project has a running swarm:
- Modal: 'Swarm in {currentProject} is still running ({N} agents, {M} ready beads).'
- Choices:
  - **Continue (default keeps swarm)** — switches view; swarm continues on VPS (per §1.2); top-bar swarm-state pill shows the *target* project's swarm state, not the source's.
  - **Pause swarm first** — invokes `tend-swarm` agent to send pause-marching-orders + pauses ASR (audit), then switches.
  - **View swarm** — cancels switch, navigates to Swarm stage of the source project.

The 'still running on VPS' status is the §1.2 invariant — Hoopoe Desktop closing or switching projects does NOT halt the swarm. The dialog wording reflects this so the user understands they can navigate freely.

STATE RESTORATION
=================
On app launch:
1. Load `~/.hoopoe/userdata/desktop-settings.json` (per t3code three-store split, hp-4bt) — read `lastActiveProjectId`, `pinnedProjectIds`.
2. Load `~/.hoopoe/userdata/client-settings.json` per-project state — `{<projectId>: {lastStage, lastBeadDrawerId, lastBeadDrawerScrollY, activityPanelOpen, activityPanelFilter, activityPanelScrollPosition, dagViewport: {zoom, pan}}}`.
3. After daemon connect + project activate (hp-spx), restore the per-project state.
4. If the last project no longer exists (deleted from VPS), fall back to most-recent-existing project; if none, show 'No projects' empty state.

Per-project state is saved on every navigation event with a 250ms debounce + atomic write (per t3code lift). State that depends on remote data (e.g., 'last-open bead drawer') is best-effort — if the bead no longer exists, just open the project root view.

OBSERVABILITY: SWITCH AS AN AUDIT EVENT
========================================
Project switch is itself an auditable user action (per §1.4):
- Audit entry: `{actor: user, action: 'project.switched', from: projectIdFrom, to: projectIdTo, source: 'top-bar' | 'cmd-p' | 'deep-link', durationMs}`
- Activity-panel event in the *target* project ('User switched to this project').
- Tracking lets Diagnostics → Audit log (hp-1wg8) answer 'when did the user move to this project?'

UNIT TESTS (10)
================
1. **Project picker rendering** — given 5 fixture projects (2 pinned, 3 recent), picker renders Pinned section (2 items, in pin order) + Recent section (3 items, ordered by lastActivatedAt desc).
2. **Fuzzy search** — typing 'demo' matches 'demo-project' and 'live-demo'; typing 'fe/X' matches 'frontend-X' (slash treated as separator).
3. **Active-swarm-confirmation modal** — current project has `swarm.activeAgents=4`; switching opens the modal; 'Continue' click invokes router.push without halting swarm; 'Pause swarm first' invokes tend-swarm pause RPC then router.push.
4. **State save debounce** — 5 navigation events within 100ms result in 1 atomic write after the 250ms debounce.
5. **State restore on launch** — given seed desktop-settings.json + client-settings.json, app starts → router lands at `/projects/{lastActiveProjectId}/{lastStage}`; after daemon connect + activate, bead drawer reopens at lastBeadDrawerId.
6. **Last-project-deleted fallback** — `lastActiveProjectId` no longer in /v1/projects → app falls back to most-recent-existing; if none, /no-projects shown; structured log `project_switcher.last_active_missing`.
7. **Pin / unpin** — right-click pin → pinnedProjectIds updated atomically; reordering pins via drag-and-drop persists.
8. **Top-bar dirty badge** — current project's GitStatus shows clean/dirty/unpushed correctly per hp-w8m events.
9. **⌘P keyboard** — pressing ⌘P opens picker; ↑/↓/Enter/Esc work; arrow nav doesn't bleed into other ⌘-shortcut handlers.
10. **Concurrent switch attempt** — clicking switch twice rapidly (race) → second click is debounced; only one switch event fired; structured log `project_switcher.duplicate_switch_suppressed`.

E2E TESTS (7)
==============
1. **Multi-project flow** — Two fixture projects A and B → import both → switch from A to B via ⌘P → confirm router URL + top bar updated → switch back via top-bar button click → confirm.
2. **Restart with swarm running** — Project A swarm running; close desktop; reopen → app reconnects to daemon → top bar shows A active and 'X agents running' pill → bead drawer (last open) reopens.
3. **Swap projects mid-swarm** — Switch from A (swarm running) to B → confirmation modal → 'Continue' → URL changes → swarm in A still reported as active per /v1/swarms.
4. **Pause-swarm-first path** — Switch from A (swarm running) → 'Pause swarm first' → tend-swarm receives pause action → swarm reports paused → switch proceeds → audit chain visible in hp-1wg8 explorer.
5. **Project deletion while pinned** — Pin project C; delete C from Hoopoe (via Settings → Projects); restart → C no longer in pinned list; structured log captured.
6. **State restore covers Activity-panel filter** — Open Activity panel; apply filter 'agent=ag-7'; close drawer; switch project away; switch back → drawer reopens with the same filter applied.
7. **Audit chain** — Project switch → switch event in audit log → click audit entry's correlationId → all related events for the switch (state-save, daemon-activate, view-restoration) cluster under the same correlationId.

DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (per hp-lxs)
==========================================
- `project_switcher.opened` { trigger (top-bar-click | cmd-p | deep-link), pinned_count, recent_count, search_term? }
- `project_switcher.selected` { from_project_id, to_project_id, modal_shown (active_swarm | none), modal_choice? }
- `project_switcher.duplicate_switch_suppressed` { project_id, suppress_count }
- `project_state.saved` { project_id, fields_saved (set), bytes, took_ms }
- `project_state.restored` { project_id, fields_restored (set), missing_artifacts (set), took_ms }
- `project_switcher.last_active_missing` { last_active_project_id, fallback_project_id }

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §1.5 'Build for restartability'
- §7.6 'Top-bar … Project / repo / branch — clickable to switch project'
- §1.2 'The desktop app is not the orchestrator of record' (swarm continues on switch)
- hp-z1x (four-stage routed shell), hp-spx (project activate), hp-ilt (project create/import), hp-4bt (settings store), hp-jxe (Phase 4 epic), hp-r3i (schemas), hp-elx (design system), hp-i62 (component set), hp-g73 (audit log), hp-1wg8 (audit explorer)

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Top-bar project button + ⌘P picker
- Pinned + recent sections, fuzzy search
- Active-swarm-confirmation modal with 3 choices
- State restoration covering 7 fields (project, stage, beadDrawer, beadScroll, activityPanelOpen, activityPanelFilter, dagViewport)
- desktop-settings.json + client-settings.json schema additions (with migration via hp-9xtt)
- Daemon endpoint or read-model wiring as needed
- 10 unit + 7 e2e tests passing
- Structured logging events emitted
- 250ms debounce + atomic write under all state changes
- Documentation: 'Hoopoe remembers where you left off.'
close_reason: Top-bar project switcher, persisted per-project shell state, active-swarm switch confirmation, ProjectRegistry scaffold

title: Phase 5: Plan locking + version history + bead-delta-on-amend
description:
WHAT
====
The 'lock plan' action (§7.1 'Locking') and the post-lock amendment flow.

LOCKING (§7.1)
===============
- Writes final plan.md.
- Creates a snapshot hash.
- Requires unresolved decisions to be either accepted or resolved.
- Marks meta.json `locked: true`.
- Enables 'Convert to beads' (Phase 6).
- Audit entry written.

POST-LOCK AMENDMENTS
======================
Amendments to a locked plan create a NEW VERSION of the plan (not in-place edit). Diff vs previous version is rendered. Changes can trigger 'bead delta analysis' — re-running plan-to-bead conversion against just the diff to surface additions/removals.

UNRESOLVED DECISIONS
======================
.hoopoe/plans/<plan-id>/unresolved-decisions.md — list maintained by refinement rounds + user. Each entry has options + chosen / accepted / deferred state. Lock gate reads this list; refuses lock if any are still 'open'.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 — locking.
- Phase 6 — bead conversion gate is 'plan locked or override'.
- §4.2 gate invariants — 'Plan locked' = plan self-contained, major decisions explicit, testing strategy present, unresolved decisions listed or accepted.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Lock action implemented end-to-end.
- Lock gate enforces unresolved-decisions rule.
- Amendments create new version + diff view.
- Bead-delta analysis surfaces changes after re-amend.
- Unlock action available with confirmation (audit-logged).
- E2E test: plan → lock → 'Convert to beads' button enabled; amend → new version + diff visible.

title: Phase 6: Polish-rounds jobs (6 rounds, each tracked artifact, beads-workflow polish prompt)
description:
WHAT
====
Polish-round jobs (§7.2). Each round is a tracked job with its own artifact. The user runs them iteratively until steady-state per the beads-workflow skill ('Run polish prompt 6-9 times until steady-state').

ROUNDS (§7.2)
==============
Round 1: plan coverage  — does each plan section have at least one bead?
Round 2: dependency correctness — are blocking edges accurate? cycles? false deps?
Round 3: granularity and split/merge — are beads sized for one focused agent session?
Round 4: test obligations and acceptance criteria — every bead has acceptance criteria + test plan?
Round 5: parallel execution tracks — bv --robot-plan parallelism check.
Round 6: fresh-eyes review of bead graph — brand-new agent session, no prior bead-set context.

PROMPT (beads-workflow 'THE EXACT PROMPT — Polish (Standard)')
================================================================
The exact polish prompt from the skill is the basis for every round. Round-specific framing is wrapped around it.

ARTIFACTS PER ROUND
=====================
- prompt sent to model
- detections + suggestions
- diff applied to bead set
- per-round quality metrics
- audit links per change

UI (§7.2 'Rounds view')
=========================
Rounds view exposes the literal prompt sent to each polish-round model alongside the action summary, making each round's artifact directly inspectable per §1.4 (design/mockups/v1/beads.jsx RoundsModal).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 — polish rounds list.
- beads-workflow skill 'Polishing protocol'.
- §1.4 — every meaningful automated action must be inspectable.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Steady-state detection: when a round produces no meaningful changes, recommend stopping.
- Polish rounds are user-triggered (not automatic); user can run any subset, any order, multiple times.
- Cross-model polish: optionally run the final round through a different primary model (Codex / Gemini / GPT-Pro) for cross-model perspective.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All 6 rounds implemented as job templates.
- Round artifact viewer + RoundsModal per mockup.
- Steady-state recommendation working.
- Each round audited end-to-end.
- Test: polish a fixture bead set → round 3 splits an oversized bead correctly.

title: Phase 7: bv --robot-triage panel + dependency editing + cycle warnings
description:
WHAT
====
A right-side panel showing bv --robot-triage output (current ready frontier, recommended next bead, parallelism plan from --robot-plan) + dependency-editing UI + cycle-warning panel.

PANEL CONTENTS
================
- Triage summary: top-N recommended beads.
- Parallelism plan: tracks of beads that can be worked in parallel (from bv --robot-plan).
- Insights: graph-health metrics from bv --robot-insights (cycles, bottlenecks, branch factor).
- Cycle warnings: list of cycles detected, with click-through to a cycle-resolution UI.

DEPENDENCY EDITING
===================
- From DAG view: drag from node to node to add a dep.
- From drawer: 'Add dependency' / 'Remove dependency' actions.
- Each edit: PATCH /v1/projects/{id}/beads/{beadId} → calls br dep add / remove → audited → bv re-run on save.

CYCLE WARNINGS
================
- If user-initiated edit creates a cycle, refuse with clear message + show the cycle.
- bv --robot-insights detects cycles on every change; warnings panel auto-updates.
- Cycle resolution UI: highlights the smallest set of edges to remove to break the cycle.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.2 — bv --robot-* usage.
- §1.3 — never bare bv.
- bv adapter — Phase 6.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Triage panel renders bv --robot-triage / --robot-plan / --robot-insights.
- Dependency editing works from DAG + drawer.
- Cycles detected before save; refused with clear message.
- bv re-runs after every edit; insights panel updates.
- Test: deliberate cycle attempt → refused; resolve via removing one edge → bv passes.

title: Phase 5: Oracle adapter for ChatGPT Pro browser mode (oracle serve on Mac, VPS calls via --remote-host)
description:
WHAT
====
Oracle adapter for the planning pipeline's ChatGPT Pro path. Desktop-side oracle serve lifecycle management (start/stop, health check, port pinning); VPS-daemon --remote-host/--remote-token integration; browser-auto-reattach for long Pro runs; explicit 'Mac must stay awake' UI warning when a Pro round is active.

WHY (§7.1, §17 oracle reference)
=================================
Oracle is the harness Hoopoe uses to reach ChatGPT Pro web from the planning pipeline. Browser-mode automation drives a logged-in chatgpt.com session — the only way to use a ChatGPT Pro subscription (no API equivalent). MVP: oracle serve on user's Mac (Chrome already signed in); VPS daemon calls via --remote-host over the SSH tunnel. Post-MVP: VPS-resident Oracle.

DESKTOP-SIDE ORACLE LIFECYCLE
==============================
- Detect Oracle install (brew oracle binary path or npm install -g @steipete/oracle).
- Wizard's Phase 3 extension installs Oracle if missing.
- Manage oracle serve start/stop:
  - Start oracle serve on a chosen local port (configurable, default 7800).
  - Configure --remote-token (32-byte random secret).
  - Health-check via oracle serve status.
- Surface Oracle status in Diagnostics ('Restart Oracle' repair, §10.2).

VPS DAEMON --REMOTE-HOST WIRING
================================
- Daemon's Oracle invocation: oracle --engine browser --model gpt-5.4-pro --prompt … --file … --write-output … --remote-host <mac>:<port> --remote-token <token>.
- The VPS speaks to oracle on the Mac via the SSH tunnel (reverse-forward; the SSH session already establishes a path to Mac:7800).
- Output captured into plan-job artifact stream.

BROWSER-AUTO-REATTACH FOR LONG RUNS (§7.1)
============================================
Extended-reasoning runs can be 5-30 min. Hoopoe surfaces this clearly: 'this will pause the active ChatGPT Pro round' if user about to close the lid. Oracle auto-reattaches to the same Chrome tab if the prior connection drops; daemon retries the call once on transient failures.

'MAC MUST STAY AWAKE' UI
=========================
- Plan-job UI shows: 'Mac active for ChatGPT Pro round — sleeping pauses this round.'
- Top-bar indicator 'Oracle ACTIVE' visible.
- Optional: powerSaveBlocker.start('prevent-app-suspension') during active Pro round (only with user opt-in; respect macOS power UX).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 — execution modes.
- §10.2 — Diagnostics 'Restart Oracle' repair.
- §17 oracle reference.
- Phase 3 wizard — installs Oracle.
- Phase 0 fixtures — Oracle integration contract.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- Oracle's --remote-token provides a second layer of auth on top of the SSH tunnel.
- Cookie persistence on the user's Chrome profile is Oracle's concern, not Hoopoe's.
- If oracle serve is unreachable, fall back to a CLI-backed primary model with a clear warning (§7.1 'Fallbacks').
- Post-MVP: VPS-resident Oracle option (headed Chrome + Xvfb + persistent automation profile, manual login during onboarding).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Oracle adapter integrated as one of the planning pipeline's options.
- Desktop manages oracle serve lifecycle.
- VPS daemon successfully runs an Oracle call against the local Mac.
- 'Mac must stay awake' warning visible during active Pro rounds.
- 'Restart Oracle' Diagnostics repair works.
- Smoke test: planning pipeline kicks off a real ChatGPT Pro candidate; output appears in candidates/chatgpt-pro.md.

title: Phase 1: Clone & pin t3code, initialize monorepo (Turbo + Bun + workspaces)
description:
WHAT
====
Clone github.com/pingdotgg/t3code into /tmp/t3code-pinned and pin its commit SHA in docs/source-provenance.md. Initialize the Hoopoe monorepo: Turbo + Bun workspaces, apps/{desktop, daemon}, packages/{schemas, design-system}.

WHY (§16 Phase 1 week 1, Appendix B)
=====================================
T3 Code is MIT-licensed; we MUST preserve copyright notice in any substantial portion of the source. Pinning a commit SHA gives us a fixed reference — they ship fixes (auth hardening, updater improvements) we'd want, but we can't cleanly merge because we strip/refactor half the surface. Pin → quarterly review their CHANGELOG → cherry-pick deliberately, not automatically (§14 risks 'Upstream t3code drift').

DELIVERABLES
============
1. /tmp/t3code-pinned with a recorded commit SHA written into docs/source-provenance.md (file format: tool, repo, license, pinned-sha, lift-date, license-file-path, attribution-rules).
2. Monorepo skeleton at the project root:
   - apps/desktop/ — Electron + React + TS + Vite + Tailwind
   - apps/daemon/ — Go module
   - packages/schemas/ — OpenAPI + generated TS + generated Go
   - packages/design-system/ — tokens + components + Storybook
   - packages/fixtures/ — Mock Flywheel corpus
3. Turbo turbo.json with task pipelines for build/test/lint/typecheck.
4. Bun workspaces configured in root package.json, version-controlled bun.lockb.
5. NOTICE file at the project root with attribution to t3code MIT lift.
6. apps/desktop/src/vendored/t3code/LICENSE copied verbatim from t3code.
7. README.md placeholder (will be expanded later) covering monorepo layout.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- DO NOT lift apps/server/, apps/web/, apps/marketing/, packages/effect-acp/, packages/effect-codex-app-server/, packages/contracts/ from t3code (Appendix B 'Files we explicitly do NOT lift').
- The vendored layout: lifted files land under apps/desktop/src/vendored/t3code/ with the MIT notice preserved at top of each file. Adaptations (rebranding, rewiring) happen in our own files that import from vendored/. Do NOT edit vendored/ files in place except for mechanical mass renames — keep the diff against upstream small enough to re-merge later if needed.
- Use Bun (not pnpm/yarn). T3code's whole build pipeline assumes Bun; deviating creates friction.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- bun install at the repo root succeeds.
- bun run typecheck passes (empty smoke).
- bun run lint passes.
- A single dummy test passes in each package via 'bun test'.
- docs/source-provenance.md and NOTICE files exist with correct attribution.
- Clean git tree; commit message references this bead's ID.
close_reason: Cloned t3code @ 460d9c3e, scaffolded Turbo+Bun monorepo (apps/desktop, apps/daemon, packages/{schemas,design-system,fixtures}); NOTICE + docs/source-provenance.md + apps/desktop/src/vendored/t3code/LICENSE in place; bun install + typecheck + lint + test + build all green

title: Phase 8: Daemon-side PTY plumbing for tending + Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' debug toggle (audited)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side PTY plumbing per §7.3 'PTY plumbing still exists on the daemon side'. Wired ONLY to:
1. orchestrator-chat tending agent (Phase 10).
2. tend-swarm long-no-output / pattern-matched stuck-state heuristics (Phase 10).
3. Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' debug toggle per agent (this phase).
4. Post-mortem investigation tools (pane snapshots into audit log when pt-driven kill fires).

PTY TRANSPORT LADDER (§7.3)
=============================
- NTM WebSocket / robot-tail PREFERRED.
- tmux pipe-pane fallback.
- tmux capture-pane polling LAST.
- NEVER one-SSH-per-pane and NEVER aggressive renderer polling.

PERSISTENCE (§7.3, §2.7)
==========================
- Pane output is byte-addressable, ring-buffered, persisted as logs with offsets.
- Never rendered to the user except behind explicit Diagnostics toggle.
- Logs retained for 30 days (§10.3); configurable per project.

DIAGNOSTICS 'SHOW RAW PANE' TOGGLE (§7.3, §10.2)
==================================================
- Per-agent toggle in Diagnostics — explicit opt-in.
- Audited on toggle ON and toggle OFF.
- Auto-closes after configurable idle window.
- Read-only — does not let the user type into the pane.
- Streams the agent's tmux pane output to a Diagnostics-only TerminalPane (xterm.js).

WHY THIS BEAD IS PHASE 8 NOT 10
=================================
The PTY plumbing is needed by Phase 8 acceptance: 'opening Diagnostics Show raw pane toggle for one agent reveals scrollback for forensic inspection and writes audit entry.' Phase 10 USES this plumbing for tending; Phase 8 INSTALLS it.

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.3 — full PTY plumbing spec.
- §10.2 — Show raw pane repair detail.
- §14 — PTY streaming fidelity risk.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- PTY plumbing installed on daemon side; pane output ring-buffered + persisted.
- Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' toggle works per-agent.
- Toggle ON/OFF audited.
- Auto-close after idle window.
- Read-only enforced.
- Phase 8 acceptance: pane scrollback visible in forensic mode.

title: Phase 4: ru --json adapter for VPS-side multi-project ops (sync, status, list, prune)
description:
WHAT
====
GitAdapter integration with `ru` for VPS-side multi-project operations per §2.3 + §17 'narrowly adopted':
- ru sync --json --non-interactive — N-project origin sync.
- ru status --no-fetch --json — cheap multi-project status.
- ru list --paths — project enumeration.
- ru prune --archive — orphan-clone detection (wired as Diagnostics repair, §10.2).

DELIBERATELY NOT ADOPTED (§17 closing notes)
=============================================
- ru review, ru agent-sweep, ru ai-sync, ru dep-update — overlap with §7.4 / §8 / §9 in primitives, but workflow shape is different:
  - GitHub work items vs beads
  - Dirty-repo sweep vs planned swarm
  - Per-repo sessions vs bead claims
- Each owns its own state store under ~/.local/state/ru/** — shelling out would create a parallel source of truth for session state (§1.1).
- Same rationale as §7.1's 'Resolved' paragraph on apr.

REFERENCE-IMPLEMENTATION VALUE
================================
~17,700 lines of worked Bash solving problems Hoopoe hits in §2.7 / §2.8 / §5.4 / §7.4 / §8 / §9. Read those code paths during corresponding phases and PORT THE PATTERNS into Go — do NOT invoke ru to execute them. Patterns to port:
- NTM robot-mode integration (--robot-spawn/send/wait/activity/status/interrupt) with IDLE/TYPING/THINKING/TOOL_USE/COMPLETE/ERROR → unified state mapping
- Priority scoring (type/labels/age/recency/staleness weights)
- Blocking-prompt risk classification (high=Password:/passphrase/OTP; medium=merge-conflict/host-key; low=commit-message)
- Quality-gate auto-detection by project type (npm/cargo/go/pytest/shellcheck)
- Secret-scan patterns (sk-/ghp_/xox/AKIA/BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY) — already used by audit redaction epic
- Global backoff coordination (shared backoff.state with pause_until + exponential + jitter + cap)
- Work-stealing queue via atomic mkdir locks
- GraphQL alias batching (25 repos/query)
- Idempotent GitHub-action execution (gh_actions.jsonl dedupe log)
- Digest caching

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.3 — adapter precedence.
- §17 — narrow adoption rationale.
- §10.2 — Diagnostics 'Detect / archive orphan project clones' (ru prune --archive non-destructive; --delete requires confirmation).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- ru sync / status / list / prune wired in GitAdapter.
- ru --schema parsed at adapter init so output shapes are pinned.
- Multi-project status read efficient (no per-project fetch when --no-fetch).
- ru prune --archive wired as a Diagnostics repair action (non-destructive default).
- NDJSON per-repo result format and exit-code vocabulary documented.
- Patterns from ru reference-impl stub-tracked in TODO comments for porting in later phases.

title: Phase 1: Build four-stage routed shell (Planning, Beads, Swarm, Debugging) + Activity panel drawer
description:
WHAT
====
Implement the four-stage shell using TanStack Router with typed stage routes plus a global Activity panel drawer (§7.5) overlaying any stage. STAGE N — VERB chrome on each stage matches design/mockups/v1/.

STAGE LIST (§7 Hoopoe v1)
==========================
- 01 — PLAN
- 02 — BEAD
- 03 — SWARM
- 04 — HARDEN (a.k.a. Debugging / Hardening)
+ Activity panel — cross-stage drawer (NOT a stage, no route).

WHY ROUTED-AND-NOT-MODAL
========================
Each stage owns its URL so deep links into specific projects/plans/beads work, browser back/forward semantics work for power users, and the renderer's TanStack Query cache keys to route. Activity panel is a drawer because §7.5 says it overlays the current stage and persists across stages — a route would require restoring the stage on close.

REQUIRED COMPONENTS (this bead, scaffolding only)
===================================================
1. RootLayout — left sidebar with stage rail (icons + labels) + persistent top bar (§7.6) + main content area + Activity panel drawer toggle (right edge).
2. StageHeader (per stage) — STAGE N — VERB framing, breadcrumb (project name → plan / bead / swarm), stage-specific actions slot.
3. EmptyState placeholder per stage (data wiring comes in later phases).
4. Activity panel drawer skeleton — open/close, takes max ~480px wide, slides in from right, overlay backdrop, escape-to-close, persistence flag in zustand.
5. Routes:
   - / — project picker / first-run
   - /:projectId — default to /:projectId/plan
   - /:projectId/plan — Stage 01
   - /:projectId/bead — Stage 02
   - /:projectId/swarm — Stage 03
   - /:projectId/harden — Stage 04
   - /:projectId/diag — Diagnostics (§10.2)
6. Project picker on root — list configured projects, '+ Add project' empty state.
7. Persistent top bar (§7.6 chrome) — slot wired but content empty for now (filled in Phase 4 wiring).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §3 — TanStack Router (typed stage routes), Zustand (ephemeral UI state), TanStack Query (server cache).
- §7.6 — top bar layout (project / repo / branch, tool health, swarm state, beads pulse, code-health pill, subscription pill, Activity panel toggle).
- §7.5 — Activity panel: drawer + persistent across stages, badged with unread count.
- design/mockups/v1/* — visual reference.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- DO NOT couple the stage shell to any data sources yet; that's Phase 4+.
- The shell should support Mock Flywheel Mode out of the box (so visual review can happen on fixture data per Phase 1 exit).
- Keyboard navigation between stages: ⌘1/⌘2/⌘3/⌘4 or similar (registered via command registry, not bare hotkey listeners).
- Active route surface state is reflected in the sidebar (highlighted icon).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/ contains the four stage routes; bun run dev launches the app and stage navigation works.
- Storybook stories for RootLayout, StageHeader, Activity drawer (empty state).
- Visual diff against design/mockups/v1/* clears the four-stage shell + Activity drawer.
- e2e Playwright test: app boots → opens Planning → opens Beads → toggles Activity panel → closes → opens Diagnostics.
close_reason: shipped in 629cc71 (routed shell scaffold)

title: Phase 5: Plan-input chat box UX — single textarea + per-plan model picker + clarify/take-first-shot toggle
description:
WHAT
====
The new-plan empty-state chat box (§7.1 'Plan-input chat box'). Single textarea for the user's description + three controls underneath.

CONTROLS (§7.1 plan-input chat box)
=====================================
1. **Primary model** picker (does synthesis + refinement). Default: ChatGPT Pro via Oracle browser mode. Fallbacks (greyed out if subscription not configured): Codex CLI (GPT-5 Pro), Claude Code (Opus), Gemini CLI (Gemini 3 Pro / Deep Think).
2. **Competing-candidate models** (up to 3, in addition to primary). Default: Claude Opus, Gemini 3 Pro, one of {Grok Heavy, GPT-5.4 via Codex CLI}. User can deselect; picker greys unavailable models (subscription check via CAAM).
3. **'Let Hoopoe choose models for me'** toggle — picks the 4-way default if user doesn't want to think.

KICKOFF-MODE TOGGLE (§7.1)
============================
Binary: 'Ask clarifying questions' (models interview user before drafting) vs 'Take a first shot' (models go straight to draft). Default: clarify.

ONE-SHOT INPUT (§7.1)
======================
After 'Generate plans' click, the screen flips to a live progress view showing each candidate model's status (queued → drafting → completed/failed) and the side-by-side artifact rail. The chat box itself is NOT an ongoing conversation; it's a one-shot input.

EXISTING-CODEBASE BUNDLE (§7.1)
=================================
A third sub-mode applies to both 'import' and 'create-from-rough-idea' flows: when the project already has a codebase (non-empty repo), Hoopoe automatically attaches an existing-codebase context bundle:
- README, AGENTS.md, architecture docs
- package manifests
- test layout
- existing beads via br list --json
- current health hotspots from §7.4

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §7.1 — full chat-box spec.
- design/mockups/v1/wizard.jsx StepBrief — visual reference.
- §13 — subscription requirement empty-state message.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- Chat box UI matches mockup.
- Primary picker greys unavailable models.
- Competing-candidate selector works.
- 'Let Hoopoe choose' applies the agent-flywheel default ratios.
- Kickoff-mode toggle wired to the planning-pipeline job.
- Existing-codebase context bundle attached when project has a populated repo.
- Empty-state without subscriptions: clear message pointing to subscription pages.

title: Hoopoe v1: Mac Electron cockpit for the Agentic Coding Flywheel
description:
TOP-LEVEL PROJECT EPIC. All other beads roll up here.

WHAT IT IS
==========
Hoopoe is a macOS Electron desktop app that turns the Agentic Coding Flywheel (ACFS) — a powerful but currently manual collection of CLIs, tmux sessions, agent prompts, mail messages, bead graphs, and build/test logs — into one staged operational cockpit. The product is organized into FOUR numbered stages plus one cross-stage UI surface:

  01 Planning
  02 Beads
  03 Swarm
  04 Debugging / Hardening
  + Activity panel (cross-stage drawer)

NON-NEGOTIABLE STRATEGIC THESIS
================================
"Hoopoe is the cockpit, not the engine. The VPS is the execution plane. The existing Flywheel tools remain the source-of-truth systems."

Hoopoe centralizes/visualizes/automates/audits the Flywheel. It does NOT replace br, bv, ntm, Agent Mail, rch, ACFS, CAAM, DCG, CASS, Git, or the agent CLIs. Its job is to make the workflow visible, resumable, safe, and less manually operated.

ARCHITECTURE (3 components)
============================
1. Hoopoe Desktop  — Electron + React + TS + Tailwind. Mac-only v1.
2. Hoopoe Server    — GO daemon (chi/echo, modernc SQLite, gorilla/nhooyr WS, creack/pty). Binds to 127.0.0.1; desktop reaches it through SSH tunnel by default.
3. ACFS CLIs        — installed by bootstrap, never reimplemented. Adapter precedence: official APIs (NTM serve) → --robot-* JSON → .beads/issues.jsonl → read-only SQLite → CLI text parsing as last resort.

PRODUCT POSITION (load-bearing)
================================
- Subscription-only: Claude Max / GPT Pro / Gemini Ultra / ChatGPT Pro. NO BYOK. NO direct provider API calls. No OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY field anywhere. Linter/CI rule: import of any provider SDK in daemon or desktop fails the build.
- Mac-only v1 (lifted CI matrix supports Win/Linux but we ship only Mac arm64+x64).
- Existing-VPS-first onboarding for v1; provider plugins (Contabo first) are post-MVP.
- Default network posture: SSH tunnel + bearer + WS-token. mTLS public mode is advanced/team mode, not v1.
- Default Swarm UI is TERMINAL-FREE — bead state + agent state + Activity panel only. PTY plumbing exists daemon-side for tending and forensics; raw scrollback is reachable only via Diagnostics 'Show raw pane' toggle (audited).

LIFT STRATEGY
=============
Desktop lifecycle, auth, settings, keybindings, build pipeline are vendored from github.com/pingdotgg/t3code (MIT) — see Appendix B. Effect framework is NOT adopted, only patterns. Go daemon is greenfield (auth/settings/protocol shapes from t3code, implementation ours). The 2,175-line t3code main.ts gets decomposed on day one into BackendLifecycle / UpdateMachine / IpcRegistry / WindowManager / SettingsBridge / AuthBridge.

OUT OF SCOPE (deliberate, not deferred)
========================================
- BYOK / direct provider API mode
- Desktop-only planning escape hatch
- OpenCode multi-backend harness (covered by Claude Code + Codex CLI + Gemini CLI + Oracle)
- apr as planning backend (Hoopoe owns the pipeline directly per §7.1)
- Raw terminal panes as default user-visible surface
- wa (WezTerm Automata) — committed to tmux/NTM

DEFINITION OF SUCCESS
=====================
A successful Hoopoe session: user opens app → reconnects to VPS+project → top bar shows full state → Planning surfaces artifacts → Beads show ready/blocked/critical/stale/in-review work → user launches mixed NTM swarm → agents follow per-bead lifecycle → Hoopoe streams state through stage views and Activity panel → tending jobs intervene when warranted (most ticks wakeAgent:false / [SILENT]) → Debugging/Hardening surfaces health, runs review rounds → findings become fixes or beads → convergence is visible → session lands with synced beads, clean Git, passing tests.

WHY THIS MATTERS
================
The agent-flywheel methodology unlocks 10+-agent swarms running 24/7 for under \$700/month — but only if a human can actually steer them. Today that steering is happening in tmux + multiple CLIs + scattered logs, which is why most users bounce off. Hoopoe is the surface that makes the methodology approachable without compromising its rigor.

REFERENCES
==========
- Plan: /Users/osekkat/hoopoeAppCockpit/plan.md (~2,362 lines, decisions + roadmap)
- Methodology: agent-flywheel.com/complete-guide
- Setup: github.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
- Tools: br (beads_rust), bv, ntm, Agent Mail, ru, CAAM, DCG, caut, casr, srp, sbh, pt, UBS, Oracle
- Pattern: github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent (tending scheduler shape)
- Lift source: github.com/pingdotgg/t3code (MIT)

CHILD ROLLUP
============
This epic rolls up: 14 phase epics (Phase 0 → Phase 13), cross-cutting concerns (schemas, design system, audit log, approvals queue, capability registry, mock flywheel mode), and the test-harness work. Closing this epic means a new user can install Hoopoe, connect a VPS, import a project, plan, convert to beads, launch agents, tend, review, and ship — per the §15 definition of success.

title: Phase 1: Decompose t3code main.ts into BackendLifecycle/UpdateMachine/IpcRegistry/WindowManager/SettingsBridge/AuthBridge
description:
WHAT
====
Split t3code's apps/desktop/src/main.ts (2,175 lines, monolith) into discrete modules under apps/desktop/src/main/ on day one. This is THE single biggest anti-pattern lift could carry (Appendix B 'Anti-patterns to refuse' #4); doing it once at lift time is dramatically cheaper than refactoring after features have been built atop the monolith.

WHY (§16 Phase 1 task 4, Appendix B)
=====================================
The 2,175 lines tangle: Electron app lifecycle, multi-window management, daemon spawn/health/restart, update polling/state machine, IPC handlers (~30 of them), shell-env sync, confirm dialogs, settings bridge, auth bridge, runtime-arch detection, and crash reporting — each in implicit string-switches and nested closures. Decomposing now means every later feature has a clean place to plug in. Decomposing later means a 6,000-line monolith.

DECOMPOSITION TARGETS
======================
1. BackendLifecycle.ts
   - daemon spawn (Hoopoe's Go binary, NOT process.execPath with ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE — drop that codepath per Appendix B).
   - findOpenPort(preferred) implementation (NEW — fills the t3code anti-pattern #5 gap).
   - ServerListeningDetector wiring (parse stdout for 'listening on' line).
   - waitForHttpReady (health ping with backoff).
   - ExpectedBackendExitChildren WeakSet to distinguish wanted-exit vs crash.
   - stopBackendAndWaitForExit: TERM → grace → KILL escalation.
   - Local-demo FD-3 envelope path (DEFERRED for v1, remote daemon is the default; keep code for future local-demo-mode per Appendix B).
2. UpdateMachine.ts
   - Lift t3code/updateMachine.ts whole (no adaptation).
   - 15s startup delay before first check; 4hr poll; channel selection from desktop-settings.json.
3. IpcRegistry.ts
   - Real command registry: commandRegistry.register(id, handler, {whenContextKeys?}).
   - Replaces t3code's implicit string-switch IPC dispatch (Appendix B anti-pattern #6).
   - Extends to renderer-side ⌘K command palette (separate bead) so per-agent commands and tending-job commands can register without touching renderer code.
4. WindowManager.ts
   - Window creation, multi-window state, restore-on-dock-click, reveal helpers (lifted from windowReveal.ts).
   - Strict CSP, contextIsolation: true, sandbox: true where compatible, nodeIntegration: false (§5.4).
5. SettingsBridge.ts
   - Three-store split (~/.hoopoe/userdata/{daemon-settings.json, desktop-settings.json, client-settings.json}) with hot-reload + atomic write + PubSub change stream.
   - relaunchDesktopApp(reason) for restart-on-toggle settings.
   - Schema-validated reads (per packages/schemas/).
6. AuthBridge.ts
   - Capture pairing token from bootstrap stdout (over SSH session, see §5.2 / §6.3 step 10).
   - Exchange pairing → 30-day bearer over the SSH tunnel.
   - Persist bearer via safeStorage.encryptString.
   - Just-in-time WS-token issuance before each WebSocket connect.

ADDITIONAL: drop the ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE codepath
====================================================
T3code uses process.execPath with ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE to run Node code from the Electron binary as a way to share the binary. Hoopoe's daemon is a separate Go binary, so this trick is unnecessary. Removing it shrinks BackendLifecycle, eliminates an entire class of env-leak bugs, and avoids the Electron-as-Node footgun.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- IpcRegistry should validate handler input/output against generated TS types from packages/schemas/ — this is what makes the IPC contract typed end-to-end.
- WindowManager: strict CSP. No 'unsafe-inline'; use nonces. No 'unsafe-eval'.
- SettingsBridge: PubSub uses BOUNDED channels (Appendix B anti-pattern #1: PubSub.unbounded is rejected).
- AuthBridge must NOT log or expose pairing tokens or bearer tokens; redaction layer applies (audit log epic).

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- apps/desktop/src/main/ contains the six modules above; no main.ts file > 200 lines.
- Each module has a clear interface; cross-module communication is via typed events or function calls, not via shared globals.
- Unit tests cover at least: BackendLifecycle (spawn → ready → kill), IpcRegistry (register + dispatch + when-clause filter), SettingsBridge (atomic write + watch debounce + relaunch trigger), AuthBridge (pairing → bearer round trip with mock daemon).
- Smoke E2E test: app boots, navigates Planning → Beads → Swarm → Debugging stages, cleanly shuts down.
close_reason: Decomposed into 6 modules under apps/desktop/src/main/ + thin main.ts (101 lines, ≤200 DOD): BackendLifecycle (Go-binary spawn, findOpenPort, ServerListeningDetector wiring, TERM→KILL escalation; ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE codepath dropped); UpdateMachine (typed wrapper around vendored reducers); IpcRegistry (typed registry with when-clause filter, replaces string-switch); WindowManager (strict CSP, contextIsolation/sandbox/nodeIntegration:false, ready-to-show + did-finish-load reveal); SettingsBridge (three-store atomic write, BOUNDED PubSub, relaunch hook); AuthBridge (pairing→bearer→WS-token, redacted errors, no token logging). 15 unit tests across the 4 DOD-required modules pass; full suite 24 desktop tests; typecheck/lint/test/build green; UBS clean

title: Phase 4: Daemon RPCs for VPS WIP reads (getWorkingTreeStatus, getStagedDiff, getUnstagedDiff, getUnpushedCommits)
description:
WHAT
====
Daemon-side endpoints exposing the VPS working tree's WIP state, since the local clone (which mirrors origin) can't see uncommitted/unpushed work.

ENDPOINTS (§2.6, §7.7)
=======================
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/status — branch, head SHA, ahead/behind, dirty file counts.
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/staged-diff — diff of staged changes.
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/unstaged-diff — diff of working tree vs HEAD.
- GET /v1/projects/{projectId}/git/unpushed-commits — list of commits ahead of origin/<branch>.

WHY (§7.7 'What it shows vs what daemon RPCs show')
=====================================================
Anything on the VPS that hasn't been pushed yet (committed but not pushed, staged but not committed, working-tree edits in flight) is NOT in the local clone and shouldn't be. Those WIP reads come from these RPCs that inspect the VPS working tree directly. The cockpit composes both: file history and current pushed contents from the local clone, plus a small 'VPS WIP' overlay layered on top from the daemon.

CONSIDERATIONS
==============
- These endpoints expose POTENTIALLY large diffs for big edits — paginate via line ranges or chunked response.
- Status endpoint is cheap; can be polled every few seconds while a swarm is running.
- Diff endpoints are expensive; cache results keyed by project + commit SHA + working-tree mtime.
- Audit reads — these go through the §1.4 'every automation must be inspectable' principle (read accesses logged but at lower verbosity than writes).

CROSS-REFERENCES
================
- §2.6 — Git endpoints.
- §7.7 — composed view.
- §1.1 — VPS working tree is canonical for uncommitted state.

DEFINITION OF DONE
==================
- All four endpoints return data matching `git status` / `git diff --staged` / `git diff` / `git rev-list origin/<branch>..HEAD`.
- Pagination tested for large diffs.
- Caching tested.
- Used by Beads drawer (Phase 7+) and Swarm dashboard 'agent activity overlay' (Phase 8).
